name: Center for Student Engagement
description: Root organization
summary: Registered student organizations organization

name: Sport Clubs
description:
The University of Tennessee Sport Clubs program provides the perfect venue and resources for a wide variety of student led clubs. Sport Club members participate in competitive, recreational, and instructional activities, and their experiences range from competitive team play and national travel to casual involvement and outdoor adventures.

Clubs provide students with lifelong friends, a healthy lifestyle, and opportunities for leadership and personal development. More than likely there is a Sport Club for you, but if not, new clubs are always welcome.
summary: The Sport Clubs program oversees the 35+ clubs here at the University of Tennessee. This page provides information for club officers and members. Please search for specific Sport Clubs if interested in joining a club.

name: Men's Rugby Club at The University of Tennessee
description: UTRFC was founded in 1970 and has grown into a cornerstone of collegiate rugby in the southeast. The club competes in the Southeastern Collegiate Rugby Conference against other southeastern universities in the Division 1AA collegiate rugby league. Players practice 2-3 times a week to improve themselves on the field as players/club members and off the field to ensure their success at the university. A Tennessee Rugby player follows the standards of personal responsibility, sound decision making, integrity, pride, organization, and respect to represent their university and club in the highest arena of competition. Tennessee Rugby players always keep their priorities in order and lead a disciplined lifestyle in compliance to the Tennessee Rugby Maxim: Family, School, Rugby.
summary:
Men's Rugby Club at The University of Tennessee, 
D1AA/SCRC Men's Rugby Club
Follow our Instagram and Twitter page to stay up to date on all things men's rugby!

name: Advancement of Neuroscience at UTK
description:
Fall 2025 Interest Meeting: August 28, 2025 @ 5:45PM in HSS 111

We here at the Advancement of Neuroscience share one simple goal, a mission that we believe echoes our university's volunteer spirit: to support individuals with a passion for science, equipping and preparing them to use their skills and experience to elevate their own communities. To accomplish this mission, we offer several kinds of events including journal clubs and lectures, STEM-focused professional development workshops, neuro-based community service opportunities, and engaging socials.  If any of this description resonates with you, please join our GroupMe here:

https://groupme.com/join_group/109624473/u1nhecMz
summary: An undergraduate student organization to promoting interest in neuroscience-related topics including research, and to striving to network like-minded students, faculty, and other professionals.

name: Adventist Christian Fellowship @ UTK
description: We are a branch of Adventist Christian Fellowship on the campus of University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Our house is about relationship, conversations, dialogue about all things in life-hopefully in ways that are uplifting, engaging and fun. The purpose of the Adventist Christian Fellowship @UTK is to provide a place of belonging to a community, believing in an unbelievably loving God, and becoming a messenger of that love to others. Membership is open to all students, and faculty/staff members where appropriate, regardless of race, color, national origin, religion, sex, pregnancy, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, physical or mental disability, or covered veteran status.
summary: Adventist Christian Fellowship @ UTK is an organization dedicated to sharing the Love of Jesus Christ and connecting with others to share the Joy of an unbelievably loving God.

name: African Student Association
description:
Mission:

At The University of Tennessee, Knoxville, we the African Student Association are committed to developing unity, strength, and community among African and non-African students. We work to raise awareness about African culture and issues, as well as clarify misconceptions and educate people about Africa. We aim to serve the African diaspora by celebrating and promoting African culture at the institution, in the local community, and beyond, with the purpose of bridging the gap between the African diaspora.
summary: The African Student Association (ASA) is a campus organization that welcomes both African and non-African students. Our primary objective is to promote mutual understanding, appreciation, and collaboration among individuals from diverse backgrounds.

name: All Campus Theatre
description: All Campus Theatre is the University of Tennessee's undergraduate theatre association. We produce several events and activities that offer the experience of theatre to students, including full productions, original works, and more. Everyone is welcome, no matter your experience, interests, or major. If you are interested in acting, directing, stage managing, designing, writing, or anything else in theatre, this is the place for you!
summary: All Campus Theatre is the University of Tennessee's undergraduate theatre association. We produce  several events and activities that offer the experience of theatre to students, including full productions, original works, and more. Everyone is welcome.

name: Alpha Chi Omega
description:
Alpha Chi Omega is a women’s fraternity founded on Oct. 15, 1885, at DePauw University in Greencastle, Ind. Today, there are more than 200,000 members in more than 130 collegiate chapters and 200 alumnae chapters nationwide who are served not only by the fraternity’s National Council, but also by the Alpha Chi Omega Foundation and our National Housing Corporation.

Our national philanthropic focus is supporting the victims of domestic violence, and locally Delta Pi raises money for and volunteers at the YWCA and the Family Justice Center. We raise hundreds of dollars annually for those organizations through events we sponsor such as CarniVol and Dodging Domestic Violence Dodgeball Tournament.

Delta Pi encourages its members to excel in academics and leadership, and Alpha Chis are involved all over campus and throughout the community individually. But we also believe in being active as sisters, and we proved our Greek spirit by winning Greek Week 2012!

Alpha Chi Omega originated within a music school, and music and the arts are an important emphasis within the fraternity – as symbolized by our badge, a Grecian lyre. We designate each February as MacDowell Month (named for the MacDowell artist colony in New Hampshire, Alpha Chi’s first philanthropic project) and encourage members to attend, celebrate, patronize and participate in the arts.

As one of the first groups to take residence in UT’s Sorority Village, we look forward to a new era in Delta Pi sisterhood. With room for 52 residents and facilities that will accommodate both collegiate and alumnae events, our beautiful house represents our commitment to the past, present and future of Alpha Chi Omega.
summary: Alpha Chi Omega, Delta Pi chapter, is a women’s fraternity.

name: Alpha Delta Pi
description:
Our story begins in 1851 at Wesleyan Female College in Macon, Georgia, where six incredible women changed the world. Led by Eugenia Tucker Fitzgerald, our founders formed The Adelphean Society for  “the mental, moral, social, and domestic improvement of its members.” The Adelphean Society, later renamed Alpha Delta Pi, was the first secret society for college women. The principles of scholarship, leadership, sisterhood, and service guide over 215,000 women in more than 140 active collegiate chapters and over 150 alumnae associations of Alpha Delta Pi.

The Alpha Kappa Chapter at The University of Tennessee was founded in 1920. Our sisters strive for excellence in philanthropy, academic performance, and campus leadership. In keeping with our motto, “We Live for Each Other,” our members have found a place for support, shared values, and personal development. 

See a glimpse into our sisterhood here: ADPi Sisterhood Video 2024
summary: Established in 1851, Alpha Delta Pi is committed to sisterhood, values and ethics, high academic standards and social responsibility.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta: National Health Preprofessional Honor Society
description: Alpha Epsilon Delta is a pre-health honor society that strives to serve students interested in pursuing professional careers, including medicine, dentistry, pharmacy, optometry, and physician assistant. We offer professional speakers, philanthropic opportunities, and visits to professional schools. If you are interested in joining, please email us at aedutk@gmail.com and visit http://aedutk.wixsite.com/aed-utk to learn more about our club!
summary: Alpha Epsilon Delta National Health Preprofessional Honor Society

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated
description:
Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated had its humble beginnings on Wednesday January 15, 1908 on the campus of Howard University by 16 illustrious women with a purpose to cultivate and encourage high scholastic and ethical standards, to promote unity and friendship among college women, to study and help alleviate problems concerning girls and women in order to improve their social stature, to maintain a progressive interest in college life, and to be of service to ALL mankind. Since then, the sorority has flourished into a globally-impactful organization of nearly 300,000 college-trained members, bound by the bonds of sisterhood and empowered by a commitment to servant-leadership that is both domestic and international in its scope

The Zeta Delta chapter of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated was the FIRST black greek lettered organization for women on the campus of the University of Tennessee at Knoxville chartered on Saturday, December 12, 1970 by 16 oh so phenomenal women who believed in bringing leadership and service to the community. As a chapter of leaders and scholars we pride ourselves in holding the highest GPA amongst all the NPHC organizations.
summary: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc. is a service organization composed of distinguished women committed to bettering the community, excelling in academics and strengthening relationships among all women.

name: Alpha Omicron Pi
description: Alpha Omicron Pi was founded on January 2, 1897 at Barnard College in New York. The Omicron chapter at UTK, founded in 1902, is the longest existing chapter of AOII. Our members are heavily involved throughout campus in organizations such as Student Alumni Associates, SGA, Orientation Leaders, Ambassadors, ACE, Order of Omega, various honor societies, and the Panhellenic Council, just to name a few! However, AOII’s involvement is not limited to the University of Tennessee. We continually strive to improve our community through service, education, and engagement. We work closely with our philanthropy, the Arthritis Foundation, to raise money and teach others about the affects of arthritis in all ages. Our primary philanthropy events include "Strike Out Arthritis" and "AOII Barbecue", which is the longest standing philanthropy event at UTK. Along with the Arthritis Foundation, support our local philanthropy, Blood Cancer United, and advocate for mental health awareness through our annual "Alpha Love Day". AOII is excited about sharing our sisterhood and involvement with each potential new member coming through recruitment this fall!
summary: Alpha Omicron Pi

name: Alpha Phi Alpha
description:
Since its founding on December 4, 1906, Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc. has vcolor around the world.

Alpha Phi Alpha, the first intercollegiate Greek-letter fraternity established for African-Americans,was at Cornell University in Ithaca, New York by seven college men who recognized the need for a strong bond of Brotherhood among African descendants in this country.

The visionary founders, known as the "Jewels" of the Fraternity, are Henry Arthur Callis, Charles Henry Chapman, Eugene Kinckle Jones, George Biddle Kelley, Nathaniel Allison Murray, Robert Harold Ogle, and Vertner Woodson Tandy.

The Fraternity initially served as a study and support group for minority students who faced racial prejudice, both educationally and socially, at Cornell. The Jewel founders and early leaders of the Fraternity succeeded in laying a firm foundation for Alpha Phi Alpha's principles of scholarship, fellowship, good character, and the uplifting of humanity.

The opening of the school year, 1975 - 1976, found at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, Tennessee, a group of African - American students distributed in the various colleges of the University who were desirous of maintaining more intimate contact with one another than their classroom study permitted. These students aspired to become members of Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Incorporated. And on, May 29, 1976, the Mu Iota Chapter was chartered in Knoxville, Tennessee.

This group of men became known as Genesis 15 and sparked the initial flame that ignited the light of Alpha on the University of Tennessee's campus.
summary: Mighty Mu Iota Chapter of Alpha Phi Alpha

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description: Alpha Phi Omega is a Co-ed National Service Organization founded on the principles of Leadership, Friendship and Service. It provides its members the opportunity to develop leadership skills as they volunteer on their campus, in their community, to the nation, and to the organization. With more than 400,000 members on over 375 campuses, Alpha Phi Omega continues to provide more service on more campuses than any other collegiate service organization.
summary: Leadership, Frendship, Service.

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
description:
We are the student chapter of AIChE at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville committed to enhancing the Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering network on campus. AIChE as a global organization is dedicated to promoting the success and professional ethics of chemical engineers.

Meetings are held every other week on Wednesday evenings during the Fall and Spring semesters hosting guest speakers, with numerous additional networking sessions, social events, research panels, and service opportunities in between.

Our suborganizations include Women in Chem-E (WIC) and the Chem-E Car Team.
summary: American Institute of  Chemical Engineers (AIChE)

name: American Institute of Aeronautics & Astronautics
description:
With more than 31,000 members, AIAA is the largest professional and technical society, principal voice, and information resource devoted to the progress of engineering and science in aviation and space. Our mission is to advance the arts, sciences, and technology of aeronautics and astronautics, and to promote the professionalism of those engaged in these pursuits. At both the national and state level, AIAA fulfills its commitment to undergraduate students by providing scholarships for exceptional engineering students.

The student chapter of the Tennessee section of AIAA at the University of Tennessee is actively involved in enriching the undergraduate experience of Aerospace Engineering majors by providing aerospace-related professional, educational, and social experiences.
summary: American Institute of Aeronautics & Astronautics (AIAA) is an international professional based organization rooted in the Aerospace industry. We hold general body meetings with industry speakers, social events, and industry trips.

name: American Medical Student Association @ UTK
description: The American Medical Student Association (AMSA) is committed to improving healthcare and healthcare delivery to all people; promoting active improvement in medical education; involving its members in the social, moral, and ethical obligations in the profession of medicine; assisting in the improvement and understanding of world health problems; contributing to the welfare of medical students, premedical students, interns, residents and post–MD/DO trainees; and advancing the profession of medicine.
summary: The American Medical Student Association is an independent, national organization that exists to support, inform, and inspire tomorrow's physicians. We are committed to making our campus a better place through healthcare education and advocacy.

name: American Nuclear Society
description: Founded in 1959, the University of Tennessee ANS Student Sections is one of the oldest sections in the Country. We represent anyone who aspires to work in the nuclear industry or advocate for nuclear science, nuclear engineers, and other majors alike. Throughout the year, we host social events and meetings. Some events are nuclear-related, others not so much. We hope to create an enjoyable experience for students so they continue to stay active in the nuclear field.
summary:
Student Section of the American Nuclear Society

Working to promote nuclear science and connect our members with research and career opportunities. Students outside of Nuclear Engineering are welcome!

name: American Society of Agricultural and Biological Engineers
description: ASABE is the professional society for Agricultural and Biological Engineers internationally. The University of Tennessee Student Chapter is very active both locally, regionally, and internationally. Our chapter hosts several events such as: industry tours, professional development speakers, networking activities, social outgoings, and service projects. We also attend the Southeastern Regional Rally each spring and regularly have an officer elected to the Regional Officer Board. Our chapter competes and places in engineering competitions at the university level, regionally and internationally.
summary: American Society of Agricultural and Biological Engineers (ASABE)

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
description:
The UTK ASCE Student Chapter strives to prepare young engineers for the professional working environment. The chapter is dedicated to volunteering in the community as well as providing learning opportunities for all levels of students. 

  • Professional speakers attend every meeting to discuss challenges and projects in their profession. 
  • Students participate in volunteering events throughout both semester, helping organizations in the community as well as performing clean-up at local creeks. 
  • The ASCE Mid-South Regional Student Symposium in the spring semester brings universities from Tennessee and Arkansas together to compete in competitions spanning all concentrations of civil engineering. Some examples are the concrete canoe, steel bridge, surveying, professional paper, innovation contest, and sustainable solutions.
summary: Founded in 1852, the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) represents more than 140,000 members of the civil engineering profession worldwide and is America's oldest national engineering society, consisting of student and professional chapters.

name: Student American Veterinary Medical Association Chapter
description: We are the Student American Veterinary Medical Association Chapter at the University of Tennessee College of Veterinary Medicine. Our mission is to provide the students of the UTCVM with educational, leadership, and professional development opportunities through our clubs and scholarships as well as to promote a friendly spirit among the students enrolled in our college.
summary: Student American Veterinary Medical Association Chapter (SAVMA Chapter)

name: Volunteers in Earth and Water
description:
Volunteers in Earth and Water was created by merging and revamping the student chapters of American Water Resources Association (AWRA) and American Water Works Association/Clean Water Professionals (AWWA/CWP).

The University of Tennessee's Water Professionals is a water and environment focused organization aiming to bring together students with a variety of backgrounds to not only network and collaborate with one another, but also to give back to the community of Knoxville.

The goal of this group is to create a platform for community involvement, networking, and learning for students across any department at UTK interested in all things water and environment!

Our 2025 Fall meeting dates are TBD, but you can follow us on Instagram @utk_view to learn more!
summary: We are an interdisciplinary club focused on assisting Knoxville in all things water and the environment. We specialize in hosting lunch-and-learns in which professional speakers present on topics relating to civil engineering and water resources.

name: Asian American Association
description: Promotes awareness of the Asian American community and create a space for students to share experiences and make friends.
summary: The Asian American Association is made up of undergraduate and graduates students of the University of Tennessee promoting diversity, culture, and inclusion through the Asian American student body.

name: Bachelor's Social Work Student Organization
description:
BSWO is a Student-Run Organization in the undergraduate College of Social Work. It stands for Bachelor's Student Social Work Organization. Meetings will be held in The Student Union, Room #169 on Tuesday's 11:20 AM- 12:35 PM. 
 
Purpose of BSWO:
1. To enhance personal and professional development of undergraduate social work students;
2. To facilitate open conversations with faculty regarding social justice, current events, and ideas for making our college a safe, inclusive, diverse and welcoming environment;
3. To provide community service to local social service organizations;
4. To increase visibility of the social work profession and the UTCSW;
5. To create a community within the UTCSW for students to explore passions and future careers in social work;
6. To foster personal and professional connections and provide networking opportunities; and
7. To acknowledge systemic injustices in our school, community, and country, and collectively commit to listening, learning, and working tirelessly to be accepting of all religions, to be anti-racist, anti-ableist, anti-ageist, anti-sexist, and to be an ally to the LGBTQ+ and BIM-PoC communities and other underrepresented populations.
summary: Bachelor's Social Work Student Organization (BSWO)

name: Badminton Club at the University of Tennessee
description:
The University of Tennessee–Knoxville Badminton Club is a registered student organization located on the main campus of the University of Tennessee in Knoxville, TN.

 

We promote the sport of badminton with regular practices, organizing competitions, and participating in regional tournaments.

 

We are undergraduates, graduates, faculty, and staff who are enthusiastic about Badminton.
summary: Badminton Club at the University of Tennessee

name: Ballroom Dance Team at the University of Tennessee
description:
We strive to introduce the art and sport of ballroom dancing to the local Knoxville and campus community in an inclusive and encouraging environment. We teach our members American Smooth, American Rhythm, and a handful of other fun social dances (see full list below), preparing them for the dance floor.  We offer support for those who are interested in intercollegiate competitions and small performances around the campus area (NOT REQUIRED). All personal backgrounds and levels of experience are welcome.

 

Dances we teach include the following:

 

American Smooth:

- Waltz

- Foxtrot

- Tango

- Viennese Waltz

 

American Rhythm:

- Rumba

- East Coast Swing

- Cha cha

- Bolero

- Mambo

 

Miscellaneous:

- Salsa

- Bachata

- Merengue 

- Jive

- Samba

- Hustle

- West Coast Swing

- Country Swing

- Country Two Step
summary: We teach social and competitive ballroom dance to the campus community in an inclusive and encouraging environment.

name: Bangladesh Students Association
description: BSA is a student organization aims to promote and highlight the rich and diverse culture, history, and tradition of Bangladesh by organizing multifarious events at University of Tennessee, Knoxville.
summary: Bangladesh Students' Association

name: Baptist Collegiate Ministry
description:
The Baptist Collegiate Ministry (BCM) at The University of Tennessee is a place where students can come to connect with other students, connect with the UT campus, and connect with God on their own spiritual journey. It is a place to come and hang out with people in-between classes, learn more about doing missions, worship with us on Thursday nights, and just experience life with those around you at UT. We hope that you come to visit and hopefully find your place on campus here at the BCM.

 

The Baptist Collegiate Ministry is supported by The Tennessee Baptist Mission Board, but encourages all denominations to participate.
summary: The BCM seeks to make Christ known at the University of Tennessee through providing opportunities for spiritual growth, making friends, leadership training, service, and missions.

name: Bass Fishing Club
description: The Bass Fishing Team at UT is one of the top performing collegiate bass fishing organizations in the nation. We strive to grow as a team, anglers, and to show our efforts in environmental conservation.
summary: Bass Fishing Club at UT

name: Beta Alpha Psi - Alpha Lambda Chapter (Accounting)
description:
Founded in 1919, Beta Alpha Psi is an honor organization for accounting and financial information students and professionals. There are over 300 chapters on college and university campuses with over 300,000 members initiated since Beta Alpha Psi's formation. All of our chapters are AACSB and/or EQUIS accredited. We are not a fraternity or sorority but an honors organization.

 

The mission of Beta Alpha Psi, as a premier international honor and service organization for financial and business information students and professionals, is to inspire and support excellence by:

  • encouraging the study and practice of accountancy, finance, business analytics and digital technology;
  • providing opportunities for service, professional development, and interaction among members and financial professionals; and
  • fostering lifelong ethical, social, and public responsibilities.

Membership in Beta Alpha Psi is limited to those persons of good moral character who have achieved scholastic and/or professional excellence in the fields related to accounting, finance, business analytics or digital technology; who have been initiated according to the official membership ceremony; and who are in good standing.
summary:
Beta Alpha Psi, Alpha Lambda Chapter #34
University of Tennessee Knoxville

name: Beta Theta Pi
description:
Becoming a Member

 

Membership in Beta Theta Pi is based on finding men who believe and live by the same set of fraternal values. These values, the Fraternity's objects, are the basis for recruitment, pledging and a lifelong membership in Beta Theta Pi.

Recruitment

Formal recruitment is usually a one or two week period at the beginning of each semester hosted by the local Interfraternity Council (IFC). Most fraternities will host events and provide the opportunity to meet members and learn about the organization's values. However, you don't necessarily need to participate in formal recruitment to join Beta Theta Pi. Informal recruitment lasts all year and you should seek out Betas on your campus anytime to learn more about the Fraternity. Beta Theta Pi isn't for everyone. The Fraternity seeks to extend membership to a select few individuals who maintain a lifelong devotion to intellectual excellence, high standards of moral conduct and responsible citizenship. By joining Beta Theta Pi, you commit yourself to a high standard that all members are expected to meet.

Pledging

Chapter pledging programs are designed to orient new members to Fraternity history, traditions and practices while preparing each future brother for a lifetime of fraternal brotherhood. The primary objective of a pledge program is to produce informed, enthused and principled new members ready to step into leadership roles. Beta Theta Pi has no place for programs that create artificial probation, second-class citizenship, or hazing. Every chapter is required by Standard Chapter Operating Expectations to submit a written pledge program to be certified annually as 100% positive.

Initiation

Initiation is a special ceremony where the deepest symbolic meaning of the Fraternity is revealed and the full privileges of membership are extended to new members. While the ceremony has taken different forms, more than 180,000 members have been initiated since 1839. For all members in Beta Theta Pi, it is the beginning of a lifetime of friendship, learning and personal growth.

Lifelong Friendships

Beta Theta Pi is not just a four year experience. It is an lifelong experience that will engender lasting bonds of friendship and brotherhood. From undergraduate leadership opportunities to local alumni associations and General Conventions, the lifelong bonds of Beta brotherhood are inescapable. Learn more about how Betas remain involved after graduation.
summary: Beta Theta Pi, a Social IFC fraternity, which strives to uphold the values of Aid and Mutual Assistance, Cultivation of Intellect, Trust, Responsibility, and Integrity. We strive to uphold the values we were founded on in 1839. http://www.betathetapi.org

name: Beta Upsilon Chi - Alpha Zeta Chapter
description:
Beta Upsilon Chi (BYX) exists to establish brotherhood and unity among college men based on the common bond of Jesus Christ. BYX is more than a 4-year experience; it is a lifelong brotherhood that extends to over 35 chapters and 2,500 members across the nation. Whether it be date parties, formals, trips, philanthropy events, community worship nights, homecoming, weekly small groups, member retreats, intramurals, or professional networking events, BYX aims to develop men spiritually, socially, and academically.

 

The Alpha Zeta Chapter of Beta Upsilon Chi at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville was founded in 2012 and joined the Interfraternity Council (IFC) in 2018. The Alpha Zeta Chapter has excelled since its founding, having one of the fastest and most sustainable growths in BYX history. Today, BYX has grown to over 150 members, who are highly engaged in the UT and Knoxville community. BYX proudly supports its local philanthropy, Knox Area Rescue Ministries (KARM) by service hours and fundraisers to help fulfill KARM’s mission of “restoring lives in Jesus’ name” for the homeless of Knoxville.

 

In its young existence, the Alpha Zeta Chapter has excelled on and off campus, receiving many awards and recognitions, most notably: 2016, 2021, and 2023 Homecoming Champions, 2018 BYX Chapter of the Year, 2020 IFC Chapter of the Year from the University of Tennessee, 2020 IFC Chapter President of the Year from the University of Tennessee, and 2025 BYX Nationals Pledgeship Award. BYX has also achieved the highest GPA every year since joining IFC, currently touting a chapter-wide 3.57 GPA.

 

If you are interested to learn more about joining BYX, please visit byx.org or tennbyx.org, or check out our Instagram @tennbyx.

 

“Behold, how good and how pleasant it is for brothers to dwell together in unity!”

Psalms 133:1
summary: Beta Upsilon Chi, a National Christian Fraternity, exists for the purpose of establishing brotherhood and unity among college men based on the common bond of Jesus Christ. The Alpha Zeta Chapter of The University of Tennessee was founded in 2012.

name: Biomedical Engineering Society Student Chapter
description: BMES at UTK is a student-led organization focused on academic and pre-professional development and outreach with an emphasis on the field of Biomedical Engineering. However, students of all majors are welcome to join.
summary: UTK Student Chapter of the Biomedical Engineering Society

name: Black Cultural Programming Committee
description:
The Black Cultural Programming Committee at the University of Tennessee was established in 1979 by the Office of the Dean of Students. Since its inception, the Committee's objectives have been to:

  • Develop and present programs and activities that enhance public awareness of African-Americans and their accomplishments within the international society;
  • Foster an atmosphere for the interaction and unity of African-American students within the University community;
  • Provide an opportunity for African-American students to maintain positive and continuous communication/involvement with the academic and local communities;
  • Serve as a catalyst to motivate the African-American students' self-development, discovery, and esteem during their educational career at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville.
summary: The Black Cultural Programming Committee develop and present programs and activities that enhance the University community’s awareness of African-American culture & its contributions to our society.

name: Black Educators of Tomorrow
description:
We are a trailblazing group of young Educators who want to increase awareness of issues related to diversity and its influence on the teaching profession. 

Our objective is to build a network of young leaders that will provide support, enhance development, and increase communication among miniority and all future educators at the University of TN, Knoxville.  Educators Rock!
summary: Black Educators of Tomorrow. We are a trailblazing group of young, future Educators who want to increase awareness of issues related to diversity and its influence on the teaching profession. If you believe in the power of education, we are for you!

name: Block and Bridle Club
description:
To promote a more complete understanding of the livestock industry among student members through hands-on activities, guest speaker sessions, and developing relationships with like-minded individuals. 

To promote animal agriculture by participating in a program of activities that will supplement students' interest in animal industries.

To enhance professionalism in those who will one day be leaders in the animal agriculture industry.

To bring about a closer relationship among all students pursuing some phase of agriculture as a profession.

Block & Bridle Club welcomes students of all majors!
summary: Block and Bridle Club

name: Student Dental Association
description:
We are a student-led organization whose goal is to help fellow students prepare for Dental school through undergraduate experience, community service, and exposure to careers in dental medicine.

What more information? Follow us on Instagram! @utk.sda

What to join? Fill out this Google Form! https://forms.gle/rtxPYHpLNjGW1NaB6
summary: Student Dental Association

name: Christian Volunteers
description: At Collegians for Christ (Christian Volunteers), we believe that God's Word provides the only solid foundation on which to build our lives. We believe that God has a purpose for every life here on campus and in the world. We desire to help students find God's will through teaching His Word. Our desire for this campus is that they would believe on Jesus Christ for eternal life.
summary: We are a group of students following Jesus Christ by studying God's Word, and meeting together to provide a supporting community for the students of the University of Tennessee.

name: Bridges International
description: Bridges International is a caring community of Christ-followers committed to serve, promote social connections and engage in spiritual conversations with international students so that students become leaders internationally.
summary: Bridges International

name: Calvary College
description:
Calvary College Ministry is a religious-based organization that seeks to serve the University, Knoxville community, and world with Christian principles in mind.  The Calvary College Ministry is centered on developing intimacy with God, community with believers, and influence on others.  The ministry is a part of Calvary Baptist Church in Knoxville, TN. 

Our mission, above all, is to lead people to a growing relationship with Jesus Christ. We at Calvary strive to know Jesus and to make him known! We hope you’ll join us soon!
summary: Religious-based service organization affiliated with Calvary Baptist Church

name: Cru
description:
Cru (formerly Campus Crusade for Christ) is an international, inter-denominational Christian organization. We are student-led and staff-directed. Whether investigating Jesus/Christianity from a distance or wanting to jump in and serve, all students are welcome at Cru!

We meet Thursday nights at 8:28 in AMB 210 for praise, message, and fellowship.

We also have Bible studies for each dorm as well as for off-campus students. If you are interested in a Bible study, please contact us so we can get you connected to a Bible study leader!

 

Lastly, we love building fellowship and community. Keep an eye out for fun events or student hangouts on campus. Along with these, Cru offers retreats throughout the year. These retreats typically take place over spring and fall breaks and are a great opportunity to get away from school and have fun with some new friends!

We would love to connect with you, and we hope to see you soon!
summary: Cru is a caring community that is passionate about connecting others to Christ. Join us for weekly meetings, Bible studies, and other fun events and retreats!

name: Canoe and Hiking Club
description:
The Canoe & Hiking club is a member-driven club, composed of diverse, open-minded individuals who share a common love for the outdoors. We organize and lead group trips for club members, provide an open forum for members to plan their own outings, and provide access to gear. Our trips include, but are not limited to: backpacking, canoeing, caving, climbing, kayaking, mountain biking, & rafting.

MEETINGS ARE 7:00 EVERY WEDNESDAY IN AMB 27!

 

2025-2026 GroupMe:

https://groupme.com/join_group/107532542/IWSaGazV
summary: Canoe and Hiking Club

name: VolCatholic
description:
The members of UT’s VolCatholic are so excited to meet you and welcome you to our thriving Catholic community! VolCatholic is a unique group on campus which aims to help you develop a deep relationship with God and provide a community that promotes a Catholic lifestyle through faith, fellowship, and service. No matter where you stand in your journey of faith, there is something for you at Saint John XXIII. 

For questions or more info about VolCatholic or VolCatholic events, contact VolCatholic co-directors:   Joe Furka at jfurka@vols.utk.edu and Rachel Rines at rrines@vols.utk.edu You can also follow us on instagram: @volcatholic and Facebook: VolCatholic
summary: VolCatholic strives to promote authentic Christian community through faith, fellowship, and service. Our main VolCatholic meetings are on Wednesday at 7pm, and Spirit & Truth is on Thursday at 6:30pm, at St. John XXIII University Parish located on campus

name: Women's Coordinating Council
description:
The Women's Coordinating Council is a student-led organization that prioritizes programming and activism and seeks to empower folks from all gender, ethnic, social, and economic backgrounds using an intersectional feminist lens. & advocate for equity among all social groups.

 

The programs we provide focus on highlighting our four core pillars:

I. Anti-Racism

II. Gender Equity

III. LGBTQ+ Inclusivity

IV. Reproductive Justice
summary: The Women's Coordinating Council is a student-led organization that prioritizes programming and activism and seeks to empower folks from all gender, ethnic, social, and economic backgrounds using an intersectional feminist lens.

name: All Campus Events
description: As a student-led organization created and run for the benefit of the students, faculty, alumni, and community at the University of Tennessee, ACE's mission is to provide fun, safe and inclusive events to foster the competitive and fun loving spirits of our participants and keep some of the most longstanding UT traditions alive and prosperous. We strive to not only maintain but amplify and enrich the traditions that our fellow Volunteers have enjoyed for years and will continue to enjoy for years to come.
summary: All Campus Events Committee

name: Chess Club
description:
We accept all levels of chess play from beginner to master. If you would like to come and learn to play, feel free to drop by or contact us. We mostly play casual games with each other but sometimes solve chess puzzles and play in tournaments within the university as well as some throughout the state. Occasionally, we play chess variants such as bughouse, atomic, and 4-way chess in our tournaments and meetings.

This year we have been meeting for over-the-board chess twice a week, and we have been hosting unranked tournaments. We also host official ranked tournaments a couple times a year, generally 1-2 (UT Open and TN Open) in the Fall semester and none in the Spring.

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/53153174/cNZyYV9J
summary: The Chess Club at UTK aims to provide a social space for all players to enjoy and learn the intrinsic and challenging attributes of the game within the scope of chess puzzles, matches and tournaments.

name: Chi Alpha Campus Ministries
description:
The mission of Chi Alpha shall be to develop the spiritual life and abilities of the students in relation to God, to each other, and to the world at large within a Pentecostal Christian context and in a manner consistent with the teachings of the Assemblies of God USA. As part of this mission, Chi Alpha seeks to introduce fellow collegians to an authentic relationship with Jesus Christ. Chi Alpha plans to do this through:

 

  1. Prayer - Communication with God.
  2. Worship - Expressions of thanks through word, music, and service.
  3. Fellowship - Community of students developing quality relationships.
  4. Discipleship - Learning about Jesus and following His example.
  5. Mission - Living a life that reflects our relationship with God in what we say and do.

We meet weekly as a large group and hold a service for all interested students as well as having weekly D-Groups, and other events throughout the semester like retreats and conferences. We also give students the opportunity to serve and grow in leadership.
summary:
Chi Alpha is a God loving, people loving, missional community on campus at UTK.  We have weekly Discipleship Groups and a weekly worship gathering on campus to honor God and live as family. 
Worship - Family - Mission <www.utkchialpha.com>

name: Chi Epsilon, The Civil Engineering Honors Society
description: Chi Epsilon is a Civil Engineering Honors Society for civil engineering students that are in the top third of their sophomore, junior, or senior class. These students must also embody the 4 pillars of character, scholarship, sociability, and practicality. The mission of Chi Epsilon Honors Society is to achieve its vision through its 4 pillars: 1. Scholarship: Recognizing excellence and achievement in academic and professional endeavors. 2. Character: Upholding the integrity and responsibility of the civil engineering profession through our service. 3. Practicality: Educating and encouraging our members to improve our world through innovative and impactful solutions. 4. Sociability: Connecting our members while engaging the broader engineering community.
summary: Chi Epsilon is a Civil Engineering Honors Society for civil engineering students who are in the top third of their sophomore, junior, or senior class. These students must also embody the 4 pillars of character, scholarship, sociability, and practicality

name: Chi Omega
description:
Founded in 1895 at the University of Arkansas, Chi Omega is the largest women's fraternal organization in the world with over 310,000 initiates and 174 collegiate chapters. Throughout Chi Omega's long and proud history, the Fraternity has brought its members unequaled opportunities for personal growth and development. 
 

To learn more about our mission, vision, and symphony, please visit our national website:  http://www.chiomega.com/Home
summary: Chi Omegas are well balanced women who are involved on their campuses and in their communities. As a prominent national women's fraternity, Chi Omega provides countless opportunities for fun and friendship during college and beyond.

name: Knox CHOP
shortName: Campus House of Prayer at UTK
description:
Campus House of Prayer at UTK is comprised of students from various Christian faith traditions who convene regularly to build relationships, connect to promote mutuality and understanding, and collaborate through prayer & mission to serve the University of Tennessee community.

Convening and Connecting Events like weekly student-led worship and prayer sets foster trust across denominational lines, deepen our relationships, and create a common desire to impact the communities we serve. Other ways we convene is through affinity groups, like our weekly ROTC Bible study, that gathers UT cadets at the CHOP to fellowship, study God’s Word, and encourage one another in their Christian faith, or our Cohort gatherings that focus on spiritual growth using short-term intensives like our God Module. 

We collaborate across the body of Christ by aligning Kingdom resources and efforts. Onsite Prayer Tent events partner local ministries, churches and individuals to pray and bless our campus during stressful seasons of the year, like Finals Week. The Food Truck, designed to alleviate food insecurity on campus provides healthy, nutritious food to any member of the UT community who needs this assistance (or even prayer!) in a friendly, welcoming, judgment-free environment.

For more info, visit our website at www.knoxchop.org
summary: Campus House of Prayer is a community made up of students from various Christian faith traditions who meet regularly to build relationships, promote understanding and collaborate through prayer & mission to serve the University of Tennessee community.

name: Circle K International
description: Circle K International is the world’s largest student-led collegiate service organization, with thousands of members on hundreds of campuses on five continents across the globe. The collegiate branch of the Kiwanis International family, CKI is committed to developing leaders by offering unique service, leadership and fellowship opportunities that let CKI members leave college with real-life networking and advocating skills and service leadership. CKI members are dedicated to childhood development, environmental justice, serving those who are food insecure or lack housing and mental health. We also partner with UNICEF USA to raise awareness about the need of clean drinking water worldwide. insta: @circlek.utk
summary: Circle K is a community service organization that promotes leadership and good citizenship. Follow our instagram @circlek.utk for more news!

name: Classics Club at UT
description:
We are the primary club for the Classics Department, and we welcome all UT students who have any interest in the ancient Mediterranean World. The primary purpose of the club is to provide opportunities for UT Classics students and all UT students interested in Classics to meet other people with a shared interest in Classics. 

We also organize twice a month to give Classics students opportunities to socialize, mingle, network, hangout, and have fun together! Events include:

  • Saint George Orthodox Church's Annual Greek Fest
  • Nashville Parthenon Trip
  • Trivia 
  • Movie Nights
  • Game Nights

When sending out information about our upcoming events, our primary method of communication is through GroupMe, so if you are interested in joining Classics Club, contact Harper Smith or Aderynna Silavong, and they will add you to our GroupMe. Alternatively, simply request to join our organization here on VOLink, and we will add you to our mailing list to keep you up to date about all of our fun upcoming events. Please feel free to look at our Instagram (https://www.instagram.com/classicsclub.utk?igsh=MXh6eG80MmczeXg4OA%3D%3D) as well!

Classics Club is sponsored by the Classics Department, and our faculty adviser is Dr. Jessica Westerhold.
summary: Classics Club

name: Clement Hall Residents' Association
description:
Welcome! The Clement Hall Residents' Association (CHRA) is the student-led representative body of Clement Hall. Membership in CHRA is open, without discrimination, to all students living in Frank G. Clement Hall at the University of Tennessee--Knoxville. CHRA is a component of the United Residence Halls Council (URHC), the governing body of all campus residence halls. 

CHRA is here to serve the residents of Clement, and stives to promote harmonious community living, establish educational and social programming(such as our Alcohol Awareness programs...and Bacon Day!), and enhance the quality of residence hall life for the students in our hall--and across campus. 

 

The CHRA executive board consists of  positions often including a President, Vice President, Secretary, Treasurer, Event Coordinator, an Advertising Coordinator and two co-coordinators, two Resident Assistant liaisons, and an advisor [positions are subject to variation, according to the needs of the residence hall]. We encourage each floor of Clement Hall to have Floor Representatives, and we invite all residents to attend general body meetings.
summary: CHRA is a body of student representatives for Frank G. Clement Residence Hall. Any resident of Clement Hall is automatically a member of CHRA. CHRA strives to provide programs and opportunities for residents to engage with one another and our campus.

name: Clinic Vols
description:
Clinic Vols was established in 2002 to combat the shortage of school nurses in inner-city schools by training UTK students through East Tennessee Children's Hospital to be health clinic volunteers. While volunteering, you will assist elementary students who come to the nurse's clinic with cuts, scrapes, and bruises with basic first aid. 

Interested in joining our club? Email our VP of membership, Tyler Ebbert, at tebbert@vols.utk.edu, or join our messaging app at https://bumble.com/bff/invite/5d1093dc-ae2d-4a5a-98ff-3e8788fdf660
summary: We are a clinical volunteering organization that gives students the opportunity to work in the elementary schools of Knox County. Students are trained in basic first aid and then volunteer in school nurse's offices.

name: American Sign Language Club
description: We are a student-run organization whose goal is to educate people about the Deaf community, culture, and language in Knoxville and the surrounding areas. We welcome students to practice ASL and meet people who have similar interests regarding ASL and Deaf culture. All ASL levels are welcome to join, including people with limited or no experience who are interested in the Deaf community or American Sign Language.
summary: ASLK is a space for students interested in Deaf culture and American Sign Language to connect with each other on campus and interact at social events. ASLK is a space for people of all signing levels.

name: Theta Tau
description: The University of Tennessee's only professional co-ed engineering fraternity. Our three main pillars are community service, brotherhood, and professional development. Recruitment takes place the beginning of both spring and fall semesters. Receive more info by contacting us at contact@tennesseethetatau.com
summary: Theta Tau: The University of Tennessee chapter of Theta Tau Co-ed Professional Engineering Fraternity.

name: Tumble Club at the University of Tennessee
description:
Welcome to Tumble Club, where the pursuit of fun, fitness, and friendship collide! Our club is dedicated to providing a welcoming environment for individuals of all skill levels to explore the exciting world of flips, tricks, and stunts. Whether you're a seasoned tumbler or a complete beginner, our club is designed to cater to everyone

- Those who wish to carpool must contact an officer via the Groupme. We leave at 8:30 pm SHARP to head to Diamonds All-Stars gym.
- Fear not if you're new to tumbling, our club offers a supportive atmosphere with a dedicated coach to assist you. After a brief warm-up, you're free to explore your style, with guidance available whenever you need it. NO previous tumbling experience is required!

- Practice runs from 9:00 pm to 10:30 pm.
-We usually arrive back to campus around 11 P.M.

The first TWO times you come to Tumble Club are FREE! Bring along your friends and enjoy this time together. There's no obligation to join after these trial sessions. The third session you come, you are required to pay an officer club dues and become an official member. If you pay with a check, please make it out to "Tumble Club at UT." We understand if things come up and money is tight, luckily for you, we do offer a payment plan. Reach out to one of the officers for more information. 

If you plan on coming: We need you to fill out a waiver on V.O.L Link and a second waiver for the gym:
    Go to go.utk.edu 

    In the selection bar, click on "V.O.L. Link"
    Click the big, orange "ENTER" button
    Click "Log in" in the top right corner and log in
    Click "Organizations"
    Search "tumble" and click on the "Tumbling Club (Tumbling)" result
    Click on "Forms" on the left bar
    Click on "Sport Clubs Release Form 2023-2024"
    Complete and submit it

Next, when you get to the gym:

When you enter in the front door of the gym there is a scannable code DIRECTLY next to the door where you can access the waiver for Diamond All-Stars. This is also a mandatory waiver, and you must fill it out before you can practice.

You can wear whatever you feel comfortable wearing. Typically we all wear a t-shirt and some shorts. No shoes are required but you can not practice in socks. Shoes or barefoot only!

We do not compete. Tumble Club is a non-competitive, all-levels club. We don't require mandatory practices, offering flexibility to our members. Enjoy full access to gym facilities and expert coaching during practices – it's all about the joy of learning and doing cool tricks!
 
PERKS OF JOINING:
1- Full access to the gym facilities and coaches during practices.
2- Learn to do flips and other cool tricks!
3. Long lasting friendships!
 
For ANY questions or additional information, feel free to reach out via email, GroupMe, or Instagram.
Join Tumble Club for an exciting journey of skill development, fitness, and friendship!
summary: Tumbling Club

name: Tyson House Episcopal and Lutheran Campus Ministry
description:
Tyson House Lutheran-Episcopal Campus Ministry is a unique and vibrant ministry serving the University of Tennessee and surrounding community in Knoxville since the 1930s. As an ecumenical (i.e. multi-denomination) campus ministry, Tyson House welcomes students, faculty, and staff from diverse Christian traditions, creating an inclusive space for worship, fellowship, and service.

 

Tyson House's core mission revolves around providing spiritual nourishment and community through weekly events including Worship services, Communal meals, Bible/Faith studies, and Social & Service events. Our primary time for gathering together is on Sunday evenings at 6:04pm, offering a time to share a home-cooked meal provided by supporting congregations, followed by a worship service of the Holy Eucharist (Communion) in a blend of ancient traditions and modern style, seeking relevant engagement with each of our real, lived experiences. 

 

All are welcome, come and see!

 

Tyson House is made possible by the generous financial and relational support of our denominational partners: the Southeastern Synod of the Evangelical Lutheran Church of America (ELCA) and the Diocese of East Tennessee of the Episcopal Church.

Our Mission and Vision

Mission:
Gathering as community, grounding in worship, growing as disciples, giving in love.

Core values:
Worship (Word and Sacrament), Community (Affirming and Relationship), and Discipleship (Faith Formation, Leadership Development, and Outreach). 

Vision:
To be a Christ-centered place where all are known, affirmed, and fed.
summary: Tyson House is a Christian (Episcopal (Anglican) & Lutheran) community of students and young adults. We seek to foster a community of welcome, inclusion, and curiosity. So come with your questions and doubts, and let's explore a deeper faith together!

name: Undergraduate Anthropology Association
description:
The Undergraduate Anthropology Association encourages learning, promotes a community within the Anthropology Department for undergraduates, graduates, and faculty. We introduce students to professors and field professionals, giving them a chance to interact with others through lectures, trips, socials, fundraisers, and other events.
 
The UAA is an undergraduate association with a range of members from freshmen to seniors. Attending UAA meetings is the best way to become familiar with other anthropology students and to find out what is going on in the anthropology department at UT. The officers include helpful representatives for archaeology, cultural, and biological anthropology who are happy to answer any questions pertaining to their areas of interest.
 
**Stay up to date with UAA!**
- Follow us on Instagram: @UTK_UAA
- Join our GroupMe: UAA GroupMe
summary: An organization for undergrads interested in learning about and connecting with the anthropology community at UTK!

name: Tennessee Mock Trial
description: The University of Tennessee, Knoxville Mock Trial team is a student-run organization for those interested in litigation and the law, acting and improvisation, public speaking, and development of critical thinking skills. Students participate as attorneys and/or witnesses competing in mock trial competitions across the nation. The team generally consists of 25-40 UT students after completing fall auditions, making up 3-4 competition teams. We hold auditions every fall in late August and early September, and students selected for the team will compete throughout the entirety of that academic year. In the fall, we attend private invitational tournaments, and in the spring, we compete in the American Mock Trial Association Championship Series. This series consists of a Regional Tournament, an Opening Round Championship Series (ORCS) Tournament, and a National Championship Tournament, consecutively. We also host our own private invitational tournament in the fall. If you have any more questions, please email us at utmocktrial@gmail.com or visit our website, utmocktrial.com
summary: The University of TN, Knoxville Mock Trial team is a student-run organization for those interested in law, acting, and/or public speaking, where we compete in tournaments that mimic real-life jury court trials. We hold auditions every Fall.

name: Undergraduate Nutrition Student Association
description:
UNSA is an organization at The University of Tennessee for nutrition majors and nutrition-interested students. We offer networking opportunities for students and many community outreach endeavors. We have monthly meetings during the school year where speakers with a Nutrition degree discuss their careers and how they got to where they are. It is our goal that the University of Tennessee undergraduate nutrition community can join together and connect throughout their time at this university.

If you would like to join UNSA, please first make sure you have logged in to VolLink with your NetID and password. Once you have done this, you will be able to see the "Join" button near the top right-hand corner of our page. Please reach out with any questions for us!
summary: Undergraduate Nutrition Student Association (UNSA)

name: Undergraduate Research Ambassadors
description: The Undergraduate Research Ambassadors are dedicated to promoting undergraduate research, helping connect students and faculty members to research positions, and creating an undergraduate research community at UT. As our capstone event each year, we host UT's Annual Undergraduate Research Symposium. More information about that, as well as our other activities, can be found at http://www.ursautk.org/.
summary: Undergraduate Research Ambassadors

name: United Residence Halls Council
description:
What is URHC?

The United Residence Halls Council can be traced back as far as 1972 and is the University's unique example of the nationwide Residence Hall Associations.

Our Purpose:

The purpose of URHC is to unite the students living in residence halls through leadership, programming, service, and education.

What we do:

Throughout the year, URHC strives to carry on long-standing traditions while building programs we have organized in the past. Successful programs we have organized include Football Watch Parties, Carnival, Craft + Game Nights, and more. Each of our members also carries out a passion project which can be a smaller program formed around a topic of their choosing.

Membership:

All residents currently living on campus at the University of Tennessee in University Housing are full members of URHC. Members are not required to pay any dues.
summary: United Residence Halls Council (URHC) is a student organization that serves on-campus residents, as well as the residence hall councils and resident assistants, with the goal of making University Housing more of a "home away from home."

name: Men's Volleyball Club
description:
The main goal of the Men's Volleyball Club is to promote men's volleyball throughout the University of Tennessee campus. We have members of all skill levels, ranging from beginner to years of experience. We travel throughout the year to various schools including UGA, Auburn, Alabama, North Carolina, as well as others. We play throughout both semesters, ending with our National Tournament.

In addition to competing in tournaments, we use the club to make friends and promote leadership and team building skills. Many of our members become long-lasting friends, even after their collegiate career. Through this club, we hope to not only improve volleyball skills, but also increase leadership and a sense of family throughout the members of our club.
summary: Men's Volleyball Club at the University of Tennessee

name: Model United Nations at UTK
description: As a student-led organization created and run for the benefit of the students, Model United Nations at UTK’s mission is to provide fun and safe opportunities for our members to engage in competitive debate and promote the spirit of diplomacy in all things we do. Members will have the opportunity to travel to other schools and cities across the eastern United States and compete against delegates from all over the world. When not traveling, they’ll have the chance to help organize similar events at our own two conferences hosted annually: VolMUN & TennMUN. Model UN is for all students interested in traveling and competing or those who are just looking to find a community. Newcomers are always welcome to stop by and discover what Model UN is like. To join, simply contact us or attend one of our meetings. We hope to see you there!
summary: Model United Nations at the University of Tennessee

name: Multicultural Greek Council
description:
The Multicultural Greek Council (MGC) at the University of Tennessee-Knoxville is composed of six fraternities and sororities: Delta Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc., Lambda Phi Epsilon Fraternity, Inc., Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Inc., Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc., Sigma Beta Rho Fraternity Inc., and Sigma Sigma Rho Sorority, Inc. These six organizations, acknowledging their significance on campus and in the community, came together to bring solidarity to the Multicultural Greek society and increase awareness through philanthropic endeavors, academic achievements, and the development of campus leaders.

For more information on the individual organizations that make up MGC, check out the national websites of the organizations:

Delta Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc.

https://www.deltaphilambda.org/

 

Lambda Phi Epsilon Fraternity, Inc.

https://lambdaphiepsilon.com/



Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Inc.

http://lambdalady.org/

Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc.

https://thelambdas.org/

 

Sigma Beta Rho Fraternity, Inc.

https://www.sigrho.org/

 

Sigma Sigma Rho Sorority, Inc.

http://www.sigmasigmarho.com/
summary: The Multicultural Greek Council is the governing body of the University of Tennessee-Knoxville's six culture-based fraternities and sororities

name: Muslim Student Association
description: The Muslim Student Association at the University of Tennessee-Knoxville creates a community on campus for students and engages with the greater UTK and Knoxville community through our events! In maintaining that connection to the community, we hold weekly, monthly and annual events! This includes weekly halaqas and open board meetings; monthly community service, prayer, and bonding activities; Ramadan events; discussion panels; an annual retreat and Fast-a-thon; and so much more!
summary: The MSA at the UTK serves to educate students and faculty through recreational activities as well as academic events. In this way, we strive to establish a bridge of understanding between people of all backgrounds, races, and creeds.

name: NABA Inc.
description:
The mission of NABA, Inc. is to address the professional needs of its members and to build leaders that shape the future of the accounting and finance profession with an unfaltering commitment to inspire the same in their successors.

       NABA seeks to unite membership of accountants, finance and business related professionals and students who have similar interests and ideals, are committed to professional and academic excellence, possess a sense of professional and civic responsibility, and are concerned with enhancing opportunities for minorities in the accounting, finance and business related professions.

       The goals include, but are not limited to, the following:

  • To promote and develop the professional skills of our members.
  • To encourage and assist minority students entering the accounting profession.
  • To provide opportunities for members to fulfill their civic responsibilities.
  • To promote public confidence in our members and the services they provide.
  • To encourage cordial relations among members and cooperative relationships with other professionals.
  • To represent the interests of current and prospective minority accounting professionals.
  • To ensure long-term financial stability and provide adequate resources to implement chapter, regional, and national programs.
summary: NABA seeks to unite the membership of accountants, finance and business-related professionals, and students who have similar interests and ideals who are committed to professional and academic excellence.

name: Women's Organization of MBAs
description: WOMBA is a not-for-profit organization dedicated to the advancement of business women as corporate leaders, executives, and entrepreneurs - enriching workforce diversity around the world. This organization is targeted toward graduate students in the MBA program. However, associate members are accepted from other graduate programs.
summary: Women's Organization of Masters of Business Administration at the University of Tennessee

name: National Residence Hall Honorary
description: The National Residence Hall Honorary (NRHH) is the premiere honorary supported by the leading international organization advocating for the interests and welfare of residence hall students, while also providing opportunities for their personal growth and development. NRHH strives to provide recognition for individuals who have contributed to the advancement of college and university housing. It ensures the advancement of member chapters through resource sharing, programming, and leadership development opportunities to contribute and support the vision of the National Association of College and University Residence Halls, Incorporated.
summary: NRHH is an organization dedicated to recognition and service, specifically for students living on campus in our residence halls. We set up service projects and foster connections with other dedicated members.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
description:
The National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), with more than 29,900 members, is one of the largest student-governed organizations in the country. Founded in 1975, NSBE now includes more than 700 College, Pre-College, and Technical Professional/Alumni chapters in the United States and abroad. 

Our chapter at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville strives to uphold our national mission through various academic, professional, and outreach programs. Our goal is to help recruit, retain, and graduate minority engineering students from the University of Tennessee.
summary:
Mission: 

To increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community.

name: Nepali Student Association
description:
The Nepali Students Association at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville (UTNSA) is a non-profit and non-political organization established by Nepali students enrolled in the University. The primary objective of the UTNSA is to enhance the cultural, social, and educational experiences of Nepali students and to foster a better understanding of Nepali culture and traditions among the diverse student population of the University.

The UTNSA works to bring the Nepali community in the Knoxville area, including students and staff from the University of Tennessee and Oak Ridge National Laboratory, together to celebrate the cultures and traditions of Nepal. Through various events and activities, the NSA aims to create social interaction, promote peace, and foster harmony among the different cultures within the University.

In its role as a cultural ambassador, the UTNSA strives to play an important role in promoting the rich and diverse heritage of Nepal to the broader community at the University of Tennessee and beyond.
summary: The UTNSA is a non-profit and non-political student organization aimed at promoting social interaction between Nepalese students and the wider UT community through cultural events and activities.

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Incorporated
description:
Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is the first international fraternal organization founded on the campus of the historically black college Howard University located in Washington, D.C.

The founders were three undergraduates -- Edgar Amos Love, Oscar James Cooper and Frank Coleman. Joining them was their faculty adviser, Ernest Everett Just.

From "friendship is essential to the soul," the name Omega Psi Phi was derived. That phrase was selected as the motto.

Manhood, Scholarship, Perseverance and Uplift were adopted as Cardinal Principles.
summary: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is the first international fraternal organization founded on the campus of the historically black college Howard University located in Washington, D.C.

name: Order of Omega
description: Order of Omega is a leadership honor society for members of Greek organizations. Order of Omega recognizes sophomores, juniors, and seniors who have exemplified high standards in the areas of scholarship, leadership, involvement within their respective organization, and within the Greek, campus, and local community.
summary: A Greek  community  experience  with  the  goal  of  promoting  leadership,  one  that  pushes  members  to  be  better  in  all  regards.

name: Paintball Club at the University of Tennessee
description: We are the Paintball Club at the University of Tennessee. We compete in the College Paintball Players League and NXL events against other college paintball teams. We practice once a week on campus, and once a month will travel to paintball fields to play.  Our season is usually made up of 2 tournaments a semester, and the CPPL National Championship in April.  All skill levels welcome and beginners encouraged! We don't just play competitively, we also play to have fun so if that is more your speed come on out!
summary: University of Tennessee's competitive collegiate paintball team.

name: Tennessee Club Baseball
description:
** For inquiries, best to contact us via Instagram: @clubvols **

Club Baseball was founded in 2007 and is a Division I member of the National Club Baseball Association (NCBA). We currently compete in the South Atlantic West Conference with Vanderbilt, Georgia Tech, Kennesaw State, and Chattanooga. Conference series against each of these opponents, in addition to non-conference games, make up our Fall and Spring schedules. Our season comprises of 25-30 games, with home games being played in and around Knoxville at local high school fields, depending on field availability and weather. We travel to other colleges for away series' which is always competitive and great for team bonding. We are a highly competitive team that expects a winning culture, with back to back Conference Championships in 2024 and 2025, making the regional playoffs in Georgia both years. Our expectation is similar to lower level college baseball (D3, NAIA, JUCO) and goal is to make it to the NCBA World Series.
summary: Official Club Baseball Team of University of Tennessee, Knoxville.

name: Association for Geographers and Sustainers at UT
description: UTAGS is the official student organization working to unify and strengthen the body of Geography and Sustainability majors within the Department of Geography and Sustainability. Our purpose is to provide these students with networking, academic, and professional development resources, as well as social connections with other areas of study within the department. We also aim to promote awareness of Geography and Sustainability as interdisciplinary tools for creating a better future both on campus and in the greater Knoxville community.
summary: Official student organization for undergraduate Geography and Sustainability majors/minors within the Department of Geography. As a group, we provide resources for unification and development to all majors in the Department.

name: College Democrats
description: The College Democrats at the University of Tennessee-Knoxville is the official college chapter of the Democratic Party under the statewide Tennessee College Democrats (TNCD) and the national College Democrats of America (CDA). We work to get Democrats elected at the local, state, and federal levels, to promote liberal and progressive values, and to serve our community through volunteerism. Join us today to bring change and progress to our city, state, and country!
summary: The College Democrats at UTK strive to inform students about the Democratic Party's liberal and progressive ideals. We pledge to help elect Democratic candidates at every level through campus outreach, volunteer opportunities, and paid positions.

name: College Republicans
description:
The College Republicans are the foremost authentic conservative student group at the University of Tennessee. Joining College Republicans is an opportunity to get involved in conservative politics -- making a difference on local campaigns, networking with like-minded individuals, enhancing your career opportunities, and freely speaking your mind on any topic.

Interested in joining? Fill out this form!
summary: Organization for members of the Republican Party on campus. We host speakers, foster political debate and insight, and provide volunteer/job opportunities in local politics.

name: College Student Personnel Association
description:
As stated in the constitution, the purpose of CSPA is to:

  1. Support the social, professional, and academic network of members

  2. Enhance the professional development of members through timely and relevant programming (e.g. workshops, conferences, panels, etc.)

  3. Build and enrich the student-faculty community in the CSP program

  4. Assist the CSP program coordinator with signature program events, including, but not limited to: interview weekend, hooding, visit days, and orientation.

  5. Create and facilitate a continuously growing network of current students and alumni of the CSP program
summary: To complement the socialization of masters level student affairs in the College Student Personnel (CSP) program at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville through educational, professional, and social development opportunities.

name: Collegiate Farm Bureau, 4-H, and FFA
description:
Collegiate Farm Bureau, 4-H, and FFA welcome all students, regardless of prior involvement in 4-H or FFA. Our mission is to foster leadership, service, and career development by connecting members with meaningful opportunities in agriculture and beyond.

We meet once a month! Check out our Instagram page to get the latest updates @utkcollegiatefb4handffa
summary: Collegiate Farm Bureau, 4-H, and FFA welcome all students, regardless of prior involvement in 4-H or FFA. Our mission is to foster leadership, service, and career development by connecting members with meaningful opportunities in agriculture and beyond.

name: Entrepreneurs Club at UT
description: Entrepreneurs Club is a place for student entrepreneurs to gather and discuss business ideas, meet local successful business owners, learn about what it takes to be an entrepreneur, and feel supported by their community! We ask members to pay dues to help cover t-shirts and snacks.
summary: Entrepreneurs Club is a place for student entrepreneurs to gather and discuss business ideas, meet local successful business owners, learn about what it takes to be an entrepreneur and to feel supported by their community!

name: College Communication and Information Graduate Student Association
description: A student based organization for the CCI Graduate students and ran by the CCI Graduate students
summary: College of Communication and Information Graduate Student Association (CCI GSA)

name: Tennessee Association of Supply Chain
description:
The Tennessee Association of Supply Chain (TASC) is dedicated to preparing future leaders in supply chain management. Through professional development, exclusive networking opportunities, and direct industry engagement, TASC empowers students to build meaningful connections and gain a competitive edge in today’s job market.

 

Visit our website for more information on becoming a member or attending any events! 

https://www.tascutk.org/
summary: Supply Chain Management Professionals at UTK

name: Creative Writing Club
description: We are a student-organized club that meets once a week to discuss and share our experiences and pieces of creative writing from fiction, to poetry, and all works creative writing. We provide a relaxed environment and love to read all different kinds of works. Although we are a group based on submissions, it is never necessary to submit.
summary: The Creative Writing Club at UTK workshops pieces members provide on a weekly basis. We give great feedback for the revision process and are here for one another during the writing process.

name: Crew Club at UT
description:
Tennessee Crew Club is the coed Rowing Club for the University of Tennessee at Knoxville. Established in 1972, Tennessee Crew Club is not only one of the oldest club sports at The University of Tennessee, but is also one of the oldest collegiate Rowing programs in the Southeast. 

Tennessee Crew Club offers The University of Tennessee at Knoxville students and faculty the opportunity to participate in one of the oldest, most challenging, and most rewarding intercollegiate sports in the United States.
