A team sport, rowing demands team work, physical endurance, and a competitive spirit.

Like many other rowing programs, Tennessee Crew Club is not a varsity sport, which allows for a competitive team without the demands of a varsity program. No experience is required to join. As an organization, the team is both an opportunity to achieve a high level of athleticism and develop a social group of friends that will last throughout college and life.

If you are interested in joining, please email us at rowtncrew@gmail.com or utknovicerowing@gmail.com with the subject line "Join Crew." Our Instagram and Facebook accounts share the handle @tennesseecrew. Also, check out our website, http://www.tennesseecrew.com/!
summary: Co-ed Rowing Team at the University of Tennessee-Knoxville

name: Environmental and Soil Science Club
description: The mission of the Environmental and Soil Sciences Club is to create an open environment for students to enhance their education and engage in activities in the environmental and soil sciences. This mission is executed through the following objectives: 1. Create networking opportunities for students to meet local professionals. 2. Foster social interaction and collaboration among the various environmental- and soil science-related degree programs at the University of Tennessee. 3. Gain valuable experience in the industry and give back to the community through participation in relevant community service projects and public education. 4. Promote and grow the Environmental and Soil Science program.
summary: The mission of the Environmental and Soil Sciences Club is to create an open environment for students to enhance their education and engage in activities in the environmental and soil sciences.

name: Dance Society at the University of Tennessee
description:
The Dance Society was founded to increase awareness of the need for Dance and Dance Education both on campus and in our everyday lives.

The main goals of the Dance Society are:

  1. Promote dance education on campus by providing dance technique classes to the students.
  2. Encourage the University to re-establish a dance program for the students.
  3. Provide performance opportunities to the dancers.

One of the main performance opportunities for dancers to perform is through the BOSS Dance Company. BOSS Dance Company holds auditions early in the fall and rehearses all semester for the spring showcase. BOSS brings in local choreographers and allows students the opportunity to showcase some of their own choreography.
summary: Dance Society at the University of Tennessee is a student-run organization that provides a safe and inclusive environment for any and all dancers at the University of Tennessee Knoxville to express their passion through artistic and athletic movement.

name: Delta Delta Delta
description:
Delta Delta Delta is an assembly of college women who strive to be Brave, Bold, and Kind in all aspects of our lives. We are passionate about empowering each other to become the best versions of ourselves while committing to help women Live, Learn, and Lead with purpose, for a lifetime. Our chapter is composed of diverse women who have united in shared values, sisterhood, love, and compassion. Delta Sigma is a place where you can authentically be yourself and belong to something bigger while always being supported by sisters who are ultimately doing the same.

The Delta Sigma chapter at the University of Tennessee has over 400 sisters. Our house can be found on Ann Baker Furrow Blvd and was built in 2013. Here, 46 women can live comfortably, enjoy delicious meals prepared by two wonderful chefs, and make daily memories with fellow sisters. Our members are involved in many organizations on campus such as Leading Women of Tomorrow, the Torch, Student Government Association, Boss Dance Company, Women of Haslam, and Ambassadors, just to name a few.

Throughout the year, we work hard to raise money for our national philanthropy, St. Jude Children’s Hospital, and our local philanthropy, East Tennessee Children’s Hospital. Some of our fundraising events include Sincerely Yours Letting Writing, Chili Night, Sweets for St. Jude, $20K in a day, and our annual Game Day Tailgate. We are proud to announce that during the 2023-2024 school year, we have raised over $187,000 For St. Jude and over $12,000 for East Tennessee Children's Hospital. We are so proud and honored to also be named "Top Fundraising Chapter" for the 2023-2024 school year, as well as many years in the past. In 2024, Greek Life recognized our chapter with the Panhellenic Program of the Year Award, which honored our ASL learning event. 

Our sisterhood extends beyond campus life. Whether we are participating in sisterhood events or ritual, we are always developing our strong sisterhood. With our annual tailgate, Sweets for St.Jude, and Founders day in the fall, then Chili night, parents weekend and Pansy brunch in the spring, we have much to look forward to each year. We possess an unbreakable bond that extends and develops after graduation. We are so grateful to be apart of this Greek community here at the University of Tennessee!
summary: Delta Delta Delta

name: Delta Gamma
description:
"The objects of this Fraternity shall be to foster high ideals of friendship among women, to promote their educational and cultural interests, to create in them a true sense of social responsibility and to develop in them the best qualities of character."

Delta Gamma was founded in 1873 by Anna Boyd, Mary Comfort, and Eva Webb at Lewis School in Oxford, Mississippi with the vision to “Do Good” - which remains our motto and drives all that we do today.

The Gamma Alpha chapter at the University of Tennessee was established in 1947. With over 450 members, we are dedicated to supporting one another in every aspect of life. We strive to create an environment where our members can grow, lead, and make a lasting impact on the campus and community.

Whether through academic excellence, personal growth, or campus and community involvement, we aim to inspire each member to "Do Good" in everything they do.

Read more about our chapter at https://www.utkdg.com/ !
summary: Delta Gamma

name: Delta Sigma Pi
description: A co-ed business fraternity that focuses on professionalism, networking, interviewing, and preparing for the business world. In addition focusing on brotherhood, teamwork with like minded business students.
summary:
Delta Sigma Pi
Professional Co-Ed Business Fraternity

name: Delta Tau Delta
summary: Delta Tau Delta is a fraternity dedicated to academic achievement, philanthropic success, community involvement, and social participation. The men of Delta Tau Delta are committed to lives of excellence.

name: Delta Zeta
description:
"The purpose of this sorority shall be to unite its members in the bonds of sincere and lasting friendship, to stimulate one another in the pursuit of knowledge, to promote the moral and social culture of its members, and to develop plans for guidance and unity in action; objects worthy of the highest aim and purpose of associated effort. The purpose of this sorority shall be advanced through the National Convention, the National Council, and the college and alumnae chapters."
Constitution of the Delta Zeta Sorority, Article II, Sections 1. and 2.
summary: We share a common purpose: to make life fuller, more meaningful, more rewarding. Within Delta Zeta, we call it enriching. To our members, it is the promise of friendship, personal development and opportunity. To the community we are valued citizens.

name: Disc Golf Club at the University of Tennessee
summary: Disc Golf Club at the University of Tennessee

name: Volunteer Hall Residents Association
summary: Volunteer Hall Residents Association

name: Volunteers for Christ
description: Volunteers for Christ is an outreach ministry of Cornerstone Church of Knoxville. Our primary purpose is to glorify God through the proclamation and demonstration of the Gospel of Jesus Christ at the University of Tennessee in Knoxville. We desire for students to know Christ and practically apply their faith in the local church.
summary: Volunteers for Christ

name: Wakeboard Club at UT
description: VolWake is the Wakeboarding Club at the University of Tennessee. With over 80 members, VolWake is an organization that both competes at national collegiate competitions and provides university students with the opportunity to engage in water-sports recreationally. Current sponsors include: Redbull, Ronix, and Bling Sauce.
summary: VolWake is the Wakeboarding Club at the University of Tennessee. From beginners to semi-pros, VolWake provides university students with the opportunity to engage both competitively and recreationally in water-sports.

name: The Water Polo Club at the University of Tennessee
description:
​​What is water polo you may ask? Water polo is a fast-paced, competitive team sport that combines swimming, basketball, and soccer. We are a co-ed water polo team that accepts all abilities and skill levels. Whether you are a seasoned player or new to the sport our club offers a dynamic and inclusive environment for all skill levels to grow and develop. We are dedicated to fostering athletic excellence, a solid work ethic, and a vibrant community encompassed by good attitudes.  

 

Our primary co-ed season is in the Fall and the women's season is in the spring. Practices, tournaments, and more continue throughout each semester. Come join us where passion meets unparalleled support and opportunities. Dive in and discover the thrill of water polo!!
summary: ​​We are a co-ed water polo team that accepts all abilities and skill levels. Our primary co-ed season is in the Fall and the women's season is in the spring. Practices, tournaments, and more continue throughout each semester.

name: Wesley Foundation - United Methodist Student  Center
description:
The Wesley Foundation "strives to be an open, diverse Christian community, extending God's love on campus."  We eat, play, worship, study the Bible, discuss important questions, watch movies, and hang out for no particular reason.  We are a community that welcomes all.

The Wesley Foundation is open each day from 10 am until 8 pm. Come by and hang out or study between classes.

Everyone is welcome!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B4osUCOyUjU&t=13s
summary: Wesley Foundation - United Methodist Student Center

name: Wildlife Disease Association, Student Chapter
description:
What is the Wildlife Disease Association?

The Wildlife Disease Association (WDA) was started in 1951 with goal of researching and understanding wildlife health. In recent years, after the Covid-19 Pandemic, it has become increasingly important to study One Health and its impacts. From the Centers for Disease Control (CDC), One Health is an approach which considers how humans, plants, and animals interact with each other in their environments. In the UTK student chapter of the WDA, members will be encouraged to pursue research, volunteer work, and continuing education with wildlife utilizing a One Health approach. Check out our Student Chapter Website.

 

 

Why Should I Join WDA?

Students interested in wildlife or wildlife health, disease, and/or One Health would fit right into the WDA. We will have guest speakers from UT College of Veterinary Medicine, the Wildlife and Fisheries department, and the Animal Science department, as well as local and international speakers. We also welcome members to talk to the organization about current research, volunteer work, or programs that they become involved with. The goal is educate the members of the WDA UTK Student Chapter about local, national, and international opportunities within the wildlife, disease, and One Health fields.

 

How Do I Join?

Undergraduate, Graduate, and Veterinary Students:

Students are encouraged to join the organization through their VoLink accounts. Simply login using your UTK NetID and password. Then search "WDA", go to the organization's profile, and click the big blue "Join" button. It may take a couple of days for a board member to approve you, but there are no specific requirements for you to join, so you are automatically a member!

 

 Already a Member?

Request to join our groupme: @utkwda

Add your impact here: WDAServeUTK
summary: Wildlife Disease Association, Student Chapter

name: Women's Lacrosse Club at the University of Tennessee
description: The Women's Lacrosse Club is a perfect place for students to get involved on campus. We love having new players join, those both with and without prior experience! We are apart of the Southeastern Women's Lacrosse League and play competitively with other colleges and universities in the southeast region.
summary: This page is for the Women's Lacrosse Club at the University of Tennessee founded in 2001. The team is a part of the Southeastern Women's Lacrosse League in the WCLA.

name: Women's Volleyball Club
description:
Hello!

Thanks for your interest in the Women's Club Volleyball Team at UT. Below, you can find information about our club. If you have any other questions, please feel free to email us at tennesseeclubvolleyball@gmail.com OR dm us on Instagram @utkclubvolleyball. 

 

This season we will have both travel ($250 per semester) and practice players ($90 per semester). There will be a select number of travel players who get to come to every practice and compete in tournaments at other colleges as well as the home tournament we host each semester. Travel players will also attend nationals in the spring semester. Practice players get to compete in our home tournament and come to weekly practices. 

For current updates follow our Instagram and join the GroupMe! 

GroupMe
summary: Please click "Read More" for detailed information about our club!

name: Women's Ultimate Club
description:
The wonderful sport of ultimate is a competitive, strategic game involving a flying disc. Although an uncommon sport, it attracts people from all areas of the globe - quickly becoming a competitive sport in colleges nationally. Our team focuses on the spirit of the game always with sportsmanship. We practice twice a week and attend various tournaments about once a month. We have fun and love making memories through the awesome sport of ultimate!

 

If you're interested in joining or learning more about the club, please contact us at bosultimate@gmail.com.

 

"..ultimate frisbee combines speed, grace and powerful hurling with a grueling pace."

THE WALL STREET JOURNAL
summary: Women's Ultimate Frisbee

name: Wrestling Club at the University of Tennessee
description:
The Wrestling Club at UTK is a student run, competitive organization for individuals wanting to further their involvement in wrestling. We practice every Monday, Wednesday, and Friday from 6:00PM-8:00PM in Court B of HPER (the same room as the climbing wall).

We wrestle under the NCWA, National Collegiate Wrestling Association, and compete with schools both regionally and nationally. We have active men's and women's teams. Every year we have wrestlers qualify for Nationals, and we’re pushing for even more this year.

You don’t have to compete to be in the club and are welcome to just attend practices. We welcome anyone, regardless of gender, and whether you have previous wresting experience or not.

Work hard. Wrestle harder. Live easy.
summary: The Wrestling Club at UTK is a student run, recreational, and/or competitive organization. We wrestle in the NCWA against schools both regionally and nationally. We welcome everyone, whether they have previous wrestling experience or no

name: Young Americans for Freedom
description:
The purposes of this Chapter are as follows:
A. To provide an organizational framework to promote the general principles of Young Americans for Freedom which were adopted in Sharon, Connecticut on September 11, 1960, as follows:
In this time of moral and political crises, it is the responsibility of the youth of America to affirm certain eternal truths.
We, as young conservatives, believe:
That foremost among the transcendent values is the individual's use of his God-given free will, whence derives his right to be free from the restrictions of arbitrary force;
That liberty is indivisible, and that political freedom cannot long exist without economic freedom;
That the purpose of government is to protect those freedoms through the preservation of internal order, the provision of national defense, and the administration of justice;
That when government ventures beyond these rightful functions, it accumulates power, which tends to diminish order and liberty;
That the Constitution of the United States is the best arrangement yet devised for empowering government to fulfill its proper role, while restraining it from the concentration and abuse of power;
That the genius of the Constitution- the division of powers- is summed up in the clause that reserves primacy to the several states, or to the people, in those spheres not specifically delegated to the Federal government;
That the market economy, allocating resources by the free play of supply and demand, is the single economic system compatible with the requirements of personal freedom and constitutional government, and that it is at the same time the most productive supplier of human needs;
That when government interferes with the work of the market economy, it tends to reduce the moral and physical strength of the nation; that when it takes from one man to bestow on another, it diminishes the incentive of the first, the integrity of the second, and the moral autonomy of both;
That we will be free only so long as the national sovereignty of the United States is secure; that history shows periods of freedom are rare, and can exist only when free citizens concertedly defend their rights against all enemies;
That the forces of international Communism are, at present, the greatest single threat to these liberties; That the United States should stress victory over, rather than coexistence with, this menace; and
That American foreign policy must be judged by this criterion: does it serve the just interests of the United States?
B. To conduct educational activities and provide conservative leadership to bring about the objectives outlined in the Sharon Statement.
C. To assist students in their efforts to understand and promote conservatism on campus and in the surrounding community.
summary: Young America's Foundation is the leading organization for young conservatives. With more than 60 years of experience and contacts on more than 2,000 campuses, we help students like you find support, promote conservatism, and take action.

name: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated
description:
Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated was organized on January 16, 1920 on the campus of Howard University in Washington, D.C.  Our founders, known as “The Five Pearls”, were illustrious women who believed sorority elitism and socializing should not overshadow the real mission for progressive organizations—to address societal mores, ills, prejudices, poverty, and health concerns of the day, particularly affecting the African American community.

As a result, they founded Zeta Phi Beta Sorority Inc. to encourage the highest standards of scholarship through scientific, literary, cultural and educational programs; promote service projects on college campuses and in the community; foster sisterly love amongst its members and exemplify the ideal of Finer Womanhood.

The Prestigious Pi Epsilon chapter was charted at the University of Tennessee on October 10, 1974 by eight zealous women. The chapter has achieved many accomplishments and accolades on various levels. Prestigious Pi Epsilon was the 2020 NPHC Chapter of the Year. Pi Epsilon prides themselves in the events and acts of service they bring to campus year to year, which include African American Women's Appreciation Awards, We've Got the BlueZ Mental Health Awareness, Culture Night, Random Acts of Kindness and Sapphire Circle Mentorship Program.

Throughout the years, Prestigious Pi Epsilon may not have always had the highest quantities of members, but they have always had the highest quality of members!
summary: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. was founded on January 16, 1920 on the campus of Howard University upon the principles of Scholarship, Service, Sisterly Love, and Finer Womanhood. The Prestigious Pi Epsilon chapter was chartered on October 10, 1974.

name: Zeta Tau Alpha
description:
The Zeta Tau Alpha Tradition began in 1898 when nine friends wanted to honor the permanence of genuine friendship . . .

 

Zeta Tau Alpha was founded on October 15, 1898, by nine women at the State Female Normal School (later Longwood College) in Farmville, Virginia. Maud Jones, Alice Bland Coleman, Ethel Coleman, Ruby Leigh, Frances Yancey Smith, Della Lewis, Helen M. Crafford, Alice Grey Welsh, and Mary Jones began as a small group of friends wishing to add more permanence to their relationship. 

Over the years, thousands of women of all ages have shared the common bond created by Zeta Tau Alpha's nine founders. This organization has grown through the century to become the third-largest women's Greek organization in North America. Today, Zeta Tau Alpha has 257 chapters at colleges and universities, as well as alumni chapters throughout the U.S. With now over 206,000 initiated members, the original founders succeeded in creating a group that emphasizes scholarship, sisterhood, service, and values that stand the test of time.

Consistent with our Founders' thoughts in 1898, our primary purpose is to create an environment for our members in which lasting friendships and our vows of sisterhood emanate all aspects of our Fraternity. We encourage an atmosphere that will foster high ideals of friendship among college women, promote their educational and cultural interests, create in them a true sense of social responsibility and develop in them high qualities of character. 

 

Mission Statement: 

To make a difference in the lives of our members by developing the potential of each individual through innovative programming, which emphasizes leadership development, service to others, academic achievement and continued personal growth for women, with a commitment to friendship and the future based on the sisterhood, values and traditions of the past.   

 

Purpose:

The purpose of Zeta Tau Alpha is the intensifying of friendship, the fostering of a spirit of love, the creation of such sentiments, the performing of such deeds, and the molding of such opinions as will be conducive to the building up of purer and nobler womanhood in the world.

 

Open Motto: 

“Seek the Noblest”

 

National Philanthropy: 

The Zeta Tau Alpha Foundation fulfills the founding principles of our Fraternity by providing support for programs that are educational, philanthropic, and leadership-oriented. Zeta Tau Alpha’s National Philanthropy is Breast Cancer Education and Awareness.

  • THINK PINK!® — In February 2004, the ZTA Foundation announced that Think Pink® had become a registered trademark of ZTA with the United States Patent and Trademark Office. Since then, this term has become the umbrella theme for all of our breast cancer education and awareness projects. Chapters distribute Think Pink® ribbons, breast self-examination (BSE) calendar stickers and shower cards throughout the year.
  • Bright Pink® —To educate, equip and empower our members to be advocates for their own breast and ovarian health, we’ve partnered with Bright Pink®, a national nonprofit organization focused on prevention and early detection of breast and ovarian cancer. We bring the Brighten Up®Educational Workshop to each of our collegiate chapters. Interested alumnae chapters are encouraged to join collegians for this fun and informative workshop. We plan to grow this program over time to include Panhellenic women and others in the campus community.
  • American Cancer Society® — Through our premier partnership with American Cancer Society®, ZTA is the National Survivor Ambassador of Making Strides Against Breast Cancer®. At the nearly 300 Making Strides walks held each October in communities across the country, Zetas provide assistance and focus on survivor recognition. Over time, we will help grow the survivor recognition program at these events.
  • NFL — Since 1999, Zeta Tau Alpha alumni and collegiate members have worked with all 32 NFL teams to increase awareness of breast cancer at professional football games. ZTA is an official fan outreach partner of the National Football League. Beginning with the Washington Redskins in 1999, Zetas have helped spread the message of breast cancer awareness to over 5 million football fans across the country. The Zeta Chapter participates in a crucial catch game for the Titans in Nashville, TN.
  • Pink Out — Each year, Zeta Tau Alpha chapters across the country host Think Pink or Pink Out games with their university athletic departments to raise awareness for breast cancer.
  • Breast Cancer Awareness Month — October is Breast Cancer Awareness Month, and across the country, members of Zeta Tau Alpha hold special events to increase awareness about early detection and to raise funds for education and research. 
  • SELF — As a benefit of membership, each collegiate Zeta Tau Alpha receives a complimentary subscription to SELF, including its annual Women's Breast Cancer Handbook.
summary: Zeta Tau Alpha (ΖΤΑ) is an international women's fraternity, founded on October 15, 1898, at the State Female Normal School (now Longwood University) in Farmville, Virginia.

name: Hillel at UTK
description: Hillel is a foundation for Jewish Life on more than 850 campuses across the world. Jewish student life at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville was established in 1946. Nationwide, Hillel has had a major global impact on the future of the Jewish people. Locally, Hillel at UTK is supported through the Knoxville Jewish Alliance and Hillel International. in the Jewish value of hospitality (Hakhnasat Orchim), we welcome and respect all wishing to be engaged in our home away from home. We build community (Kehillah) with other Jews, our university, and the broader community. We motivate and cultivate leadership (Manhigut). And most importantly, we promote well-being, celebration, and friendship through joy (Sincha) in our everyday lives!
summary: Hillel at UTK inspires students to explore, embrace, and celebrate their Jewish life - no matter what kind of Jew-ish they are!

name: Ice Hockey Club at the University of Tennessee
description: Founded in 1966, the Tennessee Hockey program is the oldest and most established hockey program in the southeastern United States.  Throughout the years, Tennessee has enjoyed its share of success while forging great rivalries and great memories for all of the players involved. Tennessee Hockey fields two teams, both of which compete in the Amateur Athletic Union (AAU).
summary: Ice Hockey

name: Indian-American Association
description: The Indian American Association seeks to create a community between South Asian students from the University of Tennessee. IAA is not exclusive to solely Indian American students, and we encourage those who are interested in learning about the South Asian culture to join and participate. Our goal is to help unite the community, our school, as well as our friends and family. We plan on doing this by spreading awareness of the culture of India through cultural, educational and social events, volunteering, and community service.
summary: Indian-American Association

name: Institute of Electrical & Electronics Engineers
description: The purpose of IEEE at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville is to be a social organization that provides events targeted at its members who are in the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science but open to any student who wishes to attend. Events include but are not limited to workshops and industry talks. In addition, the organization can sponsor clubs. One such club at the university is the robotics team. This organization will host industry leaders, academic researchers, and graduate students for professional and technical workshops.
summary: The IEEE student group is a sub branch of the main IEEE Professional Organization. This group holds four annual professional development talks, two technical development workshops, and hosts the IEEE SEC Robotics Competition team annually.

name: Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers - UTK Chapter
description: The Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers @ UT is an organization governed by the students within the Department of Industrial & Systems Engineering. Its main focus is to provide students with outside resources regarding their future professional careers. In addition, it is a way in which industrial engineering students can get to know one another better outside of the classroom. The highlight of the ISE year is the annual regional conference, which we will be hosting at UT in February 2025. Historically, participation has been excellent. Visit IIE’s national site for more information.
summary: Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers - UTK Chapter

name: Institute of Nuclear Material Management
description:
Welcome to the UTK of the Institute of Nuclear Materials Management!

A few words about INMM...

The Institute of Nuclear Materials Management was formed in 1958 to encourage:

  • The advancement of nuclear materials management in all its aspects.
  • The promotion of research in the field of nuclear materials management.
  • The establishment of standards, consistent with existing professional norms.
  • The improvement of the qualifications of those engaged in nuclear materials management and safeguards through high standards of professional ethics, education, and attainments, and the recognition of those who meet such standards.
  • The increase and dissemination of information through meetings, professional contacts, reports, papers, discussions, and publications.
We maintain an active student chapter and host presentations, special visits, and other activites. Meetings are diverse in nature, interesting, and give students the chance to interact with area experts from ORNL, Y-12, and other prestigious organizations. There is always FREE FOOD!
summary: Institute of Nuclear Materials Management

name: International Law Students Association
description: The International Law Students Association at the University of Tennessee College of Law is a member chapter of the International Law Students Association (ILSA). The International Law Society at the University of Tennessee College of Law is organized to promote awareness of issues that affect the international law profession and to promote and foster awareness of international affairs generally, throughout the Law School and wider UT community. The organization will serve as an informational resource for persons who are interested in careers in both public and private international law, and those who want to study or work abroad.
summary: The International Law Students Association at the University of Tennessee College of Law is a member chapter of the International Law Students Association (ILSA).

name: Italian Club at UTK
description:
The Italian Club at UTK is an organization which hosts both on and off campus events in order to promote knowledge of the Italian culture while also helping support students seeking knowledge of the Italian language and culture. We host meetings and events where students interested in Italian or involved in Italian classes can gather with fellow students as well as professors to enjoy the company of other students with Italian interests and learn about Italian culture.

https://italclub.utk.edu/
summary: UTK Italian Club - All students welcome to come and learn about Italian culture!

name: Japanese Animanga Research Society
description:
Come join the anime-manga club and chitchat with folks about anime, manga, VNs, LN, movies, VTubers, and basically anything else as we vibe casually watching anime both old and new! 

Our club meetings tend to be an amalgamation of watching anime, playing animanga-related games, activities, movie nights, outings, and everything in between. 

We also run a parallel weekly meeting on learning Japanese through anime. 

We have a Discord! Email rgl919@vols.utk.edu for more info on how to join and get started!
summary:
Japanese Animanga Research Society 
The official UTK Anime-Manga club.

name: Kappa Alpha Order
description: Kappa Alpha Order, founded in 1865 at Washington & Lee University, is proud to return to the University of Tennessee Knoxville this Fall to restore our Pi Chapter. KA was first established at UT in 1883 as one of the first fraternities at UT. Pi chapter is one the most historic and prominent KA chapters in the country with more than 1,300 living alumni. With deep roots in Tennessee and the Southeast, KA’s extensive alumni network includes more than 8,500 accomplished alumni throughout Tennessee, including influential leaders such as Tennessee Governor Bill Lee, one of three sitting governors in KA. KA’s core values of Reverence, Gentility, Knowledge, Leadership, Brotherhood, and Excellence define who we are and will guide our reestablishment of Pi Chapter. The restoration of Pi chapter at UT will designate KA as the only national fraternity with a chapter at every SEC school. KA is deeply patriotic and committed to supporting wounded veterans through our national philanthropy with the Independence Fund where chapters raise money to donate all-terrain track chairs to wounded veterans at halftime football games. We invite you to learn more about the opportunity to become a re-founding father of this historic chapter and contribute our tradition of excellence in the SEC.
summary: Kappa Alpha Order is a Moral Compass for the Modern Gentleman. As an Order, our chapters bring together men who already possess our shared values: Reverence, Gentility, Knowledge, Leadership, Brotherhood, and Excellence.

name: Kappa Delta
description: The Alpha Epsilon chapter of Kappa Delta was founded at The University of Tennessee on September 26, 1925. Since then, our members have lived by our motto and strive to make this chapter that which is honorable, beautiful and highest. Known as one of the most involved chapters on campus, our KD ladies can truly be called Volunteers.
summary: Kappa Delta Sorority is committed to providing opportunities and experiences that inspire women to greatness.

name: Kappa Kappa Gamma
description:
At the University of Tennessee, as the Epsilon Lambda chapter, Kappa Kappa Gamma is committed to helping women reach their full potential and aspirations, while never losing sight of its dedication to the community. Kappa offers its members experience ranging from friendship to mentoring, from leadership to service, and from campus activities to a lifetime of community involvement. With 480 members, Kappa Kappa Gamma at UT represents a diverse group of women coming together to make a difference, a difference that the entire Knoxville community will never forget.

Kappa Kappa Gamma Fraternity is one of the oldest women's fraternities with a total membership of more than 260,000 women and 140 collegiate chapters in the United States and Canada. At the University of Tennessee, Kappa Kappa Gamma prides itself on the tradition of leadership. The chapter strives in every aspect to uphold the fraternity's national mission statement: "Kappa Kappa Gamma is an organization of women, which seeks for every member throughout her life bonds of friendship, mutual support, opportunities for self-growth, respect for intellectual development, and an understanding of and an allegiance to positive ethical principles." Being a member of Kappa is really about opportunity: to grow, to connect, to serve, to befriend, to aspire.
summary: Kappa Kappa Gamma Epsilon Lambda chapter at the University of Tennessee is a women's fraternity that focuses on the importance of dedication to friendships, academic excellence, leadership, campus involvement and community service.

name: Kappa Sigma
description: As a Fraternity here at the University of Tennessee, Kappa Sigma is devoted to promoting fellowship, leadership, scholarship, and service among our members. Our organization is built upon a strong brotherhood as we hold each and every one of our members accountable, and make sure our members are aware of the things that truly matter. This way, our members leave college ready for the real world.
summary: Kappa Sigma Fraternity is committed to the advancement of young men through fellowship, scholarship, leadership and service.

name: Korean Student Association
description: The University of Tennessee Korean Student Association was established to provide an opportunity for UT students interested in Korean culture to come together and build lifelong friendships. Our main goal is to spread awareness and help students achieve a greater understanding of Korean culture through social and educational events.
summary: Korean Student Association

name: Men's Lacrosse Club
description: Lacrosse was introduced on the campus of the University of Tennessee in 1974. The UT Men’s Lacrosse has had many successful years since the introduction of the sport. We are members of the MCLA (Men’s Collegiate Lacrosse Association), which is a national organization of non-NCAA, college lacrosse teams. There are ten other universities in our conference, the ALC, which include schools like Clemson, North Carolina State, West Virginia, Liberty, and Virginia Tech. Our team travels and plays universities from other conferences as well. University of Tennessee lacrosse has won four SELC Championships and made three appearances in the national tournament since 1993.
summary: Lacrosse Club, Men's

name: Student Nurses Association
description: The purpose of the Student Nurses Association at the University of Tennessee is to engage student nurses in a professional development organization in order to facilitate student-nurse relationships, commitment to philanthropy and community service, and growth as a future nurse.
summary: The SNA is a student-led organization that supports nursing students through leadership opportunities, professional development, community service, and peer connection.

name: Students Promoting Environmental Action in Knoxville
description:
We work to increase awareness of and seek creative solutions to local, regional, and national environmental issues. In the spirit of thinking globally and acting locally, we aim to build a community of leaders who act on stewardship and sustainability. We are also the creators and managers of the student-run garden on campus, the S.P.E.A.K Garden, located at the UT Culinary Institute.

Please email mosburn4@vols.utk.edu for details or questions.
summary: Students Promoting Environmental Action in Knoxville (SPEAK) is a student-run advocacy group for environmental issues at UTK.

name: Swim Club at UT
description:
Vols Swim Club is open to all UTK students, faculty, and staff who wish to swim competitively or simply for fun. All levels of swimmers are welcome. The team practices four nights a week at the Student Aquatic Center and has multiple social events. The club competes against other collegiate club teams across the country and hosts various home meets throughout the year.

 

If you are interested please contact:

Connor Chan

Swim Club President

cchan9@vols.utk.edu
summary: Vols Swim Club

name: Tau Beta Pi
description:
Tau Beta Pi is the only engineering honor society representing the entire engineering profession. It is the nation's second-oldest honor society, founded at Lehigh University in 1885 to mark in a fitting manner those who have conferred honor upon their Alma Mater by distinguished scholarship and exemplary character as students in engineering, or by their attainments as alumni in the field of engineering, and to foster a spirit of liberal culture in engineering colleges.


There are now 251 active collegiate chapters, 47 active alumnus chapters in 16 districts across the country, and a total initiated membership of approximately 624,000.
summary: Tau Beta Pi Engineering Honors Society

name: Tennessee Collegiate Horsemen's Association
description: Tennessee Collegiate Horsemen's Association is a group dedicated to providing exposure to the equine industry for any student who is interested in learning more about horses regarding care, health, sport, etc through events, trips, and seminars.
summary: Tennessee Collegiate Horsemen's Association is a group dedicated to providing exposure to the equine industry for any student who is interested in learning more about horses regarding care, health, sport, etc through events, trips, and seminars.

name: Tennessee Intercollegiate State Legislature - UTK Delegation
description: TISL is a non-partisan organization that aims to bring college students from across the state of Tennessee to focus on and debate public policy, politics, and state government. Every November, we convene at the State Capitol in Nashville to participate in a mock state government assembly. Students have the opportunity to participate in both houses of the legislature, lobbying, media, or the Appellate Moot Court Challenge (AMC3). On campus, we provide programming about public policy and state and local government. As an organization, we strive to show that policy and politics is paramount to everyone's lives.
summary: TISL aims to bring students together to discuss and debate public policy and state government. Through our annual General Assembly at the State Capitol, and programming on campus, we seek to showcase the impact of policy and politics at the state level.

name: Tennessee Journal of Law and Policy
description: The Tennessee Journal of Law and Policy is a non-partisan journal of law and public policy scholarship published by the students of the University of Tennessee College of Law.  The Journal will advance the public discourse by providing the academic community, the legal profession, policymakers, journalists, and students with innovative and insightful scholarly works that encourage a deeper understanding of the role of law and policy in American life.
summary: Tennessee Journal of Law and Policy

name: TOMBA at the University of Tennessee
description: TOMBA is the premier professional development organization for MBAs at the University of Tennessee. It is a student-managed organization that serves as an interface between UT MBA full-time students, faculty, alumni, and community. Its members not only enjoy serving their campus and community, but connecting with each other through mentorship, social, and intramural activities.
summary: Tennessee Organization of MBAs (TOMBA)

name: Tennessee Pre-Veterinary Association
description:
We are a University Student-Led Organization that focuses mainly on helping students gain experience, information, and connections for their future endeavors after graduation. The club is open to students in all majors interested in the field of Veterinary Medicine, not just those in Animal Science. You must be a full-time student at UT to be a member.

Meetings are held biweekly on the first and third Thursday of every month in the TLC lecture hall in the University of Tennessee College of Veterinary Medicine, room 105. Meetings typically include guest veterinary speakers, hands-on labs, fun activities, and vet school preparation. Members of the Pre-Vet Association have the opportunity to shadow in different departments at the UTCVM.

Social events within the organization are offered to members throughout the semester. Club dues are $10 per semester and are collected by the third meeting. All other membership requirements can be found within our club's constitution.  

Pets are welcome to club meetings unless otherwise specified for a specific meeting. They must be friendly and properly restrained via a leash or a carrier.

Website:https://utkprevetassociati.wixsite.com/utk-pre-vet-associat
summary: Tennessee Pre-Veterinary Association

name: Running Club at UT
description:
Check out our website for more information!
 
  • The purpose of the Tennessee Running Club is to offer physical exercise, team membership, and a competitive running experience to all UT students who are interested in improving their running ability. Whether you just recently got into running or you have years of experience, looking for a weekly jogging group or would like to train and compete in peak condition, we encourage everyone to come on out and see what we're all about!
  • Open to all students, faculty, and staff, regardless of ability, who enjoy running and would like to improve their running ability and run and compete as part of a team.
  • Compete in cross country, track, and road races against other schools' club teams and college teams. We also compete in local road races with the Knoxville Track Club!
  • Semester dues include race entry fees, travel to meets, uniform if you are a new member, membership with Knoxville Track Club, membership with the National Intercollegiate Running Club Association (NIRCA), and professional personal coaching!
  • We strongly encourage anyone who thinks they might be interested to come to a few of our practices, go for a run, and check things out! We meet Monday through Thursday at 5:30 pm in front of the SAC across from TRECS.
summary:
Competitive Club-Level Running Team.
Check out our website for more information!

name: Club Tennis
description:
Join Club Tennis! ��

Whether you’re a seasoned player or just picking up a racket, we’ve got a spot for you!

  • Competitive Team – For intermediate to advanced players who want to compete in tournaments and take their game to the next level. A great way to challenge yourself and represent UT!

  • Practice Team – More relaxed and social. Perfect if you’re looking to improve your game, meet other players, and just have fun. We’ll run drills, play games, and help everyone get better together.

 

All skill levels are welcome!

Shoot us an email if you’re interested and don’t forget to request membership on VOLink.

For the most up to date Club Tennis information such as tryout dates and practice times,click this link to be added to our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/108763996/Bh3tRtzn

Come hit with us! 🧡��
summary: This club is for playing tennis. We have a team for those who want to play competitively and a team for those who just want to meet people around campus who play tennis. No matter your skill level, if you like to play tennis, this is the club for you.

name: Lambda Chi Alpha
description: In January 1927 a group of UT engineering students interested in intramurals formed a local fraternity, Zeta Tau Lambda. In 1930 the group petitioned Lambda Chi Alpha for a charter. The 14th General Assembly of Lambda Chi Alpha granted the charter, and the Epsilon Omicron Chapter of Lambda Chi Alpha was installed at UT on March 19, 1932. The chapter returned to the UT-Knoxville campus in spring of 2021.
summary: Lambda Chi Alpha

name: OUTLaw
description: The OUTLaw is the LGBTQIA+ Legal Society at the University of Tennessee College of Law. OUTLaw is dedicated to building awareness about LGBTQIA+ legal issues, eliminating stereotypes about LGBTQIA+ individuals, and fostering a supportive LGBTQIA+ and ally community throughout the law school.
summary: The OUTLaw (previously the Lambda Legal Society) includes members, friends, and allies of the LGBTQIA+ community, and people who are interested in the issues of civil rights and gender and sexuality in the law.

name: VolOUT
description:
Welcome LGBTQ+ and Ally VOLS! 

VolOUT is a student organization on campus that provides a means of communication and community among queer and ally students at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. We are a primarily social club that wants to make the experience of LGBTQ+ students at UTK as positive as possible.

VolOUT meetings are held weekly on Thursdays at 7pm in Hodges Library 213. More detailed information will be shared within our Announcements GroupMe. These meetings will offer a comfortable, safe-space on campus to exchange ideas and cultivate community among queer students.   

Along with weekly meetings VolOUT also hosts many social events throughout the semester; these events serve as a social outlet wherein members are encouraged to make friends and build relationships. Social events such as themed parties, movies nights, dinners, etc. allow for VolOUT members to interact with others like themselves.

We accept members throughout the year. Become a member of VolOUT now!
summary: VolOUT is a student-led social organization that facilitates communication and community among LGBTQ+ and ally students at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville.

name: Language and World Business Student Association
description:
We are an organization dedicated to helping declared Language and World Business students maximize their college experience and degree through sharing ideas and information. It really is all about connecting with peers with shared interest and ambitions. We host professional development events where we network with faculty, students, alumni, and businesses.

Additionally, we volunteer together and contribute to the Knoxville community. We help each other in study abroad processes by sharing knowledge and experiences through peer review.
summary:
Language and World Business Student Association
"Where Learning Language is Good Business"

name: Latin American Student Organization at the University of Tennessee Knoxville
description: The Latin American Student Organization (LASO) is a student run organization welcoming not only latin/hispanic nationalities and cultures, but also anyone seeking to learn. Through our social events, fundraisers, service opportunities, and collaborations with other organizations, we emphasize inclusivity and promote visibility of the Latinx community. LASO promotes equality and connections among the community on campus as well as empower all students at UTK.
summary: LASO is a vibrant community dedicated to celebrating and promoting Latin American culture, heritage, and diversity. Through engaging events, educational initiatives, and social gatherings, we foster a welcoming environment for students.

name: Latter Day Saints Student Association
description: The Church of Jesus Christ Student Association seeks to embrace the volunteer creed and give back to the Knoxville community. Members of our organization will be provided many opportunities to foster self-development, leadership skills, and a sense of community. We encourage students of all races, religions, sexual orientations, and gender to join our club. We are inspired by the quote “When you have more than you need, build a longer table, not a higher fence.” Through a diverse group of students, we seek to become apart of the campus and Knoxville community by completing monthly service projects. We strive to provide a community and embrace all students. In addition to service, our club will provide a safe space for social and religious engagements.
summary: Our mission is to engage students in meaningful service through community immersion and provide a place for all students to foster self-development through servant leadership and to build the community around us in positive ways.

name: Love United Gospel Choir
description: The mission of the Love United Gospel Choir is to make available and minister the abundant love and fullness of Jesus Christ through song while providing students an opportunity to sing and share gospel music, form mutual encouragement, and support the University of Tennessee and surrounding communities.
summary: Love United Gospel Choir is an organization that strives to form strong bonds within the group and around the campus/community, but also to share the love of music through song.

name: Manthan - Indian Students' Association at the University of Tennessee
description:
The purpose of the organization is to provide a platform for students of Indian origin to come together and celebrate the rich cultural heritage of India and to promote cultural understanding and exchange of ideas and views between the people of India and those of USA and other countries.

As is it's meaning, Manthan strives to extract the 'AMRIT' of cultural exchange between these two countries from the sea of happiness and despair. Manthan WELCOMES the new applicants to UTK, with an eagerness to assist them in all possible ways.

Join our WhatsApp group to stay in touch- https://chat.whatsapp.com/Br70YAhnnYU8Bwp2ajT1sQ

- Manthan Executive Body
summary: Manthan - Indian Students' Association

name: Martial Arts Club
description:
Club Objective:
The Martial Arts Club at the University of Tennessee (MAC) exists to:

1. Offer interested persons an opportunity to learn the principles of the Martial Arts as a means toward physical and mental discipline.

2. Enjoy and practice the Martial Arts for fun and/or competitive purposes.

3. Promote general interest in the Martial Arts as an aid in developing sportsmanship, leadership, cultural diversity, physical and mental well-being, self-control, self-confidence, self-defense, and an overall better lifestyle

 

More Information:

A copy of our schedule for the 2025 Fall Semester can be found under documents, as well as a copy of the club constitution. 

The MAC website can be found here or by clicking the earth icon below

Completion of the Sports Club Release Form (found below under forms) is required to participate. The release form is valid for one school year. This form is accessible on mobile, and we ask that you complete it when joining our club.

Dues are $45 per semester. You are allowed to attend 2 meetings for each sport before paying dues; however, you are free to turn these in whenever the semester begins.

For the most up-to-date information, please email khollid8@vols.utk.edu for access to our GroupMe and Discord.

Class Schedules (subject to change):

Judo

  • Location: HPER Building 202 B
  • Time: every Monday from 8pm-9pm and Thursday from 8pm-10pm (attendance permitting) 
  • Instructors: Mike Takada (miketakata@gmail.com) and Josh Elliott (joshuahelliott@gmail.com) 

 

Jiu Jitsu 

  • Location: HPER Building at Vol Wall
  • Time: every Thursday from 6:30pm-8pm
  • Instructors: Dan McMillan (danielsmcmillan@gmail.com) and Josh Elliott (joshuaelliott@gmail.com) 

 

Isshin-Ryu Karate

  • Location: HPER 202 C
  • Time: Every Thursday from 6pm-8pm
  • Instructors: Darren Gilbert (dgilber1@utk.edu) and Alex Perkins (alexperkins18@yahoo.com) 

 

Kendo 

  • Location: TRECS Studio 9
  • Time: Every Friday from 6pm-8pm
  • Instructor: Shigetoshi Eda (seda@utk.edu) 

 

Iaido

  • Location: HPER 202 C
  • Time: every Monday and Wednesday from 4pm-5pm 
  • Instructor: Alexander Walton (zanderwalton@gmail.com) 

 

Chinese Kung Fu/Gong Fu

  • Location: Fulton Bottoms Rugby Field
  • Time: Wednesdays and Fridays 4-6:30pm
  • Instructor: Angus Creech (acreech4@vols.utk.edu) 

 

Taekwondo 

  • Location: Student Aquatic Center 2nd Floor 
  • Time: Mondays and Fridays 7:30pm-8:30pm
  • Instructor: Alexander Walton (zanderwalton@gmail.com) 

 

Kajukenbo 

  • Location: TRECS Studio 9
  • Time: Thursdays 8pm-10pm
  • Instructor: Ethan Dumont (ekdumont@vols.utk.edu)
summary: The MAC was founded in 1991. Since the beginning, the club has given students the opportunity to compete nation wide and develop effective self-defense skills. The club offers instruction to all levels of athletes and contributes to their needs.

name: Master's Social Work Organization
description:
The Master’s Social Work Organization (MSWO) is open to all enrolled master’s students at the UT College of Social Work. M.S.S.W. students are represented on various campus and college-wide committees, including steering and faculty committees.

STUDENT LEADERSHIP:  MSWO members at each campus elect their local officers and representatives on an annual basis.

PURPOSE: To provide student leadership, act as an advocate in student issues, foster group cohesiveness among students, faculty, administrators, alumni, and social work professionals in the University and in the local community, provide a social network, and enhance the positive experience of pursing an M.S.S.W.

ACTIVITIES: MSWO engages students in such activities as student forums, agency and local issue involvement, guest lectures, and student-planned social interactions. 

MEETINGS: Zoom for Knoxville & Online.  (See VOLink for dates and link to join).  Meetings are held on a regular basis throughout the academic year, and all students are encouraged to participate.
summary: Master's Social Work Organization

name: Materials Research Society - University of Tennessee, Knoxville Chapter
description:
From https://mrsutk.github.io/MRSofUTK.github.io/:

Materials Research Society is an organization of materials researchers from academia, industry, and government that promotes communication for the advancement of interdisciplinary materials research to improve the quality of life.


The UT Student Chapter of MRS works together with the Materials Science & Engineering department with seminars and networking with representatives from industry as well as developing scientific presentation and community service skills via Outreach to local K-12 schools.
summary: Materials Research Society-University of Tennessee, Knoxville Chapter seeks to promote professional development, provide an environment that encourages interdisciplinary discussions, and enhance both undergraduate and graduate student involvement.

name: Men's Soccer Club
description: Men's Soccer is a club that promotes the game of soccer to any and all skill levels. We play at a competitive level against other teams and colleges around the Southeast and the country. We typically have practice twice a week on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 8-10pm. Everyone is welcome to come out and play. To make sure that you have all dates correct for the beginning of each season, be sure to follow our instagram for dates and times, or find us at the club sports conventions at the beginning of each year!
summary: Men's Soccer is a club that promotes the game of soccer to any and all skill levels. We play at a competitive level against other teams and colleges around the Southeast and the country.

name: Men's Ultimate Club
description:
We are the official Men's Ultimate Frisbee club team at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Contact TennMUltimate@gmail.com or follow our IG @tennesseeultimate for information on joining.

Our Website For More Info

2025 Highlights
summary: We are the official Men's Ultimate Frisbee club team at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville.  Contact TennMUltimate@gmail.com or follow our IG @tennesseeultimate for information on joining.

name: American Society for Microbiology Student Chapter at the University of Tennessee
description: MUC is the Microbiology Undergraduate Club; the UTK chapter of the American Society for Microbiology. This club was formed to promote interest in microbiology within UT; to foster interactions among students interested in microbiology and enhance their experiences at UT; encourage exchanges between students and the faculty in the microbiology department and other microbiologists across the UT campus; learn about ongoing research and research opportunities in UT microbiology labs; introduce career options to microbiology students.
summary: This is a club dedicated to helping undergraduates who are in the field of microbiology. Whether future plans include professional or graduate programs, we are here to help you explore all possibilities.

name: Multicultural Mentoring Program
summary: The Multicultural Mentoring Program's (MMP) mission is to provide personal support, assistance, social guidance, and positive campus survival skills to first-year students of color by developing activities and interactions with upper-class student.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Fraternity, Lambda Chapter
description: The vision of Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Fraternity at the University of Tennessee is to foster a community of individuals interested in pursuing careers within the legal and government fields. We are committed to providing our brothers with the tools necessary to succeed academically, professionally, and socially, and in doing so create leaders for the future. Our members receive exclusive access to our own LSAT course, a google drive of law school application resources, as well as government officials and lawyer guest speakers. We are apart of a national organization that lends us additional resources exclusively for KAPi members
summary: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Co-ed Professionally Fraternity at the University of Tennessee

name: Phi Alpha Honor Society
description: The purpose of Phi Alpha Honor Society is to provide a closer bond among students of social work and promote humanitarian goals and ideals.  Phi Alpha fosters high standards of education for social workers and invites into membership those who have attained excellence in scholarship and achievement in social work.
summary: Phi Alpha Honor Society is a nationally recognized organization that invites social work students to give of themselves to the communities in which they serve.

name: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc.
description: Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc. is founded on the principles of brotherhood, scholarship, and service. Our national Motto is Culture for Service and Service for Humanity. Our fraternity is committed to fostering positive change in our communities. Since the Kappa Chi Chapter of Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc. was chartered on the University of Tennessee campus in 1979, our brotherhood has played pivotal roles in numerous aspects of campus life. Our brothers have held memberships in SGA, Black Cultural Programming Committee, NAACP, Student African American Brotherhood, UT football and track teams, Collegiate Black Christians, and numerous other student organizations. Our alumni have graduated and had successful lives and careers and still hold the spirit of Kappa Chi dear in their hearts. The Chapter motto is "The Ball is Rolling."
summary: Phi Beta Sigma is founded on the principles of brotherhood, scholarship, and service. Our motto is "Culture for Service and Service for Humanity." Our fraternity is committed to fostering positive change in our communities.

name: Phi Eta Sigma
description:
Phi Eta Sigma is a national honor society inspired by the past and dedicated to the future whose goal is to encourage and reward academic excellence among freshmen in institutions of higher learning. 

The oldest and largest freshman honor society, Phi Eta Sigma was founded at the University of Illinois on March 22, 1923, and now has more than three hundred chapters throughout the United States and more than 700,000 members. 

Induction is an outward recognition of personal accomplishment and serves as an incentive for continued high scholarship. It provides members the opportunity to meet freshman scholars from all academic disciplines on their campuses and to join them in promoting academic excellence. Members may apply for scholarships available from national and from local chapters.


Type of Organization: Honor Societies.

Membership Criteria: Must have 3.5 GPA, join as a freshman, and have less than 20 transfer hours.

Purpose: To encourage and reward academic excellence among freshmen in institutions of higher learning.
summary: This is the official UTK Phi Eta Sigma page.  If you have been invited by email and are a current member, please log in and join so that we can keep in contact with you.  Phi Eta Sigma is a lifetime national honor society founded in 1923.

name: Phi Kappa Psi
description:
The Phi Kappa Psi Fraternity is a private association of educated men who endeavor to live honorably and humanely. Its Brotherhood is open to men of talent and character, but to those in particular who as gentleman feel an affinity with one another in the common ways of college life and, moreover, in the pursuit of excellence. Such men may differ in their origins, backgrounds, and beliefs, and even be set upon diverse purposes, interests, and vocations. Yet as Brothers in Phi Kappa Psi, they are vitally united in their dedication to the highest standards of intellectual, emotional, and spiritual integrity.

Phi Kappa Psi encourages all its Brothers to express the true measure of their abilities and personalities through the great joy of serving others.
summary: Phi Kappa Psi

name: Phi Mu
description:
Phi Mu is a women’s organization which provides personal and academic development, service to others, commitment to excellence, and lifelong friendship through a shared tradition. Phi Mu Fraternity was founded at Wesleyan College in Macon, GA, in 1852. Originally founded as a literary club named the Philomathean Society, we are the second oldest sorority in the nation. For over 170 years, our sisterhood has provided social opportunities, a support network, and personal enrichment resources to the women who have joined Phi Mu. The Kappa Chapter of Phi Mu was established at the University of Tennessee Knoxville in 1908. As one of the oldest and longest standing chapters of Phi Mu, we’re proud of our letters and our traditions!

 

The first line of the Phi Mu creed is, “To lend to those less fortunate a helping hand,” and it has been a call for our members for over 100 years. On a national level, Phi Mu’s philanthropy is Children’s Miracle Network Hospitals. Our chapter is incredibly fortunate to have a Children’s Miracle Network hospital located just a few blocks from our campus: East Tennessee Children’s Hospital. We’re proud to support Children’s Miracle Network Hospitals in our area, specifically ETCH, through events such as Hospital Parties, VOLthon, Miracle Week, and Cheesin' For Children. In 2025, The Kappa Chapter of Phi Mu raised over $196,527 for East Tennessee Children’s Hospital and we’re on track to accomplish even bigger goals this year!

 

The University of Tennessee Knoxville has a vibrant campus life full of activities for all interests. The Volunteer Spirit runs tried and true throughout our chapter. Our members are involved in several aspects of Rocky Top, and we’re proud that our members opt to dive into our campus and all it has to offer. From honor societies and intramural sports to student government and outreach programs, Phi Mu members play a role in organizations across campus that help our student community thrive. 

 

Love, honor, truth—These words are the cornerstones on which our Fraternity was founded and are the principles which guide our sisterhood today. Within our cherished sisterhood, each member finds purpose and belonging. Phi Mu promotes vibrant living, encouraging members to achieve their personal best!
summary: Phi Mu

name: Phi Sigma Kappa
description: To Promote Brotherhood, To Stimulate Scholarship, To Develop Character.
summary: Phi Sigma Kappa

name: Pi Beta Phi
description:
The Tennessee Gamma chapter of Pi Beta Phi was installed at the University of Tennessee in 1947. With a clear commitment to service and a culture that develops the whole person, Pi Beta Phi Fraternity is a values based organization in which individual development is a key component of the mission and vision of the organization. Pi Beta Phi attracts women who possess a desire to be a part of a dynamic organization that will promote growth in all areas of life. 

A dedication to our core values is at the heart of our organization. Pi Beta Phi offers its members the opportunity to be a part of an organization where members care for one another and share the bonds of sisterhood.
summary: Pi Beta Phi is a sorority committed to the cultivation of sisterhood, service, and a culture that lends to the personal and intellectual growth of all members.

name: Pi Kappa Phi
description:
Pi Kappa Phi is a national social fraternity with a strong emphasis on leadership, service, and brotherhood. Founded on December 10, 1904, at the College of Charleston in South Carolina, the fraternity has since grown to over 175 active chapters across the United States. The organization is known for its commitment to inclusivity and its founding principles of character, leadership, academics, responsibility, and integrity.


One distinctive feature of Pi Kappa Phi is its philanthropic arm, The Ability Experience. Established in 1977, this initiative focuses on serving individuals with disabilities through various programs and events. The fraternity is dedicated to creating a more accessible and understanding world for people with disabilities, and members actively participate in events such as "Build America" and "Gear Up Florida" to raise awareness and funds for these causes.

 

Pi Kappa Phi places a significant emphasis on leadership development, providing its members with opportunities to hone their skills through workshops, conferences, and hands-on experiences. The fraternity's commitment to academic excellence is evident through its scholarship programs and initiatives that encourage members to prioritize their education.

 

Known for its vibrant brotherhood, Pi Kappa Phi fosters a sense of community among its members. The fraternity is guided by a strong set of values that promote personal growth, responsibility, and a commitment to making a positive impact on society. Overall, Pi Kappa Phi stands as a distinguished institution in the realm of Greek life, with a rich history and a commitment to fostering well-rounded individuals who contribute positively to their communities.
summary: The Alpha Sigma Chapter of Pi Kappa Phi was founded On Campus in 1931, and Re-Chartered On Campus in 2017. We pride ourselves on building Exceptional Leaders and providing Uncommon Opportunities to our members.

name: Platypus Affiliated Society
description:
The Platypus Affiliated Society, established in December 2006, organizes reading groups, public fora, research and journalism focused on the problems and tasks inherited from the "Old" (1920s–30s), "New" (1960s–70s) and post-political (1980s–90s) Left for the possibilities of emancipatory politics today.

http://platypus1917.org/about/statement/
summary: Platypus organizes reading groups, public fora, research, and journalism focused on problems and tasks inherited from the “Old” (1920s-30s), “New” (1960s-70s), and post-political (1980s-90s) Left for possibilities of emancipatory politics today.

name: Print Club
description:
Print Club is an official student organization of the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. The club works to foster the understanding and appreciation of printmaking through sponsoring visiting artists, workshops, print shop open houses, print club exhibitions, and works to raise funds through print sales for students to attend the annual SGC International Conference and the American Print Alliance's Print Dialogue Day.

The club is open to any students, graduate and undergraduate, previously or currently enrolled in a printmaking course at UTK.
summary: UTK Print Club

name: Progressive Student Alliance
description:
Description:

The Progressive Student Alliance is a grassroots student movement focused on organizing for student and worker power at the University of Tennessee.

 

Mission:

Our mission is to empower student and worker voices through solidarity and action.

 

Vision:

“Nobody’s free until everybody’s free”

-Fannie Lou Hamer

 

We envision a world in which society and human relationships are organized cooperatively, not competitively. We struggle towards a world in which all people live in freedom from oppression, in which people are valued as whole human beings rather than exploited in a quest for productivity and profits.

 

Current Campaigns:

  • Living Wages for Campus Workers: We are working alongside United Campus Workers to advocate for a wage that meets the standard of living. This campaign humanizes the workers and provides a space for them to speak out.
  • End Deathtraps: In response to the Rana Plaza factory collapse and ongoing violations of worker safety in Bangladesh, PSA is joining with over a hundred universities to demand that college apparel companies sign the Accord on Fire and Safety in Bangladesh and make a real commitment to worker safety.
  • Put The People First: PSA is a member of the Put The People First coalition, a statewide coalition organization that advocates for living wages, democratic rights and funding for higher education.

 

Past Victories:

  • In Fall 2013, PSA and United Campus Workers won a dollar raise for campus workers, from $8.50 to $9.50.
  • Assisted in the formation of the United Campus Workers
  • Succeed in affiliating UT with the Workers Rights Consortium, an independent labor rights watchdog that doesn’t accept money from the brands it regulates.
  • Brought Alta Gracia, a living wage, union made, clothing brand, to the UT bookstore.
  • Succeed in forcing Adidas to pay 1.8 million in legally owed severance wages to former workers of the PT Kizone factory in Indonesia, alongside USAS.

 Community and University Solidarity Actions

  • Participation in a Mike Brown Walk-Out in early December. Join students as we marched to shut down the intersection at Cumberland with student. I peace and strong moment when UTK Students chose to stand in solidarity for justice. A pivotal moment for millennials once again organizing to see triumph.
  • Participants and volunteers for the Market Square/Christmas Parade shut down with over 300 community and UTK student. We met in market square in it's center to the Christmas tree where we chant, sang, and shared grieve, love, and hurt. We marched through the crowds during the Christmas parades. Through abuses we remained peaceful, we died in for 4 minutes with blacks only, in solidarity with Black Lives Matter and Brown brother and sisters. And the help and courage from helpful and active allies.
  • Dec. 4 2014 PSA Co-organized and participated in the Fight for Fifteen Strikes and Strikers down Cumberland visiting fast food restaurants along the way until we literally left Cumberland. Lead by Fight for 15 Workers first and foremost.
summary: The Progressive Student Alliance is a grassroots student movement organizing for student and worker power at the University of Tennessee.

name: Psi Chi - The International Honor Society in Psychology
description:
Psi Chi is the International Honor Society in Psychology, founded in 1929 for the purposes of encouraging, stimulating, and maintaining excellence in scholarship, and advancing the science of psychology. The aims of Psi Chi are summarized in two Greek words: psyche, suggesting "mind" or scholarship, and symbolizing enrichment of the mind, and cheires, meaning "hands" and symbolizing fellowship and research.

In order to be a part of UTK's chapter of PsiChi, you must apply through the national website first. Then, you must submit your DARS report to Dr. Bob (drbob@utk.edu) to ensure that you qualify. Once you meet both of these requirements, you will be an official member.

*You can still attend events and meetings as a part of PsychVOLogy!
summary: Psi Chi - The International Honor Society in Psychology

name: Public Relations Student Society of America
description: The UTK PRSSA chapter is a place where students from all majors can learn more about the field of communications and public relations through speaker series, networking with peers, and engaging events. We are affiliated with PRSA which is a national organization that creates opportunities for public relations professionals to learn and grow in their field. Being apart of our organization opens doors for students as they try to navigate the professional world of public relations.
summary: Public Relations Student Society of America

name: Reformed University Fellowship
description: RUF is a community of students that is trying to love God, love people, and love the University of Tennessee. The way that we do that is to create safe places (large groups, small groups, one-on-ones) for students of all types and backgrounds to process the story of Jesus and become equipped to live their lives in light of His story. For more information, check out our website www.utk.ruf.org or our Facebook group "RUF at UTK."
summary: Reformed University Fellowship

name: Climbing Club
description:
Climbing Club meets at the VolWall located in HPER room 202-b on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 8-10pm! Club dues are currently $20/semester or $35/year. We go on outdoor bouldering and top rope trips, as well as camping!  Come on out and get psyched and ready to crush!

(newcomers are encouraged to try out a few of the meetings to get a feel for how Climbing Club works. We ask you to try no more than 3 meetings before making your decision)

Easy to Join! Just go into the page and click the blue button "join" (assuming you are logged in) and then scroll down to sign our release form!
summary: A club for rock climbers of any skill level that are excited to come out, make new friends, and climb! Climbing Club has exclusive climbing hours after the VolWall has closed. We also attend competitions like Triple Crown and go on outdoor trips!

name: Rotaract UTK
description:
Join us at Rotaract UTK! We are a service organization helping students get involved in community service on campus, in Knoxville, and internationally, with support from the experience and structure of Rotary International. Reach out by email or check out our Instagram to join us for an upcoming volunteer event!

 

Contact us at: utk.rotaract.club@gmail.com
summary: The Rotaract Club of The University of Tennessee A service organization that connects students to volunteering opportunities on and off campus Affiliated with the Knoxville Rotary Club

name: Women's Rugby Club
description: UTWRFC's mission is to compete, promote and encourage the sport of rugby while proudly representing the University of Tennessee. The club aims to compete at their highest athletic level while exemplifying sportsmanship. Additionally, the club seeks to promote the sport and culture of rugby in hopes that it will continue to grow at all levels, high school, college, and beyond in east Tennessee.
summary: University of Tennessee Women's Rugby Club

name: Sailing Club at UT
description:
Follow @tennesseesailing on Instagram for club updates!

The University of Tennessee Sailing Club has been an actively promoting the sport of sailing to university students almost 3 decades. We teach those with little to no experience as well as develop sailors with years of racing experience. Annually, we hold our home regatta, in the spring and fall, for SAISA North schools. This provides a variety of competitors including club sports and varsity sailing teams. Concord Yacht Club graciously hosts this regatta as well as all of our sailing events throughout the week. Our US Sailing Level 1 instructors teach our Learn to Sail courses and have the option to coach local high school sailing teams.
summary: The University of Tennessee Sailing Club has been an actively promoting the sport of sailing to university students for well over a decade. We teach those with little to no experience as well as develop sailors with years of racing experience.

name: Sculpture Club
