description: The Sculpture Club at the University of Tennessee provides an environment that encourages students to develop as artists and provide them with opportunities and events within the field of contemporary sculpture.
summary: News, events, and happenings from the Sculpture program at the University of Tennessee.

name: Sexual Empowerment and Awareness at Tennesseee
description:
Mission Statement: 

SEAT is an organization with the mission of fostering a comprehensive and intellectual discussion on sex, sexuality, and relationships with the purpose of educating the University of Tennessee student body and the Knoxville community through innovative, captivating, and entertaining methods.

 

Hi Students, Faculty, and Staff of UTK! We're SEAT (Sexual Empowerment and Awareness at Tennessee). SEAT is the student organization that organizes and presents Sex Week each year at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville. We believe in a comprehensive, sex-positive understanding of sexuality that promotes sexual health, pleasure, and empowerment. The dialogue we hope to cultivate is LGBTQ-friendly, relevant to a wide array of audiences, medically accurate, held in a safe space, and enlightening. The event does not presume everyone is having sex, so Sex Week includes programming for people who are not sexually active. SEAT addresses issues of gender, sex, sexuality, and all the aspects of our lives that are affected by these issues, including culture, politics, religion, and even the economy. SEAT is sensitive to sexual discourse within larger issues. Sex has historically been a site of oppression; therefore, discussions about sex and sexuality are important because they involve ideas not only about our personal physical and emotional lives but also about the sociocultural context in which we're immersed. This annual week-long event fosters ongoing, open, and productive dialogue and adds to the value of student life and the co-curricular education at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville. The week-long event is composed of a variety of events focusing on education and entertainment, including basic tabling, seminars, keynote speakers, theatrical events, a film series, and more. These events explore concepts of love, gender identity, relationships, sexual orientation, and sex. Our end-all goal is that everyone feels comfortable attending any of our events and each person leaves having a comprehensive understanding of sexuality, including health, pleasure, and empowerment. We look forward to bringing an urgently needed discussion to our campus and inviting you to lend your voices to the conversation! 

On Instagram and Twitter @SEATUTK

General Board Application: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfcpgenQglADa3xEA5Gf0OL4LIgn__saCUENWK95HMTk67PZA/viewform?usp=sf_link
summary: Sexual Empowerment and Awareness at Tennessee is an organization with the mission of fostering a comprehensive discussion on sex, sexuality, and relationships to educate the University of Tennessee student body and the Knoxville community.

name: Sigma Alpha Epsilon
description:
Sigma Alpha Epsilon was founded in 1856 at the University of Alabama under the creed of the True Gentleman. As brothers, we strive to reach the highest standards of friendship, leadership, scholarship, and service.

 

Founded on the campus here at the University of Tennessee in 1879, the brothers continue to challenge one another to become better not just academically but also within the community. With over 200 brothers involved with the Tennessee Kappa Chapter as of 2025, SAE is one of the larger fraternities on campus while still creating a bond unlike any other. Our Chapter's history runs deep and wide across the Campus and the Realm. John Moseley- former national president of SAE and dean of students at UT- once elegantly remarked that Tennessee Kappa was "the brightest jewel in Minerva's crown." To this day, Brothers strive to uphold that legacy.

 

The bond among members of Tennessee Kappa is hard for many to comprehend, as the teachings of strong convictions, focused principles, and the strength to be true gentlemen have defined this Chapter since 1879 and will far exceed one's collegiate years.
summary: Sigma Alpha Epsilon strives to give young men the leadership, scholarship, service and social experiences they need to excel in the walls outside their campus and once they graduate. We firmly believe membership is for life.

name: Sigma Alpha Sorority
description:
Sigma Alpha stands for Sisters in Agriculture. We are a professional sorority and take pride in promoting our pillars of scholarship, leadership, service, and fellowship among all sisters. Although most of our members belong to the Herbert College of Agriculture, we love to expand our roots and do accept women from any major here at UT! 


The sorority was founded in 1978 at the Ohio State University by five women who wanted an alternative to the social Greek sorority system. Since that time, Sigma Alpha has become a national organization consisting of more than 70 chapters across the US, comprised of both undergraduate and alumni members. Today, more than 7,000 members have been initiated into Sigma Alpha Sorority. We're growing steadily as others in agriculture learn about us.

The Sigma Alpha Theta Chapter at the University of Tennessee was founded on October 7, 1990. We were the eighth Sigma Alpha Chapter founded in the nation.
summary: Sigma Alpha Sorority

name: Sigma Beta Rho Fraternity, Inc.
description: Our pillars of Society, Brotherhood, and Remembrance were established upon the formation of the Alpha Chapter at the University of Pennsylvania in Philadelphia, on August 16, 1996. Our founders realized the unique promise of a Greek-lettered organization. Our official emblem is the King Cobra and our colors are Red, Black, and Green.
summary: Our pillars of Society, Brotherhood, and Remembrance were established upon the formation of the Alpha Chapter at the University of Pennsylvania in Philadelphia, on August 16, 1996. Our founders realized the unique promise of a Greek-lettered organization

name: Diverse Organization of Business Students at The University of Tennessee
description: The organization provides empowerment, networking, awareness, links to professional and academic opportunities, and community to students of all diverse populations and the University of Tennessee campus. Membership is open to all students who identify themselves as diverse. Membership is open to all students and faculty/staff members regardless of race, gender, disability, religion, or national origin.
summary: Diverse Organization of Business Students at The University of Tennessee

name: Sigma Chi
summary: Sigma Chi

name: Earth, Environmental, and Planetary Sciences Student Association
description: Geo Club works to facilitate community amongst students interested in learning more about earth, environmental, and planetary sciences. This is done through local outreach and clean-up events as well as field trips to learn more about local and regional geology and observe once in a lifetime planetary and astronomical events.
summary: Earth, Environmental, and Planetary Sciences Student Association (Geo Club) promotes fellowship among Earth, Environmental and Planetary Science Students through community building events and local geoscience outreach.

name: Tennessee Undergraduate Economics Society
description:
The Tennessee Undergraduate Economics Society comprises the student faces of economics at UTK. The club focuses on career and professional development, travel opportunities, networking, and faculty/alumni engagement. 

We meet on the first Wednesday of every month from 7:00 - 8:00 PM in room 403 of the Haslam Business Building.
summary: The Tennessee Undergraduate Economics Society is an academic organization which strives to enhance the knowledge of students interested in or studying economics.

name: Equestrian Club at UT
description:
The University of Tennessee Equestrian Team (UTET) is affiliated with the Intercollegiate Horse Shows Association (IHSA). We are a member of Zone 5 Region 1, and travel across Tennessee and Kentucky for competitions. Post season competition for the highest placing riders during the regular season include Regionals, Zones (Hunt Seat), Semi-Finals (Western), and Nationals at locations across the country. Hunt Seat riders compete in Equitation on the Flat and Equitation over Fences. Western riders compete in Horsemanship, Ranch Riding, and Reining. Since 1975, the UTET has helped collegiate riders compete without needing to own a horse. All levels of experience are welcome, and riders who have never ridden before are as valuable as those who have competed at the National and World levels.

The team practices at 2 private barns in West Knoxville (Hunt Seat) and Roane County (Western). While we highly encourage our riders to compete with us, showing is optional. Riders have the opportunity to ride in weekly lessons based on horse availability, with preference given to competing members.
summary: An equestrian organization allowing UT students to ride and compete during college with no need to own a horse. All levels welcome!

name: FarmHouse
description:
FarmHouse is a fraternity dedicated to the building of men. It's a college home in which a man can embrace leadership, share responsibilities, make lasting friendships, express his inner self in an atmosphere of trust and understanding, learn lessons in living in a community of which he is an integral part, be accepted as a participant, learn the essence of brotherhood, and learn how the caring of one man - and of a community of men - for the individual, the brother, for the community, and for the mutual welfare can benefit the greater world 

FarmHouse is found on college campuses throughout the United States and into Canada, mostly at Land-Grant colleges and universities. It ranges from coast to coast - North Carolina in the East, Washington and Idaho in the West, Mississippi and Texas in the South, and Minnesota and Alberta in the North. Growth has been slow and careful. FarmHouse is dedicated more to the propagation of quality than of numbers.

This home, this brotherhood of students, is the center of campus activities for hundreds of men in agriculture and related sciences, for men who are reared on the farm and those who have an interest and/or respect for rural life.

This FarmHouse home, this gathering place of alumni - of this chapter and other chapters - is the tie between the past, the present, and the future, of men who have an abiding interest in FarmHouse ideals and in the welfare of men of like mind and heart.

FarmHouse is dedicated to promoting the moral and intellectual welfare of its members, to creating an atmosphere of learning which all will inspire men in their scholarly endeavors, to continuing growth through new avenues of learning when school days are past.

FarmHouse encourages social growth and an awareness and practice of the conduct of a gentleman that will become so much a part of the man that he is no longer conscious of effort in achieving social amenities.

FarmHouse stresses faith in God and urges each man to worship in the tradition of his own Church and in accordance with his own beliefs with tolerance for his brothers.

FarmHouse stresses loyalty among its members to: their country, their community, their university, their fraternity, their families, and each other.

FarmHouse strives for: excellence in scholarship (knowing that scholarship is the key which will open many doors later in life), the doors of the mind as doors to fuller appreciation of life, to answers to ethical and practical questions, and to success in industry, business, and agriculture.

And so FarmHouse strives for scholarship. But it strives in larger measure for the well-rounded personality, the development of all facets of a human being important to the kind of person he will become.

FarmHouse sows the seed today which will, with time and nurture, produce the leaders of tomorrow's world: the farmers, the research workers, the teachers, the statesmen, the business executives, and the professional men of the future whose task it will be to: solve the problems of increasing food production, feeding the exploding world population, promote peace among nations, and learning and teaching the essentials for humane existence in a world of technology and science.

FarmHouse Fraternity is what it is by reason of the faithful, loyal and enthusiastic efforts of its members. Whatever greatness it may boast, whatever influence it wields, whatever reputation it may have, all of these and each of these is to be credited to the members of the fraternity.

The goal of a member should be not only to uphold the ideals and reputation he has inherited, but to improve and expand them, for to stand still, to maintain status quo is to take a step backwards.

FarmHouse charges each member to search to his very depths to know, "If every member were just like me, what kind of fraternity would FarmHouse be?"
summary: FarmHouse Fraternity is an agricultural based IFC fraternity that is accepting of all majors. Our motto is "Builder of Men", building men intellectually, spiritually, socially/morally, and physically.

name: Fencing Club at UT
description:
The only club at the University of Tennessee practicing the Olympic sport of fencing. The club is an official member of the United States Fencing Association (USFA) in the Tennessee Division and competes in USFA-sanctioned tournaments across the southeast. We are also a member of the United States Association of Collegiate Fencing Clubs (USACFC) as well as the Southern Intercollegiate Fencing Association (SIFA).

We are open to all students and staff, regardless of skill level, and provide all necessary instruction and equipment!
summary: The University of Tennessee's Official Fencing Club, representing the university in the United States Fencing Association (USFA) throughout the Southeastern U.S.

name: German Club
description: A club dedicated to learning about and celebrating the German language and culture, as well as connecting with German majors, minors, and professors to discuss how you can utilize the German language skills in the workplace.
summary: A club dedicated to learning about and celebrating the German language and culture, as well as connecting with German majors, minors, and professors to discuss how you can utilize the German language skills in the workplace.

name: Graduate Nutrition Student Association
description: This organization is for graduate nutrition students who wish to develop professional relationships; deepen understanding of  biomedical nutrition sciences, clinical nutrition, and community and public health nutrition; have a positive impact in communities and public policy; and cultivate meaningful relationships with peers.
summary: The Graduate Nutrition Student Association is a student organization open to all graduate students in Nutrition.

name: Graduate Student Senate
description:
The Graduate Student Senate, or GSS, represents the interests of all graduate and professional students at the University of Tennessee. As the official voice of graduate students and professional students, GSS provides the opinion of the graduate student body to administrators and works towards the betterment of the graduate and professional student experience on campus. In addition, GSS has partnerships with various organizations and departments on campus to provide valuable programming for graduate and professional students, including Graduate Student Appreciation Week (GPSAW), GSS Travel Awards, providing funds to graduate and professional students traveling to conferences to present original work, and a GSS Awards Breakfast, recognizing outstanding graduate and professional students and faculty and staff that support them.

The GSS participates in the establishment of policies and regulations relating to the graduate program, in consultation with and subject to the final approval of the dean of the Graduate School and university officials. Furthermore, the GSS also makes recommendations to appropriate decision-making officers in matters concerning graduate and professional students. Each academic department offering a graduate or professional degree elects a representative to serve in the senate; the GSS President and Vice President are elected in the annual Student Government Association elections. The voting membership of the GSS consists of the President and Vice President plus representatives from each academic graduate program, the College of Law, and the College of Veterinary Medicine. In addition to internal strategic planning and legislative committees, GSS senators serve as representatives on various university-wide committees, including the Graduate Council.
summary: The Graduate Student Senate represents graduate and professional students at UTK.

name: Gymnastics Club at the University of Tennessee
description:
The Gymnastics Club at the University of Tennessee is a co-ed
recreational club for all experience levels, from those who have never stepped
foot in a gymnastics facility to those who have done the sport for most of
their lives and would like an outlet to continue in college. The Club members
are also given the opportunity to compete in the National Association of Intercollegiate
Gymnastics Clubs (NAIGC) meets. The association and club both foster the values
of facilitating communication and building a community of support and
camaraderie that encourages sportsmanship, leadership, teamwork, and fun.
summary: This is a club for people to try the sport of gymnastics at any level of skill while having fun and making great friends while doing so.

name: Habitat for Humanity Campus Chapter
description:
**Please email utkhabitat@gmail.com as we don't communicate via VOLink. Thanks!

Find us on Instagram @utkhabiat


The purpose of the Habitat for Humanity Campus Chapter at the University of Tennessee Knoxville is to support the work of Habitat, while also developing a strong community dedicated to Habitat's mission at our University. We work closely with the Knoxville Habitat Affiliate, and one of our ultimate goals is to raise enough money to fund a Habitat house for a deserving family here in Knoxville.  The family will then pay the house off with a ZERO interest 30 year mortgage and those funds will be kicked back into the organization to help other families and their homes.

Habitat for Humanity International is an ecumenical organization which seeks to alleviate the burden of poverty specifically through the provision of lasting homes to deserving families. However, Habitat's mission is not merely physical—they also focusing on restoring the dignity, hope, and pride that are often lost as a result of impoverishment and inequality.
summary:
The Habitat for Humanity Campus Chapter supports Habitat for Humanity through volunteer work, fundraising, and spreading awareness about Habitat's mission.

**Please email utkhabitat@gmail.com as we don't communicate via VOLink.  Thanks!

description:

name: Sigma Nu
description:
Sigma Nu Fraternity has been a tradition at the University of Tennessee since 1921 and thrives upon its ideals of Love, Truth, and Honor. At UT, Sigma Nu is active on campus and participates in intramurals, university events, and engages in services with in the Knoxville Community. Over 2,000 young men have been initiated into the Epsilon Eta chapter of Sigma Nu at the University of Tennessee.

Sigma Nu Fraternity officially began on January 1, 1869 when three honorable cadets at Virginia Military Institute joined together in the spirit of brotherhood to oppose the hazing they witnessed at VMI. With some 180+ active chapters between the United States and Canada, men of honor today are continuously being shaped into the leaders of tomorrow. The brotherhood still strives to uphold the same ideals of love, truth, and honor set forth by our founders. Nationally, Sigma Nu is a Tier One fraternity—meaning over 150 chapters. Only eight national fraternities hold this distinction.

Socially, athletically and academically Sigma Nu stays active on the campus. The Epsilon Eta chapter fields teams in all intramural events offer by UT. Socially the chapter offers a strong social calendar and traditionally joins with a sorority in large campus events, such as Homecoming and All-Sing. Academically, the chapter offers financial discounts to new candidates who display academic excellence.

Today the Epsilon Eta chapter of Sigma Nu is one of the fastest growing fraternities at the University of Tennessee. We encourage you to become a part of a brotherhood that values Love, Honor and Truth.
summary: Sigma Nu is a Greek social fraternity within the Interfraternity Council. With our values of Love, Honor, and Truth, we strive to build better men through academic success, leadership development, and campus involvement.

name: Sigma Phi Lambda
description:
Sigma Phi Lambda is a sorority that strives to glorify God by providing a source of fellowship for Christian college women who sincerely seek to know His person, His will, and His ways. It is a non-denominational organization of sisters where fellowship with our most high God is fostered by growth in unity with one another.

Our heart and passion can by summed up in our sorority verse, Romans 15:5-6:

"May the God who gives endurance and encouragement give you a spirit of unity among yourselves as you follow Christ Jesus so that with one heart and one mouth you may glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ."
summary: Sigma Phi Lambda is a non-denominational sorority that strives to glorify God by providing a source of fellowship for Christian college women who sincerely seek to know His person, His will, and His ways.

name: Sigma Sigma Rho Sorority, Incorporated
description: Sigma Sigma Rho is historically a South Asian-based sorority and we were the first sorority of its kind to be established on the East Coast. Our motives and goals are expressed in what are letters stand for Sisterhood, Society, and Remembrance. Sisterhood is what we work for. Society is whom we give to. Remembrance is what lies in our hearts. Sigma Sigma Rho embodies unity amongst women, strives to serve society on a small and large scale, and holds fast in instilling a sense of pride in the background of each individual sister.
summary: Sigma Sigma Rho is historically a South Asian-based but not exclusive sorority founded on the principles of Sisterhood, Society, and Remembrance.

name: Women's Soccer Club
description:
We are a great group of fun girls who enjoy playing competitive soccer. This is a great way to create new friendships while also staying active!

We're awesome, join our club!
summary: Soccer, Women's

name: Society of American Archivists Chapter University of Tennessee
description: The purpose of this organization shall be: Serve as a means of introducing new archivists into the profession. Enhance the educational function by providing an additional focus for the students to discuss archival issues, identify with the profession, and engage in professional activities. Promote communication among student members and with other members of the Society. Develop the leaders of tomorrow’s archival profession. Attract new members into SAA.
summary: The SAA-UTK serves as an organization to connect students with an interest in archival practices and to facilitate learning and networking within the profession.

name: Tennessee Baja Racing
description: Tennessee Baja Racing is a student design team supported by the Tickle College of Engineering's department of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering, with connections industry partners in manufacturing, automotive, and other fields. Our mission is to create an environment where students grow, learn, and apply skills through the fast paced and demanding motorsports environment, specifically the Society of Automotive Engineers Baja Series. Each year, our team designs and builds a high performance, single seat off-road race car which then competes against other colleges from around the country at national competitions. Our organization is open to students from all majors and years.
summary: Tennessee Baja Racing is a student design team competing in the Society of Automotive Engineers Baja SAE competition.

name: Business Analytics Society
description:
BAS has a mission to allow all interested undergraduate and graduate students at The University of Tennessee-Knoxville to learn about opportunities within the growing Business Analytics field while creating a network of peers and professionals. We are bridge connecting students to the professional world of analytics.

 

If interested, join our discord!
https://discord.gg/mF9UDVBX

 

 

Please check out our website for more information: https://utk-bas.github.io/website/index.html
summary: The Business Analytics Society serves as UT's main platform for all students interested in the growing field of Business Analytics and data analysis.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers at UTK
description:
The Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE) was founded in Los Angeles, California, in 1974 by a group of engineers employed by the city of Los Angeles. Their objective was to form a national organization of professional engineers to serve as role models in the Hispanic community.

The concept of Networking was the key basis for the organization. SHPE quickly established two student chapters to begin the network that would grow to encompass the nation as well as reach countries outside the United States. Today, SHPE enjoys a strong but independent network of professional and student chapters throughout the Nation.

The organization was founded to empower the Hispanic community to thrive in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). SHPE’s mission is to change lives by empowering the Hispanic community to realize its fullest potential and to impact the world through STEM awareness, access, support, and development. Its vision is a world where Hispanics are highly valued and influential as leading innovators, scientists, mathematicians, and engineers.

SHPE is a national organization that empowers the Hispanic community in STEM through access, support, and development. SHPE’s core values—Familia, Service, Education, and Resilience—shape everything it does. Familia creates a sense of unity and belonging, Service reflects a commitment to uplifting others, Education fuels growth and opportunity, and Resilience drives members to overcome challenges. These values are vital to SHPE’s mission of building a strong, supportive network of future Hispanic leaders in STEM.
summary: SHPE changes lives by empowering the Hispanic community to realize their fullest potential and impacts the world through STEM awareness, access, support, and development.

name: Society for Human Resource Management Collegiate Chapter
description:
Over the course of the semester, students come together to learn new skills and further our education about Human Resources. This organization helps students engage in professional development opportunities through SHRM meetings and networking events, enhancing human resources skills and industry knowledge to transition students into the corporate world.

If you are interested in becoming a part of National SHRM please visit their website at www.shrm.org/students to sign up!
summary: Society for Human Resource Management Collegiate Chapter 5254

name: Society of Physics Students
description: The Society of Physics Students is dedicated to increasing professional development of its members, and connecting students with diverse backgrounds through their interest in the sciences and engineering. We spend an equal time having fun with other scientifically-minded people as well as bettering ourselves for the professional world. We go on multiple trips a year to various physics conferences and the SPS annual zone meeting where we meet up with other SPS chapters in nearby states. This is the perfect club for making friends in a tough major, and we have lots of great activities planned for you.
summary: Professional development and resources for students interested in physics.

name: Society of Women Engineers at University of Tennessee
description:
UTK SWE was charted on June 21, 1978. SWE empowers women to succeed and advance in those aspirations and be recognized for their life-changing contributions and achievements as engineers and leaders. For membership purposes, our section is The University of Tennessee (D070). 


For more information, check out our website here!
summary: Empower women to achieve full potential in careers as engineers and leaders, expand the image of the engineering and technology professions as a positive force in improving the quality of life, and demonstrate the value of diversity and inclusion.

name: Panhellenic Council
description: The Panhellenic Community is over 110 years old and rich with history. The Panhellenic community lives out its core ideology: "promote the positive, develop women and create an environment of unity and fairness." Panhellenic Council strives to develop women through the relationships they make while at UT, the leadership skills they gain, the challenge of academics, and the philanthropic and community service experiences in which they participate. For more information, visit the Office of Sorority and Fraternity Life Website!
summary: The Office of Sorority and Fraternity Life is a unit of the Office of the Dean of Students. The Panhellenic Council operates within this office and is comprised of 14 women's sororities and a governing body called the Panhellenic Council.

name: South Carrick Residents Association
description:
South Carrick Residents Association is a undergraduate student organization through South Carrick Residence Hall.  The purpose of SCRA is to foster community in the residence hall through programming for the building. 

 

Previous events include Pinterest nights and a Thanksgiving dinner.
summary: South Carrick Residents Association

name: Sports and Entertainment Law Society
description:
The Sports & Entertainment Law Society is dedicated to fostering an interest in and understanding the complex and dynamic issues associated with sports and entertainment law. SELS seeks to create a vibrant community for students and professionals in the field who share a common interest in the legal aspects of the sports and entertainment industries. This organization aims to provide valuable resources and opportunities for its members, including access to information about various careers available within the sports and entertainment fields through guest lectures, networking events, and job postings. 

SELS recognizes the diverse nature of the sports and entertainment industries and strives to encompass as many aspects pertaining to these fields as possible, including but not limited to entertainment, sports, NIL, compliance, collectives, venues, and other relevant areas. While providing a supportive and inclusive environment for community and engagement, this organization aims to provide a platform for its members to learn, grow, and connect in the ever-evolving sports and entertainment law fields.
summary: Sports and Entertainment Law Society

name: Strange Fruit On Stage Productions
shortName: Strange Fruit or Strange Fruit Dance Company
summary: Strange Fruit Dance Company has been gracing the stages of UT since 2001. Strange Fruit is composed of dedicated dancers who are passionate about their craft, and dedicated to expressing the art of dance.

name: Student Alumni Associates
description:
UT Knoxville’s Student Alumni Associates (SAA) is a student organization that advances the university by enriching the lives of Vols – current students, alumni, and future Volunteers.

SAA Activities

  • Serve as official host for UT at university and community events
  • Facilitate campus-wide programming and events that bolster students’ connection to UT, focusing on the importance and impact of philanthropy and lifelong involvement as alumni
  • Provide support and leadership for the Division of Advancement and Office of Alumni Affairs
summary: Student Alumni Associates (SAA) is a organization that aims to strengthen bonds between UT students and alumni. Members are campus leaders who represent UT at philanthropy and alumni events.

name: Animal Legal Defense Fund UT Law Student Chapter
description:
The Animal Legal Defense Fund is dedicated to providing a forum for education, advocacy, and scholarship aimed at protecting the lives and advancing the interests of nonhuman animals through the legal system, as well as raising general awareness of animal law.

The Animal Legal Defense Fund is a national organization. UT Law's organization is a student sub-chapter of the national organization.
summary: ALDF is dedicated to providing a forum for education, advocacy, and scholarship aimed at protecting the lives and advancing the interests of nonhuman animals through the legal system, as well as raising general awareness of the field of animal law.

name: Student Composer Organization at the University of Tennessee
description: A campus-wide organization open to anyone at UTK interested in music composition. We are planning on rebuilding the organization this year, with new events such as an open Coffee House and Composing Competetions.
summary: A campus-wide organization open to students with a passion for composing music at UTK.

name: Student Government Association
description: The Student Government Association comprises students pursuing the betterment of this institution so that all members of the university community are beneficiaries of the opportunities and advantages available in an environment conducive to growth. SGA exists to provide the student body with a means to deal with the affairs of students and as a forum for the expression of student views concerning student life within the University. Everyone's a member!
summary: The Student Government Association exists to provide the student body with a means to deal with the affairs of students and as a forum for the expression of student views concerning student life within the university.

name: Christian Student Center
description: Our mission is a simple one: To develop a community of students KNOW, LOVE, and SERVE Jesus. All are welcome, no matter what your religious background is. Our "main event" is a weekly devotional every Wednesday at 6 or 7:30pm at the Christian Student Center (located at 1821 Melrose Avenue on UT's campus). Come check us out to find out more about our events or visit our website at cscutk.org.
summary: Christian Student Center

name: The Navigators
description: The UT Navigators are an interdenominational community of students who are seeking a deeper relationship with God. As we grow in that relationship, we are trusting God to use us to impact the lives of others and the world around us. The heartbeat of our ministry is helping students grow to know Christ in a deep and life transforming way. We desire to help students develop the vision, passion, and skills to follow Christ for the rest of their lives. We are about: being real about who we are, loving God, loving people, developing leaders, one-on-one discipleship, spiritual transformation, and impacting our world for Christ. If you get plugged in with us, here are a few things you can look forward to: growth in your relationship with God and others, relevant and life impacting Bible studies, lasting friendships, life on life mentoring, a deeper sense of purpose and direction in life, exciting conferences, road trips, service projects, socials, fun, and much more... The ministry of The Navigators began over 80 years ago and today we have ministries on over 150 college campuses in the US. We currently have over 4500 Navigator staff in over 100 countries around the world.
summary: The Navigators at the University of Tennessee are an interdenominational Christian ministry. We seek to know Christ, make Him know, and help others do the same.

name: Women in Nuclear (University of Tennessee Chapter)
description: The University of Tennessee Student Chapter of Women in Nuclear is part of an extensive national organization that supports nuclear energy and nuclear technology fields by promoting an environment in which both women and men are able to succeed. This organization allows and encourages networking with women in the fields to further professional development. Additionally, WIN aims to provide an organized association through which the public is informed about nuclear energy and nuclear technologies.
summary: The UT Chapter of Women in Nuclear is part of a national organization that focuses on professional development and supporting cultures in which women and men in nuclear energy and technology fields can succeed.

name: Christian Student Fellowship
description: The CSF is a community who gathers together to experience life, encounter Christ, and engage our world.
summary: The CSF is a community who gathers together to experience life, encounter Christ, and engage our world.

name: American Library Association, University of Tennessee, Knoxville Chapter
description: ALA-UTK is the University of Tennessee, Knoxville student chapter of the American Libraries Association parent organization. We encourage participation in students' state library associations and broader national events and relationships. We host virtual and in-person events throughout the academic year that highlight the work of libraries and librarians across the country. We also send out a monthly newsletter with important and relevant information. While membership particularly benefits students in the School of Information Sciences Master of Science in Information Sciences graduate program, we invite students from across the UTK campus to join.
summary: ALA-UTK is the University of Tennessee, Knoxville student chapter of the American Libraries Association parent organization. We encourage participation in students’ state library associations, as well as broader national events and relationships.

name: Poultry Science Club
description: Poultry Science Club members participate in activities aimed at promoting the poultry industry. The club also participates in at least one service project each year. Members generally attend the International Poultry Exposition in Atlanta, where they can meet and interview with company representatives. Participation in this activity often leads to internship positions or permanent employment within the poultry industry. In order to fund this activity, the club prepares and sells chicken lunches annually.
summary: Poultry Science Club is an organization that participates in activities aimed at promoting the poultry industry.

name: Christian Legal Society
description:
As the University of Tennessee chapter for the Christian Legal Society, our missions and goals include the following: 
  • Cultivate spiritual growth among its members through communal prayer, fellowship, and worship; learning to share one's faith; and devotional study of the Bible and classic Christian works 
  • Show the love of Christ to the campus community and community at large by proclaiming the gospel in word and in deed, such as through a life of integrity and charitable good works; as Martin Luther put it, "to be as Christ to our neighbor."
  • Address the question, "What does it mean to be a Christian in law?" that is, learning to submit every aspect of one's calling in the legal profession to the Lordship of Jesus Christ.

We welcome all law students, alumni, and others in the community to join us in the Taylor Law Center as we seek to grow in our faith and understand how we can love others no matter their faith or background.
summary: CLS is dedicated to seeking justice with the love of God by following his principles and helping members faithfully serve Jesus in their legal professions, relationships, communities, and churches.

name: InterVarsity Graduate Christian Fellowship
summary: Grad IV is a supportive place for graduate students of all backgrounds who wish to have a spiritual community with other graduate students. Some sample offerings are monthly potlucks and special lecture events.

name: Graduate Association of MABE Engineers
description: GAME aims to provide community and academic support to the graduate students in the MABE department. Community is provided through events such as but not limited to cookouts, competitions, outreach events, social events etc. GAME provides support to the department in the form of a liaison between graduate students and the department if issues arise, providing recommendations to help strengthen the program, and providing training and orientation to incoming graduate students.
summary: We aim to be a community group within the MABE department for all graduate students in programs associated with the department. We would like to bring about a greater sense of comradery among MABE grad students and strengthen the program overall.

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description:
Our goal is to provide ASME members here at UT with experiences that will help advance our careers, as well as create a community of fellow engineering students to bond and grow with throughout our college journeys.

 

During our monthly meetings, we look to bring in potential employers who hire Mechanical Engineers from a variety of industries to discuss their line of work, types of opportunities, and what they look for in potential candidates. Additionally, we attempt to provide help with resume building through recruiters as well as offices and resources on campus. Our meetings typically consist of announcements followed by a presentation and/or icebreaker activity, with pizza typically provided.

 

We look to build a Student Design Team each Spring to travel and participate in one or more ASME competition. These projects typically consist of designing and building some sort of robot-like device to complete a set of challenges. Everything from electronics to RC controls to 3D printing are typically involved!

 

Join our GroupMe to participate in our ASME Student Section and learn about upcoming events:

https://groupme.com/join_group/109276657/55Ow221w

 

Become a member of ASME National here: 

https://www.asme.org/about-asme/professional-membership/join-asme

(Note: joining ASME international is not a strict requirement to participate in many of our student section functions)
summary: We are a student organization that represents the American Society of Mechanical Engineers at the University of Tennessee. Our primary focuses are career development, professional networking, building technical skillsets, and growing academically.

name: Amateur Radio Club at the University of Tennessee
description:
We are the Amateur Radio Club at the University of Tennessee Knoxville

We meet every Tuesday at the Min Kao Building in room 634.

Our meetings are open to anyone who would like to come. <b>x</b>
summary:
We are the Amateur Radio Club at the University of Tennessee Knoxville

We meet every Tuesday at the Min Kao Building in room 634.

Our meetings are open to anyone who would like to come.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
description: Alpha Kappa Psi is a Co-ed Business Fraternity founded in 1904 with the purpose of developing its members into principled business leaders. Originally founded by four men at New York University, our professional organization now has over 298,000 initiated members at 219 universities in 4 countries. Alpha Kappa Psi has grown to be the oldest and largest business fraternity in the world! With an extensive network of alumni, members are able to land opportunities at some of the most profitable companies in the world. As the premier professional co-ed business fraternity, we are truly preparing our brothers for a life of success.
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi is a Co-ed Business Fraternity founded in 1904 with the purpose of developing its members into principled business leaders.

name: Environmental Law Organization
description: The Environmental Law Organization at the University of Tennessee’s activities will include planning guest speakers and proposing sustainable initiatives for the law school. The organization will direct an annual conference, the Appalachian Public Interest and Environmental Law Conference (APIEL), at the College of Law. APIEL is an annual gathering of lawyers, scientists, students, and members of the general public to discuss environmental issues and happenings in Appalachia, public policy, and grassroots initiatives.
summary: The Environmental Law Organization seeks to connect its members to each other and the broader environmental law community and to inform them of the environmental law and policy issues they are likely to encounter during their careers.

name: Office of Development and Alumni Affairs
summary: Office of Development and Alumni Affairs

name: International Business Club
description: Our mission is to prepare students at the University of Tennessee to become effective and thoughtful business leaders in an increasingly globalized business environment. We provide a forum for students to hone their Global Intelligence, start building their global networks, explore international career opportunities and learn about global issues. We welcome all students at the University of Tennessee who have an interest in international business and diplomacy: as an academic pursuit, a personal passion, and/or a professional ambition.
summary: We offer social and professional networking between students and executives in a wide variety of roles who are passionate about issues surrounding international business and diplomacy.

name: Special Libraries Association, University of Tennessee Chapter
description: The SLA-UTK mission is to promote the study of special libraries and institutions that fall outside the scope of public and academic libraries. SLA-UTK also aims to offer vocational guidance and enhance leadership skills for students in the SIS program, and cultivate connections between SIS, SLA, UTK and our communities.
summary: This student organization is open to all students interested in studying or working toward a career in special libraries. We represent a student chapter of the national Special Libraries Association.

name: Mindfulness and Meditation
description: We are an organization that promotes living a mindful lifestyle through awareness and meditation practices. Our goal is to give others the tools to create their own meditation practice.
summary: We are a club that provides instruction in various forms of meditation and promotes awareness in everyday life.

name: Presbyterian Campus Ministry (a UKIRK ministry)
description:
We are not your average campus ministry. We’re not huge, you won’t get lost in the crowd.  We’re not tiny though either. There’s enough of us to go around. We like to eat. We like to laugh. We like to hang out together. We’re probably a lot like you. We don’t think we have all the “right” answers and we’re definitely willing to listen to your ideas. Though we frequently mess up, we’re trying to figure out how to be Christ followers, together. We believe in God's all-inclusive love.  We love Jesus and we want others to know how much they are loved.

At UKirk UTK, we believe that every person, without qualification, should have the opportunity for God’s amazing love to be made known to them. We believe what God intends for the world is a reality in which people recognize God’s image in the other and value diversity of perspective, race, class, gender, sexual orientation, ideological and political beliefs, or any other point of contention or division in our society. We believe all people are invited to Christ’s table, not to fit in, but rather to bring their honest, authentic, and, at times, broken selves to experience the grace of God.
summary: The campus ministry of the Presbyterian Church (USA) and the Cumberland Presbyterian Church at UTK.

name: Academy of Student Pharmacists
description: This organization is to help connect pre-pharmacy students with members in the field of pharmacy. Our parent organization is APhA-ASP at the University of Tennessee Health Science Center in Knoxville. We have monthly meetings that invite local professionals to talk about different areas of pharmacy as well as current pharmacy students which provide their perspective. The club also informs students about admission to pharmacy school by going through the application process, providing mock interviews, and having speakers on UT's pharmacy admission committee.
summary: This organization is to help connect pre-pharmacy students with members in the field of pharmacy. Our parent organization is APhA-ASP at the University of Tennessee Health Science Center in Knoxville.

name: VOLthon
description:
VOLthon is UTK’s largest student-run philanthropy organization which raises funds and awareness for Dolly Parton Children’s Hospital with the ultimate goal of fighting pediatric illness & supporting families.

VOLthon is a part of Miracle Network Dance Marathon, a movement across over 400 universities and high schools to raise funds for their local Children’s Miracle Network hospital. 

Students who participate in VOLthon work year-round to fundraise, plan events and campaigns, and engage in leadership, teamwork, and nonprofit business experience. There is truly something for everyone in VOLthon, whether that be volunteering with the hospital, spending time with miracle kids, running social media accounts, or working with corporate sponsors. 

Participants register online in the Spring of 2027! 

VOLthon hosts events and campaigns year-round to raise funds for the hospital, ultimately culminating in the main event: VOLthon’s Dance Marathon. This event takes place in the spring semester and celebrates a year of fundraising! It is 12 hours long (meant to resemble a typical nurse’s shift) and is full of line-dancing, games, hearing miracle stories from patients and tier families, fundraising, and the final reveal of the annual fundraising total.

In VOLthon’s 32 year history, students involved in this program have raised more than $2 Million for Dolly Parton Children's Hospital (formerly East Tennessee Children’s Hospital). Each year the funds go to life-saving medical equipment in the hospital’s greatest area of need. 

If you are looking to make a difference and make miracles, VOLthon is the place for you.
summary: VOLthon is a Miracle Network Dance Marathon and the largest student-lead philanthropy on UT's campus. We work year-round to raise funds and awareness for areas of need within Dolly Parton Children's Hospital.

name: The Cross - Greek Christian Ministry
description: The Cross is student-led ministry here on campus, and is unlike anything you'll find at any other university in the country. Our main purpose is to reach out to the students on UT's campus and help them take their first or next step in a relationship with Jesus. Our Wednesday night meeting allows folks to connect to their friends and to the larger community of The Cross. We always have food to gather around, a fun and uplifting social atmosphere, familiar worship you can sing along to, and an intentional message about how Jesus helps navigate our college lives. The Cross is open to all students here at UT, Greek or otherwise, and provides an avenue to starting, growing, or re-booting your relationship with God.
summary: Student Led Christian Ministry for Greek Life

name: Alpha Omega Campus Ministry
description: Alpha Omega Campus Ministry comprises students who desire to pursue a daily walk with Christ as we seek to spread the Good News. It is not easy being a college student trying to juggle new experiences, ideas, homework, jobs, etc.  During a time in life when many are losing sight of God, we hope to help students find God in a new and lasting way. One of the most beneficial ways to learn about God or to grow in one’s faith while in college is to be a part of a group of students devoted to learning and living out the teachings of the Bible.
summary: A beneficial way to learn about God & to grow in one’s faith while in college is to be part of a group of students who are devoted to learning & living out the teachings of the Bible. AO Knox is made up of students who desire to pursue a walk with Christ

name: Upsilon Theta
summary: Upsilon Theta is a chapter within Chi Sigma Iota (CSI), an international organization designed to promote excellence in the counseling profession. CSI is committed to the welfare and wellness of society by promoting excellence in its members.

name: Tabletop Gaming Club at UTK
description:
The Tabletop Gaming Club is a casual student organization dedicated to all tabletop games, including board games, DnD, etc. Everyone is welcome to stop by and join our meetings. Board games are supplied and provided during the meetings, and you are more than welcome to bring your games.

We have a wide variety of board games for everyone, hardcore and competitive games like Brass: Birmingham and Root. Casual and Social games like Codename, One Night Werewolf, and Coup. As well as cooperative games like Pandemic and Spirit Island.

We meet regularly on Tuesday and Thursday at HBB 130. Join our Discord for more information!

Join our Discord: https://discord.gg/GFFYDsjfgV
summary: The Tabletop Gaming Club at UTK seeks to allow people to enjoy card, board, and role-playing games of various types among friends. They can learn from experienced veterans or compete in tournaments, or simply enjoy casual play.

name: Iranian Student Association of UTK
summary: Iranian Student Association of UTK is a cultural, non-religious and non-political student organization. Our mission is to bring together students with Iranian background or interest and introduce Persian cultures to international communities.

name: Esports Club at UTK
description:
UTK Esports welcomes those who love playing video games. We have a variety of teams for games such as Valorant, League of Legends, Overwatch 2, Smash Bros., Call of Duty, Counter-Strike 2, Rocket League, Mario Kart, Rainbow 6 Siege, and more! Whether you're playing at a casual or competitive level, our club doors are always open to you. Gamers of all skill levels are welcome!

Join our discord to learn more about the club! https://tiny.utk.edu/esports
summary: Our organization provides a friendly environment for students to play, talk, and watch all things video games. We also offer the opportunity to compete against other universities in the competitive Esports scene.

name: Russian Language and Culture Club
description: The purpose of the Russian Language and Culture Club at the University of Tennessee is to provide an extra-curricular activity to those students and/or faculty interested in Russian language and culture. Membership to the Russian Language and Culture Club at the University of Tennessee will be open to all students and, faculty/staff members where appropriate, regardless of race, gender, disability, religion, or national origin.
summary: An organization for students and staff at UT who are interested in learning more about the Russian language and culture.

name: Volunteer Short Films
description:
VSF is a student-run organization at the University of Tennessee-Knoxville.
We are committed to fostering a community of filmmakers and creatives who aid one another in content creation.
We are a network of screenwriters, producers, directors, cinematographers, editors, and actors ready to assist
one another in our creative projects. All experience levels are welcome, from novices to professionals. Our goal as an organization is to help one another learn and create within the medium of film. Throughout each semester, we will host workshops focusing on various aspects of filmmaking, including screenwriting, pitching, cinematography, and editing.
summary: Volunteer Short Films is a community of filmmakers and creatives who aid one another in content creation. Our goal is to create films and have fun while doing it. All experience levels are welcome!

name: Student Council for Exceptional Children
description:
The Council of Exceptional Children is the largest international professional organization dedicated to improving the educational success of individuals with disabilities and/or gifts and talents. CEC advocates for appropriate governmental policies, sets professional standards, provides professional development, advocates for individuals with exceptionalities, and helps professionals obtain conditions and resources necessary for effective professional practice. SCEC is the student chapter of CEC that promotes professional development, and advocacy opportunities for pre-service teachers and any stakeholder interested in improving the lives of persons with disabilities.

Join our GroupMe! https://groupme.com/join_group/109139893/Obs1ncDN
summary: The Council for Exceptional Children (CEC) is the largest international professional organization dedicated to improving the educational success of individuals with disabilities and/or gifts and talents. SCEC is the student chapter of CEC.

name: Food Science Club
summary: The Food Science Club at the University of Tennessee fosters strong connections between Food Science students and faculty, promoting leadership development and broadening students' understanding of the field of food science.

name: The Ambassadors
summary: The Ambassadors are the official campus tour guides for the University of Tennessee. They lead organized campus tours, work Office of Undergraduate Admissions events, and share their excitement and knowledge of UT with prospective students.

name: Animal Science Graduate Student Association
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide a forum for professional and social development of graduate students within the Animal Science department.

name: Drive UT: Automotive Enthusiast Group
description:
DriveUT exists in order to encourage community in the college automotive atmosphere.  Our mission is to provide valuable learning experiences and to give members opportunities to network with fellow enthusiasts and professionals.  We also stand to promote safe and controlled environments for members to improve their driving skills.

https://discord.gg/Gp4RKuJa28
summary:
Drive UT is a community of students with automotive interests ranging from racing to building who come together to seek relationships within the automotive industry as well as develop their own automotive interests.

Join our Discord to get involved!

name: CLiP - The Portuguese Language Club
description:
CLIP is a student organization that helps students connect with the Portuguese-speaking world, particularly Brazil. Activities are coordinated to help students learn about Brazilian culture and language in a more interesting setting.

Brazil is an up and coming world power, which means that there are many career opportunities sprouting up in the major cities of Brazil. Majors that can benefit from Portuguese language and culture include but are not limited to: Civil Engineering, Architecture, International Business, African Studies, Religious Culture and History, and Music Studies.

Benefits of Studying Portuguese

  • Ability to understand the 6th most commonly spoken language in the world
  • New career opportunities in many fields
  • Advantage of being bilingual in a world with a lot of job competition
  • Complete UT’s foreign language requirements
  • Opportunity to study abroad in a beautiful country
  • A beautiful new culture to experience
summary: Clube da Língua Portuguesa

name: Massey Hall Council
summary: Massey Hall

name: Entomology and Plant Pathology Graduate Student Association
description: Entomology and Plant Pathology Graduate Student Association is a professional student organization that involves students above and at the undergraduate level in the fields related to Entomology and Plant Pathology. As a group, we focus on interacting with the community, sustaining an open dialogue between students and faculty, engaging in local activities, promoting awareness of job opportunities and professional development opportunities, and sharing research ideas.
summary: The Entomology and Plant Pathology Graduate Student Association is a student organization focused creating a positive environment and dialogue between students, community outreach events, and presenting research in our field to parties that may benefit.

name: Health Physics Society at University of Tennessee
description:
The primary objective of the Health Physics Society Branch at University of Tennessee the same as that of the Health Physics Society. We aim to promote the development of scientific knowledge and practical means for protecting persons and their environment from the harmful effects of radiation, thus providing for its utilization for the benefit of society. We are committed to provided resources and speakers for Health Physics and Medical Physics.

The major goals of the Branch are:

  1. To promote the professional development of its members by its programs and by its relations with other student Branches, the regional Health Physics Society, and the Health Physics Society
  2. To contribute toward the development of the science and technology of radiation protection at the University of Tennessee through activities in which student members, staff and faculty, and members of the local HPS Chapter may participate
  3. To enhance the public awareness of the profession of health physics and radiation protection through its educational programs
summary: The primary objective of the Health Physics Society Branch at University of Tennessee promote the development of scientific knowledge and practical means for protecting persons and their environment from the harmful effects of radiation.

name: Asian American InterVarsity
description: AAIV exists to be a place of belonging, a space for students to feel safe to explore faith and Asian American identity, to flourish as individuals and leaders and to grow in their individual and collective walks with Jesus. We are an engaged Asian American community that seeks to build personal relationships while exploring the intersection of ethnicity and faith.
summary: Asian American InterVarsity (AAIV) at UTK is a Christian Asian American community following Jesus' example and exploring the intersection of ethnic identity and faith.

name: Reese Hall Residents Association
description: The purpose of the RHRA shall be to provide a means of representation whereby the residents of Reese Hall may work together for their common good, and for that of the University; to sponsor activities and programs that will enrich the educational, social, and recreational life for the residents of Reese Hall; and to maintain comfortable living conditions above and beyond the basic housing requirements for the residents of Reese Hall.
summary: Reese Hall is a residence hall located in the Presidential Complex and home to 514 male freshman and upperclassmen students. The Reese Hall Resident Association exists to advocate and provide programs for the residents of Reese Hall.

name: Herbert Ambassadors
description: The purpose of the Herbert Ambassadors is to serve the college, its student body, and their community through the recruiting of prospective students, representing and supporting of the college, and promoting public awareness of opportunities within the college and the field of agriculture. New ambassadors will be selected by current ambassadors and the advisor in the spring for the following academic year.
summary: Herbert Ambassadors serve the college, student body, and community through recruiting of prospective students, representing and supporting of the college, and promoting public awareness of opportunities within the college and the field of agriculture.

name: Alpha Gamma Rho
description:
The Alpha Kappa Chapter of Alpha Gamma Rho was chartered at the University of Tennessee in 1951 by a group of individuals holding a common interest in agriculture and brotherhood.  Since our chartering in 1951, 1489 men have been initiated into our chapter at UT.  Active chapter membership currently totals 120 brothers, making the UT AGR chapter one of the largest AGR chapters in the country.  

Alpha Gamma Rho is the only professional and social fraternity at UT that stands true to its history of excellence in academics, activities, and campus leadership. The chapter consistently ranks near the top of academics each semester by concentrating on the GPA. Besides academics, the brothers of Alpha Gamma Rho are very involved with the university by participating in activities such as Intramural Sports, Homecoming, All Sing, and Carnicus and taking on leadership positions outside of AGR like IFC Exec, Ag and Main Campus Ambassadors, Spirit Team, Resident Advisor, and other clubs and organizations. We value accountability, hard work, dedication, and respect.
summary:
To be the premier fraternity building values, skills and relationships
for lifelong personal and professional success in all areas of life

name: The National Society of Leadership and Success
description:
The NSLS is the largest leadership honor society in the United States. Our NSLS chapter at University of Tennessee, Knoxville was started in 2014. Our local chapter is part of the national organization with 750+ chapters and over 1.4 million members. 

Students are selected based on exemplary academic achievement. As of 2023 we have 5,006 members who have access to:

  • More than $400,000 in scholarships.

  • An exclusive NSLS job board.

  • A personalized letter of recommendation. 

The NSLS leadership program teaches the interpersonal skills that employers seek to give you an edge in the job market. Hear directly from current members as they share how their experience has changed their life.

If you become a member, some of the other benefits are:

  • Learn from exceptional leaders like former President Barack Obama during exclusive Speaker Broadcasts.

  • Meet new friends and make lifelong connections (on-campus and virtually).

  • Overcome challenges with success coaches shortcutting your way to success.

  • Access to private networking events.

Visit the NSLS website to learn more about and accept your nomination. 

Chapter Advisor: Dr. James Williams

Chapter Presidents: Olivia Caraway, Allyson Zazzara, and Stephanie Bozarth

Frequently Asked Questions:

Some of the common questions we receive are listed below. If you have any other questions, visit nsls.org to chat with a team member or email help@nsls.org.

What do I have to do to become inducted?

Our five-step program takes approximately 10 hours over the course of the semester at your pace. 

Is this recognized on campus?

We are a Registered Student Organization (RSO). As of 2021, we had 3,974 in our chapter and there are more than 1.4 million members nationwide.

Disclaimer:

You MUST log into NSLS UTK to become an official member.
summary: We Build Leaders Who Build a Better World

name: Chinese Culture Club at UTK
summary: The purpose of the Chinese Culture Club is to promote interest and understanding of Chinese culture - new and traditional - on campus at the University of Tennessee in an apolitical environment and to provide a platform for those studying Chinese.

name: Global Studies Student Association
description: The Global Studies Club is a student-led academic organization that focuses on fostering a a community of globally minded students by providing opportunities for academic enrichment, professional development, and cross-cultural engagement. Through events, discussions, and networking initiatives, the association aims to promote global awareness and prepare students for meaningful careers in international fields, while contributing to a more informed and interconnected campus community at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. We plan to host one to two meetings per month with focuses on global current events, global engagement through UTK programs, cultural learning, and netoworking in order to give students a comprehensive view of many global issues, as well as valuable interaction with faculty at the university. This club is completely open to all majors and minors.
summary: The mission of the Global Studies Student Association is to foster a community of globally minded students by providing opportunities for academic enrichment, professional development, and cross-cultural engagement.

name: Minorities in Agriculture, Natural Resources and Related Sciences
description:
What is MANRRS? 

MANRRS is a national society that welcomes membership of people of all racial and ethnic group participation in agricultural and related sciences careers. We provide role model and networking opportunities for both undergraduate and graduate students. MANRRS is open to any student with a major or interest in any area of agriculture, natural resources or related sciences. We offer students opportunities to enhance leadership, organizational, and public speaking skills, along with experiencing professional critique of scholarly work in a "user-friendly" environment. Check out our Student Chapter Website

Why should I join MANRRS?

Join. Get Involved. Belong

At MANRRS, you can become a member for life, where you can get involved at any stage and shape the future of agriculture, natural resources, and related sciences. 

Our Mission at MANRRS is to promote academic and professional advancement by empowering minorities in agriculture, natural resources, and related sciences. Here, you have a valuable voice, and your participation matters.

EDUCATIONAL TRAINING SESSIONS

Prepare for new roles or improve and refine your knowledge and skills for your ideal job or internship.

INSPIRING SPEAKERS

Learn about the food, agriculture, natural resources, and related science fields and how you can grow, adapt, and stay motivated.

CAREER OPPORTUNITIES

Connect with students and professionals from more than 65 colleges and universities that represent government, industry, academia, and nonprofit organizations.

 

How do I Join ?

Students are encouraged to join the organization through their VolLink accounts. Simply login using your UTK NetID and password. Then search "MANRRS", go to the organization's profile, and click the big blue "Join" button. It may take a couple of days for a board member to approve you,

Click on the icons to view our socials.

Instagram Groupme HerbertSite
summary: The MANRRS chapter at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville strives to increase and support the number of minorities seeking degrees and careers in agriculture, natural resources, and related sciences.

name: Society of American Foresters-University of Tennessee Chapter
description: The Society of American Foresters is an educational and fun way for students to connect with fellow forestry students and forestry professionals, gain hands-on experience as a forester, participate in timbersports competitions, test their forestry technical skills, and have fun! We are the University of Tennessee’s chapter of the national organization, the Society of American Foresters. Although the Society of American Foresters is a professional society for future foresters, this club is not only for forestry majors. Anyone can join. We go camping and hiking, host physical and technical sporting events, and pair up with the Wildlife Society for some events. If you like the outdoors and wish to try something unexpected, this is your club!
summary: The Society of American Foresters is an educational and fun way for students to connect with fellow forestry students and forestry professionals, gain experience as a forester, participate in physical and technical competitions, and have fun!

name: Pi Delta Phi
description: Pi Delta Phi provides an important bridge between students, teachers and new opportunities.  Each semester, the society organizes a variety of events, including academic panels, cooking demonstrations, language immersion days, cheese-tastings, networking opportunities, and our annual induction dinner.  Through PDP, students have the opportunity to pursue leadership positions and apply for study abroad summer scholarships.  Pi Delta Phi aims to give students a real taste of the French language and culture that they are learning about and to help build a strong community within the French department.
summary: Pi Delta Phi is the student's link to a greater experience of French life. The society organizes events that introduce students to faculty and that expose students to francophone culture. Members are also eligible for PDP travel scholarships.

name: International Agriculture Society
description: International Agriculture Society UTK is a local chapter of the International Association of Students in Agriculture and Related Sciences - United States of America (IAAS USA). IAAS is dedicated to the exchange of experience, knowledge, and ideas, and to improving the mutual understanding between students in the field of agriculture and related sciences on an international scale. Our local chapter will host events pertaining to the discussion of international agriculture while providing a forum for scholarship, internship, and job opportunities in this field.
summary: IAAS UTK connects students with study abroad, travel, and professional opportunities throughout the organization. Club meetings/events focus on international agriculture discussions at a local level and provide students with various connections.

name: Construction Science Club
description: Construction Science Club is comprised of students of the Construction Science concentration within the Environmental and Soil Science degree in the Department of Biosystems Engineering and Soil Science. The primary goals of the club are the development of relationships with national and state professional organizations including the Associated Builders and Contractors (ABC), the Associated General Contractors (AGC), the Construction Specifications Institute (SCI), and the Tennessee Road Builders Association (TRBA), leading to increased visibility of the program and internship opportunities for all students in the program. The club is also interested in identifying and completing appropriate construction related community service projects.
summary: The C.S. Club is a student-governed organization, which provides engagement with the construction industry. Extracurricular activities incorporate introduction to professional societies, volunteerism, seminars, trips and fellowship.

name: Phi Gamma Delta
description:
The Kappa Tau Chapter of Phi Gamma Delta, affectionately known as "FIJI," is a prestigious fraternity at the University of Tennessee, founded on the principles of Friendship, Knowledge, Service, Morality, and Excellence. Our chapter is deeply rooted in tradition and dedicated to upholding the highest standards of brotherhood and personal development.

Founded in 1890, our chapter has been a prominent part of the University of Tennessee's Greek life, contributing significantly to the social, academic, and service aspects of campus culture. We are committed to creating a supportive environment that encourages individual and collective growth, nurturing leadership skills, cultivating a lifelong commitment to personal and academic excellence, and promoting philanthropy and community service.

The Kappa Tau Chapter currently boasts a membership of diverse and dynamic individuals who come from various backgrounds, majors, and interests, united by their commitment to the fraternity's values. Our members are actively involved in a wide range of campus activities, leadership positions, and academic pursuits, reflecting our chapter's emphasis on a well-rounded college experience.

Our philanthropy efforts are centered around our "Island Week" initiative. Each year, we organize various fundraisers and community service events, contributing both financially and through volunteer work to local charities and causes. Our Chapter undertakes a dedicated campaign to support the USO (United Service Organizations), which is a nonprofit-charitable corporation providing live entertainment, such as comedians, actors and musicians, social facilities, and other programs to members of the United States Armed Forces and their families. Additionally, we aim to make a meaningful contribution to the USO and extend its impact in providing a bridge between civilians and the military personnel who serve our nation. This annual philanthropic commitment underscores our fraternity's values of service and community, demonstrating our firm belief in giving back and supporting those who defend our freedom.

Through our various programs, including academic support, leadership development, community service, and social activities, the Kappa Tau Chapter strives to ensure that our members are not only successful in their collegiate endeavors but also prepared for post-graduate life.

We strive to embody the Phi Gamma Delta motto, "Not for College Days Alone," as we foster a brotherhood that extends beyond the university setting, sustaining friendships, and shared experiences that last a lifetime.

Our fraternity is more than just an organization; it's a brotherhood committed to personal growth, service, and a lifelong connection with our fellow members.
summary: Phi Gamma Delta - Men's Social Fraternity

name: Delta Alpha Pi
description:
Due to negative stereotyping associated with disability, students have been reluctant to identify themselves publicly. Delta Alpha Pi Honor Society presents an opportunity to change that perception by recognizing students with disabilities for their academic accomplishments. In addition, this Honor Society facilitates the development of skills in leadership, advocacy, and education for participating students.

Delta – D for Disability, but also the triangle, a symbol of strength. Members of Delta Alpha Pi demonstrate strength as leaders on campus to help break down the barriers of negativism. Also, Members serve as mentors and role models for other students with disabilities—alpha – A for Achievement. Alpha is the beginning and academic achievement must come first. A is also for advocacy because students must advocate for themselves before they can advocate for others. Members of Delta Alpha Pi enhance advocacy skills for themselves and for the rights of all individuals with disabilities to be included fully in society. Pi – P for Pride, pride in academic achievement and accomplishment, not just as students with disabilities but as university community members. Pi is a mathematical symbol taught in elementary arithmetic, so Pi is an appropriate symbol for education. Members of Delta Alpha Pi participate in activities designed to educate the community and society regarding the need to apply the principles of universal design in learning.

 

Visit DAPi website: https://sds.utk.edu/dapi-membership-requirements/
summary: Delta Alpha Pi is dedicated to recognizing the academic accomplishments of students with disabilities. In addition to developing leadership and advocacy of its members, the organization promotes awareness of disability through educational programming.

name: Vol Tango
description: The purpose and aim of Vol Tango is to create and promote a social Argentine Tango community on the University of Tennessee campus.
