This includes educating and organizing social tango dancers on campus, hosting open practice sessions (practicas), lessons, social dances (milongas), and inviting guest instructors and liaising with the off-campus social tango community.
summary: We seek to bring the passion of Argentine Tango dance to the students of the University of Tennessee. We will host regular lessons (beginner), practicas, and social dances (milongas) - no partner required! Experience the thrill of tango with us!

name: Laurel Hall Residents' Association
summary: Laurel Hall Residence Association

name: Systers: Women in EECS @ UTK
description: As of 2023, 17.2% of declared undergraduate students and 17.6% of graduate students in the department of Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, and Computer Science (EECS) majors at the University of Tennessee are women. In response to a 2013 Industrial Advisory Board (IAB) session regarding the gender disparity in the department, seven graduate students in EECS formed Systers: Women in EECS @ UTK (Systers) to better support and encourage women in the field.  Systers' mission is to recruit, retain, and mentor women in the department. Systers organizes and sponsors a department-wide research and community-building events, panels on graduate school admissions and funding, and helps members build a support network through other smaller events. Systers also offers a mentoring program for women in EECS, and members have attended multiple women in computing conferences.
summary: The mission of Systers is to recruit, mentor, and retain women in the fields of Computer Engineering, Computer Science, and Electrical Engineering.

name: Gamma Beta Phi Honor Society
description: The objectives of the organization are to recognize and encourage excellence in education; to promote the development of leadership ability and character in its members; and to foster, disseminate, and improve education through appropriate service projects. This Chapter of the Society shall implement these purposes by procedures consonant with the local situation and in cooperation with the faculty and administration of The University of Tennessee, Knoxville.
summary: The objectives of the organization are to recognize and encourage excellence in education; To promote the development of leadership ability and character in its members.

name: American Marketing Association at UT
description:
The UT American Marketing Association welcomes all majors! From skill-building workshops and guest speakers to networking and internship opportunities, learn how to market yourself.

UT AMA Membership is valid for ONE FULL YEAR from the time your application and payment have been processed. Membership benefits include access to:

  • Access to the AMAze Group (Resume Material through Experiential Learning)
  • Lucrative Scholarships provided by the Knoxville American Marketing Association (AMAK)
  • Leadership opportunities
  • AMA National's website (ama.org) and its Career Resource Center, student job board, career advice, Marketing News publication
  • Direct connections to local professionals (through AMAK)
  • Local business site tours 
  • Social Events
  • Volunteer Opportunities 
  • Networking Opportunities
  • AND SO MUCH MORE!
summary: The UT American Marketing Association welcomes all majors! From skill building workshops and guest speakers to networking and internships, learn how to market yourself.

name: International House
summary: As the University of Tennessee International House, we aspire to educate and engage all students in order to help them participate as members of a global society.

name: Pre-Physician Assistant Student Association
description: The Pre-Physician Assistant Student Association was established to bring together UT students that are interested in becoming a Physician Assistant. PASA is dedicated to helping our members prepare for the Physician Assistant school application process. We do this through informative meetings founded on general information about the profession, the GRE, and the CASPA online application process. We also gather information through the expertise of guest speakers in or related to the PA field.  We will also provide members with the opportunity to expand their qualifications for PA school through shadowing opportunities, academic advice, mock and resume assistance, and volunteer opportunities.
summary: PASA is an organization dedicated to students interested in becoming a PA. Our meetings focus on necessary steps to becoming a PA by offering opportunities to learn about the profession, prepare oneself for applying, & engage in community service work.

name: Spanish Club
description: The mission or purpose of the Spanish Club is to increase the interest and awareness of the Spanish language and culture, to be active in the Spanish community through conversations, games, and volunteer activities, as well as to be active and enjoy the companionship of others who enjoy the language on the UT campus and in the surrounding area of Knoxville. We want members to be excited about and involved in practicing their language skills around other students either currently studying or not studying the language.
summary: The Spanish Club @UTK provides a comfortable environment to practice speaking Spanish, teaches students about Hispanic Culture, and participates in events on campus and in the local area.

name: Christian Students at UTK
description: Christian Students is a group of believers from various backgrounds who want to grow in their relationship with the Lord during college and beyond. We are a non-exclusive club and have no denominational affiliation. Our goal is simply to build up the body of Christ together in the love of God (Jude 1:20-21) and bring His kingdom more to UT. As Christians, we desire to give Christ the first place in all things (Col. 1:18) which we do through Bible studies, worship, home fellowship, conferences, retreats, and more!
summary: Simply put, we're Christians and we're students! We love the Lord Jesus Christ and His Word and seek to give Him "the first place in all things." (Col. 1:18) CSUTK activities include Bible studies, conferences, retreats, outings, and more!

name: Remote Area Medical, The University of Tennessee Campus Chapter
description:
Remote Area Medical at UTK is UTK's collegiate chapter that primarily supports the Knoxville-based, nonprofit organization Remote Area Medical (RAM). RAM is a volunteer-based organization devoted to providing free healthcare to underprivileged and uninsured individuals across the country. 

RAM operates by organizing mobile clinics to provide free dental, vision, and medical care for patients in medically underserved areas. RAM staff arrange the clinics that support the organization of local healthcare providers volunteering their services alongside general volunteers that assist with nonclinical work. More information about clinic operations is available at ramusa.org. 

These operations require large amounts of fundraising and volunteer work, which we as a collegiate chapter aim to support. As a student organization, we serve as a community of undergraduate students at the University of Tennessee Knoxville who are passionate about volunteering for RAM and representing their mission on campus. 

Being involved in this chapter is especially helpful for those who have devoted themselves to a career in healthcare since volunteering with RAM provides exposure to different medical environments through volunteer work. However, since RAM is a non-profit organization run by several departments, RAM UTK would also be helpful for public health, sociology, business, and communications majors. 

If you are interested in reducing health disparities in the United States or just generally interested in public health, this chapter is highly recommended for you! Our chapter opens membership at the beginning of each fall and spring semester.
summary: "To prevent pain and alleviate suffering by providing free quality healthcare to those in need." Remote Area Medical is a Knoxville-based, nonprofit, volunteer organization devoted to providing free healthcare to underprivileged individuals.

name: Kinesiology Student Association
description:
An organization for students interested in kinesiology and related fields. We host many events for our members, including socials, fitness activities, and networking nights with other students, faculty, and professionals in different fields of interest. We also host presenters from graduate admissions programs to help you explore kinesiology. 

 

Follow us on Instagram @ksa_utk
summary: Organization for students interested in Kinesiology and related fields.

name: Center for Career Development & Academic Exploration
description: We are your first stop for all things career-related. We provide career coaching, career exploration classes, interest and personality assessments, and resources to help you choose a major and career. If you seek employment, we can help you identify part-time jobs, internships, and full-time positions through our Handshake system. If you haven’t already, register with Handshake and gain access to job postings as well as updates about workshops, job fairs, and employer visits. Please take a few minutes to look around our website and familiarize yourself with all we have to offer!
summary: We provide a wide array of career-related services for all students. View our full description for more details.

name: Center for Student Engagement (Org)
description:
The Mission of The Center for Student Engagement is to contribute to the holistic education of all students by providing programs, resources, and leadership opportunities that enhance the student learning experience. The Center for Student Engagement is guided in mission, spirit, and action by a set of core values:

Student Development: Students are presented with opportunities to learn, lead, and grow.
Student Engagement: Students are provided with opportunities to connect to their peers and university through positive experiences.
Responsible Stewardship: Students are equipped with the skills and knowledge to meet the broad needs and interests of the university community.
summary: The Mission of The Center for Student Engagement is to contribute to the holistic education of all students by providing programs, resources, and leadership opportunities that enhance the student learning experience.

name: Darwin Day UT
description: Darwin Day UT celebrates Charles Darwin's birthday each year with a series of lecture, public events, field trips, and activities for K-12 students and the greater Knoxville Community. The mission of the group is to promote evolutionary science education and foster an understanding of evolutionary science across a number of disciplines.
summary: The goal of Darwin Day UT is to promote evolutionary science education while fostering an interest in science in both the student body and the Knoxville community through lecture series, field trips, and other activities.

name: Multicultural Graduate Student Organization
description: The Multicultural Graduate Student Organization (MGSO) is a student-run organization that focuses on promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion within the graduate student community at UT. MGSO has a primary goal of supporting the social, cultural, and academic needs of graduate students from all backgrounds. The organization aims to foster an inclusive and welcoming environment that values and celebrates diversity and encourages mutual respect and understanding among its members. Activities and initiatives of MGSO include social events, cultural events, networking opportunities, and professional development activities.
summary:
The Multicultural Graduate Student Organization (MGSO) is a student-run organization that focuses on promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion within the graduate student community at UT.

For recent updates, follow our Instagram page: @mgso.utk

name: Interfraternity Council
description:
The Interfraternity Council provides assistance to these fraternities in efforts of recruitment, membership, education, scholarship, housing, and chapter management.

The mission of the Interfraternity Council is to develop men who lead with integrity, encourage a high level of scholarship, support the development of each chapter that we represent, promote a sense of community and strive to set a standard of excellence for ourselves and others at the University of Tennessee.

The Interfraternity Council holds an executive board meeting in every other Wednesday at 6:00 PM. All presidents of member fraternities meet every other Wednesday at 6:00 PM (alternating weeks)
summary: The Interfraternity Council represents 21 fraternities at the University of Tennessee. Our community offers chapters of 15 to 225 men with 17 housed groups. Through philanthropic events and community service, the IFC strives to build the best men.

name: Pi Sigma Alpha Honor Society
summary: Pi Sigma Alpha is a nationally recognized Political Science Honor Society.

name: International Tuba Euphonium Association at The University of Tennessee
summary: group that strives to develop at the local level the purpose and ideals of the ITEA

name: Office of Sorority and Fraternity Life (org)
description:
Mission & Vision

 

Mission:

  • To support a premier sorority and fraternity experience by providing educational opportunities, fostering collaborative relationships, and empowering students to uphold a mentality of ethical leadership and citizenship.

Vision:

  • To be considered the model sorority and fraternity community among peer and aspirant institutions.

 

5 Key Values:

The Office of Sorority & Fraternity Life supports values congruence by:

Scholarship Advancement

  • Striving to create an environment that supports academic excellence through providing resources and initiatives

Relationship Building

  • Engaging members and organizations in opportunities that foster cross council collaborations

Diversity

  • Fostering opportunities for members and organizations to develop their multicultural competency through interactions and experiences within the sorority and fraternity community
  • Creating welcoming environments where all communities are appreciated and respected

Personal Development

  • Setting a standard for our students to live the values of their organizations that promote safe environments, social accountability, stewardship, and leadership development; yielding members and organizations to lead with high fraternal purpose

Communication

  • Emphasizing the importance of maintaining and strengthening local and national relationships with stakeholders to provide support for all fraternal organizations
summary: To support a premier sorority and fraternity experience by providing educational opportunities, fostering collaborative relationships, and empowering students to uphold a mentality of ethical leadership and citizenship.

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council
description: The National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC) at UT is comprised of four historically black fraternities and four historically black sororities. These organizations live by the values of tradition, academic achievement, community service, leadership, and lifetime brotherhood/sisterhood. NPHC organizations are committed campus leaders that produce and engage in political, social, and cultural activities. They are active in numerous philanthropic and community service events such as Boys and Girls Club, Big Brothers Big Sisters, St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital, food drives, coat drives and Habitat for Humanity. NPHC also hosts a plethora of events such as NPHC Community Service Week, Meet the Greeks, and other sponsored programs.
summary: It is the mission of the National Pan-Hellenic Council to promote cooperation and harmony between member organizations and between members and other campus organizations in developing the best possible image for the Greek community.

name: Office of Sustainability
description:
The Office of Sustainability is housed in Facilities Services. It was formed by the creation of a full-time sustainability manager position in 2007. The team has since added 3 full-time staff at the UT Compost Facility, 4 AmeriCorps members, 6 student workers, and numerous Work Study students and student interns. 

Functional Areas

Sustainability is often defined as meeting today’s needs without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their needs (Brundtland Commission 1987). The Office of Sustainability supports environmental sustainability initiatives that

  • Reduce air, water, and land pollution from campus operations.
  • Reduce greenhouse gas emissions and the use of nonrenewable resources.
  • Promote waste diversion and conservation practices among the UT community.
  • Protect and enhance the beauty of campus grounds and facilities.
  • Demonstrate the university’s commitment to environmental stewardship.
summary: The Office of Sustainability works to coordinate, manage, advise and report on sustainable initiatives at UT, through education, engagement, research, data collection and collaborations with a variety of on-campus and community stakeholders.

name: Delta Kappa Epsilon
description: Founded in 1844 at Yale University, DKE is one of the oldest collegiate fraternities in the world. A Deke is described as a gentleman, scholar, and jolly good fellow. DKE focuses on developing future leaders in the professional world, as evidenced by notable alumni including United States Presidents, ambassadors, scientists, and businessmen, as well as providing a friendly home to college men.
summary: Founded in 1844 at Yale University, DKE is one of the oldest collegiate fraternities in the world. A Deke is described as "equal parts gentleman, scholar, and jolly good fellow".

name: Vols Japanese Culture Association
description: Vols Japanese Culture Association (VJCA) of the University of Tennessee, Knoxville (UTK) is a student-run organization whose mission is to foster understanding of the Japanese culture, language, and mindset in the UTK community. VJCA meets each week throughout the semester to discuss different aspects of Japanese culture, participate in Japanese themed events around Knoxville and have fun growing our collective knowledge about Japan.
summary: Vols Japanese Culture Association (VJCA) of the University of Tennessee, Knoxville (UTK) is a student-run organization whose mission is to foster understanding of the Japanese culture, language, and mindset in the UTK community.

name: Plant Sciences Club at The University of Tennssee
description: The Plant Sciences Club is a great way to meet other plant enthusiasts at UTK and get connected to the Knoxville plant community! We welcome all people interested in the beauty and utility of plants, being a plant science major is not required. As a club, we meet in the South Greenhouse for meetings to learn about a plant topic from a speaker and complete a related hands-on activity!
summary: The Plant Sciences Club at The University of Tennessee will promote the exchange of experience, knowledge and ideas about plants between students of all majors.

name: Black Student Union at University of Tennessee
description: ­­The BSU will build awareness for political, social, and cultural movements and issues affecting minority students. We will promote unity and beneficial change on campus, as well as act as a liaison between students and administration. The BSU will encourage respect, service, high academia, and the true Volunteer spirit.
summary: The goal of the Black Student Union is to unify black and minority students on the campus of UTK. Through dialogues, programming, and service, we will create a supportive and inclusive environment.

name: Out in Science, Technology, Engineering, & Mathematics at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville
description: Out in Science, Technology, Engineering & Mathematics at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville (oSTEM at UTK) is the campus chapter of the national oSTEM organization, dedicated to supporting LGBTQ+ students in STEM and their allies. We meet bi-weekly for a mix of social events and professional development, giving students a space to make friends, network, and have fun while growing in their careers.
summary: Out in Science, Technology, Engineering & Mathematics at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville (oSTEM at UTK) is the campus chapter of the national oSTEM organization, dedicated to supporting LGBTQ+ students in STEM and their allies.

name: VolVoices
description: VolVoices is a student organization within the College of Music with the mission of providing undergraduate performers opportunities to grow their performing and musical skills. The group generally meets weekly, however meeting times will be dependent on the rehearsal needed for various performances throughout any given semester. Throughout the Fall and Spring semesters, various productions will include a range of musical performances (some include, but are not limited to: recitals, showcases, operas, musicals, and more). Additionally, the group will host various music-related social events with the purpose of offering a safe space for members to meet, discuss, and learn about all aspects of the musical world. This group is open to undergraduate Vocal Music Education majors, Vocal Performance majors and minors, as well as any undergraduate student of the University with a passion for performing.
summary: VolVoices is an organization created with the purpose of fostering valuable performance and educational opportunities for undergraduate performers with an interest in the operatic and theatrical world.

name: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc.
description: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. was founded on November 12, 1922 on the campus of Butler University in Indianapolis, Indiana by 7 illustrious educators. The organization is built on the foundation of Sisterhood, Scholarship and Service. The sorority's slogan, " Greater Service, Greater Progress," is exemplified through every Sigma woman. Some of our national programs that are built on this slogan include Project Reassurance, Operation Big Book Bag, and the Youth Symposium. The Mu Beta Chapter was chartered on March 23, 1991 by 3 magnificent women.
summary: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority,Inc. is built on the foundation of Sisterhood, Scholarship and Service. The sorority's slogan, "Greater Service, Greater Progress," is exemplified through every Sigma woman.

name: HackUTK
description:
The mission of HackUTK is to promote student interest in the field of computer and network security through participation in and sponsorship of capture the flag competitions and related activities that inspire, develop, and empower a future generation

 

We will meet every Thursday from 6:00-7:30 in MK 525
summary: The mission of HackUTK is to promote student interest in the field of computer and network security through participation in and sponsorship of capture the flag competitions and related activities that inspire, develop, and empower a future generation

name: Partners in Sports
description:
Partners in Sports is the student organization for Sport Management majors, minors, and other students at the University of Tennessee who are interested in pursuing careers in the sport or recreation industry. The organization is active within the university and regional sports community. The mission of the organization is to create opportunities for tomorrow’s sports leaders through practical experiences and professional development opportunities.

 

Contact Ally Davis (clanier4@vols.utk.edu) or Audrey Stanley (astanl12@vols.utk.edu) for information on joining and upcoming activities.

  • Tennessee Sport Management Facebook Page
  • Tennessee Sport Management Twitter/X Account
  • Tennessee Sport Management LinkedIn Group
  • Information about the Sport Management major can be found here.
  • Information about the Sport Management minor can be found here. Click here to complete a form to declare the minor.
  • Information about the Sport Analytics Certificate can be found here. 
  • Information about the Sport Management master's program can be found here.
  • Information about the Adaptive Sport & Recreation Graduate Certificate can be found here.
summary: Partners in Sports is the student organization for Sport Management majors, minors, and other students at the University of Tennessee who are interested in pursuing careers in the sport or recreation industry.

name: Fred Brown Residents Association
summary: Residents Association for the Fred Brown Residence Hall

name: Ad Club
description: AdClub is a student-run organization that provides students with a social experience outside of the classroom. By joining Ad Club, students inside and outside of the major get the chance to meet and interact with advertising professionals, talk about supplemental topics not covered in traditional advertising classes, and learn about internships and the latest trends in advertising.
summary: The purpose of Ad Club is to unite + inspire + encourage + guide students of any major with interests in advertising, marketing, or general creative business practices.

name: Future Physical Therapists Association
description:
Inspiring the future generation of physical therapists to be successful through sharing experiences, encouraging academic excellence, and establishing connections among like-minded students at UTK.

If you'd like to join, please click the link to join our GroupMe.
summary: A group of students interested in both pursuing careers in Physical Therapy and serving the Knoxville community.

name: Reformed University Fellowship International
description: RUF International is a UT organization that seeks to welcome students and scholars from around the world so that their experience here at UT is the best it can possibly be.  As leaders who have experienced the kindness of God in Jesus Christ, we aim to embrace internationals from all ethnic and religious backgrounds by offering them genuine friendship, as well as regular opportunities for relaxation and recreation in local homes, cultural celebrations, off-campus adventures, and discussion groups for personal and spiritual growth.
summary: RUF International is a Christian-based organization that warmly welcomes UT internationals from all ethnic & religious backgrounds. We offer opportunities for invitations to local homes, cultural celebrations, off-campus adventures, & spiritual growth.

name: Student Space Technology Association
description: The SSTA builds rockets, robots, high-altitude balloons, hypersonic vehicles, and more complex space technologies right here on campus. Our team performs monthly community outreach with local middle and elementary schools, hosts conferences to bring together both students and professionals in the space ecosystem, and collaborates on research papers with departments across campus. The organization is open to all students, both undergraduate and graduate, regardless of skill set or major, and is based in Room 207 of UT Drive Services Building B at 2233 Volunteer Blvd.
summary: The Student Space Technology Association (SSTA) is a student-run research lab pushing the envelope of collegiate space technologies.

name: VOLT
description: VOLT is an 11 year accapella group at UTK! Our goal is to create a group of all different majors and styles from campus. We all have the same passion for singing but have decided not to pursue it within our college majors. We practice three days a week and compete within many different competitions throughout the school year. We want anyone with a passion of the arts especially singing to feel as though they have an equal opportunity to participate in a singing group outside of being within the music program!
summary: The University of Tennessee's premiere Co-Ed a cappella group!

name: Cumberland Striptease
description: We practice and perform improvisational comedy on the UTK campus.
summary: We practice and perform improvisational comedy on the UTK campus.

name: Brothers United for Excellence (BUE)
description: Brothers United for Excellence (B. U. E.) assist with matriculation of the academic realm by providing the necessary resources and guidance to men of color, to promote student engagement at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville and the surrounding community, and uplift the professional attainment through the promotion of academic success and leadership development while ultimately molding future influential leaders. Brothers United for Excellence is recognized as the premier developer of principled leaders by providing opportunities for leadership development, professionalism and common etiquettes, academic prosperity, and engagement at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville.
summary: Brothers United for Excellence is a student organization that promotes academic excellence, professionalism, social development, and health and wellness attainment. B.U.E. strives to serve as a resource for students to succeed through college years.

name: Vols on the Fly
description:
First off, please email us at volsonthefly@gmail.com to find out more and get involved!

 

The purpose of ‘Vols on the Fly’ Fly Fishing Club is to promote fly fishing as a form of outdoor recreation by bringing together students and faculty, and providing an environment conducive to learning about the art of fly fishing and to developing a conservation ethic.


This club is a part of Trout Unlimited’s 5 Rivers College outreach program, and the club is affiliated with the Great Smoky Mountain (Chapter #047) Chapter of Trout Unlimited so that the two organizations may jointly participate in conservation projects and fishing excursions. Trout Unlimited’s mission is to protect, reconnect, and restore North America’s cold-water fisheries and their watersheds.
summary: Vols on the Fly is dedicated to the sport of fly fishing. We aim to help connect fellow anglers, teach the sport of fly fishing, conserve our fisheries, and to give back to the community. Email us to find out more at volsonthefly@gmail.com

name: Hellenic Society
description: We thoroughly enjoy the opportunities given to us to keep our Hellenic culture alive and present in our community. Just as our families have done for us, we will continue to celebrate our Greek culture though customs, traditions, and kinship. We will hold meetings and events to bring together people of Greek heritage and foster close friendships rooted in the love of our culture.
summary: We thoroughly enjoy the opportunities given to us to keep our Hellenic culture alive and present in our community. Just as our families have done for us, we will continue to celebrate our Greek culture though customs, traditions, and kinship.

name: Academic Quizbowl Team at The University of Tennessee
description: The Academic Quizbowl Team at the University of Tennessee is an organization that participates in collegiate academic tournaments. There are weekly practices during which practice rounds are conducted. Students interested in a wide range of academic fields- for example, from chemistry to history to mythology- are welcome to join. Beginners are also welcome.
summary: The Academic Quizbowl Team at the University of Tennessee is an organization that participates in collegiate academic tournaments.

name: Recreational Therapy Student Association
description: The purpose of this club is to provide a professional organization for students interested in the field of Recreational Therapy. The club will provide resources, and opportunities to professionally and developmentally support Recreational Therapy students and community around The University of Tennessee – Knoxville, through outreach and engagement.
summary: The purpose of this club is to provide a professional organization for students interested in the field of Recreational Therapy.

name: Gender Minorities and Women in Physics
description: The Gender Minorities and Women in Physics (GMaWiP) organization at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville is a student led organization that was created to bring together those that wish to empower and support women and gender minorities in physics. We are here to provide an open environment to discuss issues that directly affect women and gender minorities in physics. Additionally, GMaWiP strives to support other minority groups within physics as well as women and gender minorities in all STEM fields. This organization is open to any student interested in physics and supporting women and gender minorities, so neither being a physics major nor identifying as a woman or other gender minority is required.
summary: The Gender Minorities and Women in Physics (GMaWiP) organization at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville is a student led organization that was created to bring together those that wish to empower and support gender minorities in physics.

name: People of Style and Education
description: POSE is the exclusive organization on campus in which members are able to showcase their unique sense of fashion, create their own definition of swagger, and most importantly uphold an academic standard. POSE is a diverse group of students who have a passion and drive for honing modeling skills and techniques, while also gaining valuable experience in the fashion industry. Nevertheless, our sole purpose is not only to focus on the fashion and modeling aspects, but we also aim to help develop individuals’ self-esteem so that members exude confidence on and off the runway. Throughout the year, POSE partners with a variety of organizations across campus to host and participate in fashion shows and workshops. In addition, POSE also hosts an annual fall and spring fashion show, in which we showcase a variety of brands by designers ranging from our student peers to local vendors in the surrounding Knoxville area.
summary: POSE is the exclusive organization on campus in which members are able to showcase their unique sense of fashion, create their own definition of swagger, and most importantly uphold an academic standard.

name: Sports Analytics Club at UTK
description: This organization is led by UTK Business Analytics Graduate/Undergraduate Students but open to all UTK students and faculty. Please contact bsooley@vols.utk.edu or ssiegel4@vols.utk.edu with any questions.
summary: SAC is a student-led club using data-driven insights to explore college & pro sports. Open to all UTK students, we host competitions, attend conferences, bring in guest speakers, and build a fun, analytical community of fans who love sports and the Vols.

name: Table Tennis Club at UTK
description:
Whether you are a beginner, a tournament player, or an aspiring tournament player, you are welcome at our club.


Join our GroupMe for announcements!

https://groupme.com/join_group/89224943/59VVlgIT
summary: Whether you are a beginner, a tournament player, or an aspiring tournament player, you are welcome at our club.

name: AIM Accounting Alliance
description:
AIM Accounting Alliance (AAA) is a student organization dedicated to providing accounting students with opportunities for professional development, networking, leadership, and service while connecting them with like-minded peers, faculty, and industry professionals.

Our Mission

AAA strives to support the professional growth of its members, foster a strong sense of community, and develop leadership skills through:

  • Weekly Meetings: Featuring industry professionals and faculty who share insights on career opportunities, professional development, and industry trends.
  • Mentorship Program: Connecting members with honors and upper-division accounting students to gain valuable knowledge and guidance.
  • Service & Outreach: Engaging in group projects that give back to the community.
  • Social Events: Strengthening relationships among peers through fun and engaging activities.

Membership Benefits

By joining AAA, students gain:

  • A deeper understanding of accounting career paths and opportunities.
  • A network of professional contacts for internships and job opportunities.
  • Leadership experience that enhances applications for internships, jobs, and graduate programs.
  • Meaningful connections with students and faculty in the Accounting and Information Management Department.
  • Opportunity for recognition for members who actively participate through Gold Star award.
summary: An alliance between students, businesses, and the Accounting and Information Management Department of the Haslam College of Business created to promote professional and community relationships as well as personal growth.

name: Filipino-American Association
description: FAA connects the Filipino community at UTK! We'll have events like karaoke night, potlucks, learning about Filipino culture, and more!
summary: FAA connects the Filipino community at UTK! We'll have events like karaoke night, potlucks, learning about Filipino culture, and more!

name: Dairy Club at UT
description: The University of Tennessee Dairy Club is an organization for students interested in working with dairy cattle and the dairy industry. Our purpose is to provide UT students with educational and leadership opportunities by housing opportunities to network and collaborate with others with similar interests. Students of any major and with any background are more than welcome to join, we're here to help you learn!
summary: The University of Tennessee Dairy Club is for students interested in learning more about dairy cows and the dairy industry.  No prior knowledge or background is needed - just a desire to learn more.

name: Forum on Science, Ethics, and Policy
description: The purpose of FOSEP is to foster an environment for students with both STEM and policy-oriented backgrounds and mindsets to collaborate, learn, and advance their skills. The University of Tennessee, Knoxville, is situated near a variety of powerful institutions across East Tennessee, including the Baker School of Public Policy, the Tickle College of Engineering, the Bredesen Center, and Oak Ridge National Laboratory. This organization seeks to introduce undergraduate and graduate students in both science and policy in the hopes that they promote their own scientific research through the benefit of community advocacy. Additionally, FOSEP will provide students with resources at the community, state, and national level to advance their career. Lastly, FOSEP seeks to develop a platform for advocacy in key scientific and policy issues, particularly in climate change and the environment, cybersecurity and artificial intelligence, and health technology and access.
summary: FOSEP is an organization where STEM and policy-oriented students can collaborate, learn, and expand their knowledge of how the work they are doing as students can translate into real-world impact.

name: Eta Theta Chapter of Phi Sigma Pi National Honor Fraternity
description: Phi Sigma Pi is built on a Tripod of three ideals: Scholarship, Leadership and Fellowship which support the ultimate goal of social service. This Tripod provides direction in our organization, our programming and in our actions as Members. Combined, the Tripod guides our Members to be successful contributing citizens while at campus and into their professional careers.
summary: Phi Sigma Pi is a gender inclusive national honor Fraternity, with the goal of bringing the best and brightest students together to make a larger impact in their campus, community and world.

name: InVOLuntary Sports Party
description:
Involuntary Sports Party (ISP) is an improvisational and sketch comedy troupe dedicated to furthering the education and presence of comedy on campus through weekly workshops and regular performances.

ISP consists of UTK students who perform sketch and improv on campus, in Knoxville, and at comedy festivals. ISP members host workshops once a week to teach improv fundamentals and technique to the UTK community.
summary: InVOLuntary Sports Party is an improvisational comedy troupe dedicated to furthering the education of comedic improv and performing.

name: SACNAS Chapter at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville
summary: To further the work of, to improve the effectiveness of, and to enhance the public understanding of and appreciation for Chicanos, Latinos, Native Americans, Alaska Natives, Native Hawaiians, and other underrepresented minorities in the sciences.

name: Institute of Transportation Engineers
description:
The Institute of Transportation Engineers (ITE) is an international educational and scientific association of transportation professionals who are responsible for meeting mobility and safety needs. ITE facilitates the application of technology and scientific principles to research, planning, functional design, implementation, operation, policy development and management for any mode of ground transportation. Through its products and services, ITE promotes professional development of its members, supports and encourages education, stimulates research, develops public awareness programs and serves as a conduit for the exchange of professional information.

The ITE Student Chapter at the University of Tennessee falls under the Tennessee Section of ITE in the Southern District of the International ITE.
summary: The Institute of Transportation Engineers (ITE) is an international educational and scientific association of transportation professionals who are responsible for meeting mobility and safety needs.

name: Big Orange Consulting
description: Student Organization dedicated to preparing grad students for the world of consulting.
summary: Big Orange Consulting's mission is to foster connections between the local community, alumni consultants, and the University of Tennessee MBA student population as well as to prepare students for case interviews. This is a graduate level club.

name: Biochemistry & Cellular and Molecular Biology Graduate Student Organization
description: The organization is aimed to give an opportunity for the graduate students at the Department of Biochemistry, Cellular and Molecular Biology to come together to support each other through different activities. These activities are geared to enhance their experience in the department, develop necessary skills required for their academic success and give a platform to voice their opinion and ideas.
summary: Organization of grad students in the BCMB department.

name: Campus Outreach at the University of Tennessee
summary: Campus Outreach is a Christian ministry dedicated to providing opportunities for students from multiple cultures and affinity groups on the campus to be successful in their spiritual life.

name: Material Advantage - UTK
description: Professional student organization for materials science and engineering. Chapter hosts and participates in a variety of materials science and engineering related activities, events, and competitions.
summary: Professional student organization for materials science and engineering. Chapter hosts and participates in a variety of materials science and engineering related activities, events, and competitions.

name: Theodorus Chapter of Alpha Rho Chi
description:
For more information: 

Instagram: @apxtheodorus

National Website: alpharhochi.org
summary: Alpha Rho Chi is a professional and social fraternity of Architecture and the allied arts. We aim to create connection between students in the arts as well as practicing professionals.

name: Delight Ministries
description: We are a college women's community that grows together, serves together, learns together and does life together while chasing the heart of God. We provide the opportunity for women to Delight in the Lord together throughout their college journey.
summary: We are a college women's community that grows together, serves together, learns together and does life together while chasing the heart of God. We provide the opportunity for women to Delight in the Lord together throughout their college journey.

name: Public Health Student Association
description:
PHSA is a voluntary student organization for students in the Department of Public Health. PHSA allows public health students, both new and seasoned, to develop leadership skills, enjoy time together, and develop health-promoting initiatives.

  • PHSA activities include: planning and leading the new student orientation each fall semester, participating in campus wellness events, and engaging in other service learning activities that promote health.
  • Public health students are elected each year for the positions of president, vice president, secretary, treasurer, and philanthropist. Additionally, there are two undergraduate representatives.
  • Associate Professor Dr. Jiangang Chen serves as faculty adviser of the PHSA.
summary: PHSA is a voluntary student organization for students in the Department of Public Health. PHSA allows public health students, both new and seasoned, to develop leadership skills, enjoy time together, and develop health-promoting initiatives.

name: Alpha Epsilon Pi Psi Deuteron
summary: We are the Psi Deuteron Chapter of the Alpha Epsilon Pi Fraternity at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. AEPi is a fraternity founded upon Jewish principles but open to all who are willing to espouse it's values.

name: White Hall Residents Association
summary: Residents Association for White Hall

name: Black Law Students Association
description: The Marilyn Yarbrough Chapter of the University of Tennessee, a member of the Southern region, effects NBLSA's goals through a variety of initiatives, including academic and professional workshops, mentoring programs, the annual Blackshear Banquet, the charity ball, the BLSA buddy program, and the College Student Division. The chapter voted in 2011 to adopt the moniker of Marilyn Yarbrough, the first African-American law school dean at UT and the entire Southeast, who was a tireless advocate for diversity and academic excellence.
summary: The Marilyn Yarbrough Chapter of the University of Tennessee, a member of the Southern region, effects NBLSA's goals through a variety of initiatives, including academic and professional workshops, mentoring programs, and additional events.

name: First Year Programs - Peer Mentors
description:
Peer mentors are undergraduate students who assist faculty and staff instructors in a section of FYS 101, FYS 129, and TRNS 201 for the fall and spring term. These campus leaders serve as role models, advisors, mentors, campus resource experts, and trusted points of contact for students participating in  First-Year Program seminars. Peer mentors work collaboratively with other peer mentors, peer mentor coordinators, and instructors while assisting students in solving problems and getting involved on campus!

As a peer mentor, you get to serve as a role model and point of contact for a core group of first-year students. You also have the opportunity work cooperatively with other peer mentors and instructors and develop significant relationships with other peer mentors and instructors. By serving as a resource for first-year and transfer students, you will help new students connect with campus services and opportunities for engagement, and assist students in navigating the first-year experience. In addition, you will have the opportunity to enhance your leadership and interpersonal skills.

To learn more about being a peer mentor, visit the First-Year Programs website: https://fyp.utk.edu/peer-mentors/
summary: Peer mentors are undergraduate students who assist faculty and staff instructors in a section of FYS 101 for the fall and spring term.

name: Advocates For Autism
description: Advocates For Autism aims to raise awareness and educate the campus and community about Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD). We provide an inclusive environment for autistic students as well as serve as allies. We are a non-profit organization and do not raise money to fund research to find a cure, and instead focus on acceptance and support.
summary: Advocates For Autism aims to raise awareness and educate the campus about Autism and provide an inclusive environment for autistic students as well as serve as allies.

name: The Volunteer Channel
description:
TVC is a student-run television station at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, that allows students to get hands-on experience in many aspects of the Television and Film industry. Students can create content in front of the camera, or help with the behind-the-scenes production.

Established in 1996, TVC is reaching it’s 30th year of being one of the rising student media sources on campus.

We produce and film a weekly newscast, featuring sports news and weather forecasts, host a sports commentary program, bring together a talk show, and post daily updates on everything you need to know about what's happening around campus.
summary: TVC is a student-run television station at the University of Tennessee that allows students to get hands-on experience in many aspects of the Broadcast News,Television and media creation (preproduction, production and post-production).

name: Communication Studies Club
description:
Club Goals

  • Stimulate interest in the field of communication
  • Give students the opportunity to establish relationships with alumni, faculty, and students
  • Help students gain exposure to various internships and job opportunities in the field of communication
  • Foster a fun and casual atmosphere to enhance interpersonal communication skills


Club Activities

  • CMST Club meetings are designed to offer students academic, professional, and social opportunities. Alumni and guest speakers are invited to the meetings to discuss career opportunities, offer career advice, and hold various networking workshops.
  • Throughout the year CMST club sponsors several events such as:
    • Annual networking trip to Washington, D.C. to visit alumni working in the area
    • Networking day-trip to Nashville, TN to visit alumni working at several firms and recording labels.
    • Etiquette Dinners
  • These events give students the opportunity to expand their network, promote professional development, and help create unique friendships among the Communication Studies community.
  • Ideas under consideration for the upcoming year include networking day-trips to meet with alumni working in Atlanta, Georgia and organizing philanthropy events to raise money for the Nathan Boals Scholarship Fund.

 

Join our GroupMe to get updates for the club!

https://groupme.com/join_group/95811693/CskDqMJn
summary: CMST Club is a student-driven organization designed for all CMST majors and any student interested in learning about the communication field. CMST Club focuses on providing opportunities for students to further develop communication knowledge and skills.

name: Women of Business Analytics at Tennessee
description: The mission of the Women of Business Analytics at Tennessee (WomBAT) student organization is to provide professional development for women (undergrad and grad) in analytics at the Univ. of Tennessee by organizing networking events and workshops and providing an environment to discuss issues women encounter in the workplace constructively.
summary: Women of Business Analytics at Tennessee (WomBAT) provides professional development and networking opportunities for women (undergrad & grad) interested in pursuing careers in analytics.

name: Ratio Christi
description: The mission is to equip university students and faculty to give historical, philosophical, and scientific reasons for following Jesus Christ.  The purpose for which the Ratio Christi, Inc. (Ratio Christi) is organized is to encourage and strengthen the faith of Christian students at public and private institutions around the world, while sharing Christ's message and love with those who have not yet accepted Him. By situating apologetic clubs at universities nationally and internationally, Ratio Christi will take part in the battle for the mind by encouraging dialogue and stimulating discussion directed towards secularism and answering life’s pressing questions.
summary: Christian Apologetics Group

name: Pi Kappa Alpha
description: Pi Kappa Alpha is dedicated to developing men of integrity, intellect, and high moral character and to fostering a truly lifelong fraternal experience. We are a student organization of nearly 200 members, all of which are involved around campus and in the community. We strive to better ourselves as men and hold each other to the highest standard possible. Pike is known for holding philanthropy, community service, social, and brotherhood events that make a lasting impact on us as members and those who are involved in them.
summary: Zeta Chapter of the International Fraternity Pi Kappa Alpha.

name: Best Buddies UTK
description:
Best Buddies UTK works directly with the Future program to create one-to-one friendships between people with and without intellectual and developmental disabilities.

https://youtu.be/JPft0oW6MB0 

Join our GroupMe!

GroupMe Link
summary: Best Buddies UTK works directly with the Future program to create one-to-one friendships between people with and without intellectual and developmental disabilities.

name: Association for Information Science and Technology
description:
ASIS&T, just celebrated its 80th birthday in 2017, is an international multidisciplinary organization with more than 4,000 members from fields such as librarianship, computer science, linguistics, management, engineering, law, medicine, chemistry, and education. ASIS&T helps these professionals keep up with the rapid advancements and changes in library and information science, communications, networking, and computer science.

Benefits of membership in the student chapter and the international organization include:  

    • Networking opportunities with information professionals and other Information Sciences students
    • Professional development and publications
    • Discounted registration fees for ASIS&T Summits and Annual Meetings
    • Leadership opportunities
    • Access to ASIS&T's JobLine and Digital Library

Related links:

- Instruction to join ASIS&T
summary: ASIS&T is a multidisciplinary organization with members from fields such as librarianship, computer science, linguistics, management, engineering, law, medicine,and education. It is interested in bridging the gap between information research and practice

name: Pre-Optometry Club at The University of Tennessee
description:
The purpose of the Pre-Optometry Club at The University of Tennessee is to focus on bringing students around the campus together who are interested in seeking the optometry profession. 

We will discuss topics such as:

-Optometry Schools in the United States

-Optometry Admissions Processes

-Eye Care today

-OAT workshops

-FAQ's about Optometry schools

For this organization, there will be several activities and community service events that the Pre-Optometry Club will be involved in to help others in the community. Optometrists, guest speakers, and optometry schools around the United States will be invited to speak to the Pre-Optometry Club at The University of Tennessee to help us gain more knowledge about the profession.

GroupMe Link: https://groupme.com/join_group/107043782/X1haZKxW
summary: The purpose of the Pre-Optometry Club at The University of Tennessee is to focus on bringing students around the campus together who are interested in seeking the optometry profession to discuss topics to help students with the admission process.

name: Student Members of the American Chemical Society
description: SMACS is an organization affiliated with the American Chemical Society which targets undergraduates who are interested in Chemistry. We aim to educate and engage the student population through guest speakers, networking opportunities, and community events. All Majors Welcomed and Appreciated.
summary: SMACS aims to connect, engage, and support the scientific community to foster and promote chemistry education and enterprise.

name: Student Bar Association
summary: The Student Bar Association is the student representative organization of The University of Tennessee College of Law and acts as the voice of the student body and a liaison between the law school administration and students.

name: HCB Student Leadership Council
summary: HCB Student Leadership Council

name: Sophisticated Queens Unafraid And Driven
summary: Sophisticated Queens Unafraid And Driven (S.Q.U.A.D.) aims to unite, support, and promote success for Women of Color on campus and throughout the community, by encouraging academic and personal achievement, while simultaneously building self-confidence.

name: Baking Club
description:
THIS IS NOT A REAL STUDENT ORGANIZATION, IT IS USED FOR TRAINING PURPOSES.

Are you interested in baking?  Do you want to try new treats to bake?  Join the Baking Club!  A great way to learn new recipies and test out old ones with your closest baking friends.  We meet each Monday at 5 pm in HSS Room 65.  We also host baking demos each month on Ped Walkway!  Come join us!
summary: THIS IS NOT A REAL STUDENT ORGANIZATION, IT IS USED FOR TRAINING PURPOSES. The Baking Club is the home for all students who love to bake!  Join us for our next meeting or baking demo on Ped Walkway (THIS IS NOT A REAL ORGANIZATION)

name: The Astronomy Club
description:
Welcome to the new and refreshed Astronomy Club! 

We are open to all majors, undergrads, grad students, alumni, and staff! The club is free to join, and there are no requirements!

If you even like the idea of black holes, galaxies, astrophotography, space travel, aliens, or anything else space-related, this is the club for you! 

Also, make sure to join our GroupMe, as 99% of our communications and information will be shared there. Please post any messages/questions there! And check out our new Linktree for all our social media and website links! 

https://linktr.ee/astronomyclubatutk

https://groupme.com/join_group/106065830/Gnc0BAZy
summary: Enthusiastic astronomy lovers who enjoy looking/learning about the night sky through interactions with telescopes, planetarium shows, and other science activities!

name: Investment Group at the University of Tennessee
description:
Founded in Fall 2010, the University of Tennessee Investment Group (UTIG) is a student-run organization that promotes financial literacy, introduces students to the importance of personal finance, and provides hands-on experience managing an investment portfolio.

Each semester, UTIG hosts a trading competition where members gain practical experience managing a portfolio of equities and options. Winners are awarded cash prizes and trophies. Additionally, UTIG hosts bi-weekly portfolio workshops in the investment center, utilizing Bloomberg software to enhance members' understanding of financial markets. Membership is open to students of all academic levels and majors, though dues are required.
summary: The University of Tennessee Investment Group's mission is to develop a better understanding of financial markets through trading, investing, and networking with industry professionals.

name: VOLS 2 VOLS Peer Health Educators
description:
The mission of the VOLS 2 VOLS Peer Health Educators is to promote healthy lifestyles and better decision making to the university community by providing unbiased and inclusive information with easily accessible resources. VOLS 2 VOLS strive to help build a supportive environment for all Volunteers by creating an open dialogue about health issues that matter to students. 

Vision
The VOLS 2 VOLS Peer Health Educators seek to promote a healthy and connected student body where students are equipped with the knowledge to make informed decisions about their health and wellness. Through peer-to-peer engagement, VOLS 2 VOLS hope to increase accountability amongst fellow peers and encourage them to speak up and intervene in potentially harmful situations. Through these efforts, the VOLS 2 VOLS strive to become a respected resource for student health and wellness at UT.

Requirements:

  • Must have a minimum GPA of 2.5
  • A 3 credit hour course offered in the spring upon joining. 
  • Must be able and willing to engage to engage with the general student body on various health related topics. 

Benefits:

  • This is a paid position!
  • Upon completing the 3 credit hour course you receive a certification for Peer Health Education. 
  • Builds fantastic leadership skills. 

VOLS 2 VOLS Peer Health Educators’ Areas of Focus

  • Interpersonal Wellness (healthy relationships, consent, sexual violence prevention)
  • Sexual Health Promotion
  • Alcohol and Other Drug Education
  • General Wellness (e.g. nutrition, sleep, stress)
summary: VOLS 2 VOLS is a student led organization that has the unique benefit of working with a campus office, The Center for Health Education and Wellness (CHEW). We are passionate about spreading information and awareness for health promotion.

name: LEAD at the University of Tennessee
description: The mission of UT LEAD is to promote undergraduate success, academic excellence, and persistence to graduation through a support program for generation students at UT. UT LEAD is built on the principles of Leadership, Excellence, Achievement, and Diversity.
summary: UT LEAD

name: Psychology Graduate Student Association
description:
The goal of the University of Tennessee Psychology Graduate Student Association (PGSA) is to bring together students from across the department working with a diverse faculty and with a variety of interests to improve the academic and social climate from within. In addition to fundraising to support student travel, professional development, guest speakers, and other academic activities, PGSA plans social events for all students in the department. These vary from year to year, but in the past have included tailgate parties and grouped seats for football games and other sporting events, hiking and camping trips in the Smokies, BBQs and cookouts, and much  more, not to mention our monthly “happy hours” at local pubs.

More than just a social organization, PGSA is here to help you navigate Knoxville, your graduate program, your department, the university, and grad school in general. We also proudly serve our fellow graduate students as a liaison to the faculty and administration, both in the department and out.
summary: PGSA a Department of Psychology organization.  It's mission is to connect students, faculty, and staff through diverse social, academic, and professional engagement opportunities.  PGSA also raises funds to support students' academic/professional growth.

name: Management Society
description: The Management Society shall be a semi-professional student-led organization whose goal is to foster the personal and professional development of its members for careers in business. By building relationships with business professionals, as well as amongst its other members, it will foster an environment where members can all learn from one another and carry that knowledge and experience with them into the professional world.
summary: The Management Society is a 100% student-led organization at Haslam College of Business at the University of Tennessee working to empower students to lead meaningful, impactful professional lives.

name: The Daily Beacon
description: The Daily Beacon, an editorially independent newspaper operating within the Department of Student Media, has served the University of Tennessee Knoxville campus as the official newspaper since 1906. The students, faculty and staff rely upon The Daily Beacon for up-to-date news, sports and entertainment coverage, locally and nationally. The Daily Beacon is a member of College Media Association and publishing of a daily newspaper is one of the oldest student traditions at the University of Tennessee.
summary: The Daily Beacon is the editorially independent newspaper of the University of Tennessee.

name: Philosophy Graduate Student Association
description: The PGSA is designed to help prepare graduate students in Philosophy for professional success in the field. We provide workshops run by faculty members that help graduate students hone their skills in paper writing, conference presentations, grant applications, publication, and teaching. We also serve as a support network for the graduate students to share their ideas with one another and ask advice about whatever may come up.
summary: The PGSA is designed to help prepare graduate students in Philosophy for professional success in the field. We provide workshops run by faculty members that help graduate students hone their skills in paper writing, conference presentations, and more.

name: WUTK (90.3 The Rock FM)
summary: 90.3-FM WUTK is an educational non-profit radio station licensed to the board of trustees of The University of Tennessee at Knoxville.

name: Pursuit - The Undergraduate Journal of Research at the University of Tennessee
description: Pursuit is the first and only student-managed undergraduate research publication service at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. The journal accepts advanced, original, research-based submissions from undergraduate students in all academic disciplines. Graduates may submit their work within one year of their graduation date. The Editors and Reviewers of Pursuit work in tandem with the Office of Undergraduate Research and Fellowships to uplift opportunities for involvement with undergraduate research and streamline the publishing process while maintaining its scholastic rigor.
summary: Pursuit is the first scholarly peer-reviewed journal at UT Knoxville managed and edited by undergraduates and devoted specifically to their research achievements.

name: The Maker's Club
description:
The Maker's Club is dedicated to encouraging and supporting creativity on campus. To do this, we work on a variety of projects started by members, host workshops, and provide opportunities for members to make whatever ideas the come up with.

 

Join our Discord server for more information: https://tiny.utk.edu/MakersClub

 

Members: You can access the project proposal form here : https://volink.utk.edu/submitter/form/start/560004
summary: We promote creative problem solving by using a variety of tools and methods of making. All skill levels welcome!

name: Future Occupational Therapists Association
description: The Future Occupational Therapists Association is open to all individuals at The University of Tennessee. This club allows members to connect and network with other students pursuing this profession. Members can also interact with guest speakers by asking questions about undergraduate school, prerequisite courses, graduate school applications, shadowing opportunities, and what an occupational therapist does day-to-day. Having the opportunity to build strong relationships with similar peers is a great way to establish long-term connections that can be beneficial as you enter your career.
summary: The Future Occupational Therapists Association encourages members at UTK to build relationships, network, and gain experience within this allied health profession with like-minded peers.

name: Anthropology Graduate Student Association
description: The objectives and purpose of the Anthropology Graduate Student Association (AGSA) shall be (1) to hold meetings and to facilitate communication within the Department of Anthropology graduate students; (2) to promote fellowship among members; (3) to host annual events to promote social, research, and academic camaraderie; (4) to promote a greater interest in Anthropology within the University of Tennessee student body and the general public; (5) to use our positionality, time, and resources as anthropologists to promote equity and social justice within the discipline, our department, and the greater Knoxville community, past, present, and future.
summary: AGSA holds meetings to facilitate communication within the Department of Anthropology graduate students;  hosts annual events to promote social, research, and academic camaraderie; and promotes interest in Anthropology within UT as well as the public.

name: Global Brigades at the University of Tennessee
shortName: Global Brigades at UTK, GB at UTK
description: For over 20 years, Global Brigades has mobilized thousands of university students and professionals through eight skill-based service programs to improve the quality of life in under-resourced communities through partner organizations in Honduras, Belize, Panama, Guatemala, Ghana, and Greece. These programs include medical, dental, public health, water, business, human rights, environmental, and engineering. Our mission is to empower volunteers and under-resourced communities to resolve global health and economic disparities and inspire all involved to collaboratively work towards an equal world.
summary:
Global Brigades works with rural communities in Belize, Ghana, Greece, Guatemala, Honduras, and Panama to address the major health and development needs in the region
through a holistic and sustainable approach.

name: FUTURE at the University of Tennessee
description:
FUTURE is a two-to-three year Postsecondary Education Program for young adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities including autism. FUTURE is designed to help students successfully transition from high school to adult life. Our goal is to empower our 30 FUTURE students to achieve gainful employment in their communities!

With the help of faculty, staff, graduate assistants, and volunteer peer-mentors, we provide students with the opportunity to take specially designed FUTURE courses, audit undergraduate courses, and to attain internships on and off campus to develop their academic, vocational, social, and decision-making skills. Upon completion of the program, students receive a vocational certificate to ultimately increase their employability and economic opportunities.

We are always recruiting volunteer peer mentors! If you are a student at the University of Tennessee (undergrad OR grad), are ready to take part in ensuring FUTURE student success, AND you want to make some friends along the way, you can be a peer mentor with FUTURE! Some of our positions are:

- Academic Supports

- In-Class Supports

- Job Coaches

- Workout Buddies

- Lunch Buddies

- Class TAs

- Morning Check-Ins

- Fun Friday Mentors

If you'd like to become a volunteer peer mentor with FUTURE, please fill out the form on our website at futureut.utk.edu!
summary: UT FUTURE Program

name: Financial Management Association
description: FMA is an organization that works to guide students through a vast and difficult job market as a finance major. FMA's goal is to expose students to all areas of finance so that students are best prepared to recruit for all career paths. FMA also provides opportunities to resume and LinkedIn workshops, as well as the opportunity to network with business professionals from around the country.
summary: The Financial Management Association hosts monthly business speakers, and active members are eligible to take trips to New York, among other potential locations.

name: National Organization of Minority Architecture Students
description: We, the students of the University of Tennessee, Knoxville Chapter of the National Organization of Minority of Architects, have come together to enhance our education, network with professionals, and begin to define solutions that will ensure a healthy living and working environment for the entire community. This organization is meant to foster communication, cooperation, solidarity, and fellowship among minority students of architecture. We establish this organization, which is built on the bonds of the common professional interests that bring us together and, equally, on the bonds of friendship and fraternity that will sustain and enrich our association.
summary: As the National Organization of minority architecture students, we strive to create a safe space where design students can come together and have their voices heard regardless of race, gender, sexuailty, etc.

name: Arnold Air Society
description:
Arnold Air Society is a professional, honorary service organization advocating the support of aerospace power.

The primary mission of Arnold Air Society is to build and develop leaders and officers for the United States Air Force by participating in service projects and professional development workshops.
summary: The primary mission of Arnold Air Society is to build and develop leaders and officers for the United States Air Force by participating in service projects and professional development workshops.

name: Women's Club Softball
description: Club Softball welcomes women with all types of softball backgrounds to come out and join our club! We love to compete and win while also providing a fun love for the game experience!
summary:
The purpose of the Woman’s Club Softball Team at the University of Tennessee is to
provide an avenue for women to play softball competitively during their time at the University of
Tennessee.

name: Food and Agricultural Business Club
description: To provide students with the ability to meet with professionals in the agricultural business world, and give students the skills to be successful at networking and job seeking in any industry in which they are interested.
summary: To provide students with the ability to meet with professionals in the agricultural business world, and give students the skills to be successful at networking and job seeking in any industry in which they are interested.

name: GAYmerz
description: Since its founding in 2018, GAYmerz has provided a safe environment for people of all identities to come together over a shared love for gaming. Since then we have cultivated a strong, welcoming community in which many of our members have found close friendships. Membership is casual, our only goal is to provide our members with a place to chill out and have fun. Read more about us here!
summary: A group made to provide people of all sexual orientations and gender identities a safe environment to play and discuss video/board games amongst others in the community. Always accepting new members, just stop by!

name: MEDLIFE at UTK
description:
The mission of MEDLIFE at UTK is to help families achieve greater freedom from the constraints of poverty, empowering them to live healthier lives. We seek to achieve this goal through partnering with motivated individuals locally and abroad to improve impoverished communities' access to Medicine, Education and community Development. Along with affiliate chapters, MEDLIFE at UTK raises money to help low-income families around the world achieve these goals, commits to learning about global healthcare, and participates in service.

MEDLIFE’s intended impact on The University of Tennessee’s campus is to help make students and the surrounding Knoxville community aware of existing disparities in health-care and to offer students opportunities to become involved in fundraising and service efforts aimed at resolving those disparities.
summary: The mission of MEDLIFE at UTK is to help impoverished communities locally and abroad achieve greater access to medicine, education, and development.

name: American Institute of Architecture Students University of Tennessee Chapter
description: The American Institute of Architecture Students (AIAS) is an independent, nonprofit, student-run organization dedicated to providing unmatched progressive programs, information, and resources on issues critical to architecture and the experience of education. The AIAS aims to promote excellence in architectural education, training, and practice; to foster an appreciation of architecture and related disciplines; to enrich communities in a spirit of collaboration; and to organize students and combine their efforts to advance the art and science of architecture.
summary:
The American Institute of Architecture Students (AIAS) is national independent, nonprofit, student-run organization dedicated to advancing leadership, design, and service among architecture students.
(From aias.org)

name: Fellowship College
description: Fellowship College is the college ministry of Fellowship Church of Knoxville. The gospel of Jesus fuels everything we do at Fellowship Church. By believing and trusting Jesus, He begins to renew everything in your life. He makes you a new person, places you in a new family, and sends you on a new mission. In each of these areas, we all have a next step of faith to take. Fellowship College offers multiple avenues for next steps of faith, including weekly college nights, adult-led community groups, and Sunday morning   connection points to the church body as a whole. Fellowship College is a great place to start making this big church feel smaller.
summary: Leading college students deeper into their faith & further into the life of Fellowship Church, for the purpose of true spiritual maturity

name: Service Learning and Outreach with 3D Printers for Engineers
description: This organization is committed to an initiative of communicating and serving the local community through inspiring engineering and learning in future students. This will be achieved with a combination of educational presentation opportunities and the opportunity to create a small item using 3D printing that was designed by our members. Manufacture may take place on site and be used as a real-world example of many engineering processes such as software use, design limitation consideration, material selection, and creativity.
summary: This group focuses on STEM exposure to the local Knoxville community by 3d printing at the East Tennessee Children's Hospital as well as working with elementary educational programs, and other outreach events.

name: Association of Graduate Business Veterans
description: The Association of Graduate Business Veterans (AGBV) is a dynamic network of military veterans who are current students or alumni of graduate business programs, dedicated to fostering robust connections across generations. AGBV facilitate meaningful communication, organize impactful events, and create valuable employment opportunities. AGBV empowers its members to leverage their unique skills and experiences. AGBV community aims to bridge the gap between past, present, and future members, enabling them to support one another in their professional journeys. AGBV’s commitment to excellence and collaboration ensures that veterans can maximize their potential and make significant contributions to the business world.
summary: AGBV is an association of current and alumni graduate business programs military veterans, focusing on strengthening the network between past, present, and future members through communication, events, and employment opportunities.

name: Future Medical Leaders
description: Future Medical Leaders at the University of Tennessee provides pre-medical, pre-dental, pre-pharmacy, and pre-physician assistant students with the necessary resources to be successful in graduate school. We facilitate shadowing opportunities, community service, and relationships with medical professionals here in Knoxville. We also provide students with CPR certification and Stop The Bleed certification. *If you are still currently interested in joining, Venmo the dues of $10 to @fml_utk, with your name and Netid, and you will be added to the official GroupMe.*
