summary: Future Medical Leaders at The University of Tennessee aims to provide students interested in healthcare with the necessary tools to succeed in their mission to become medical professionals.

name: Wildlife and Fisheries Society
summary: The Student Chapter of the Wildlife and Fisheries Society gives students the chance to gain hands-on experience that may be used in their future employment within the natural resources field. This organization also provides networking opportunities.

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated
description:
Since its founding in 1913, Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc. has clearly distinguished itself as a public service organization that boldly confronts the challenges of African Americans and, hence, all Americans. Over the years, a wide range of programs addressing education, health, international development, and strengthening of the African American family have evolved. In realizing its mission, Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc. provides an extensive array of public service initiatives through its Five-Point Programmatic Thrust of:

Educational Development

Economic Development

International Awareness and Involvement 

Physical and Mental Health

Political Awareness and Involvement
summary: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated is an organization of college educated women committed to the constructive development of its members and to public service with a primary focus on the Black community.

name: Miniatures Gaming Club at UT
description: The Miniatures Gaming Club at the University of Tennessee, also known as the Rocky Top Regulars, is dedicated to the organization of miniatures gaming on campus. We seek to provide hobbyist on and around campus an environment to meet fellow miniature gamers. Throughout the year we will support events and campaigns for various miniature games to encourage continued play between members. Additionally we coordinate directly with campus facilities to provide a regular space to play weekly.
summary: The University of Tennessee Miniatures Gaming Club is dedicated to the organization of miniature gaming on campus. We support Warhammer 40k, Age of Sigmar, Infinity, Battletech, and anything else involving miniatures.

name: Rocky Top Rowdies
description: This organization exists to provide structure to the student section for University of Tennessee sports. It is our goal to act as a bridge between students and the University of Tennessee Athletic Department. We strive to make a positive impact on the Tennessee sports experience and on the Knoxville Community. Members of the Rocky Top Rowdies make it a priority to support Tennessee sport in every situation. Go Vols!
summary: The Official Student Section for The University of Tennessee Vols and Lady Vols Basketball. Feed the Floor!

name: Eventing Club
description:
Eventing is a 3-Day equestrian competition consisting of Dressage, Cross-Country, and Show Jumping. Members are able to earn points based on their overall placing and division at the completion of a recognized competition. Awards are available at the end of the USEA season for the top ranked universities. 

Members are expected to own/lease their horse and are welcome to train with a trainer of their choice. A minimum of one lesson a month MUST be completed with the Eventing Team Coach, Erika Adams at Penrose Farm.

The club does not provide horses for lessons or competitions at this time.
summary: This club competes through the US Eventing Association Intercollegiate Eventing Program.

name: Rec Sports Outdoor Program
description:
Outdoor Center

Access a large selection of outdoor rental equipment, our full service bike shop, plus an extensive library of books, maps, and videos.

Choose Your Adventure

Choose from a variety of afternoon, all-day, weekend, and weeklong guided outdoor adventure trips. Or sign up for one of our free clinics on campus and learn things like how to roll a whitewater kayak or belay a person climbing.

Climbing Wall

Boulder or top rope on 6,000 square feet of indoor climbing wall. Weekly belay clinics and a bouldering competition during spring and fall semester. New routes put up each week to keep things fresh.
summary: East Tennessee is the perfect outdoor adventure setting, and UT is right in the middle of it. We’re here to help you pick up new skills, see awesome places, and make friends in the process.

name: Undergraduate Association of Forensic Sciences
description:
The club connects with current forensics experts from academia and applied fields to share their work with students from UT. We hope to expand our membership in order to take advantage of the resources available to young forensic scientists. Our community places a high emphasis on the importance of interdisciplinary collaboration that makes forensics so powerful. 

Please join our GroupMe for more information: https://groupme.com/join_group/95606420/dwbflVqb
summary: UAFS is an interdisciplinary club that helps those interested in forensics connect with likeminded individuals and future forensic professionals gain networking connections and participate in professional development activities.

name: Pro Bono at UT
description: Pro Bono at UT is a community service organization with significant student leadership and staff direction at the University of Tennessee College of Law. The program strives to connect law students with area attorneys providing pro bono service to clients. Working in cooperation with attorneys, local legal aid societies, other student organizations, and nonprofit organizations, Pro Bono at UT serves as a resource by providing law students for research, educational, and investigatory assistance.
summary: Pro Bono at UT is a community service organization with significant student leadership and staff direction at the University of Tennessee College of Law.  The program strives to connect law students with area attorneys providing pro bono service to clien

name: Graduate Physics Society
description:
The mission of the Graduate Physics Society is to provide professional development. The purpose of
the Graduate Physics Society is to connect graduate students with their peers and empower them to
communicate their knowledge.

 

Membership to the Graduate Physics Society is open to all graduate students at the University of
Tennessee Knoxville with an interest in physics. General meetings of the Graduate Physics Society will be held once a month.
summary:
The Graduate Physics Society (GPS) is a community of graduate students with an interest in physics.
The mission of GPS is to provide professional development.

name: Korean Pop Appreciation Association
description: Here at the Korean Pop Appreciation Association at the University of Tennessee in Knoxville, our main focus is to bring together Korean Pop music lovers on campus and discuss recent Korean Pop culture trends! We want to encourage a better understanding of a variety of K-Pop music groups and hopefully Korean culture as a whole. We feature a variety of activities, such as games, artist of the month presentations, song of the week, show and tell, club outings, movie nights, and holiday parties!
summary: Welcome to the Korean Pop Appreciation Association!

name: Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Incorporated
description:
Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Incorporated was founded at Kean University in Union, New Jersey in December of 1975 as an academic sorority. The purpose was to create a support group for Latinas pursuing higher education and to unite the Latino community on campus. The oldest and largest Latina-based sorority in the United States.

The Gamma Gamma Chapter was established at the University of Tennessee on April 28, 2001 to provide a space for Latina women to be themselves and represent a diverse group of women on campus.
summary: The goals of Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Inc are scholarly excellence, empowerment of latinas and the universal woman, political & cultural awareness and community activism.

name: En Motion Dance
description: En Motion Dance offers students the opportunity to develop skills in a variety of skills. Weekly classes will be offered and taught by experienced students or local dance instructors. Classes meet on Mondays and Tuesdays from 7-9pm in a TRECS Studio. Performances will include student-choreographed pieces. No audition is required for participation into the organization. Every student wishing to participate is guaranteed a part in each performance. We will be doing competitions as well for those wanting to participate. This is a space to dance at any level, if you feel comfortable in pointe shoes feel free to wear them! You are welcome to participate in all rehearsal days available or just the day you can come. We will do a monthly volunteering event to get engaged in the community.
summary: En Motion Dance offers students the opportunity to develop skills in a variety of dance styles. En Motions is an inclusive group open to every single student at UT, regardless of dance ability. There are weekly classes on Monday and Tuesdays in the TRECS

name: Engineers in Medicine Society
description: The mission of the Society is to provide resources and opportunities to engineering students that are interested in pursuing careers as healthcare professionals. Service and outreach to the greater campus community will be an important foundation of the Society. Moreover, the Society will promote the use of analytical thought and the engineering mindset. In pursuing these goals, the Society will create a support network of pre-health students that can share and learn from peer experiences.
summary: EMED is a pre-health engineering society dedicated to providing resources and opportunities to engineering majors in pursuit of professional school.

name: Microbiology Graduate Student Association
description: The mission of the Micro GSA is to promote the professional development and personal advancement of its members through academic events, social gatherings, and networking opportunities with the larger Microbiology community.
summary: The Micro GSA aims to promote professional development and personal advancement of its members through academic events, social gatherings, and networking opportunities with the larger Microbiology community.

name: Society for the Advancement of Material and Process Engineering at the University of Tennessee
description: SAMPE-UTK is the student chapter of an international composites industry trade group, the Society for the Advancement of Material and Process Engineering.  Students will learn about advanced polymeric composites such as carbon fiber and fiberglass, and have the opportunity to engage in hands-on workshops with topics including part design, mechanics of composite materials, mold making, resin infusion, etc.  They will also be able to hear from industry speakers at least once a semester, and learn about job opportunities in the composites sector.  The main event is the annual SAMPE conference, where students can submit entries to the bridge competition and test the mettle of their composite designs against other students from around the world.  Attendance at our meetings is open to all!
summary: SAMPE teaches students how to design and make their own composite parts. Learn about these strong, lightweight materials from industry guest speakers and hands-on workshops, and participate in the annual carbon fiber bridge-building competition.

name: Planned Parenthood Generation Action Council at the University of Tennessee
description:
PURPOSE
The mission of UTK Planned Parenthood Generation Action Council is to educate the university community about reproductive health and freedom and to translate increased awareness into activism on campus utilizing an intersectional framework as we recognize all axes of oppression (e.g race, class, sexuality, ability, citizenship, etc.). We aim to serve as a coalition partner to state, national, and international reproductive freedom efforts. We aim to empower individuals in our community by providing information about on and off-campus reproductive health resources.


VISION
The UTK Planned Parenthood Generation Action Council believes in the fundamental right of every individual to manage their body as it pertains to reproduction, identity, sexuality, and general health. Our organization advocates for full access to comprehensive and affirming health care services in settings that preserve and protect the essential agency and autonomy of each individual. We do so by advocating for public policies and reforms that guarantee these rights and ensure access to such services, supporting access to medically accurate educational programs that enhance understanding of human sexuality, and acting as a partner to coalitions that aim to free all people from systematic oppression.
summary: We aim to educate the university community about reproductive health and freedom and to translate increased awareness into activism on campus utilizing an intersectional framework as we recognize all axes of oppression.

name: Coalition of Black Social Workers
description: The CBSW is a place where Black social work students can connect through networking and mentorship with Black professional social workers.  Non-Black social work students are also able to join in solidarity with their Black peers.  The organization has numerous social events, service activities, and social justice projects throughout the year.  Students are also instrumental in the planning and execution of the annual conference.  Research shows that Black students who are connected to a mentor have better outcomes in higher education.  It is our goal that every student who would like a mentor receive a mentor.  https://youtu.be/wkdVvrVtSnE
summary: The CBSW exists to engage, connect, and empower Black social work students and professionals in the community, with the goal of increasing awareness about issues specific to the Black community and cultivating a network of support.

name: Turf Club at the University of Tennessee
description: We will provide information and help with career directions and the connections needed to become a successful Golf Course Superintendent, Sports Turf Manager, Lawn Care Operator, and in Sales. Our goal is to provide an avenue for an exchange of information and ideas between students and industry professionals.
summary: An official organization for students interested in Golf Course and Sports Turf Management, Sales, and Professional Lawn Care Operations.

name: Relay for Life at UTK
description:
Relay For Life currently serves as the largest non-profit fundraiser in the world to raise funds and awareness for cancer. Over 4-million people alone in the United States relay each year, including 100s of college campuses. Organized and executed by volunteers of the American Cancer Society, money raised by Relay for Life not only goes towards cancer research, but education and prevention programs, as well as local community initiatives to help those suffering from cancer. This community focused event, including a celebration of life for all those who have survived cancer mixed with a remembrance ceremony for those we’ve lost, is vital in the country’s attack on cancer.

 

Here at UTK, the Relay for Life Committee plans and executes a Relay for Life event in the Spring and Fall each year. Our events include a 5k, Walk a Mile in my Shoes, and our Main Event. The Committee recruits other organizations as well as individuals to create teams and participate in Relay for Life. Our job as Committee is to plan fun events, table, and ultimately be a resource to all the teams who sign up for this event.

 

We are committed to raising awareness and money for the American Cancer Society and we hope to be a light to those who are currently battling cancer.

You can follow us on Instagram @utkrelay
summary: Relay for Life is the largest non-profit fundraiser in the world to raise money for cancer. Organized by volunteers, over 4-million people come together in this event to raise money for cancer, celebrate our survivors, and remember those we have lost.

name: Phi Kappa Tau
description:
The Phi Kappa Tau - Delta Kappa Chapter stands as one of the University of Tennessee-Knoxville's premier IFC Fraternities and one of Phi Kappa Tau's fastest-growing and most successful chapters. 

Initially, Phi Tau chartered on campus in 1976 with the promise of building a strong brotherhood of good men. This phase lasted forty years and paved the way for many brothers to achieve excellence. Even now, these alumni return to Rocky Top to support the current Phi Tau brotherhood and participate in some events and rituals. After re-chartering in 2019, Phi Tau is back once again and has built a strong core of brotherhood. Phi Tau men seek to excel academically, athletically, and socially in all manners of life. We encourage you to seek the light of Brotherhood and join Phi Tau!
summary: Leadership Organization turning Men of Character into Men of Distinction

name: Life Without Limits
description: Life Without Limits is a club designed to give back to the community and beyond. We strive to use our educational skills in engineering, graphic design, etc. to design assistive devices for those who struggle to complete everyday tasks that most people don't think twice about. Focusing on CAD design through Solidworks and Autodesk as well as 3D printing, we are a club open to all majors dedicated making a difference in the lives of the disabled community and beyond.
summary: Student-Led Organization that uses 3D printing and CAD software to design and make assistive devices for those in need.

name: Women of Haslam
description: The mission of Women of Haslam is to provide educational and business networking connections that support, enrich and inspire women to develop professionally and personally.  We bring collegiate, academic and community business women together in a variety of networking venues to strengthen the member’s knowledge, experiences and resources. Our goal is to introduce women in all professions to the applicable principles of business, entrepreneurship, and leadership.
summary: Women of Haslam is a business society at The University of Tennessee that supports and inspires women to develop professionally and personally.

name: Office of Student Media
description: The Office of Student Media serves as the launching point for student journalists and those interested in the media field. Open to all majors and disciplines, the Office of Student Media offers opportunities in newspaper and magazine writing and reporting, video production, social media management, copy editing, advertising, sales, marketing, photography, leadership experience and so many other fields.
summary: The Office of Student Media serves as the launching point for student journalists and those interested in the media field. Open to all majors and disciplines, the Office of Student Media offers opportunities for any student on campus.

name: McClung Student Advisory Board
description: The McClung Student Advisory board is a group of students who engage with and advise the McClung Museum and function as a liaison for the UT student body. To apply to be a part of the board please contact our primary contact to start the application process.
summary: The McClung Student Advisory board is a group of students who engage with and advise the McClung Museum and function as a liaison for the UT student body.  To apply to be a part of the board please contact our primary contact.

name: Political Science Graduate Student Association
description: Association for the advancement and assistance of graduate students of political science.
summary: PSGSA is an organization for Political Science graduate students at the University of Tennessee that seeks to facilitate collaboration between students and faculty and works to advance the personal and professional goals of the students it represents.

name: Student Support Services
description:
From Orientation to Graduation

We Are Here For You.

Student Support Services (SSS) is a federally funded TRIO program committed to the success of first-generation, low-income, and/or scholars with disabilities. Our mission is to support and empower participants to succeed from orientation to graduation.
summary: Student Support Services

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers
description: Founded in 2019, the University of Tennessee's chapter of the Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers seeks to uplift Asian heritage STEM students at our university. Our mission is to create the Asian heritage leaders of tomorrow by balancing promoting their culture, building their professional skills, and giving back to the community.
summary: The University of Tennessee-Knoxville SASE Chapter advances students through a diverse community that encourages professional development, cultural awareness, and service to the community in regards to Asian heritage and the STEM field.

name: Nuclear Engineering Graduate Student Assembly
description: The mission of the Nuclear Engineering Graduate Student Assembly is to serve as a collaborative forum that enables University of Tennessee graduate students in the Nuclear Engineering Department to initiate and engage in discussions, events, and programs hosted by the organization to better the department and its graduate students
summary: The mission of the Nuclear Engineering Graduate Student Assembly is to serve as a collaborative forum that enables University of Tennessee graduate students in the Nuclear Engineering Department to initiate and engage in discussions, events, and programs

name: Leading Women of Tomorrow UTK
description:
The purpose of this organization is to empower, encourage, and assist women participating in careers in civil service as well as all other women pursuing career fields traditionally dominated by men. Our efforts are aimed at bridging the gender gap among public representatives and fields traditionally dominated by men. We will pursue these goals through educational programming and events, chapter meetings, networking activities, etc.

Application Form: 

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScPHK8hbHlgmxdTUqxUhNRNUYDIfF6elHIomFNTzcB5RaCF1w/viewform?usp=share_link&ouid=115312329429998038487
summary: LWT is a student-led organization at the University of Tennessee focused on professional and personal development of young women. Our organization aims to encourage and support women to pursue leadership positions and connect them with women leaders.

name: Roundnet Club at UTK
description: The goal of this club is to engage students in a fun and active sport. Roundnet, or more commonly known from the brand Spikeball, has skyrocketed in the past few years especially at the collegiate level. Whether you decide to play for fun, or compete in tournaments across the country, Roundnet at UTK provides a great way to stay active, travel, and meet new friends.
summary: Wanna play Roundnet (AKA Spikeball) competitively? You're in the right place!

name: Engineers Without Borders at the University of Tennessee
description: Engineers Without Borders builds a better world through engineering projects that empower communities to meet their basic human needs and equip leaders to solve the world’s most pressing challenges. Our vision is a world in which every community has the capacity to sustainably meet their basic human needs. In the world’s toughest places, Engineers Without Borders USA (EWB-USA) is partnering with communities to meet their basic human needs. We’re building footbridges to provide pathways to opportunities. We’re installing solar panels to bring light where it is dark. We’re digging for water so hope can spring from the ground. Each project builds the foundation for a community to thrive for years to come.
summary: Engineers Without Borders builds a better world through engineering projects that empower communities to meet their basic human needs. As UTK volunteers, we work with communities to find appropriate solutions for their infrastructure needs.

name: Supply Chain Analytics and Reporting
description:
A student organization dedicated to building scaled components of a supply chain focused on analytics and reporting. We aim to complete real world deliverables with corporate partners every semester.

If you have interest in joining SCAR, please reach out: ahende50@vols.utk.edu
summary: A student organization dedicated to building scaled components of a supply chain using robotics/automation, coding and applying supply chain principles like collaboration and data collection for process improvements.

name: Ablaze Magazine
description:
Ablaze Magazine is a student-powered magazine publication that covers women’s topics and feminist issues. We are based on the campus of The University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Our ultimate goal is to empower women on campus and those within the community who share our ideals and values. This magazine is a voice for all and we want to spotlight innovators and spark conversation. Our contributing team is made up of a diverse group of students who have a variety of interests, come from an array of backgrounds, and have infinite ambition.

Together as a team, we produce both print and digital content. Whether you have experience or are looking to learn something new, we need motivated contributors who are interested in writing, design, social media, photography, event planning, marketing, and more. 

As of Fall 2022, we have rebranded from Honey Magazine.
summary:
Ablaze Magazine is a student-powered magazine publication that covers women’s
topics and feminist issues.

Formerly known as Honey Magazine.

name: Alpha Epsilon Chi Chapter of Sigma Tau Delta, the English Honors Society
description:
The Alpha Epsilon Chi chapter of Sigma Tau Delta, the English Honors Society, was inaugurated at the University of Tennessee in 1996. In addition to recognizing English majors with outstanding records, Sigma Tau Delta brings together students with an interest in writing, literature, and language in order to enhance their educational program through co-curricular activities. In the past Sigma Tau Delta has sponsored student-faculty mixers, social events, career panels, and group attendance at cultural events in campus and in the Knoxville area.

 

Sigma Tau Delta, the English Honors Society, is composed of close to 600 chapters located throughout the United States, Europe, Canada, and the Caribbean; it is the third largest of the 66 members of the Association of College Honors Societies. In addition to sponsoring scholarships and awards, Sigma Tau Delta organizes an annual convention. Students interested in learning more about the international honor society may do so at www.english.org.

 

In order to qualify for membership a student must be enrolled as an English major or minor, have completed at least three semesters of college work and two courses in English in addition to freshman writing, and have a 3.0 GPA and a 3.2 GPA in English courses. Currently enrolled students who are not English majors may be admitted as associate members if they meet the above requirements. The organization is open to undergraduates and graduate students. All students admitted into Sigma Tau Delta pay a $45 initiation fee, which includes dues for the national organization and local chapter.
summary: Sigma Tau Delta brings together students with an interest in writing, literature, and language in order to enhance their educational program through co-curricular activities. Sigma Tau Delta is exclusively for English majors and minors.

name: Thrive College Ministry
description: The mission of Thrive College Ministry is to connect students with the Thrive program and foster community within staff and University of Tennessee students. Thrive is a nonprofit organization in Knoxville that partners with communities to pursue spiritual, emotional, academic and physical wholeness.
summary: TCM is an organization aimed to connect students with the Thrive program

name: Turning Point USA
description: Student organization focused on the importance of political action, through a commitment to principles of small government, free speech, and capitalism! We host awesome, engaging events, big-name speakers, and provide students with great opportunities for work, volunteering, and even travel for national conferences!
summary: Turning Point USA is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization whose mission is to educate students about the importance of fiscal responsibility, free markets, and capitalism through innovative campus activism and non-partisan, thought-provoking discussion.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
description:
The Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) of Tennessee Knoxville is an organization made by the student body of the UTK to celebrate Vietnamese heritage and to educate others about Vietnamese culture, traditions, and social/political issues. The organization also serves as a nexus for students of Vietnamese descent, who may not be accustomed to the American life style, to get in touch with other organizations and campus functions, encouraging them to follow their personal pursuits. We are constantly striving towards diversity, meaning that you do not have to be Vietnamese to join; a simple interest in the culture, a desire to learn new things, seeking new friends, wanting to have fun, or gaining professional development is enough to join.

VSA of UTK is part of a larger non-profit organization called the Union of Vietnamese Student Associations of the Southeast (UVSASE). As part of the Southeast region, we work together in partnership with the non-profit organization Union of North American Vietnamese Student Associations (UNAVSA).

For more information about UVSASE, please check their website.

For more information about UNAVSA, please check their website.
summary: Vietnamese Student Association exists to help educate and to bring awareness to Vietnamese culture, while pushing for inclusivity & diversity. Our purpose is to provide a safe space for students to explore & embrace Vietnamese tradition and culture.

name: Business Honors Council
description: We are dedicated to enhancing the academic, professional, philanthropic, and social endeavors of honors students in the Haslam College of Business. Members of the advisory council will organize a variety of events each semester relevant to honors students in HCB.
summary: We are dedicated to enhancing the academic, professional, philanthropic, ad social endeavors of honors students in the Haslam College of Business. Members of the advisory board will organize events each semester relevant to honors students in HCB.

name: Volunteer Team
description:
Volunteer Team

The Volunteer Team meets with and provides important information to prospective student athletes and their families.  Team members have the opportunity to share their knowledge of UT and its great traditions, including the outstanding varsity athletic programs.  Any interested students may contact our group by emailing volteam@tennessee.edu.
summary: Volunteer Team meets with and provides important information to prospective student athletes & their families. Team members have the opportunity to share their knowledge of UT and its great traditions, including the outstanding varsity athletic programs.

name: Brain Exercise Initiative at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville Chapter
description:
Let's help more seniors remember and thrive together!

Our goal is to volunteer at retirement homes to guide seniors through the brain exercise process together. With a focus on preventing memory loss in those with dementia or Alzheimer's, we hope to create a future in which all seniors remember their children and grandchildren!
summary: The Brain Exercise Initiative is a nonprofit organization that uses simple math, writing, and reading aloud exercised as an intervention to improve cognitive function in those with Alzheimer's Disease.

name: Herbert Student Engagement
summary: Herbert College of Agriculture

name: Alpha Omega Epsilon at the University of Tennessee
description: Alpha Omega Epsilon is a nationally recognized sorority composed of female and underrepresented genders STEM students looking to develop their professionalism through networking with fellow students in similar studies, as well as build their interpersonal relationships through social events and local service projects.
summary: Alpha Omega Epsilon is a professional and social sorority composed of female engineering and technical science students. We encourage our members to promote integrity, character, and honesty while promoting unity, friendship, and professionalism.

name: Net Impact University of Tennessee
summary: Net Impact - University of Tennessee educates and empowers the next generation of business leaders interested in using their talents and skills in support of various social and environmental causes.

name: Plant Sciences Graduate Student Association
description: The primary purpose of the Plant Sciences Graduate Student Association (PSGSA) is to promote the general welfare of its members and to uphold the integrity of the Department of Plant Sciences. The mission of the PSGSA is to provide a forum for graduate students to enhance their knowledge of Plant Sciences through service, social, and education-based activities and events and to promote professionalism and fellowship between graduate students. The vision of the PSGSA is to develop a strong Graduate Student Association that will benefit students before, during, and after their studies at the University of Tennessee and to further showcase and promote the excellence of the Department of Plant Sciences.
summary: An organization to unite and engage graduate students in Plant Sciences through social, educational, professional, and service activities.

name: Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Incorporated
description:
Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc. was established at the University of Tennessee on March 24th, 2002, by a group of young aspiring Latino students of various cultural backgrounds. Their mission is to create opportunities for Latinos and other individuals on campus who want to be involved and join in unification to celebrate their culture and common views. Lambda Theta Phi is a non-profit social/service fraternity emphasizing Latin unity and the celebration of Latin-American culture. With its national founding on December 1st, 1975 and its acceptance into the National Interfraternal Conference in 1992, Lambda Theta Phi became the first nationally recognized Latino Greek letter fraternity in the country.

 

The goals and objectives of the fraternity are to promote brotherhood, maintain a standard of learning, develop character, respect character, and practice chivalry. Lambda Theta Phi has set out to accomplish these goals through responsible political and social action. By working in the community, the fraternity hopes to not only provide service, but also to enhance the image of Latin culture and provide positive role models in the Latino community. Although Lambda Theta Phi is Latino by tradition, membership is open to all college males and the Brotherhood includes men representing nations of the world. Brothers of Lambda Theta Phi have gone on to become doctors, lawyers, scientists, accountants, engineers, recording artists, entrepreneurs, politicians and leaders in various areas. Lambda Theta Phi prides itself in the strength of its brotherhood and we believe that united there is nothing we cannot accomplish because “in unity there is strength".

 

NATIONAL FOUNDING DATE: December 1,1975

CHAPTER: Gamma Gamma

COLORS: Brown and White

FLOWER: White Carnation

SONG: Latinos en Estados Unidos - Celia Cruz

NICKNAME: Lambdas

MOTTO: Chivalry Above Self

SLOGANS: Leaders of the Latino Greek Movement ®, Voice of the Latino Greek Movement ®

LOCAL PHILANTHROPY: Centro Hispano de East Tennessee

NATIONAL WEBSITE: www.thelambdas.org

INSTAGRAM: www.instagram.com/utklambdas

EVENTS: National Events
summary: Lambda Theta Phi, Latin Fraternity Incorporated, is a non-profit social fraternity emphasizing Latin unity and the celebration of Latin culture. Also the first nationally recognized Latino Greek letter fraternity in the country.

name: Delta Phi Lambda
description: • Delta Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. is a non-profit organization that advocates Asian awareness and empowers women to lead through values-based programs and Everlasting Sisterhood. Delta Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. is the first Asian-Interest, but not Asian-exclusive, sorority in the state of Tennessee.
summary: Delta Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. is a non-profit organization that advocates Asian awareness and empowers women to lead through values-based programs and Everlasting Sisterhood.

name: Hack4Impact UTK
description:
We are a chapter of the National Hack4Impact organization.

Hack4Impact is an organization that develops software for other nonprofits to help make a difference in the community. We are recruiting students to join our student organization to design and develop software, manage teams, and help run our organization. Students who join will gain real-world experience working in a team to complete a project all while making an impact.

If you are interested please apply on our website.

 

 

Frequently Asked Questions:


1. What is Hack4Impact? Hack4Impact is a student-run nonprofit organization that builds powerful software for other nonprofit organizations as a tool for social good. We empower engineers, designers, activists, and humanitarians to create lasting and impactful social change through software solutions. Learn more about us at our website! https://utk.hack4impact.org/

 

2. Who can join Hack4Impact?

- Any student, regardless of major or experience level, can join Hack4Impact! We are looking for members to fill the following roles:

    - Developer

    - Designer

    - Tech Lead

    - Product Manager

    - Community Coordinator

    - Project Sourcing

    - Talent Sourcing

- Learn more about each role here: https://tiny.utk.edu/h4i/position-descriptions

- We are also looking our organization with some new roles for things like seeking sponsorships and maintaining nonprofit relations. If you think you might fit in to one of those categories, or have a new one in mind that might be beneficial, ask!

 

3. Do I need web development experience to join Hack4Impact?

- Web development experience is not required to join Hack4Impact.

- For developers and tech leads, a computer science/coding background is necessary, but there is a learning-based project for developers who may not have enough experience to jump into the developer role.

- For designers, some design know-how is required.

- For product managers and operational roles, no experience is required.

 

4. How do I join Hack4Impact? Apply on our website!

 

 

5. What kind of projects do you work on?

- Hack4Impact works on projects that benefit non-profit organizations and communities. Our projects focus on solving social problems using technology. We generally work on web applications that have some interface with data.

- Our past projects include an event management and volunteer database system for Keep Knoxville Beautiful, an interactive tree map for Trees Knoxville, an interactive map for the Second Harvest food bank, and more! Learn about our past projects here: https://utk.hack4impact.org/projects

- Our current projects include a revamped warehouse/inventory system for the Community Coalition Against Human Trafficking and a calendar tool for a content management system that can be used by other nonprofits for the Sustainable Future Center"

 

6. What tools do you use?

We use a variety of tools and technologies depending on the project needs. Some common tools we use include Git, GitHub, Typescript, and the MERN tech stack (MongoDB, ExpressJS, React, Node). If you have any more questions, feel free to send a message to any of the officers, or utk@hack4impact.org. See you around!
summary: We collaborate with nonprofits and other socially responsible organizations to develop software that meets important social and humanitarian needs.

name: Vols For Life
summary: The mission of Vols For Life is to abolish abortion in the United States by changing the hearts and minds of its citizens through education, civil discourse, and policy.

name: CHAARG at UTK
description:
CHAARG believes that working out can [+ should] be fun ++ we know that working out is a lot better with friends! Since everyone has a different preference of what fun is, we create opportunities for our members to “find their fit.” Once you join CHAARG, you’re welcomed into a supportive + empowering community of 10,000+ members nationwide! When you workout, you feel good — but, when you surround yourself with people to support you, you feel even better. CHAARG combines good friends + a good workout to keep every member feeling their happiest + healthiest self. Join CHAARG + you are instantly part of an empowering CHAARG Chapter at your university + the national community. Not only will you have access to incredible workouts, you will be immersed in a positive + uplifting community, filled with members who are as passionate about health, fitness, + positivity as you are. Once a member, you can expect to have weekly workouts with local fitness studios, small groups, + chapter socials.

 

Please explore our instagram @utk_inchaarg or visit chaarg.com to learn more about our organization + how to become a member!
summary: CHAARG is a health + wellness organization that aims to show members that fitness can [+should] be fun by partnering with a different fitness studio each week. Our mission is to empower every CHAARG member to be the best version of themselves.

name: Student Affiliates in School Psychology
description: SASP stimulates interest in the field of school psychology, generates opportunities for students to establish relationships with alumni, faculty, and other students, and helps students gain exposure to opportunities in the field of school psychology.
summary: SASP stimulates interest in the field of school psychology, generates opportunities for students to establish relationships with alumni, faculty, and other students, and helps students gain exposure to opportunities in the field of school psychology.

name: Cycling Club at UT
description:
The Cycling Club at UT is a group of mountain, gravel, and road cyclists who love UT and love to ride bikes! We do everything from casual weekly group rides, travel to regional/national races, team dinners, hangouts, and more! Overall we are just a community of Tennessee Students who all love bicycles. We want to engage and support not only the student community but also the Knoxville cycling community. Our main goal is to spread the joys of life on bicycles. From those who are just getting into cycling to those of us who have been riding since birth, we welcome you and would love to have you join the Cycling Club at UT and ride with us! If you are interested in joining the club and finding people to ride with and would like to reach out and talk to someone on the club, dm our Instagram account (ut_cycling) or join our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/86933757/TnH3Vsvp

 

For those interested in racing, our team races in the Southeastern Collegiate Cycling Conference (SECCC). The collegiate mtb season is during the fall semester from about August to October, the collegiate road season is from about February to April. Each collegiate race is hosted by or at a college in the conference. The SECCC race schedule can be found at: http://collegiatecycling.org/seccc/schedule/ In addition to collegiate races, the club travels and races in non-collegiate races and series that members are interested in. We as a club provide partial funding for our member's race fees and travel expenses through club sponsorships and the student organization travel fund. Because we understand cycling is expensive and that we as college students often are not made of money, we try and provide as much financial support possible for members who want to race.

Instagram: ut_cycling

Groupme: https://groupme.com/join_group/86933757/TnH3Vsvp

SECCC Schedule: http://collegiatecycling.org/seccc/schedule/
summary: We ride all kinds of bikes.

name: Society of Future Women in Medicine
description: The Society of Future Women in Medicine unites women pursuing healthcare professions. The organization provides support and advice from upperclassman including navigating classes, testing, application processes, MCAT/GRE prep and study sessions. Each semester we host speakers, undergraduate research workshop and resume workshop alongside service opportunities.
summary: The mission of the Society of Future Women in Medicine is to unite women across health care professions so that they may form meaningful connections and deepen their understanding of the medical field through interdisciplinary work.

name: The Real Estate Club
description: The Real Estate Club is an exciting way for students to become involved in a student-led organization. Whether you are interested in learning about Real Estate, or just looking to meet new people, we have a place for you. Our club provides opportunities for you to hear from Real Estate professionals, work with other club members, and develop your learning and leadership abilities.
summary: The Real Estate Club welcomes all students that are or may be interested in the area of Real Estate. We provide educational opportunities in all fields, including but not limited to commercial, industrial, and residential real estate.

name: National Student Speech Language and Hearing Association, UT Chapter
description: NSSLHA serves as the unifying student organization on campus for audiology and speech pathology students at the undergraduate and graduate level. Monthly meetings include news about the profession, coordination of community outreach events, planning of recreational activities, and professional development for both graduate and undergraduate students that include guest speakers.
summary: NSSLHA serves as the unifying student organization on campus for Audiology and Speech Pathology students at the undergraduate and graduate level.

name: Lambda Phi Epsilon International Fraternity, Inc.
description: Lambda Phi Epsilon International Fraternity, Inc. (LFE) is an international Asian interest fraternity but is not exclusive to strictly Asian members. Our mission is to guide men on a lifelong discovery of authenticity and personal growth. Our vision is a world where Lambda men live authentic, fulfilling lives and contribute through the pursuit of their noble purpose. We are committed to building leaders among men in our respective community.
summary: Lambda Phi Epsilon International Fraternity, Inc. (LFE) is an international Asian interest fraternity but is not exclusive to strictly Asian members. Our mission is to guide men on a lifelong discovery of authenticity and personal growth.

name: Health Law Society Executive Board
description: The mission of the Health Law Society at the University of Tennessee College of Law is to provide a venue for students interested in health law to discuss current health law issues and to generate greater awareness of those issues among the College of Law community. In addition, the Health Law Society seeks to link the College of Law community to learning opportunities regarding health law both on campus and in the community at large.
summary: The Health Law Executive Board exists to provide leadership to the College of Law's Health Law Society.

name: Office of Sorority and Fraternity Life
summary: Sororities and fraternities associated with the Office of Sorority and Fraternity Life

name: Campus Departments
summary: Campus departments tracking event attendance

name: Rocky Top Rollers
description: A community of all kind of different skaters from rhythm skaters to speed skaters to even ice skaters! We have weekly roller skating and monthly ice skating meetings. There are no club dues but you have to pay your own way into the venues.
summary: A group of campus skating enthusiasts who love roller skating, roller blading, and even ice skating occasionally!

name: Tennessee Capital Markets Society
description:
The Tennessee Capital Markets Society is a selective student-managed organization that focuses on moving students into high-profile jobs and providing members the skills required for a career in capital markets. Career paths include investment banking, private equity, consulting, sales and trading, and more. The society connects members with alumni and prepares members for the challenging interview process. Our core focus is financial analysis, modeling, integrated financial statement analysis, and deal assumptions.

In addition to the skills and industry exposure the organization provides, the organization fosters a network of like-minded, top students through social networking events and mentorship.
summary: TCMS is a selective finance organization focused on bringing together the top students at the university and helping place them in competitive positions post-grad through education and mentorship. The group focuses on high finance (IB, PE, VC, etc)

name: New Student Orientation
summary: Coming soon!

name: Phi Alpha Delta
description: The purpose of this Fraternity shall be to form a strong bond uniting students and teachers of the law with members of the Bench and Bar in a fraternal fellowship designed to advance the ideals of liberty and equal justice under law; to stimulate excellence in scholarship; to inspire the virtues of compassion and courage; to foster integrity and professional competence; to promote the welfare of its members; and to encourage their moral, intellectual, and cultural advancement; so that each member may enjoy a lifetime of honorable professional and public service.
summary: Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity strives to be the premier legal professional organization with a diverse membership that cultivates leaders and serves as a voice for the role of legal professionalism in our society.

name: UTK Veganteers
summary: We are the Veganteers at UTK, a community-focused veg club built around providing an exciting, vitalizing space to celebrate all things vegan and welcome students of all backgrounds.

name: Powerlifting Club at UT Knoxville
description:
The Powerlifting Club at UT Knoxville will be centered around developing people's strength, lifting techniques, and overall physical well being. The club will feature powerlifting programming and training regimes, which can be tailored to fit individual schedule's and abilities. The powerlifting training is available to be used by people of all strength levels. All people are welcome, regardless of experience. Whether someone is ready for a competition, or is simply picking up a barbell for the first time, everyone will be treated with gratitude and respect. 

The club will aim to meet individual goals. We will provide information on registering for powerlifting meets if one would like to compete. The meets are friendly and welcoming of all skill levels. If one simply wants to get into shape or train for fun, then they will be provided with the knowledge and support to meet their personal goals. 

The Powerlifting Club at UT Knoxville will, most of all, aim to provide a supportive, friendly, and loving community for those who attend. It is an opportunity to meet new people who you wouldn't normally meet. The only social overlap here is lifting, which provides opportunities to expand social networks. The club community will encourage kindness and catering towards others, including uplifting others during club meets. We will also plan to get together outside of the gym, simply to get to know one another and be a community.
summary: An organization committed to building strength and lifting technique centered around powerlifting exercises. Just like the sport of powerlifting itself, this club welcomes all people of all skill levels to be a part of a community.

name: Federalist Society
description:
• Law schools and the legal profession are currently strongly dominated by a form of orthodox liberal ideology which advocates a centralized and uniform society. While some members of the academic community have dissented from these views, by and large, they are taught simultaneously with (and indeed as if they were) the law.
  • The Federalist Society for Law and Public Policy Studies is a group of conservatives and libertarians interested in the current state of the legal order. It is founded on the principles that the state exists to preserve freedom, that the separation of governmental powers is central to our Constitution, and that it is emphatically the province and duty of the judiciary to say what the law is, not what it should be. The Society seeks both to promote an awareness of these principles and to further their application through its activities.
  • This entails reordering priorities within the legal system to place a premium on individual liberty, traditional values, and the rule of law. It also requires restoring the recognition of the importance of these norms among lawyers, judges, law students, and professors. In working to achieve these goals, the Society has created a conservative and libertarian intellectual network that extends to all levels of the legal community.
summary: A national legal society with branches in every law school focused on the current state of the legal order, the principles of the founding fathers, and the preservation of an originalist approach to the Constitution.

name: 4FirstGen
description: The mission of this organization is to encourage and celebrate first generation students who are taking the first step to success and to allow an organization for opportunity, accomplishment, and support for those students who are striving for a higher standard of education and achievement. This organization will also help fellow first generation students continue the challenge of attaining a four-year degree at the University of Tennessee and to provide necessary resources and support to assist and aid in their four-year college journey. We will also provide first generation role models and mentorship to first generation students attending the University of Tennessee.
summary: An organization to encourage and celebrate first-generation students who are taking the first step to success and allow an organization for opportunity, accomplishment, and support for those students who are striving for a higher standard of education.

name: Knights of the Hill
description:
Knights of the Hill is a club split into a number of spheres or "houses," because our general interest is an appreciation of the Fantasy Genre, but so much falls under the genre, we felt it important to describe each area of the club individually.  The Tabletop Sphere is the easiest to explain, because it largely includes playing Dungeons and Dragons and/or Pathfinder--this may include Character Creation Workshops or actually playing through a campaign.  Additionally, the Tabletop Sphere might involve playing Magic the Gathering, or a variety of board games.

The Film and Television Sphere simply involves watching fantasy TV shows and movies.  Examples include watching The Lord of the Rings or the Princess Bride.

The Literature and Lore Sphere involves the a Book Club element (which we intend to only host between semesters), creative writing, world-building, and discussions of the lore present within pre-established fantasy worlds (For example, discussing what happened to the Dwemer within Tamriel, or discussions of Arthurian Legend)

And finally, we hope to eventually break into Live Action Role Playing, where we basically pretend to exist within a fantasy universe and live out our escapism fantasies!

The point of having all of these spheres is to fully enjoy the fantasy genre.  Limiting ourselves to any one section sunders us from the full enjoyment of such a great genre.

Feel free to join our Discord server!  https://discord.gg/JbPzvDsTn6
summary: Knights of the Hill is a Fantasy and Tabletop Gaming club.

name: Alpha Sigma Kappa- Women in Technical Studies
description: The mission of the Alpha Sigma Kappa is to promote friendship, academic achievement, unity, and philanthropy. The organization is committed to achieving our academic goals and promoting women and other gender identities in technical fields, through leadership, friendship, and support.
summary: Alpha Sigma Kappa - Women in Technical Studies, is a social sorority for women and gender minorities pursuing technical fields.

name: American Society of Landscape Architects
description: This organization is a student chapter of the national American Society for Landscape Architects (ASLA). Founded in 1899, the American Society of Landscape Architects (ASLA) is the professional association for landscape architects in the United States, representing more than 15,000 members. Landscape architects lead the planning, design, and stewardship of healthy, equitable, safe, and resilient environments. The Society's mission is to advance landscape architecture through advocacy, communication, education, and fellowship. Our chapter represents students pursuing a Masters in Landscape Architecture. We are based in the College of Architecture and Design with strong connections to the Herbert College of Agriculture through the department of Plant Sciences and the Sustainable Landscape Design concentration.
summary: We are interdisciplinary design students pursuing Masters in Landscape Architecture, organized to promote student advocacy, communication, education, and fellowship.

name: Alpha Alpha Alpha Honor Society
description:
On May 17, 2021, the University of Tennessee, Knoxville was granted its charter to establish the Gamma Upsilon Chapter of Alpha Alpha Alpha Honors Society. Tri-Alpha , as it is known, is an honor society for first-generation college students. The purpose of the University of Tennessee Knoxville’s chapter is to recognize the academic achievements in first-generation college students, create enthusiasm for scholarship, to promote leadership, and to provide support networks for first-generation students within and across college chapters. The honor society is open to both undergraduate and graduate students, and may also include faculty and staff members, as well as honorary inductees.

 

Benefits

  • Opportunities to meet other first-generation scholars and join them in promoting academic excellence and creating a welcoming and supportive environment for all first-generation students.
  • Leadership opportunities with the Tri-Alpha chapter at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville
  • Outward recognition of personal accomplishments and a symbol of your hard work
  • Communicates to future employers, professionals, and graduate schools that you possess superior academic ability and commitment
  • Members receive a certificate and pin signifying membership. Membership is for life.
summary: The mission of the Tri-Alpha, Gamma Upsilon chapter, is to assist in the academic, social, and professional growth of first-generation college students at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville.

name: Theta Chi, Kappa Omicron Chapter
description:
Theta Chi is a social fraternity on UTK's campus that is recognized by IFC. We distinguish ourselves through our purpose and goals. The aim of this fraternity is to build long lasting brotherhood bonds, high scholarship, engage in the community, and support to our philanthropy, the USO. 

The primary goal of Theta Chi is to develop long lasting relationships among all members that extend across lifetimes. All members of Theta Chi are treated equally and we extend a helping hand to all brothers who seek it.

We also have a history and high regard for academic scholarship among our members. We believe in academics first. To be a successful member, you must first be a successful student.
summary: We are the Kappa Omicron Chapter of Theta Chi. We believe in the building of resolute men, and extending an assisting hand to all who need it. The main goal of our fraternity is to build strong and lasting brotherhood bonds amongst members.

name: The Camp Koinonia Organization
summary: Camp Koinonia is a week-long, overnight camp experience for youth and young adults with various disabilities, aged 7 through high school graduation. This program is completely student driven with roughly 100 UT students staffing the camp.

name: The Korean Graduate Student Association
description:
The Korean Graduate Student Association aims to encourage UT students to understand Korean culture and to get job opportunities in South Korea. Also, we shall inform the cherished Tennessee culture to South Korea so that let them know more about Tennessee and Knoxville.   

KGSA members may have the opportunity to meet Korean faculty members and alumni who have worked in various fields and to get some advice for developing academic and professional careers. Introducing the attractiveness of Tennessee to South Korea is also an important mission to get more positive attention from potential international students.
summary: The Korean Graduate Student Association is established to provide a path for UT students with common interests to broaden the awareness and understanding of the Korean culture and deliver the Tennessee culture to Korea.

name: Phi Delta Epsilon
description:
Phi Delta Epsilon is a medical fraternity with the values of philanthropy, deity, and equity. The mission of this organization is to create physicians of integrity with lifelong commitment to the aforementioned guiding principles. The vision of this organization is to develop physicians who positively impact the world, inspire others to greatness, and lead by example.

For more information about the TN Alpha Chapter of Phi Delta Epsilon, please visit our Instagram. @phide.utk
summary: Phi Delta Epsilon is a medical fraternity with the values of philanthropy, deity, and equity. The mission of this organization is to create physicians of integrity with lifelong commitment to the aforementioned guiding principles.

name: PERIOD. @ University of Tennessee, Knoxville
summary: PERIOD. @ UTK is a chapter of the national organization PERIOD. This chapter and the national organization are working to end period poverty and stigma through service, education, and advocacy.

name: Cook Grand Challenge Honors Program
description: These opportunities will prepare our students to become highly competitive engineering graduates and be more successful in addressing the challenges of the twenty-first century, such as securing cyberspace, engineering better medicines, and providing access to clean water.
summary: The purpose of the Cook Grand Challenge Honors Program is to provide greater intellectual challenges and broader educational experiences for our undergraduate students.

name: Engineering Fundamentals Program
description: UT is home to the Jerry E. Stoneking engage™️ Engineering Fundamentals program, one of the most innovative of its kind in the nation. It takes a leading-edge, success-oriented approach to freshman engineering education, placing problem solving through collaboration as a priority.
summary: In the success-oriented environment of engage™️, students learn by interacting with others to create solutions to engineering problems.

name: Omega Phi Alpha
summary: The purpose and goals of this sorority shall be to assemble its members in the fellowship of Omega Phi Alpha, to develop friendship, leadership, and cooperation by promoting service to the university community, to the community-at-large.

name: Pickleball Club
description:
University of Tennessee Pickleball Club

Both Competitive and Social options available!

We meet at the TRECs courts
summary: Open play pickleball for players of any level with competitive play opportunities.

name: The Fashion Society
description: The purpose of The Fashion Society is to unite a community of like-minded, fashion-driven individuals who are eager to learn more about creating clothing, styling outfits, and/or careers in the fashion industry. In addition to providing guidance and knowledge for creating fashion pieces, The Fashion Society is dedicated to serving the community and members by organizing events, activities, workshops, and fundraisers that involve fashion.
summary: The Fashion Society is dedicated to creating a community of fashion-driven individuals to create, inspire, and learn together.

name: Magic: The Gathering Club
description: Magic the Gathering Club UTK is dedicated to the TCG Magic the Gathering. We play various formats including pauper, modern, and mostly Edh. Players with any level of experience and proxy cards are welcome! We also host prized tournaments during the semester. Join our Discord: https://discord.gg/3bhxmkxG6C.
summary: A collective student recreation group for students who want to test decks, play in tournaments and trade painted cardboard. We are welcoming to every student no matter the experience. Come by and play the biggest trading card game now.

name: Professional Sales Club
description: Open to students from all majors and colleges, the Professional Sales Club (PSC) empowers the next generation of diverse sales talent through career development with corporate partners, sales-specific training session series, professional development opportunities, exclusive recruiting and networking events, and high-value professional engagement interactions with an emphasis on creating a culture that prepares students for success and placement in sales careers.
summary: Empowering the next generation of diverse sales talent through career/professional development and high-value professional engagement with an emphasis on creating a culture that prepares students for success and placement in professional sales careers.

name: Men’s Club Basketball at UT
description:
A competitive and fun environment for players to continue their basketball career at the collegiate level. The main purpose is to compete for a National Championship while embodying a recreational atmosphere.

While improving as basketball players, one of the club’s focal purposes is to improve character development, teaching young college students all the necessary skills to be a great basketball player as well as a good character both on and off the court.
summary: A competitive and fun environment for players to continue their basketball career at the collegiate level. The main purpose is to compete for a National Championship while embodying a recreational atmosphere.

name: Skydiving Club
description: The purpose of this club is to give everyone in the UTK community a chance to experience the thrill of jumping out of perfectly good airplanes and to provide a forum for people interested in the sport to share personal experiences and enjoy the bonds that are forged in the skydiving community. This club will work to serve the needs of both experienced skydivers and those interested in or new to parachuting. We strive to help anyone interested in skydiving both to learn more about it and to find safe and congenial ways to participate in aspects of the sport.
summary: This club will work to serve the needs of both experienced skydivers and those interested in or new to parachuting. We strive to help anyone interested in skydiving both to learn more about it and to find safe and congenial ways to participate in aspects

name: Collegiate DECA at the University of Tennessee
description:
DECA is so much more than just a "business club." Through DECA, you'll have the opportunity to grow as a leader, compete, and travel, all while being a part of a truly "DECA-cated" community!

EST. 2022
summary: DECA aims to prepare emerging leaders and entrepreneurs in marketing, finance, hospitality and management in high schools and colleges around the globe.

name: Delta Upsilon
description: Delta Upsilon is the world’s oldest non-secret, non-hazing fraternity. We believe that if we want our brothers to live our mission of Building Better Men and by our founding values and principles, why would we keep any of that a secret? In Delta Upsilon, you are able to tell the world exactly what your membership means, then prove it through your actions.
summary: The Tennessee Alpha Chapter of Delta Upsilon Fraternity

name: One Health Student Coalition at UTK
description:
The One Health Student Coalition (OHSC) is open to UT students of all levels and disciplines. It unites common health goals for humans, animals, and the environment to promote health for all life on earth! The OHSC typically meets Wednesday @6:30pm; please complete this short form for info about upcoming meetings. 

Wondering if you should join? The OHSC might be for you if you’re interested in:

  • Interdisciplinary learning
    One Health encompasses human, animal, plant, and environment health and offers learning opportunities outside your chosen field.
  • Leadership and networking
    Collaborate with peers from other disciplines, connect with professors and professionals working on One Health issues, and build leadership skills through projects and events.
  • Collaboration and creativity
    One Health is all about finding innovative solutions to complex challenges and is a great way to connect with a broad range of peers you may not meet otherwise.
  • Global citizenship
    Join the movement tackling complex, global challenges at a local level.

Join the movement and build bridges across disciplines to promote health and steward our Earth well. Change starts with you!
summary: The One Health Student Coalition seeks to promote a One Health approach for community involvement in all areas of health and enhance skills in leadership and communication among undergraduate and graduate students in a multidisciplinary setting.

name: Tennessee Undergraduate Consulting Club
description: TUCC is a student-led organization at the University of Tennessee dedicated to empowering undergraduate students with the skills, experience, and connections necessary to succeed in the consulting industry. Our mission is to bridge the gap between classroom learning and real-world consulting by offering hands-on project opportunities, case competitions, skill-building workshops, and professional networking events. TUCC fosters a collaborative environment where members can develop critical thinking, problem-solving, and client-facing skills through involvement in real consulting projects and exposure to industry professionals and are given internship or job opportunities.
summary: TUCC is a student-led consulting organization that helps students develop problem-solving, analytical, and business strategy skills through real-world projects and professional development opportunities while connecting them with top consulting firms.

name: Social Work PhD Student Organization
description:
The purpose of the Social Work PhD Student Organization (SWPHDSO) is to serve as a student organization specifically for social work PhD students.

The mission of the organization will be to provide PhD students with a group to organize student led activities and educate students on aspects of the science of social work.

Membership is open to all enrolled PhD students who have not graduated.
summary: The purpose of the Social Work PhD Student Organization (SWPHDSO) is to serve as a student organization specifically for social work PhD students.

name: Duck Hunt UTK
description:
We are a club based around hiding rubber ducks in whatever hidden spots we can find around campus for other club members to find. we operate out of a discord server which can be found through the university's discord hub, or by going to the external website link. This club has no requirements or commitments and you can be as casual or competitive as you want with hunting the hidden ducks. You are also free to hide ducks as you wish as long as you are not hiding them in exceedingly dangerous or otherwise inaccessible locations. We will try to have weekly meetings, but they are not required and will only serve as a time for event planning and meeting fellow hunters. Do note that we use Discord for all activities and therefore the Discord server (linked as External Website) is required to participate as other services do not work for how we operate.

Overall we want this club to be an activity to help relax between classes, have some fun, encourage campus exploration, and provide a good excuse to get some exercise. Good luck, and happy hunting!
summary: An organization that hides rubber ducks around UT campus for other students to find, in order to promote staying active, exploring campus, and just overall having some fun between classes and schoolwork.

name: Photography Club
description: Photography Club is a community where photographers from beginner to professional can come to make friends with other photographers, share their work, and learn together.
summary: A creative outlet and learning community for photographers.

name: Smokey's Cleanup Crew
description:
The mission of Smokey’s Cleanup Crew is to create an inclusive environment for all students of UTK interested in getting involved and giving back to the community. Smokey’s Cleanup Crew provides an opportunity for members to take part in the beautification of the UTK campus and the surrounding Knoxville areas while completing community service hours if needed or desired.

 

Our goal is to help keep a litter free campus and encourage community beautification by building relations with the people of Knoxville and surrounding areas. Also, Smokey’s Cleanup Crew strives to increase the number of students who will receive a service medallion upon graduation. Our core values include Service, Inclusion, and Community.
summary: The mission of Smokey’s Cleanup Crew is to create an inclusive environment for all students interested in and giving back to the community. Smokey’s Cleanup Crew provides an opportunity for members to take part in the bi-weekly cleanups of the campus.

name: Sustainable Business Organization at the University of Tennessee
summary: We work to change the way UT does business in a more environmentally friendly way. With minimal sustainable initiatives in place at UT, we want to be a student-led organization whose goal is to raise student concern on the topic and establish change.

name: First Generation Initiatives
description:
The purpose of First-Generation Initiatives is to:

· Connect first-generation scholars to campus resources, organizations, departments and professionals;

· Provide a sense of community, belonging, and support for first-generation students;

· Identify, acknowledge, and celebrate a shared identity and experiences among first-generation students;and

· Enhance the first-generation experience through four pillars of engagement:

o Academic and career engagement

o Social and cultural engagement

o Family engagement

o Campus advocacy and engagement

 

In order to achieve our mission, the First-Generation Initiatives staff commit to:

· Provide strategic direction and assessment of UTK’s retention and persistence efforts to support first-generation, low-income, and racial and ethnic minority students;

· Deliver culturally sensitive and responsive academic and social support and engagement;

· Enhance undergraduate students’ communication skills to engage in meaningful conversation and intercultural and intergroup dialogue;

· Promote collaboration with campus and community partners to close the opportunity and achievement gap of underrepresented students;

· Acknowledge, embrace, explore and celebrate differences
summary: First-Generation Initiatives creates policies and programs that promote academic excellence, social engagement, and inclusion for all students with an emphasis on meeting the needs of students who are first in their families to pursue a college degree.

name: Big Orange Pantry
description: Big Orange Pantry is our on-campus food pantry that supports food insecurity challenges faced by students, faculty, and staff. The Big Orange Pantry is a program through the Office of the Dean of Students.
summary: Big Orange Pantry is our on-campus food pantry that supports food insecurity challenges faced by students, faculty, and staff. The Big Orange Pantry is a program through the Office of the Dean of Students.

name: Haslam College of Business
description: Register for events happening in the Haslam College of Business. Events are aimed at helping students connect with faculty and staff, learn about opportunities, and make the most of your academic endeavors in the Haslam College.
summary: Register for events happening in the Haslam College of Business. Events are aimed at helping students connect with faculty and staff, learn about opportunities, and make the most of your academic endeavors in the Haslam College.

name: Psi Society
description: Psi Society was created as an opportunity for peer education on topics that impact Greek organizations. Psi Society strives to create a healthy and safe community through programming and education. The programs offered are created based on the needs of the Greek community and informed by student voice and perspective.
summary: Psi society is a group of sorority and fraternity leaders who educate their peers on topics related to leadership, health, and safety.

name: Black Students for Artistic Expression
description: BAE (Black Students for Artistic Expression) is a home to creatives of all kinds, dedicated to uplifting the Black community through art in all its forms—fashion, poetry, music, dance, and more. Our mission is to create spaces where Black artistry can thrive and inspire. We host open mic nights, art showcases, workshops, guest speakers, and more! Join us to celebrate, create, and connect—check out our events tab for more!
summary: The purpose and aim of Black Students for Artistic Expression is to create and promote the creativity of the Black community on the University of Tennessee campus. This includes art forms surrounding fashion, poetry, song, dance, music, and media.

name: Rural Outreach for Prospective Engineering Students
description: ROPES intended goals are to increase the number of incoming engineering students, amplify UTK’s recruitment efforts, and expand the available knowledge of engineering within Knoxville’s rural communities. The main objective of the organization is to engage current students with volunteer opportunities while also providing them opportunities to further their presentation skills and summarize their time at UTK. ROPES upholds and supports the Volunteer Creed, “One that beareth a torch shadoweth oneself to give light to others,” based on its foundational objective to inform and connect with the insufficiently reached rural community. Current students will learn how to condense their time at UTK, as well as make new connections, lead STEM activities during student roundups, and further practice public speaking during their time in the organization. After all, we aim to show potential students the ropes before they get to college!
summary: ROPES is an organization within the Tickle College of Engineering (TCE) whose purpose is to introduce rural community members to the University of Tennessee, Knoxville (UTK)’s engineering program through student outreach.

name: Ewing Gallery of Art + Architecture
description:
