The Ewing Gallery of Art + Architecture is located on the first floor of the Art + Architecture Building. We
are open 6 days a week with extended evening hours on Thursdays and afternoon hours on Sundays. All
exhibitions, receptions, and associated lectures are free and open to the public.
summary:
UT’s free on-campus art gallery that presents culturally thought-provoking, educational, and
engaging exhibitions, to spark dialogue and consider diverse perspectives.

name: Biosystems engineering and soil science graduate students
description: The mission of BESS Grads is to strengthen social and professional connections for graduate students in the Department of Biosystems Engineering and Soil Science in a welcoming and inclusive space. This includes developing relationships among graduate students, disseminating information about university opportunities, and other activities as desired by the membership.
summary: Social and professional development organization for BESS grad students

name: Tennessee Agricultural Communicators of Tomorrow
description: The Mission of the Tennessee Agricultural Communicators of Tomorrow is to build relationships among agricultural communication professionals and college students and faculty, to provide professional and academic development for members and to promote agriculture through communications efforts.
summary: The vision of TACT is foster developing and strengthening agricultural communication students through professional growth opportunities and educational programs.

name: Cornhole UTK
description:
Welcome! We're glad you found us. Cornhole UTK is an organization that focuses on both a recreational and competitive aspect of cornhole. In addition to weekly practices, we also have a competitive team that travels to local as well as national ACL tournaments. Our mission at Cornhole UTK is to foster a welcoming community of cornhole players at all skill levels. Our goal isn't to just create skilled players, but to also form a close-knit group of students who can feel a sense of belonging when playing cornhole together. So, no matter how much or how little cornhole experience you have, we can assure you that you will feel at home with us at Cornhole UTK! Make sure to join our group using the link below for information and updates. 

GroupMe Link: https://groupme.com/join_group/89406709/ZQjkNgP1
summary: Cornhole UTK is an organization that focuses on both a recreational and competitive aspect of cornhole. In addition to weekly practices, we also have a competitive team that travels to local as well as national ACL tournaments.

name: Latino Law Student Association
description: The Mission Statement of the Latino/a Law Student Association (“LLSA”) is to emphasize issues of concern to the Latino/a community within the legal profession and academic community. LLSA aims to provide support and resources to empower its members to address the concerns facing the community.
summary: Raise awareness and prepare members to face issues related to Latino/a’s participation in the judicial system; Promote career and networking opportunities for members within the legal profession; and Coordinate activities within the university community

name: Pokémon Go Club
summary: The purpose of the Pokémon Go Club is to bring all UTK Pokémon Go players on campus to make and foster friendships, participate in and benefit from in-game events, and promote an active lifestyle.

name: Sources of Strength @ UTK
description:
Sources of Strength @ The University of Tennessee is proud to be working under the Contact Care Crisis Hotline in East Tennessee. We are still a student run University Organization, but with support from the Crisis Hotline, we are able to do more, and create more change. 

 

Sources of Strength is a strength-based, comprehensive wellness program that focuses on suicide prevention but impacts other issues such as substance abuse and violence. The program is based on a relational connections model that uses teams of Peer Leaders mentored by Adult Advisors to change peer social norms about help seeking and encourages students to individually assess and develop strengths in their life.

Sources of Strength is most often implemented as a school-based program in middle school, high school, or college. However Sources of Strength is also often used in community, faith-based, and cultural settings. It promotes and focuses on connectivity, school bonding, peer-adult partnerships, and help seeking behaviors.

Trained peer leaders use their network of friends to:

  • Have one-on-one conversations.
  • Develop a Hope, Help, Strength messages using local faces and voices.
  • Present peer-to-peer presentations.

Mission: Our mission is to provide the highest quality evidence-based prevention for suicide, violence, bullying and substance abuse by training, supporting, and empowering both peer leaders and caring adults to impact their world through the power of connection, hope, help and strength.

Vision: We believe that many strengths are more powerful than one, and our united goal is to activate and mobilize these strengths in ways that positively change individuals and communities.

The Sources of Strength Wheel is composed of eight categories that we can draw strength from: Family Support, Positive Friends, Mentors, Healthy Activities, Generosity, Spirituality, Physical Health, and Mental Health.
summary:
Sources of Strength is a universal suicide prevention program designed to build socioecological protective influences around youth and to reduce the likelihood that vulnerable youth/young adults will become suicidal
-
The Utk chapter was Est. 9/28/22

name: Student Association of Project Management
summary: The purpose is to further understand the principles and practices of project management in any field, to network and connect with students and professionals to further education or future develop opportunities in the project management division.

name: All-Girl Club Cheerleading
description: The University of Tennessee All-Girl Cheerleading Team, a club organization, dedicates the school year to preparing for the NCA College Nationals in Daytona Beach, Florida. Established in November 2021, the team's inaugural season in 2023-24 resulted in a 3rd place finish in the nation for the season- jumping 14 placements from the first season! Our primary goal is to provide a platform for female cheerleaders to pursue collegiate-level cheerleading. Additionally, this experience offers members the chance to connect with fellow UTK students who share this same passion.
summary: To foster and encourage the sport of cheerleading in the All-Girl division. Furthermore, to represent the University of Tennessee in the All-Girl division at competitions nationally and locally.

name: Delta Chi
description: Delta Chi believes in developing the whole person and helping our men become better people, not just better Delta Chis, who are committed to making an impact on the world around them. Delta Chi values are enhanced with action verbs that require our members to demonstrate their ability to believe and Promote Friendship, Develop Character, Advance Justice, and Assist in the Acquisition of a Sound Education. These values are a true call to action that inspire and lead our members to live each day with integrity.
summary: Since 1890, Delta Chi has inspired thousands of men to do the right thing and live our values of Promoting Friendship, Developing Character, Advancing Justice and Assist in the acquisition of a sound Education.

name: PsychVOLogy
description:
For updates, please follow our Instagram page @psychvology and join our GroupMe https://groupme.com/join_group/100914506/rrg4GtKm

Psychology is one of the biggest majors on campus, but historically, there's been no organization for students interested in psychology to socialize and collaborate with others. Hence, the birth of PsychVOLogy! Our club aims to bring together students with a shared interest in psychology without limits of GPA or academic major. Club membership includes:

  • Monthly meetings
  • Multiple social/educational activities per semester
    • Movie Nights with Post-Discussion in Hodges Library Auditorium
    • Fundraising Nights
    • Panels on various topics (e.g., graduate school, careers with a psychology bachelor's degree, etc.)
    • Painting the Rock
    • Pizza socials
    • And many, many more!
  • Leadership and service opportunities
    • Psi Chi International Honors Society (for Psychology majors/minors)
    • Executive Board positions
    • Volunteer opportunities
summary: We provide a community for students with an interest in psychology by organizing social activities, learning experiences, and career development opportunities.

name: Women in Engineering Program
description: The program facilitates the full participation of women engineering students in a welcoming and empowering environment. The Women in Engineering Program provides women with a supportive network and engaging academic and professional programs to explore the possibilities within engineering.
summary: The Women in Engineering Program provides young girls and women with connections to resources, support, mentorship, and engaging programs designed for them to discover the passions that will guide their academic and professional careers.

name: The History Club
summary:
The History Club aims to promote community within the history department of UT,
to create a space to discuss historical topics and opportunities within the field, and
to connect students with professionals and faculty within the discipline.

name: Transfer Student Association
description: The mission of the Transfer Club is to make transfers feel welcome and give them a chance to connect with other students in similar positions. We strive to help students get involved on campus and within the Knoxville community. Transferring to a new school can be overwhelming so we want to help with some of that stress by building a community of students in the same position. We will provide events, meetings, and support to help transfer students love UTK and call it home sweet home! Hopefully this will help you learn and love Knoxville and this university as much as we do!
summary: The mission of the Transfer Club is to make transfers feel welcome and give them a chance to connect with other students in similar positions. We provide events, meetings, and support to help transfer students love UTK and call it home sweet home!

name: Sri Lankan Students' Association at UTK
description: SLSA aims to promote and raise awareness of Sri Lankan culture at the University of Tennessee Knoxville through representing Sri Lankan culture and community at social and multicultural activities of the campus.
summary: The organization will carry out its mission of supporting all students of Sri Lankan origin at UT Knoxville by providing a social network and building a sense of community among new and existing students  of Sri Lankan origin.

name: Women in Accounting
description:
The Women in Accounting (WIA) Club is a supportive, low-commitment community for accounting majors and collaterals of all years. It is focused on building confidence, fostering valuable connections, and providing professional growth opportunities. Our mission is to empower women in the accounting field by creating an inclusive space for learning, networking, and personal development.

Through informal meetings, speaker events, and social gatherings, we help members build meaningful relationships with female peers, mentors, and professionals from private and public firms. Older WIA members play a vital role by offering guidance, tutoring, and peer mentorship, ensuring that all members feel supported as they navigate their academic and career journeys.

Our emphasis on comfort, confidence, and self-value in all aspects of life sets us apart. WIA is dedicated to creating an environment where you can gain knowledge, expand your community, and receive professional support—all while having fun. Our club also offers opportunities for leadership roles, strengthening relationships within Haslam, and connecting with professors, students, and other organizations.

Join us to grow your network, gain valuable insights into the accounting profession, and build lifelong connections with a supportive group of women!
summary: Women in Accounting is an organization committed to empowering and connecting students in accounting with other female students, women in the accounting profession, and anyone else interested in helping women succeed in the business world.

name: Society of Women Engineers Graduate Group
summary: The mission of UT GradSWE is to provide graduate students who wish to participate in the Society of Women Engineers at the local level the support, resources, information, and financial support to do so.

name: American Choral Director's Association
summary: An organization for students who are aspiring choral directors.

name: Collegiate National Association for Music Education
description: CNAfME is an organization that helps prospective music educators with support for student teaching, hosting informative seminars in different educational areas, and helping fund and organize student trips to events such as ETSBOA, ETVA, and TMEA in Nashville. If you want to make connections with other music educators in the area, get great information and support through a challenging degree area, CNAfME is the place!
summary: A professional organization unifying music education students by hosting professional development seminars and coordinating visits to conferences around the US.

name: Nigerian Students’ Association at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville
description:
Welcome to the official website of the Nigerian Students' Association at the University of Tennessee Knoxville (NSA-UTK). We are thrilled to have this opportunity to share with you the essence and vibrant activities of our association.

At NSA-UTK, our primary goal is to foster a strong sense of community among Nigerian students at the University of Tennessee Knoxville while promoting Nigerian culture, diversity, and academic excellence. We strive to create a welcoming environment that allows students to connect, support one another, and celebrate our shared heritage.

Our association serves as a platform for Nigerian students to engage in various cultural, educational, and social activities. Through these initiatives, we aim to:

  1. Create a Community: We strive to create a strong sense of community among Nigerian students and friends of Nigeria at UTK. NSA-UTK serves as a platform for fostering connections, providing support, and building lasting friendships. We organize social gatherings, networking events, and mentorship programs to ensure that every member feels welcomed and valued.

  2. Promoting Nigerian Culture and Tradition: We take immense pride in showcasing the beauty and richness of Nigerian culture and tradition. Through cultural showcases, music and dance performances, art exhibitions, and food festivals, NSA-UTK aims to promote cultural awareness and appreciation not only within UTK but also throughout the wider U.S. community.

  3. Establishing Links and Collaboration: NSA-UTK acts as a bridge between Nigerian students in the U.S. and Nigeria. We facilitate networking opportunities, exchange programs, and collaborations with Nigerian student associations in other universities. By fostering these connections, we aim to strengthen ties between Nigerian students abroad and their home country, promoting cultural exchange, academic collaborations, and professional development.

  4. Increasing Awareness of Educational Opportunities: NSA-UTK seeks to inform students in Nigeria about the educational opportunities available at the University of Tennessee. Through partnerships with Nigerian educational institutions, outreach programs, and informational sessions, we aim to create awareness about UTK's academic programs, scholarships, and resources. Our goal is to encourage Nigerian students to consider UTK as a destination for higher education.

  5. Promoting Member Welfare: The well-being of our members is of utmost importance to us. NSA-UTK offers a support system that addresses the unique needs of Nigerian students. We provide resources, guidance, and mentorship to help our members navigate academic, cultural, and personal challenges. Additionally, we organize wellness and fun activities, workshops to promote the overall well-being within our community.

By joining NSA-UTK, you become part of a vibrant and supportive community that celebrates Nigerian culture, fosters personal and professional growth, and offers a home away from home. We encourage all Nigerian students at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, as well as friends of Nigeria, to get involved, participate in our events, and contribute to our shared goals.

Please explore our website to learn more about our upcoming events, initiatives, and ways to get involved. We look forward to meeting you and creating unforgettable experiences together at University of Tennessee Knoxville. If you have any questions or need further information, please don't hesitate to reach out to us.

 Remember! Go Vols! and Naija no dey carry last!
summary: Welcome to the Nigerian Students' Association at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville (NSA-UTK). We are a vibrant community dedicated Embracing Nigerian culture, fostering connections, promoting education, and supporting our members' welfare. Join us!

name: Association of Latino Professionals for America
description: The Association of Latino Professionals for America hopes to establish ourselves to be the largest non-profit student organization at the Haslam Business School at the University of Tennessee. The Association of Latino Professionals for America envisions bringing together students from different backgrounds with the purpose of preparing them for the world of business. We seek to empower our members through our five core pillars so that our current members continue to become today's leaders and tomorrow's executives.
summary: The Association of Latino Professionals for America hopes to bring together students shared a goal of providing the resources to excel as hispanic business professionals and mold generations of successful leaders.

name: Music Industry Club
description:
About Us

The Music Industry Club (MIC) is a student-led professional development organization at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, focused on preparing students for careers across the music industry. MIC provides hands-on experience, industry access, and career education for students interested in both the behind-the-scenes business side and the performer side of music.

Through industry speaker panels, skill-based workshops, networking opportunities, live event production, and artist development initiatives, MIC equips members with the practical knowledge and real-world experience needed to succeed in today’s music industry. Our programming spans areas such as live events, talent booking, artist management, marketing, production, and performance.

 

Our Values

Cultivating Creativity
Creativity is essential not only for artists, but for every music industry professional. MIC encourages creative problem-solving, innovative thinking, and adaptability in both artistic and business-focused roles.

Fostering Professional Growth
MIC is committed to helping members continuously develop as individuals, creators, and professionals. Through real responsibilities and leadership opportunities, students gain the skills and confidence needed to become future industry leaders.

Making the Music Happen
At its core, the music industry exists to connect artists with audiences. MIC supports this mission by giving students direct involvement in live music, artist support, and event execution—bridging the gap between creativity and industry operations.

 

How to Join

We recruit new members at the beginning of each semester. During recruitment, a link to sign up for an interview will be available in our Instagram bio. We strongly encourage prospective members to attend our recruitment events, known as MIC Week, which takes place during the first few weeks of the semester. These events are a great way to learn more about the club, meet current members, and get involved. Follow our Instagram (@micutk) for recruitment updates, event announcements, and important deadlines.

 

Applications are now open! Apply using the link:

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScZmaKILXX9zB-27YS0uHaKOhia7erpIjfA40JQcgbSO5hOEQ/viewform?usp=sharing&ouid=112363967107897498249
summary: The Music Industry Club (MIC) is a student-led professional development organization that prepares students for careers across the music industry through hands-on experience, industry education, and real-world opportunities.

name: National Society of Black Women In Medicine
description: The National Society of Black Women in Medicine (NSBWM) is a multi-disciplined organization that works to increase the recruitment and retention of black women pursuing broad careers in medicine, while simultaneously promoting the advancement of those already established in these fields. NSBWM's vision is to support current and future healthcare professionals and scientists in efforts to combat educational and professional racial inequities by promoting attainability through educational resources, unity, and mentorship.
summary: The National Society of Black Women In Medicine is dedicated to increasing the retention of underrepresented minority women pursuing broad careers in healthcare and basic science.

name: The Information Sciences Club
description:
The Information Sciences Club provides students with a community for professional growth through networking, portfolio development, and sharing applications of information science with other entities within the University of Tennessee and Knoxville area.

Our club is open to all students enrolled at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville regardless of major or grade classification. We encourage all interested students to join our weekly meetings for an in-depth experience of what our club offers as well as to connect with officers for any questions, comments, or concerns. 

We look forward to having you!
summary: The Information Sciences Club provides students with a community for professional growth through networking, portfolio development, and sharing applications of information science with other entities within the University of Tennessee and Knoxville area.

name: Club Field Hockey
summary: The purpose of the Club Field Hockey Team is to achieve goals as individuals and a team, as well as to build a team of athletic and competitive members with varying levels of field hockey experience.

name: Naturalists' Club
description: The Naturalists' Club is an undergraduate led organization within the Ecology and Evolutionary Biology department. The purpose of the club is to educate students and locals about the natural world and to promote awareness of anthropogenic effects on the environment, by organizing events that involve immersive experiences in nature. To become an official member of the Naturalist Club, students must and have a 2.5 GPA attend two meetings and/or events. For more information and frequent updates, join the club GroupMe. https://groupme.com/join_group/92445428/64G4Q5BJ
summary: The Naturalist's Club is a social group that discusses interests in the natural sciences and coordinates events that involve immersive experiences in nature such as hiking trips and faculty-led nature walks.

name: Latino Business Student Association
description:
The mission of The Latino Business Student Association is:

  • To connect Latino business students for social, professional, and academic purposes for life at university and beyond.
  • To increase Latino student engagement within the Haslam College of Business.
  • To create a welcoming community within the Haslam College of Business for Latino students and students of all backgrounds.
  • To develop and prepare future business leaders.
  • To provide and promote leadership opportunities to members.

The mission will be met through various meetings, presentations, special guest speakers, networking events, and social gatherings throughout the academic year.
summary: The Latino Business Student Association is a professional and social organization that aims to provide a community for Latinx students in the Haslam College of Business through workshops, guest speakers, networking events and social gatherings.

name: Grannies Club
description:
Join our GroupMe!

Have you ever been called an old soul? Are you interested in crocheting, knitting, sewing and other fun and relaxing activities? Grannies club is where you can gather along and sew new relationships in our tight knit community! We will be holding monthly meetings for club activities and bringing guest grannies from partnered retirement homes to teach us their favorite skills. We will also be going to retirement homes for larger events and volunteering. This club is a great way to connect to the community in Knoxville, relax, and get some volunteer hours while building meaningful connections.
summary: Grannies club is a way to get in touch with your inner granny and the other grannies in the community. We are partnered with retirement homes around campus organize community events with a focus on life skills and fun activities.

name: Asian Business Club
description:
The Asian Business Club (ABC) is a group that brings together students who have an interest in Asian business, culture, and commerce. We aim to provide a platform for members to network, learn, and share experiences related to Asian business practices and markets. Open to all students, races, and majors who are interested in getting something from the club.

The ABC organizes events such as workshops, guest lectures, cultural celebrations, networking events, and conferences. Members may also have opportunities to participate in case competitions, study tours, and internships related to Asian business.

The focus of the ABC may vary depending on the region, country, or industry of interest. Some groups may focus on specific markets, such as China, India, Japan, or South Korea, while others may explore broader topics such as international trade, finance, or entrepreneurship.

Overall, the ABC can be a valuable resource for anyone looking to deepen their understanding of Asian business culture and practices, build a professional network, or explore career opportunities in Asia or with Asian businesses.

Even though we have 'Asian' and 'Business' in ABC, our organization is open to all students, races, and majors who are interested in getting something from the club.
summary: The Asian Business Club provides a platform for students interested in Asian business, culture, and commerce to network, learn, and share experiences, often organizing events and opportunities for members to participate in.

name: Vols for Veterans Student Organization
description: The Vols for Veterans Student Organization is open to all students at the University of Tennessee Winston College of Law. We seek to provide a community within the college of law for veterans, family members of veterans and other students that want to be involved. We want to assist veterans in our community both at the college of law but also in the greater Knoxville area. Our purpose is to provide legal assistance in coordination with licensed attorneys to Veterans and support local Veteran initiatives within the greater Knoxville region while simultaneously establishing a platform of knowledge for students within the UT College of Law interested in military service upon graduation.
summary: V4V is an organization within the UT College of Law that provides a community for law student veterans, family members of veterans and the entire law school community. This support is provided through shared activities both in and out of school.

name: Office of Title IX
description: The Office of Title IX provides a comprehensive community approach to the institution’s Title IX responsibility to create and maintain a safe and non-discriminatory learning, living, and working environment for all students free from Sexual Harassment (including Sexual Assault, Dating Violence, Domestic Violence and Stalking), Sexual Exploitation, and Retaliation. Our foundation is in the policy and procedures we follow. Our goal is to prevent Sexual Harassment, Sexual Exploitation, and Retaliation before they happen. Our promise is to provide support and appropriate supportive measures to individuals involved in the Title IX process. Our commitments to due process, campus safety, and encouraging reporting guide how we investigate and resolve reports. Our responsibility is to utilize the best available research, evidence-based practice, and our own campus trends in our prevention and response efforts.
summary: The Office of Title IX provides a comprehensive community approach to the institution’s Title IX responsibility to create and maintain a safe  environment for all students free from Sexual Harassment, Sexual Exploitation, and Retaliation.

name: IA Collaborative: IIDA+ASID Student Chapter at UTK
description: IA Collaborative will act as an extension of each student cohort to build community and support among them. IA Collaborative will seek to provide resources to members and the outside community to positively impact the design community. We focus on enhancing each student's professional development and social relationships within the college and field of interiors. The IA Collaborative: IIDA+ASID Student Chapter at UTK is comprised of student members of the International Interior Design Association (IIDA) and the American Society of Interior Designers (ASID); therefore, we support the missions and visions of the International Interior Design Association (IIDA) and the American Society of Interior Designers (ASID).
summary: The mission of the IA Collaborative: IIDA+ASID Student Chapter at UTK is to provide an environment for interior architecture students at the University of Tennessee to benefit from professional development and an exchange of knowledge and support.

name: Haslam Women of Color
description: Haslam Women of Color is a student organization that aims provide a space for women of color in the Haslam College of Business and students interested in business to be able to network, build a sense of community, and strengthen their educational and professional goals.
summary: A student organization that aims provide a space for women of color in the Haslam College of Business and students interested in business to be able to network, build a sense of community, and strengthen their educational and professional goals.

name: Excel Modeling Club
description: The Excel Modeling Club fosters an environment that encourages students to think critically about excel formulas, formatting techniques, shortcuts, and financial modeling skills to best prepare them for internships and full time jobs. This is achieved by hands-on learning through both custom made and pre-built excel workbooks provided by finance and academic professional to showcase different capabilities within the program. Students are encouraged to collaborate and ask questions to further their understanding while also building a sense of community with other students that are interested in becoming more knowledgeable in Microsoft Excel.
summary: The mission of the Excel Modeling Club is to develop relevant, practical, and cutting-edge Excel modeling skills that bridge gaps between HCB undergraduate curriculum and industry practice and elevate student career trajectories.

name: Reach UTK
summary: Reach is a campus ministry connected to Basswood Church. We exist to glorify God by sharing the gospel with students on campus, incorporating students into the body of Basswood Church, and helping them grow into mature disciples of Christ.

name: Volunteer Puppy Partners with SMSD
description:
VPP has two types of memberships. Those who get trained by a trainer at SMSD to handle the dogs and
do socializing visits. The second type of membership involves no training and instead helps create
various events, fundraisers, and educational resources about service dogs.
summary:
Students working in partnership with Smoky Mountain Service Dogs to raise service dogs for veterans
with disabilities.

name: Women in Sports Collective at UTK
description: Women in Sports Collective at UTK strives to highlight and support women with a passion for athletics. The group is open to all majors. The meetings will consist of workshops, guest speakers, and other social events that will allow students to connect with others who love sports.
summary: Women in Sports Collective at UTK

name: Vols Vote
summary: Vols Vote is a nonpartisan organization, characterized by patriotism, civic engagement, voter education, equality, and positivity. Our mission is to create a culture of civic activism and democratic engagement and to defeat voter apathy on campus.

name: Herbert College of Agriculture
description:
Agriculture has been a part of the curriculum at the University of Tennessee since 1871 and has been offered under different structures including the Department of Agriculture, School of Agriculture, College of Agriculture, College of Agriculture and Home Economics, and in 1991, the College of Agricultural Sciences and Natural Resources. In June 2018, the College underwent its most recent name change to the Herbert College of Agriculture in recognition of the generosity of Jim and Judi Herbert. Our vision is to develop VOLunteers who raise the standard as global servant leaders in agriculture and natural resources and who:

● Make a difference by creating real life solutions

● Demonstrate state, region, and global impact

● Live the land-grant mission
summary: The Herbert College of Agriculture educates and prepares students for leading personally satisfying lives as productive members of society with emphasis on careers in the agricultural, environmental, life, and social sciences.

name: Baker School Student Association
summary: The mission of the Baker School Student Association is to provide professional guidance and learning opportunities to students who are interested in public policy, public service, and civil discourse.

name: Telos on Campus
description: The purpose of Telos on Campus is to glorify God above all else. We intend to share the gospel across campus and minister to interested students by helping them grow in their knowledge and maturity of the Lord. All of this shall be done through weekly meetings which will consist of Bible discussions, praise and worship, and small group time as well as other events that Telos on Campus may host across campus. Our desire is that interested and attending students would both come to know Christ personally and would also begin to serve Him in the local church.
summary: The purpose of Telos on Campus is to glorify God above all else. We intend to share the gospel across campus and minister to interested students by helping them grow in their knowledge and maturity of the Lord.

name: Undergraduate International Law Club
description: The Undergraduate International Law Club provides undergraduate students opportunities to learn about international law from those who practice, study, and teach it. International law is ripe with topics of discussion from international humanitarian law to state sovereignty. Avenues for participation in international law are abundant at the law school level, but are oftentimes scarce for undergraduate students. The Undergraduate International Law Club solves this problem by providing students with a place to discuss pressing problems in the international law realm and channels to interact with and learn from sources both professional and scholarly.
summary: The Undergraduate International Law Club provides undergraduate students with an interest in international law with opportunities to engage with and learn about the field from those who practice, study, and teach it.

name: Volunteer Boxing Club
description:
Boxing Club promotes respect, discipline, comradery, and promotes physical fitness, self-control, health, and confidence  among its members. 

Also please join the groupme if wanting to join.

https://groupme.com/join_group/95811937/LrG782ck
summary: Volunteer Boxing Club offers students an opportunity to learn and train in the great sport of Boxing.

name: Center for Global Engagement
description:
The Center for Global Engagement provides leadership to strengthen the university’s global reach and impact, foster international and intercultural awareness and understanding, assist faculty to internationalize their teaching and scholarship, and prepare students to become globally engaged citizens.

  • Enhances and develops international partnerships in collaboration with academic units
  • Provides opportunities for students to internationalize their academic experience
  • Delivers excellent support services to the international student and scholar community
  • Promotes an appreciation for diverse cultures and perspectives through a range of international and intercultural programming
summary: Reporting directly to the Office of the Provost, the Center for Global Engagement works to lead, coordinate, and support the university’s strategies for global education, research, and engagement.

name: The Society of Ecological Restoration at UT
summary: We are the UT Knoxville chapter of an international organization committed to helping the environment by restoring Knoxville to its original ecology. We are volunteer-based and do projects on campus and in our community.

name: Vol Study Center
description:
The Vol Study Center offers study space, free printing and snacks/refreshments, study sessions, and Supplemental Instruction. Services are offered by undergraduate students who are trained to assist peers in optimal and efficient learning strategies. Schedule your free study sessions today!

Check Vol Study Center locations and hours of operation here.

Strong Hall 102 | Hodges Library, Room 220K | Pendergrass Study Room E | Frieson 208, and 209 | Zeanah 275 | Zoom Online
summary: The Vol Study Center offers study space, free printing and snacks/refreshments, and study sessions located across campus.

name: Phi Chi Pre-Medical Society
description: Phi Chi is a co-ed pre-medical fraternity that supports and encourages the interests of its members and contributes to the good of others. Our goal is to provide service opportunities, networking to alumni, leadership opportunities, and support its members in academics and professional development. We meet twice a month and will hold both medical and service events.
summary: Phi Chi is a co-ed pre-medical fraternity that supports and encourages the interests of its members and contributes to the good of the community. Our goal is to provide service and leadership opportunities as well as networking opportunities.

name: Future Minority Leaders of Justice
description: The central mission of this organization is to actively promote the increased recruitment and sustained engagement of minority students who require support in their preparation for the challenges of law school or who express a keen interest in embarking on a career within the realm of politics. FMLJ, driven by its vision, endeavors to cultivate and nurture an inclusive community that facilitates networking opportunities for minority students. This collective effort is specifically geared towards combating prevalent educational and professional racial inequities, with the primary goal of fostering an environment that is not only receptive but actively supportive. This is achieved through the strategic implementation of various educational resources and the provision of valuable mentorship programs, both of which serve as vital tools in fostering an atmosphere of growth, development, and empowerment.
summary: Future Minority Leaders of Justice is a student-led organization aimed at attracting minorities who would like to attend law school or pursue a career within the realm of politics or government relation.

name: Vol Yoga Club
summary: The mission of the VYC is to promote holistic well-being, mindfulness, and fitness among students through the practice of yoga. We aim to create a supportive and inclusive community that fosters personal growth, stress reduction, a balanced lifestyle.

name: Swashbuckler's Guild
description: Ahoy thar mateys! Ye be lookin' at the official website of the Swashbuckler's Guild! We be the greatest group of pirates on the high seas. We do anything pirate related ye can think of, from sword duels to huntin' for buried treasure. We're a low obligation group, thar be no dues to pay, no required events. Just come to whatever ye wish. Thar also be prizes if ye find our treasure, and parties with free food.
summary: The Swashbuckler's Guild, SwaG, be UT's first pirate life organization. We organize duels, treasure hunts, and other pirate related activities.

name: THE 1825
summary: The 1825 is a community of college students and young adults (18 - 25) pursuing Jesus affiliated with First Baptist Concord.

name: The Walk
description: The Walk serves the students of the University of Tennessee by welcoming all to join each week for free food, live music, and fresh perspective.
summary: Founded in 2003, The Walk is a college ministry that gathers weekly to shape the way that college students view church by reflecting the purpose and personality of Jesus.

name: Road Cycling Club UTK
description:
Our mission is to cultivate a community of passionate cyclists, providing opportunities for both seasoned racers and newcomers to develop their skills and share their love for cycling. Through organized races, training sessions, and workshops, we aim to foster a supportive environment where members can challenge themselves, achieve personal goals, and build lasting friendships

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/99128795/PHQYRWMA
summary: Our new university club is dedicated to the exhilarating world of road bicycle racing, offering a dynamic and engaging platform for students to explore and excel in this sport.

name: Animal Welfare Club
description: The mission of the Animal Welfare Club at the University of Tennessee is to share and discuss Animal Welfare research and promote Animal Welfare through service events, guest speakers, and field trips. The club will also prepare students for the AVMA Animal Welfare Assessment Contest in the Fall and Spring. Any student, staff, or faculty is welcome.
summary: The mission of the Animal Welfare Club is to share and discuss Animal Welfare research, promote Animal Welfare through service events, provide social opportunities, and prepare students for the AVMA Animal Welfare Assessment Contest.

name: Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Technology Law
summary: The PIT is a student-led organization that aims to inform, educate, and expose students who are or may be interested in Privacy, Intellectual Property, or Technology Law by providing lectures, mentorships, and networking opportunities.

name: Students for Justice In Palestine at UTK
description:
Students for Justice in Palestine (SJP) at the University of Tennessee is dedicated to raising awareness about the injustices concerning the occupation of the Palestinian people. SJP at UTK advocates for democratic and nonviolent principles to promote justice, human rights, equality, liberty, and self-determination for the Palestinian people.

 

As an advocacy group, SJP at UTK is committed to a core set of principles to which it expects its members to adhere. While the Palestinian people must ultimately be able to decide their future in Palestine, certain key principles grounded in international law, human rights law, and basic standards of justice are fundamental to a just resolution for the Israel-Palestine issue. These principles include the full decolonization of all illegally held Palestinian lands, the repatriation of Palestinian refugees in accordance with the Right of Return, an end to all discriminatory laws and systems targeting the indigenous Palestinian population, and a total end to the occupation of Palestine.

 

Just as SJP at UTK condemns the discrimination underlying the policies and laws of Israel’s colonization of Palestinian land, SJP at UTK rejects and condemns all other forms of hatred, intolerance, or discrimination against any religious, racial, or ethnic group. This group is committed towards overcoming anti-Semitic sentiments towards both Arabs and Jews in every community.

 

Rooting its strength in the diversity of its membership, SJP at UTK welcomes individuals of all backgrounds to join in solidarity with the struggle for freedom and justice in Palestine.
summary: Students for Justice in Palestine (SJP) at UTK is dedicated to raising awareness about the injustices concerning the occupation of the Palestinian people and advocates for democratic and nonviolent principles to promote justice and freedom.

name: Sustainable Fashion Club
description: The club is for sustainable clothing. The reason we are starting this club is because we have been collecting and selling vintage clothing at markets in Knoxville to help people shop for affordable sustainable clothing and help keep some clothes out of the landfills. While doing these markets we have seen that many students from UT like the idea of sustainable clothing and always ask us how they can find the same items around the city on their own. In the club we would teach them where we find our favorite items, why using sustainable clothing is good for the Earth, and how to start doing their own markets by doing pop up markets in between classes on campus and letting them work with us.
summary: This club helps keep clothing sustainable when there is so much clothing waste in the world. We also try to help students on campus buy and source sustainable clothing close to campus.

name: The Beauty In Marketing Group
description: The Beauty in Marketing Group is dedicated to fostering a vibrant and inclusive community of individuals with an interest in the marketing and public relations sector of the beauty industry at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville. We strive to give members a safe space to share and collaborate, allow for opportunities for members to network and grow professionally, as well as keep members informed and updated on trends, events, and important information affecting the marketing and public relations aspects of the beauty industry.
summary: Beauty in Marketing Group fosters a vibrant, inclusive community at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Dedicated to marketing and PR in the beauty industry, we provide a safe space for collaboration, networking, and professional growth.

name: Women's Club Golf Team
description: This club is for women of all levels of golf experience, there will be minimal practices but we will work on getting girls more involved in the sport. We will work with different golf places to get discounts for the range and for playing and then play with different college clubs.
summary: This club will be a way for girls of all levels will be able to come together and play. Getting women involved in the sport and playing with different schools in the region is the goal of the club.

name: Hearted
description: Hearted is a public health initiative aimed at promoting heart health and mental health on campus and in the greater Knoxville community. Through service, we hope to support the missions of various mental and heart health organizations, such as Crisis Text Line, The American Heart Association, and The Letter Project. Through outreach, we hope to table around campus with heart healthy snacks and informational literature about heart health and mental health. In addition, we hope to write notes of gratitude to the UTK community as well as surprise deserving individuals with gratitude showers. Through education, we hope to host challenges that promote healthy behaviors (such as eating healthy and exercising) and inform the campus community on the reasons healthy behaviors are so important, especially for college students as primary preventive measures. Additionally, our Instagram page serves not only as a platform for organization updates but also as a source of evidence-based health and wellness tips.
summary: Hearted is a public health initiative dedicated to promoting heart health and mental health through service, outreach, and education. Activities include tabling, healthy behavior challenges, and fundraising/service events.

name: Young Americans for Liberty at UTK
description: Our chapter of Young Americans for Liberty (YAL) at UTK is a Christian conservative student organization. Our focus is on building a community of like-minded individuals, and fighting for the rights of students and just laws of the land. We are known for playing an instrumental role in ending the campus ban on pepper spray and are currently fighting to enact campus carry across the state of Tennessee.
summary: Young Americans for Liberty (YAL) is a libertarian conservative organization focused on students' rights, winning elections, and advancing the principles of liberty.

name: Poetry Appreciation Club
description:
A fun club for people who enjoy, appreciate, or just want to learn more about poetry. We accept everyone no matter their background and would love to find more new people who love the poetic arts. Our club wishes to educate people on the historic value of poetry and inspire people who wish to create their own poetic forms.

 

Slogan: Explore the World of Verses. Where hearts speak in stanzas. Where every word finds a home. Where every voice is a poem.
summary: The Poetry Appreciation Club aims to get students involved in activities connected to historical and present day poetic forms, in order to deepen their comprehension of the impact poetry has had on our language, culture, and society.

name: Women In Construction
description:
1. The purpose of the Women in Construction organization is to provide a student organization for women planning to pursue a career in the construction industry after graduation. The goal of the organization is to enhance student’s education and allow them to engage in construction related activities with women who have the same goals. This mission is executed through the following objectives: 
    1. Providing a support system for women in construction related majors 
    2. Aiding students of the University of Tennessee, Knoxville in forming meaningful connections with women in the construction industry.
      1.  This is achieved by working with local businesses, organizations, and individuals to create beneficial connections for pre and post graduation.
    3. Hosting/organizing events meant to foster student’s relationships with their peers.
    4. Assisting students in construction related majors with academic support as needed.
summary: Primarily for construction science majors, a group for women in construction to meet, make real-world industry connections, and work with likeminded women preparing to work in the construction industry

name: NOBCChE Student Chapter at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville
description: The University of Tennessee Knoxville NOBCChE Student Chapter is an organization dedicated to uniting aspiring and accomplished minds in chemistry and chemical engineering throughout the university and the greater Knoxville area. Our purpose is to create a vibrant community dedicated to fostering diversity, providing educational and professional development opportunities, engaging in impactful STEM outreach, and building lasting connections at the local level. This student chapter is a part of the larger national organization.
summary: The University of Tennessee Knoxville NOBCChE Student Chapter is an organization dedicated to uniting aspiring and accomplished minds in chemistry and chemical engineering throughout the university and the greater Knoxville area.

name: Future Hospitality Leaders Association
description: The Future Hospitality Leaders Association is about bringing together Hospitality and Tourism Management majors and minors to learn about different parts in the hospitality industry through various means. This includes speakers, field trips, and more. The organization is also about stimulating interest in the field, and allowing students to gain valuable career readiness skills with resume workshops and mock interviews, as well as providing exposure about internships and job and volunteer opportunities.
summary: The Future Hospitality Leaders Association is about bringing together Hospitality and Tourism Management majors and minors to learn about different parts in the hospitality industry.

name: A Page for Your Thoughts
description: Journaling is great for emotional well-being. With journaling, you are able to transfer your emotions and thoughts onto paper and gain insight into them. If you are facing struggles or challenges, journaling is also a great way to find solutions to them. College students may experience a lot of stressful situations, whether it be tackling academics or adjusting to a different environment. This organization will allow students to sit down and journal about their experiences or feelings they encounter throughout their time at UTK.
summary: An organization dedicated to provide students opportunities to de-stress by journaling their thoughts and feelings throughout their time at UTK.

name: BridgeUTK
description:
BridgeUSA is a multi-partisan student movement that champions viewpoint diversity, responsible discourse, and a solution-oriented political culture. We are developing a generation of leaders that value empathy and constructive engagement because our generation will bear the cost of polarization and tribalism for years to come.

The purpose of BridgeUSA is to build a community — one in which students from across the ideological spectrum can engage as a group working together to understand–to bridge–the various perspectives behind the important political and social issues of our time.

We rise above party. We do not force unity or compromise. We believe in the Bridge Mindset: viewpoint diversity, constructive engagement, and a solution-oriented political culture
summary: BridgeUSA is a multi-partisan student movement that champions viewpoint diversity, responsible discourse, and a solution-oriented political culture. We work hard to hold discussions for all viewpoints to represent themselves through constructive dialogue

name: Rocky Top Recovery
description: Rocky Top Recovery is a collegiate recovery program at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, dedicated to supporting Volunteers in recovery from substance use and mental health disorders. Through community building, campus programming, and advocacy, students provide a supportive environment for students in recovery or interested in recovery.
summary: Rocky Top Recovery is a collegiate recovery program at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, dedicated to supporting Volunteers in recovery from substance use and mental health disorders.

name: The Caving Club
description:
About Us

    The Caving Club was founded out of a love for the recreational sport of caving and a strong belief in caves as a valuable natural resource deserving of appreciation and protection. We live with worlds under our feet. This club is here to explore them safely and respectfully, with the goal of creating and supporting confident and enthusiastic cavers. If you are interested in outdoor recreation, climbing, geology, paleontology, ecology (pretty much any -ology), or just want to learn a new skill, we can't wait to meet you and hope that you check us out!

 

Join Our GroupMe: Caving Club at UTK
summary: The Caving Club is a student organization dedicated to teaching students how to be safe and responsible cavers. We promote awareness of the unique and important cave environment in one of the most cave-rich areas of the world.

name: Jazz Education Network - University of Tennessee Knoxville Student Chapter
summary: UTK JEN is the official University of Tennessee Knoxville student chapter to the international Jazz Education Network. The organization's mission is to build the jazz arts community by advancing education, promote performance, and develop new audiences.

name: Rocky Top Wranglers
description: Line dancing! We learn on campus, host social dances, and plan to host get togethers in at the Cotton Eyed Joe and our own social dances!
summary: We learn line dances, host social country dancing, and attend the Cotton Eyed Joe!

name: The Cheese Club
summary: THIS IS ONLY A TEST ORGANIZATION.

name: Asian Pacific American Law Student Association
description:
APALSA strives to promote academic and professional excellence for its members by serving as a liaison between UT Law, national APALSA, and NAPABA (National Asian Pacific American Bar Association) organizations. Above all, APALSA actively advocates for equality and social progress within the Asian Pacific American legal community at the College of Law.

Membership is open to all students and faculty/staff members where appropriate, regardless of race, color, national origin, religion, sex, pregnancy, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, physical or mental disability, or covered veteran status. Any undergraduate student supportive of the mission of APALSA, but who does not possess the necessary qualifications of active member status, may join APALSA as an honorary member.
summary: APALSA of UT is committed to advocating for Asian-Pacific American interests at the College of Law, promoting career and networking opportunities for its members, and facilitating allyship within the student body.

name: Diverse Organization Propelling Excellence at Haslam
description:
The Diverse Organization Propelling Excellence (DOPE) is an inclusive and empowering community dedicated to supporting the academic and professional journeys of underrepresented students in business, including—but not limited to—Black, Indigenous, and People of Color (BIPOC) and international graduate business students. Membership is open to all students, and there must be no discrimination with respect to race, gender, disability, religion, or national origin. Our community brings together graduate students across MBA, MAcc, MSBA, Marketing, and other business-related programs to foster connection, collaboration, and growth.

Grounded in the values of unity, support, and shared purpose, DOPE is committed to cultivating an environment where every member feels seen, supported, and equipped to thrive. We aim to create a space where diverse identities, experiences, and aspirations are celebrated, and where academic and professional excellence is made accessible for all.

At DOPE, we believe in the power of community. By sharing challenges, successes, and lived experiences, we build a lasting network of support that transcends the classroom. Together, we empower one another to pursue academic achievement, professional success, and personal fulfillment.

We organize job tours, providing members with firsthand experiences of various industries and connecting them with potential employers. We host guest speaker sessions, offering opportunities to engage with professionals and leaders who share their experiences, insights, and valuable advice. Additionally, we actively seek and attend conferences aligned with our members' interests and career goals, providing unique networking opportunities.

This community allows shared experiences, challenges, and successes, in creating a network of support that extends beyond the academic realm. DOPE works to empower each other to achieve academic excellence, professional success, and personal growth.
summary: Diverse Organization Propelling Excellence (DOPE) cultivates growth for BIPOC business graduate students in Haslam through job tours, speakers, conferences, fostering academic, professional, and personal success.

name: Lady Ice Vols Hockey Club
description: The purpose of this organization is to allow University of Tennessee women students the opportunity regularly compete throughout the year as an affiliate of University of Tennessee in the sport of Women’s Collegiate Hockey against teams representing colleges across the nation.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to allow University of Tennessee women students the opportunity regularly compete throughout the year as an affiliate of University of Tennessee in the sport of Women’s Collegiate Hockey.

name: The Advocates' Guild
summary: A professional group of aspiring legal professionals that will learn from each other through activities like listening to lawyers, working through cases, research, and other professional development activities.

name: Alpha Gamma Delta
description: Alpha Gamma Delta is an international women's organization founded in 1904 at Syracuse University. Inspired by the vision defined by our Founders, Alpha Gam challenges members to find meaning within the values outlined in our Purpose and to lead lives of significance that positively impact their communities. Together, we continually work to: Inspire the Woman. Impact the World.
summary: Established in 1904, Alpha Gamma Delta is committed to providing an experience grounded in sincerity, cherished friendships and high ideals.

name: Baking Club at UTK
summary: Club for any student at UT that loves to bake or talk about anything related to baking.

name: Baker Graduate Student Association
description: • The primary objective of the Baker Graduate Student Association is to provide a platform for Baker graduate students to connect, collaborate, and support one another in both academic and non-academic pursuits. Our values include but are not limited to providing students with networking events, professional development workshops, social gatherings, academic support groups, community outreach, and more.
summary: The BGSA is the official student association serving Graduate Students and Accelerated Program students enrolled in the Baker School.

name: The University of Tennessee INFORMS Student Chapter
description: The purpose of the INFORMS Student Chapter is to educate students in the fields of data and decision sciences, including, but not limited to, operations research, analytics, management science, economics, behavioral science, statistics, artificial intelligence, data science, and applied mathematics, by organizing workshop sessions, inviting keynote speakers, hosting social events, and more.
summary: The INFORMS Student Chapter exists to educate students in the fields of data and decision sciences and prepare them with the skills needed for successful careers in these fields through networking and training events.

name: Eighteen Twenty Prayer Group
description: Emmaline Bartlett and I (Alexi Fedorkevich) started Eighteen Twenty in the fall of 2023 by ourselves. Our first meeting was on Ayres Lawn with about 11 girls in attendance. We now have grown to an average of 70 girls attending every week, with our record number of attendees being 95 women. We currently partner with Psalm 89:1 ministries through a grant that we have applied for and been given. They give us the funds and resources to provide coffee every Wednesday morning at our prayer groups for the girls. Our structure for each Eighteen Twenty meeting involves fellowship at the start while everyone settles into the 3rd floor of the library, then we give a 20-minute devotional that encourages and uplifts the women there in their faith and prompts them into our next activity which is quiet, individual prayer time. During this time we usually play a worship song that relates to the devotion while the girls sit with God for a little. After this 3-5 minute prayer time, girls break off into groups of 4-7, and each share their prayer requests and things they are currently walking through. Then each group prays for each other so that as followers of Christ we can practice praying for others and feeling prayed over! We meet at 7 am each week so that girls who have 8 am classes can still come! Since we have been consistently growing and always advertise that every woman is welcome, our hope of being able to apply as a campus organization would allow us to have more access to a bigger space for our Wednesday morning meetings!!!
summary: Eighteen Twenty strives to further the Christian community for women at the University of Tennessee. We hope that by providing fellowship, encouragement, & intentional prayer time, girls will have the opportunity & desire to dive deeper into their faith.

name: The Oaks Collaborative
summary: A retreat to help UT freshmen and transfer students connect to Christ, connect to Christ-centered community, and come to campus ready to live for Christ!

name: Irish Dance UTK
description: Irish Dance UTK is the official competitive Irish dance team at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Weekly, students can come together to choreograph, learn, practice, and teach each other skills that they have learned throughout their dance careers. Our members have danced in many shows for the University and compete in intercollegiate competitions. Many of our dancers have competed and continue to compete at the regional, national, and international level. However, all skill levels are welcomed and encouraged to participate. Our dancers are dedicated to practicing the art of Irish dance and sharing Irish culture with the Knoxville community.
summary: Irish Dance UTK is the official competitive Irish dance team at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Weekly, students can come together to choreograph, learn, practice, and teach each other skills that they have learned throughout their dance careers.

name: Type 1 Vols
description: “Type One Vols” is designed to educate, advocate, and coordinate the support of university allies to better prepare students as they navigate living with diabetes on a college campus, while creating service and philanthropic endeavors for all members.
summary: The purpose of “Type One Vols” is to educate, advocate, and have the support of university allies to better prepare students as they navigate living with diabetes on a college campus, while creating service and philanthropic endeavors for all members.

name: Neurodiversity Club
summary: The mission of Neurodiversity Club is to create a community that welcomes and celebrates neurodiverse students.

name: Law Students for Life
summary: The mission (or purpose) of the “Organization’s Name” is to Members of (Your School) Students for Life work to save lives threatened by induced abortion, euthanasia, and the destruction of human embryos for research. In furtherance of these goals, membe

name: Journaling Club
description:
The purpose of the Journaling Club is to support and provide a community for students interested in journaling of any form, physical or digital. The club has several broad goals:

  • To help students hone and optimize their journaling systems to their needs, preferences, and lives.
  • To explore new and creative practices for journaling.
  • To promote journaling as a way to manage stress and improve well-being.
  • To sharpen critical, analytical, and creative thinking skills and improve writing skills through journaling and discussion.
  • To facilitate rich discussions and the sharing of tips, insights, science, and more relevant to journaling.
Our casual, low-demand club seeks to use journaling to light the way for ourselves and others!
 
Please join our GroupMe, where you'll get important announcements, reminders, journaling prompts, and a space for discussion! https://groupme.com/join_group/102160869/hwlLpcvJ
summary: The Journaling Club supports and creates a community for students interested in journaling of any form, and includes a GroupMe with regularly provided journaling prompts and a space to discuss journaling and share relevant resources.

name: Awe & Wonder Campus Mission
description: Awe & Wonder Campus Mission is a community of students from various Christian faith traditions who meet weekly in order to build healthy, supportive relationships, process big life questions and discover higher purpose during their academic studies and study the tenets of their faith and participate in discipleship and community service at the University of Tennessee.
summary: Awe & Wonder Campus Mission is a community of students from various Christian faith traditions who meet weekly in order to build healthy, supportive relationships, process big life questions and discover higher purpose during their academic studies.

name: Communication and Information Leadership Collective
description:
Mission 

The mission of the Communication and Information Leadership Collective is to support the success of students by cultivating an enrichened environment that values academic and personal growth; community building; and collaboration. 

Vision 

To become a primary hub for professional growth and community-building for students across communication and information disciplines 

Goals 

  1. Cultivate an Inclusive Community 
  1. Create an open and accepting environment for all CCI students that promotes dialogue and collaboration around access and engagement. 
  1. Enhance Marketability for Global Careers 
  1. Equip students with tools and skills for success in the global job market through targeted workshops, networking opportunities, and other programs. 
  1. Support Students’ Success 
  1. Provide tailored resources and programs that ensure students feel valued and are encouraged to persist in their studies within CCI. 

Highlighted Programs and Services 

  • Career Development and Exploration Resources 
  • Mentoring Program 
  • Speaker Series 
  • Industry Trips 
  • Social Gatherings 
  • Community Engagement 

Discrimination Policy 

It is the policy of the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, that no person shall be excluded from participation in, be denied the benefits of, or in any way be subjected to, discrimination in any program or activity of the University. Participation in the Communication and Information Leadership Collective is open to anybody, regardless of race, ethnicity or gender, if they are capable of participating towards its objectives. 

Discrimination based on age, color, disability, gender, national origin, race, religion, sexual orientation, veteran status, pregnancy, parental status, marital status, or genetic information is prohibited.  

Any person having a question regarding laws and regulations, or who feels discriminated against, is encouraged to contact UT’s Office of Investigation and Resolution, 216 Business Incubator Building, 2450 E. J. Chapman Dr. Knoxville, Tennessee, 37996, (865) 974-0717, or investigations@utk.edu.
summary: The Communication and Information Leadership Collective enhances the student experience for all by championing access and engagement opportunities that celebrate self-discovery, promote professional development, and build community within CCI.

name: Women In Politics
description: The purpose of Women In Politics is to empower and support women who are interested in and engaged with political leadership, advocacy, and policymaking. We aim to cultivate a dynamic community where women can share knowledge, experiences, and resources to foster personal growth, leadership skills, and political participation. Through education, networking, and collaboration, we strive to amplify the voices of women in political spheres, promote gender equality in decision-making processes, and effect positive change in our communities and beyond.
summary: This club aims to bridge the gender gap in politics along with empowering and supporting women who are interested in and engaged with political leadership, advocacy, and policymaking.

name: The Community Gardening Club
description: The mission of the Community Gardening Club is to foster a passion for gardening and environmental stewardship through engaging and educational activities. We aim to improve the Knoxville community by establishing and caring for gardens at elementary schools and upkeep/landscaping public spaces. Our organization is committed to empowering members with hands-on gardening experience, promoting sustainable practices, and cultivating a sense of community responsibility. Through our efforts, we strive to make a lasting positive impact on the University of Tennessee, the Knoxville campus, and the broader Knoxville community, one garden at a time. We work with nonprofits like Rooted East and Knox County Community Garden Growers Association to help combat food apartheid in Knoxville!
summary: Foster a love for gardening, community engagement, and sustainability. We do many projects like building gardens at elementary schools, upkeeping public green spaces/parks in Knoxville, and learning about various gardening techniques and plants!

name: Bredesen Center Student Advocacy Council
description:
The Student Advocacy Council (SAC) meets periodically with the Bredesen Center staff. A primary goal of the Bredesen Center is to help students feel at home. In this effort, the Council exists to listen to students and help to foster this community by providing an additional channel of communication within the Bredesen Center.

There are many ways to get in contact with the SAC. To submit an anonymous question or comment, please use the form located here. Also feel free to send an email to any of the current members and they will be happy to talk and help. In the fall and spring semesters, the SAC also hosts a town hall for all BC students to come together as a community for discussion and to voice questions or comments.
summary: The Student Advocacy Council (SAC) meets periodically with the Bredesen Center staff. A primary goal of the Bredesen Center is to help students feel at home

name: Honors Student Engagement Council
description: Honors Student Engagement Council is a UH student-driven initiative that is open to all University Honors students. Each semester HSEC coordinates inclusive, innovative events and service opportunities for University Honors students to build community.
summary: Honors Student Engagement Council is a UH student-driven initiative that is open to all University Honors students. Each semester HSEC coordinates inclusive, innovative events and service opportunities for University Honors students to build community.

name: GeoguessrUTK
description: The purpose of GeoguessrUTK is to provide students an opportunity to play Geoguessr and learn techniques within the game to improve real-life geographical knowledge. Geoguessr is a strategic, online geography game that uses Google Street View to plant players anywhere in the world, making users find context clues around them to pinpoint their location on a world map. Geoguessr is an extremely beneficial and immersive tool to learn about different countries and their culture, architecture, language, and terrain. This is unique to all currently registered organizations as it provides a perspective on the world’s culture through a game with over 66,000,000 users.
summary: Geoguessr is a beneficial, unique, and immersive tool to learning about different countries’ culture, architecture, language, and terrain. The organization will help students learn to geolocate in google street view using context clues around the world

name: Onewheel Club
summary: We are a club of amateur Onewheel riders. We ride on campus and around Knoxville. Riders of any skill level are welcome.

name: College of Law Volleyball Club
description: We are a group of law students that has a passion for playing volleyball. All skill levels are invited!
summary: We are a group of law students who have a passion for playing volleyball. All skill levels invited!

name: Unified Special Olympics Club - UTK
description: Unified Sports aims to unite those athletes and students through the form of sport and leisure. We aim to serve as a larger body org where members can create unified teams at UTK, and eventually, compete in Special Olympics events. As a branch of the Special Olympics here in Tennessee, our main objectives are to complete the 3 components of Unified Champion Schools (Unified Sports, Inclusive Youth Leadership, and Whole School Engagement).
summary: Unified Sports aims to unite athletes and students through the form of sport and leisure. We aim to serve as a larger body organization where members can create unified teams at UTK, and eventually, compete in Special Olympics events.

name: The Pulsera Project
description: The Pulsera Project is a non-profit organization that works to empower artisans in Central America by selling their handmade products, mainly colorful woven bracelets (pulseras), in schools across the U.S. The project aims to provide sustainable employment for these artisans, many of whom live in impoverished areas, while raising awareness about social issues and fair trade practices. The proceeds from pulsera sales fund education, healthcare, housing, and other community projects in the artisans' home countries. It's both an art and a social justice initiative that connects U.S. students with global issues through hands-on participation.
summary: The Pulsera Project is a nonprofit that educates, empowers, and connects Central American artists with students in more than 3,600 U.S. schools through the sale of colorful handwoven bracelets, or "pulseras" in Spanish.

name: Knox 'N' Boots
summary: Knox 'N' Boots strives to teach students how to line dance while creating a positive, energetic atmosphere! Our goal is to establish a group on campus where students at all levels meet fellow peers, while learning various line dances.

name: EMS Vols
description:
• Work towards initiating an on campus EMS squad
  • Obtain real world EMS experience
  • Find a community of other first responders
  • Educate students on different medical topics
summary: To develop a community of emergency medical responders within the University of Tennessee, Knoxville campus. We will provide opportunities for student engagement and leadership in EMS while working towards the goal of establishing a student led EMS squad

name: Women's Health Advocacy Team @ UTK
description: The mission of the Women’s Health Advocacy Team (WHAT) @ UTK is to raise awareness of women’s health and wellness issues by providing education and support to students on campus and performing outreach to disadvantaged females in the Knoxville community and abroad. WHAT will achieve its goals by giving on-campus physical and mental health seminars to educate students about women’s wellness. The organization will also have its members serve and come alongside the lives of local and international women struggling with physical and mental health concerns and advocate for their needs. Fundraising events each semester will help finance WHAT’s costs and expenses while allowing its members to build relationships and a support system with one another. WHAT VOLS will strive to impact their world through awareness, advocacy, service, and compassion.
summary: The mission of the Women’s Health Advocacy Team @ UTK is to raise awareness of women’s health and wellness issues by providing education and support to students on campus and performing outreach to females in the Knoxville community and abroad.

name: Trumpets of the Rocky Top
