description: The purpose of the Trumpets of Rocky Top is to provide a formal organization for the members of the University of Tennessee Trumpet Ensemble. The mission of this organization is to provide diverse and educational musical performances to the University of Tennessee and beyond. The Trumpets of Rocky Top aim to facilitate performance and research opportunities at by providing trumpet specific recitals, competitions, conferences, masterclasses, as well as professional development and research opportunities for those involved.
summary: Trumpets of Rocky Top is an organization for the members of the University of Tennessee Trumpet Ensemble. The mission of this organization is to provide diverse and educational musical performances to the University of Tennessee and beyond

name: Student Tennessee Education Association STEA
description:
The Student Tennessee Education Association (STEA) aims to create a community dedicated to fostering the next generation of passionate and innovative educators. We provide a supportive environment where aspiring educators can come together to learn, collaborate, and grow in their pursuit of excellence in the field of education.

Our club offers a variety of activities and opportunities, including guest speaker events, shared drives for resources, and volunteer programs in local schools. Members can engage in discussions about current trends and challenges in education and gain practical teaching experience through hands-on activities and mentoring.


Whether you are interested in early childhood education, STEM education, special education, or any other area of teaching, the STEA welcomes students from all disciplines and backgrounds. We believe in the power of education to transform lives and communities and are committed to empowering our members to become compassionate, skilled, and effective educators.

Join us as we inspire and support each other on our journey towards becoming the educators of tomorrow. Together, we can make a difference in students' lives and contribute positively to the future of education.
summary: The Student Tennessee Education Association (STEA) aims to create a community dedicated to fostering the next generation of passionate and innovative educators.

name: Christian Medical and Dental Associations at the University of Tennessee
description: CMDA offers a variety of programs and services that align with its mission to transform healthcare through faith in Christ. With a membership of over 13,00 healthcare professionals nationally and internationally, CMDA is actively shaping healthcare policies, organizing overseas medical education and evangelism initiatives, and fostering a network for Christian healthcare professionals to connect and grow their own faith. CMDA also supports student ministries in various healthcare training schools, distributes educational and inspirational materials, and hosts conferences focused on marriage and family. At CMDA UTK, we will be gather in devotion of Christ, worship as a group, and serve our community. We will bring in local healthcare professionals to speak on how they integrate their faith into their work, as well as how God has called them to serve as healthcare professionals. CMDA aims to embody the compassionate spirit of Jesus adhering to the principles of loving one's neighbor and fulfilling the Great Commission.
summary: CMDA is an organization that educates, encourages, and equips Christian healthcare professionals to glorify God. We will have come together and worship, gather in devotion, and serve in the community.

name: Arab Graduate Students Association
description:
Arab Graduate Students Association: Building Bridges and Celebrating Arab Culture 

Welcome to the Arab Graduate Students Association at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Our organization connects Arab students with their heritage and helps international students adapt to their new environment through community and cultural engagement. 

Vision: 
To foster a community that celebrates Arab cultural pride and assists Arab students in integrating into the broader university community, promoting respect and understanding across all cultures. 

Mission: The Arab Graduate Students Association focuses on: 

  • Community Building: We facilitate social and cultural events that help international Arab students feel at home and connected. 
  • Support and Inclusivity: We offer guidance to help international Arab students navigate university life. 

Activities: 

  • Orientation and Social Mixers: Introduce students to campus life and connect them with peers. 
  • Cultural Events: We host gatherings and celebrate events that honor our Arab heritage, fostering cultural appreciation and interaction among students. 

Membership:  

Open to all, with a focus on Arabic speakers, our organization is a place to celebrate shared heritage and experiences, welcoming those from all religious backgrounds in a spirit of unity and respect. 

Commitment to Diversity: 
Our commitment is to offer a respectful, inclusive community that values diversity and provides a platform for cultural exchange without political involvement.
summary: Arab Graduate Students Association enhances Arab students' lives through social and cultural events, building a warm community focused on supporting each other, welcoming members of all religions in a non-political setting.

name: Healthy Celebrations UTK
description: Healthy Celebrations is a program that helps college students come together to socialize and run in a safe and supportive environment. It encourages students to stay active and build positive connections with others. Our goal is to provide fun and healthy events where everyone can feel welcome, make friends, and improve themselves in a positive and encouraging community!
summary:
Healthy Celebrations is an organization that builds a community where college students can
socialize and run in an encouraging and safe environment.

name: The Appalachian Studies Club
description: https://groupme.com/join_group/102406494/vXocUNh5
summary: The Appalachian Heritage Society is an on-campus organization for students interested in Appalachian culture, history, sustainability, and events. Meetings include lectures, discussions, short-film viewings, and outings.

name: Students Providing Aid
description:
Join the SPAid GroupMe! https://groupme.com/join_group/104049832/MIpJTbb9

 

The following description is the official SPAid organization description and can be found on www.spaidonline.com

At SPAid (Students Providing Aid), our mission is to empower communities by providing essential first aid resources to those in need. Through the collaborative efforts of students nationwide, we aim to construct and distribute kid-friendly first aid kits to underserved areas across the globe. By fostering partnerships, engaging in educational outreach, and promoting health awareness, we strive to address the critical lack of healthcare access in third-world countries and make a tangible impact on global health. Together, we are committed to building a healthier, more informed, and compassionate world.
summary: SPAid (Students Providing Aid) is dedicated to improving global health by empowering students to construct and distribute kid-friendly first aid kits to underserved communities.

name: Student Philanthropy and Preservation Society of Knoxville
description: The Student Philanthropy and Preservation Society of Knoxville integrates topics related to various celebration days and months, offering presentations and discussions that align with these themes. These events highlight significant historical events and figures connected to East Tennessee's rich and diverse heritage. In addition to education, we actively engage in community service to inspire our peers and the next generation. Through our proposed volunteer efforts at the McClung Museum, we not only preserve history but also ensure it remains accessible and relevant to the community. By combining education with service, we hope to foster a deeper appreciation for the region's past among students and Knoxville citizens while empowering others to continue its preservation.
summary: SPPS explores key historical topics in East Tennessee, such as cultural heritage, social progress, economic development, and natural history. By combining service with education, we highlight the importance of preserving history and passing it on.

name: Horse Judging Club at UTK
description: Like many other land-grant universities, the University of Tennessee boasts a horse judging club and provides the opportunity for students to travel across the country and participate in nationally recognized collegiate contests. The Club allows students to participate in practices lead by an experienced coach to learn more about evaluating horses, both for conformation and performance. Students are not required to compete but have the ability to choose to do so. At any horse judging contest, students are expected to successfully evaluate equine conformation, equine performance across a wide range of classes and disciplines, and to give oral reasons. Each sector of these contests are incorporated into each practice and team meeting conducted by the horse judging club and their coach. In addition to improving equine evaluation skills, students can expect to improve their skills in public speaking, decision making, and critical thinking. The horse judging club is proud to represent the University of Tennessee and welcomes students from any background, major, and experience level.
summary: The Horse Judging Club is an organization focused on teaching students the skills to adequately evaluate horses across many breeds and disciplines in both halter and performance classes with the opportunity to compete at nationally recognized contests.

name: Dressage Club at UTK
description: The Dressage Club offers both noncompetitive and competitive members the opportunity to make dressage part of their college experience. Competitive members register and compete through the Intercollegiate Dressage Association (IDA). The Dressage Club is composed of undergraduate, graduate, and any other students who are considered part-time or full-­time students by UTK standards. Members must be in good academic standing with the University to participate in the club, meaning they cannot be on Academic Probation or Academic Dismissal.
summary: The Dressage Club offers both noncompetitive and competitive members the opportunity to make dressage part of their college experience. Competitive members register and compete through the Intercollegiate Dressage Association (IDA).

name: The Furry Club at UTK
description: We do friend-making activities ranging from video games to drawing, and are an open community to those with similar interests! We want all to feel included, so multiple meet up times are open for those who can't make one or the other!
summary: Our organization provides a safe place for furries and furries alike to have fun, make friends, and feel a sense of community.

name: Tri-Star Wing Booster Club
description: The club aims to foster camaraderie among cadets, alumni, and faculty, while promoting leadership, service, and excellence within the Tri-Star Wing community. Additionally, the club seeks to provide resources and opportunities to help cadets achieve their academic, physical, and professional goals.
summary:
The mission of the Tri-Star Wing Booster Club is to support the cadets of the Tri-Star Wing in their
leadership development and academic pursuits.

name: Lambda Pi Eta
description: Outstanding students in Communication Studies are invited to network and learn with fellow scholars, alumni, and faculty excelling in their field. Eligible members are sent an official invitation. *GPA & Credit Requirements.
summary: UTK's Alpha Gamma chapter of LPE is the National Communication Association’s official honor society.

name: Students Promoting Resilient and Outstanding Urban Trees
summary: SPROUT is dedicated to advancing urban and community forestry by fostering collaboration between University of Tennessee students and the Tennessee community. Our mission is to build a strong network of UT students interested in Urban Forestry, inspire c

name: Encore
description: This club is a Broadway and musical film appreciation club. It will be a place for people who love this genre to share their passion and opinions with like-minded people. We will also try to attend on-campus and off-campus theater events.
summary: This club is for people who love Broadway and musical film adaptations! We will watch one Broadway show a month and discuss it for fun! Welcome to all!

name: Students Supporting Israel at The University of Tennessee
description: Objectives include but are not limited to empowering students with knowledge regarding historical and current events in the Middle East, providing students with a safe environment to express their support for Israel, educating the greater campus community on fair media coverage in Israel, and equipping students with the intellectual tools and technical resources to challenge anti-Zionism and antisemitism on campus.
summary: Students Supporting Israel at The University of Tennessee is established to provide students with an engagement forum for pro-Israel advocacy.

name: Energy and Natural Resource Law Society
description: The Energy and Natural Resource Law Society is a College of Law affinity organization meant to create a space for Juris Doctor candidates to develop professionally and socially. This organization will be focused on providing professional, academic, extracurricular, and social programming for member students. This will range from bringing credentialed speakers such as practicing attorneys and government officials to mentorship programs.
summary: Affinity group for students in the College of Law interested in practicing law in energy, natural resource law, or conservation or are personally interested in related issues.

name: Rocky Top BBQ
description:
The Rocky Top BBQ Society plans to come together to discuss recipes, improve techniques, and cook barbecue. The club will discuss various styles of BBQ from across the nation and the world. Talk about the origins of BBQ, and the deep cultural connections that BBQ has on the south east region. This includes learning history and culture, smoking meat, creating side dishes, making sauces, and more. We will bring in area professionals who are trained in professional cooking or experienced pitmasters to talk to the club to help teach members. These opportunities will educate all members in BBQ, both experienced and novice smokers.

The club's meetings will talk about techniques to barbecuing in addition to discussing future club plans and competitions. The barbecue cooked during the club meeting will go both to the members as well as be donated to local soup kitchens to help the less fortunate.

In addition to donations, the club plans on representing the school at regional barbecue competitions. With many events year around, the Rocky Top BBQ Society will have many opportunities to compete both in the state of Tennessee, but the surrounding region as well.
summary: A social and philanthropic club that comes together to discuss recipes, improve techniques, and compete in the world of barbecue. The club comes together to improve the craft, competition, and community with monthly food donations to local soup kitchens.

name: First Generation Haslam Students
description: First Generation Haslam Students stives to provide support, resources, and a community for first-generation students at Haslam College of Business. We aim to foster academic success, personal growth, and professional development through our network of fellow first-generation students.
summary: The purpose of First Generation Haslam Students is to provide support, resources and community for first-generation students enrolled in Haslam College of Business.

name: Vols for Reproductive Justice
description:
Welcome to the Vols for Reproductive Justice! Our club is dedicated to advancing the principles of reproductive justice. We strive to create a supportive and inclusive community where members can engage in advocacy, education, and activism surrounding reproductive rights, health, and autonomy.

 

We believe that everyone deserves the right to make informed decisions about their bodies, sexuality, and reproductive futures, regardless of race, gender identity, socioeconomic status, or any other factor. We aim to explore the concept of intersectionality, recognizing that individuals experience reproductive healthcare and rights differently based on their unique identities and lived experiences. By centering intersectionality, we plan to amplify marginalized voices, elevate diverse perspectives, and advocate for policies and practices that address the reproductive oppressions faced by marginalized communities. Our club provides a platform for discussions on topics such as access to contraception, comprehensive sex education, abortion rights, maternal and prenatal health, reproductive healthcare disparities, and more. 

 

Through guest speakers, workshops, and collaborative projects, we aim to raise awareness about systemic injustices and barriers to reproductive healthcare, while also advocating for policies that promote equity and empower individuals to make choices that align with their values and circumstances. 

 

Whether you're passionate about reproductive rights or just starting to learn about these issues, all are welcome to join us in our efforts to promote reproductive equity and create a more just and inclusive society.
summary: Our club is dedicated to advancing the principles of reproductive justice. We strive to create a supportive and inclusive community where members can engage in advocacy, education, and activism surrounding reproductive rights, health, and autonomy.

name: Graduate Researchers in Ecology, Behavior, and Evolution
description: GREBE is a graduate student organization intended to support graduate students in the University of Tennessee Knoxville’s (UTK) Ecology and Evolutionary Biology (EEB) department. It does so by improving student well-being and connectivity, facilitating professional development opportunities, and advocating for EEB graduate students in different communities across the university.
summary: An EEB graduate student organization that coordinates events, organizes professional development activities, communicates with faculty, and focuses on improving student well-being.

name: Engineering Youth Education
description:
The mission of the Engineering Youth Education organization is to help the youth understand the different disciplines of engineering and show prospective engineers the options they have in the future.

We work towards our goal by visiting local schools and after-school programs where we present engineering, its disciplines, and related activities/games.

Between visits, our members are able to work on projects of their choosing either by themselves or with a team. The benefits of the projects are twofold as they can be shown off at the visits and provide our members with hands-on experience that employers look for.

Any member who is not interested in engineering projects, are able to work on crafts and posterboards that are the backbone to our presentations.
summary: The mission of the Engineering Youth Education organization is to help the youth understand the different disciplines of engineering and show prospective engineers the options they have in the future.

name: Vols Against Drunk Driving
summary: We are an organization dedicated to maintain the safety of every student on campus. Vol is a verb and VADD was created to give students the opportunity to keep their campus, friends, and family safe.

name: The Civic Engagement Roundtable
description:
The Civic Engagement Roundtable is a student-led effort to build on the momentum of the Baker school inaugural class as a hotbed of passionate students and ideas.  The society has a focus on media creation and professional interaction that positively represents Baker students and the University of Tennessee in the professional policy world.  This focus extends to interactions with members of different campus communities when sharing research and policy proposals; the CER adopts interdisciplinary membership and engagement as a priority.

Pedagogically, the society aims to create and promote accessible sources of relevant policy information starting from a campus-wide scope and student perspective. This service aims to serve both active members and the broader University community.
summary: The Civic Engagement Roundtable offers students a space to engage with policy discussion at all levels: municipal, campus, and greater. Discussion is conducted with a clear, project-based direction. All students are encouraged to lead passion projects.

name: President's Council
summary: Community of Student Organization Presidents which meet for leadership development opportunities, to address shared challenges, and foster meaningful collaboration

name: West Tennessee Graduate Student Association (WTGSA)
description: The West Tennessee Graduate Student Association (WTGSA) is an interdisciplinary organization for individuals who work at the West Tennessee AgResearch and Education Center in any capacity while completing their degree. The mission of the WTGSA is to provide a sense of community amongst the graduate students working in Jackson, TN, through social, outreach, service, and education-based activities and events. The vision of the WTGSA is to develop an association that will provide opportunities to enhance professionalism and camaraderie between graduate students in West Tennessee.
summary: The West Tennessee Graduate Student Association (WTGSA) is an interdisciplinary student organization for individuals who work or study at the West Tennessee AgResearch and Education Center in any capacity while completing their degree.

name: The Taylor Swift Club
description:
“The Taylor Swift Club” (Smokey's Version) at the University of Tennessee: Knoxville exists to build community, foster friendship, and celebrate the music, artistry, and cultural impact of Taylor Swift. We provide a welcoming and inclusive space for fans of all levels—whether casual listeners or dedicated Swifties—to connect, share, and explore together. Our mission is to promote kindness, respect, and open-mindedness while creating meaningful conversations, fun activities, and lasting connections.

Membership is open to all students, regardless of race, color, national origin, religion, sex, pregnancy, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, physical or mental disability, or covered veteran status.

Spring 2026 Meetings:

Wed Feb 04 2026 at 5pm in Hodges 212

Wed Mar 04 2026 at 5pm in Hodges 128

Wed Apr 01 2026 at 5pm in Hodges 128

Wed May 06 2026 at 5pm in Hodges 128
summary: A safe space for Swifties to discuss Taylor and to meet others who enjoy her music.

name: Art Club
summary: This is a place where student artists can collaborate and grow together. We will be doing fun art activities as well as art sales.

name: Quantitative Methodology for Agricultural Research
description:
The mission of the Quantitative Methodology for Agricultural Research can be summarized in the following statements:

  • To promote understanding and application of statistical methods within the agricultural sciences
  • To facilitate collaboration among undergraduate students, graduate students, faculty, and staff
  • To bridge the gap between statistical theory and practical application in agricultural research
  • To provide resources, workshops, and networking opportunities to enhance members quantitative skills
summary: The Quantitative Methodology for Agricultural Research Club aims to inspire and inform students, faculty, and staff of the use of statistical methods within agricultural sciences, working to bridge the gap between statistical theory and application.

name: Alexander Hamilton Society - UTK
description:
Join our GroupMe to get involved! 

The Alexander Hamilton Society’s mission is to identify, educate, and launch young men and women into foreign policy and national security careers imbued with the Hamiltonian perspective of strong and principled American leadership in global affairs. At the heart of our approach lies a commitment to vigorous debate and intellectual discourse, while offering programming that is critical to the intellectual and professional development of a new generation of American leaders. With the world more chaotic and dangerous today than it has been in a half-century, American universities are not providing our young people with an education that allows them to understand what they are defending and why. We believe that the American polity benefits from vigorous public discussion; our students frequently tell us that our events are the most engaging — and sometimes the only — debates that take place on their campuses. 

Our impact is far-reaching. With more than 80 academic chapters across U.S. college campuses and professional chapters in New York City and Washington, D.C., AHS has established a significant national footprint. Since our founding in 2010, we’ve organized thousands of events engaging more than 80,000 attendees in crucial discussions on foreign policy and national security. Our events are often cited by students as the most engaging—and sometimes the only—debates on their campuses, underscoring the vital role we play in stimulating intellectual discourse.

Our network of 2,000+ alumni has achieved remarkable success, securing key positions in the U.S. government, Congress, and leading think tanks. Many have been awarded prestigious scholarships, including Rhodes, Truman, Boren, Schwarzman, and Public Interest fellowships. By bridging the gap between academia and the professional world, AHS serves as a catalyst for career advancement in the foreign policy sphere. We offer a comprehensive support system, including mentorship, networking opportunities, and professional development resources for Hamiltonians at all career stages.

In an increasingly complex global landscape, AHS remains committed to cultivating a new generation of leaders equipped with the knowledge, skills, and principles necessary to navigate the challenges of American foreign policy and national security.
summary: A foreign policy and national security organization dedicated to launching the careers of future global affairs experts. Join our GroupMe (linked in the description) for the most up-to-date information!

name: Global Health Scholars
description: The purpose of Global Health Scholars Club is to promote interest in the field of biology of/in global health-related topics including research, and to network like-minded students, faculty, and other professionals. Activities will range from monthly meetings, events, and volunteer work in the community all legal under local, state, and federal law.
summary: The purpose of Global Health Scholars Club is to promote interest in the field of biology of/in global health-related topics including research, and to network like-minded students, faculty, and other professionals

name: The Allergen Awareness Club
description: This club will meet to learn more about allergens and their affects, spread awareness to other students, listen to those with allergies and their needs, work to make more allergen friendly options available, and overall learn more about a specific part of nutrition. It is open to anyone interested, but starting out a lot of us are nutrition majors who passionate about nutrition and want to get into a more specific field of interest. We are hoping to use this club to help fellow students and learn together.
summary: This would be a club for people interested in learning more about allergens and their affects and who want to help spread awareness and work to make UT a safer place for those with allergies. It will be open to anyone interested!

name: HEMA at UT
description: Join the University of Tennessee's Historical European Martial Arts (HEMA) Club and experience the thrill of learning medieval and Renaissance swordsmanship! Whether you’re a complete beginner or an experienced practitioner, our club offers a welcoming environment for all skill levels. We study historical combat techniques using swords, longswords, and other period weapons as outlined in ancient manuals. Our training focuses on developing strength, precision, and discipline, while embracing the rich history of European martial arts. Come connect with like-minded enthusiasts, improve your technique, and have fun while getting a great workout. Meetings are held weekly, with opportunities for sparring, drills, and even competitions. All are welcome — no experience necessary!
summary: A HEMA club for learning ancient, medieval, and renaissance swordsmanship.

name: Big Orange Mentors
description: Big Orange Mentors is a student-led organization dedicated to making a meaningful difference in the lives of local children. We currently partner with Mount Olive Elementary School, where our members provide one-on-one mentoring to students navigating difficult home lifes. Through consistent support, encouragement, and relationship-building, we aim to offer stability and guidance to children who need it most. As we grow, we hope to expand our reach to additional schools in the Knoxville area. If this sounds like something you’d love to be part of, feel free to shoot us an email at cricha91@vols.utk.edu—we’d love to hear from you!
summary: A group of individuals working together to foster relationships with elementary aged children through one on one mentorship.

name: Students for Cultivating Change at the University of Tennessee
description: The Cultivating Change Foundation is a nonprofit organization dedicated to valuing and advancing LGBTQ+ individuals in the food and agriculture industries. Through advocacy, education, and community-building initiatives such as the Cultivating Change Summit, regional caucuses, and industry partnerships, the foundation fosters inclusion, visibility, and professional development for LGBTQ+ people in food and agriculture and their allies.
summary: Students for Cultivating Change at UT is a welcoming network for LGBTQ+ students and allies in food, agriculture and natural resources. The chapter promotes education, community, and visibility while fostering belonging within the University.

name: Tennessee Trombone Choir
summary: The Tennessee Trombone Choir (UT BONES) is a student organization that focuses on enriching music performance and education through the performance of trombone literature and pedagogy.

name: Leadership and Entrepreneurial Development club
summary: Empowering students with leadership and entrepreneurial skills through workshops, networking opportunities, and hands-on experiences. Giving students the opportunity to meet like-minded individuals who aspire to be leaders in any workforce

name: Society of Manufacturing Engineers
summary: The mission of the Society of Manufacturing Engineers is to accelerate the widespread adoption of manufacturing technologies and build talents and capabilities.

name: World Languages and Cultures Graduate Student Association
description: The primary purpose of the World Languages and Cultures Graduate Student Association (WLC-GSA) is to promote the general welfare of its members and to uphold the integrity of the Department of World Languages and Cultures. That is, to bring together students from across the department working with a diverse faculty and with various interests to improve the academic and social climate from within. The mission of the WLC-GSA is to provide a forum for graduate students to enhance their knowledge of world languages through service, social, and education-based activities and events and to promote professionalism and fellowship among graduate students. More than just a social organization, WLC-GSA is here to help you navigate Knoxville, your graduate program, your department, the university, and grad school in general through the peer mentorship program. We also proudly serve our fellow graduate students as liaison to the faculty and administration, both in and outside of the department.
summary: The vision of the WLC-GSA is to develop a strong Graduate Student Association that will benefit students before, during, and after their studies at the University of Tennessee and to further showcase and promote the excellence of the Department.

name: Sanskrit UTK
description: We are a chapter of Samskrita Bharati, an organization that strives to promote spoken Sanskrit meant for every day use. No knowledge of any Sanskrit though knowledge of any Indian language is helpful. We meet every week for an hour and learn through conversation and short live-demonstrations.
summary: We are a chapter of Samskrita Bharati, an organization that strives to promote spoken Sanskrit meant for every day use. No knowledge of any Sanskrit though knowledge of any Indian language is helpful.

name: Ukrainian Club
description: The Ukrainian Club is a student-led organization celebrating Ukrainian culture, heritage, and traditions on campus. We host cultural events, language workshops, social gatherings, and discussions on history and current affairs. The club collaborates closely with Knoxville-based nonprofit koloHUB and local businesses to support humanitarian initiatives. We welcome students of all backgrounds to learn, connect, and engage with Ukrainian culture. Whether you have Ukrainian roots or a curiosity about the culture, join our vibrant community!
summary: The Ukrainian Club celebrates Ukrainian culture, traditions, and history on campus through events, workshops, and discussions, collaborating with local nonprofit koloHUB and local businesses. All students are welcome to join and engage!

name: Game Develoment Club
description: This club is for learning and applying the artistic and technical skills used in developing video games. We meet weekly as a club, and our goal is to work together on a club project. We meet each Monday in the library in room 213 at 6:15pm.
summary: A club dedicated to designing and making video games.

name: Tashan at UT
description: Tashan at UT is a student-led Bollywood fusion dance team at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, dedicated to celebrating South Asian culture through dynamic and creative performances. Blending Bollywood with classical, contemporary, bhangra, and hip-hop styles, the team fosters cultural expression, artistic innovation, and community engagement. Through dance, Tashan at UT provides a space for students to connect, collaborate, and share their passion for South Asian performing arts.
summary: Tashan at UT is a Bollywood fusion dance team at UT Knoxville dedicated to celebrating South Asian culture through dance performances. The team fosters creativity, cultural expression, and community engagement by combining Bollywood with other dance form

name: Phoenix Literary and Arts Magazine
description: Phoenix is a student-run campus publication dedicated to the artistic expression of students, faculty, and alumni. Now celebrating over 60 years in publication, the magazine is known as a home for student artistry at the University of Tennessee Knoxville. The Phoenix staff has always sought to provide students with a platform to speak truth to power and share their own stories through art, and as an organization we will continue to do so.
summary: We are a magazine that publishes art, poems, and prose.

name: Orthodox Christian Fellowship (OCF)
summary: To transforms the lives of college students by guiding them along the path to Jesus Christ through His Church, cultivating a campus community of worship, witness, service, fellowship, and education.

name: Wellness Society at UTK
description: At The Wellness Society, we envision a world where holistic health, self-care, and resilience are celebrated and accessible to all. Through our expanding network of chapters and a global online community, we strive to inspire individuals to prioritize their well-being, cultivate balance, and embrace healthier lifestyles. By promoting our thoughtfully designed merchandise, we aim to spread our message, empower self-expression, and create a shared identity that champions mental and physical wellness worldwide. Together, we are building a movement that transforms lives — one connection, one post, and one product at a time.
summary: A club focused on your physical and mental well-being.

name: NAMI On Campus at The University of Tennessee Knoxville (NOC UTK)
description:
NAMI On Campus at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville is a student-led organization affiliated with the National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI), the nation's largest grassroots mental health organization, that promotes mental health awareness, education, and advocacy within the UTK community. Our mission is to provide a supportive environment where students can openly discuss mental health, connect with resources, and engage in initiatives to reduce stigma on campus. We strive to create a campus culture that prioritizes mental well-being through peer support, guest speakers, advocacy efforts, and awareness events. NOC UTK is open to all students, regardless of their personal experiences with mental health, and works closely with local NAMI affiliates to bring impactful programming and resources to students.

 

Founded April 2025
summary: NAMI On Campus at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville (NOC UTK) is a student-led organization affiliated with the National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI) that promotes mental health awareness, education, and advocacy within the UTK community.

name: Professional Association of Design, University of Tennessee Students
description:
We the students of the University of Tennessee, Knoxville Chapter of the Professional Association of Design have come together to enhance our education, network with professionals, and begin to define solutions which will ensure a healthy living and working environment for the total community.

AIGA student groups offer aspiring professionals the opportunity to get involved, create community, and build leadership skills. We find, as students passionate about design, a community of purpose and cultural experience that warrants our combined efforts in the advancement of our future profession, our respective activities in it, and the needs of the communities we will serve.

Design-interested students represent tomorrow’s professional designers and the future of AIGA. Forming an AIGA student group is the single best link between student life and professional practice. Guided by a faculty advisor who actively supports our group, we become leaders of on-campus design programming and events, uniting other students into a larger community and preparing them for their careers in the creative field.

To these ends, we establish this organization which is built on the bonds of the common professional interests that bring us together; and equally, creating a community that will sustain and enrich our association. Follow us on Instagram to learn more, @aigautk!
summary: We, the students of AIGA UTK, unite to enhance our education, network with professionals, and create a supportive design community. Through leadership, events, and cultural engagement, we bridge student life and professional practice, preparing for caree

name: Pakistan Student Association
description:
Pakistan Students Association (PSA) is a cultural organization seeking to introduce and promote rich Pakistani culture and traditions at UT, Knoxville. It also serves to bring together Pakistani students and scholars at the University of Tennessee and Oak Ridge National Laboratory to celebrate and exchange their culture. We would wholeheartedly welcome anyone who is interested to learn about Pakistan and wish to join.

We strive to serve and help Pakistani students with their studies and lives, providing them with a platform to unite and promote friendship among members. Our ultimate objective is to build a cultural bridge between our students and other communities at UTK through cultural and social events throughout the year. Being a small group of students, we look forward to bringing a small part of our homeland to our school and exchange our culture.
summary: Pakistan Students Association (PSA) is a cultural organization seeking to bring Pakistani culture to UT, Knoxville. It serves to bring together Pakistani students and scholars at UTK and ORNL and we would welcome anyone interested in our culture.

name: Thrive Health
description:
Thrive Health is a student-led organization dedicated to exploring and promoting functional

health and holistic medicine. Our mission is to integrate evidence-based practices with natural

healing approaches to support overall well-being, balance, and vitality. We aim to empower

students with knowledge, resources, and hands-on experiences in holistic wellness while

fostering a community of like-minded individuals passionate about health.

Our club offers monthly meetings featuring guest speakers, interactive workshops, and

discussions on topics such as chiropractic care, acupuncture, aromatherapy, yoga, meditation,

functional nutrition, and holistic healing practices. We also provide opportunities for community

outreach, volunteer work, and networking with professionals in the functional health field.

Whether you are pursuing a career in chiropractic, integrative medicine, nutrition, or holistic

wellness, or simply have a passion for exploring mind-body healing, Thrive Health is the perfect

place to learn, grow, and connect.
summary: Thrive Health promotes functional health & holistic medicine by integrating evidence-based & natural healing. We empower individuals through education, community outreach, & networking. Our student-led club offers workshops, guest speakers, & volunteer.

name: Law and Politics Club
description: We would have speakers from UT Law School and local lawyers in Knoxville in order to make connections. Meet-and-Great programming with lawyers in different fields and learn about possible job opportunities. Past students could come back and speak of their experience. There would be law school admission and LSAT preparation sessions, as well as admission speakers for local law schools. Resumé building and workshopping for law admissions. For the politics side, we could have campaign members and lawyers speak on their experience.
summary: This would be an organization where students could learn about law applications, processes, and make connections. Members are encouraged to explore the law field and meet fellow students interested in law and politics.

name: Network of Enlightened Women
description: Network of Enlightened Women (NeW) educates, equips, and empowers women on U.S. campuses with leadership skills, fostering intellectual diversity through events, debates, and professional growth to inspire action for freedom and traditional values. By providing a supportive community, leadership training, and opportunities to engage in national conferences, NeW empowers its members to confidently articulate their beliefs, challenge dominant narratives, and effect change in their communities and beyond.
summary: Network of Enlightened Women (NeW) empowers women on U.S. campuses with leadership skills, fostering intellectual diversity through events, debates, and professional growth to inspire action for freedom and traditional values.

name: TriVols Triathlon Club at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville
description:
TriVols is a vibrant and inclusive community of students at the University of Tennessee who share a passion for triathlon training and competition. Our mission is to bring together individuals of all skill levels to support one another in preparing for triathlons while fostering a sense of camaraderie and personal growth.

We recognize that there isn't just one way to train for triathlons. Whether you're an experienced athlete or a beginner looking to complete your first race, TriVols is the place for you. Our members come from diverse backgrounds, each with unique strengths and areas for improvement. Some focus on swimming to build endurance in the water, while others may concentrate on running or cycling. Our training sessions are designed to accommodate different needs, helping everyone achieve their personal goals.

Throughout the academic year, TriVols actively participates in triathlons around Knoxville and the surrounding areas. These events offer a great opportunity for members to practice racing strategies, build confidence, and proudly represent the University of Tennessee. Members wear custom University of Tennessee triathlon kits, showcasing our school spirit and unity as we race under the University’s name.

The highlight of our season comes at the end of March when we send a team to compete at the USA Triathlon Collegiate Club Nationals. In 2026, the competition will be held in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, offering our athletes a chance to compete at the national level against collegiate teams from across the country.

TriVols is more than just a club; it's a supportive community that encourages personal achievement and team success. Whether your goal is to compete nationally or simply enjoy the challenge of triathlon training, you’ll find your place here. Join us to train hard, race together, and make lifelong connections!
summary: TriVols is a University of Tennessee triathlon club fostering community and training for all skill levels. We race locally and nationally, culminating in the USA Triathlon Collegiate Club Nationals. Join us to train, race, and represent UT together!

name: Vulgate
description: VULGATE is a democratic, pluralistic, nonpartisan (not neutral), and secular civic organization focused on community action, political education, and public engagement around social, political, philosophical, and religious issues.
summary: Atheists for Liberty is an educational organization dedicated to defending the fundamental freedoms that support the human quest for knowledge and the separation of religion and government.

name: Adaptive Recreation Club
description: The adaptive recreation club is an organization designed to provide inclusive recreational opportunities for individuals of all abilities, including those with physical, cognitive, and sensory disabilities. Our club focuses on fostering a supportive and accessible environment where participants can engage in a wide range of activities, such as sports, fitness, arts, and social events. Our goal is to promote physical, mental, and social well-being through activities tailored to meet the specific needs of each individual. Our club may offer modified equipment, trained staff, and specialized programs to ensure everyone can enjoy the benefits of recreation in a safe and welcoming space.
summary: The adaptive recreation club is an organization designed to provide inclusive recreational opportunities for individuals of all abilities, with the intention of offering the benefits of recreation in a safe and welcoming environment.

name: Pre-Law Pathways
description: Pre-Law Pathways at the University of Tennessee is an organization dedicated to helping students explore and identify their legal specialization before attending law school. Through workshops, guest speakers, networking events, and resources, members gain insight into various areas of law, from corporate to criminal, ensuring they make informed decisions about their legal career path. The group focuses on providing valuable exposure to the legal field, allowing students to strengthen their law school applications while discovering their areas of interest within the law.
summary: Pre-Law Pathways at UTK is a student organization offering exposure to various legal fields, helping students explore their interests and strengthen their law school applications through events, workshops, and networking.

name: First Generation Investors
description:
First Generation Investors (FGI) is a 5019(c3) non-profit organization that teaches high school students located in underserved communities about financial wellness and investing. With more than 2700 high school students impacted, we aim to teach students the important concepts of finance to allow them to make valuable financial decisions in the future.    

Our mission is to inspire students to take control of their financial futures and have practical knowledge on building generational wealth. By completing the program, high school students are given real money to invest in the stock market!

Benefits for Students:

-- Networking opportunities with top companies (JPMorgan, Northwestern Mutual, Goldman Sachs, and more!)

-- Service Hours
-- Professional and Career Development Opportunities
-- Good to add on resume
 
Qualifications:
 
-- pursuing a bachelor’s degree
-- interest in volunteering for the community
-- good oral and written communication skills
-- Ability to work effectively with a team
-- Ability to work in a diverse and inclusive setting 
 
Join a growing community today!
 
https://groupme.com/join_group/107465802/ynHMml1X
summary: We are an organization that educate high school students on financial wellness and investing to inspire them to have valuable knowledge on financial choices. We value education, service, and making a positive impact on community. All majors welcome!

name: Rocky Top Student Veterans of America
description: The Rocky Top Student Veterans of America (SVA) is an organization at the University of Tennessee dedicated to supporting military veterans as they transition to academic life. We provide a strong community, resources, and opportunities for personal and professional growth, while advocating for veterans’ success on campus and beyond. Through leadership development, networking, and service activities, we help our members thrive in their education and future careers.
summary: The Rocky Top Student Veterans of America (SVA) supports military veterans at UTK by fostering community, providing resources, and advocating for their success in higher education. The group promotes camaraderie, leadership, and veteran awareness at UTK

name: The Salt Company
description:
We exist to give GOOD NEWS TO THE VOLS!

 

Our goal is to introduce UTK students to the life of Jesus and to invite them into gospel-centered community. 

 

Every Tuesday at 7pm we come together for Bible teaching, worship, and friendship! Throughout the week, we have small groups on campus that are led by students! 

 

Check out our Instagram @saltutk
summary:
We exist to give GOOD NEWS TO THE VOLS! 

Our goal is to reach UTK students with the gospel of Jesus Christ and to invite them into gospel-centered community!

name: Midtown Community Church
description:
Midtown will hold weekly meetings on Sundays where members will gather for prayer, worship, and study of the Scriptures.

There will also be a variety of community events, small groups, Bible studies, and service opportunities regularly available for students
summary: The purpose of Midtown Community shall be to gather in fellowship and worship as followers of Jesus Christ, seeking spiritual growth, support, and encouragement in our faith.

name: Volunteer's Theme Park Engineering Group
description:
Vol TPEG aims to accomplish three goals being learn, design, and ride. 

We work to educate our members on the practices, protocols, safety standards, accidents, successes, history, current trends, and technology within the themed entertainment industry. This shall be accomplished through presentations, tours, and interviews with industry professionals.

We work to organize group trips to design competitions, safety standard committee meetings, theme parks, and other beneficial trips that will provide useful connections, beneficial updates, and educational opportunities.

We strive to create a fun and energetic environment where like-minded individuals can learn, design, and connect in the amusement industry. This will be accomplished through trips to theme parks, regular meetings, and events.
summary: We are a club for students to explore the themed entertainment industry through presentations, tours, competitions, and trips. We connect, learn, and grow through hands-on experiences, industry insights, and a shared passion for amusement design.

name: Homelessness Education & Advocacy Leaders
description: The mission of Homelessness Education & Advocacy Leaders (HEAL) is to educate University of Tennessee students on homelessness-related issues and empower them to support Knoxville’s homeless community. Members will provide access to resources by building relationships with local shelters and participating in student-led volunteer opportunities to provide support to those affected by homelessness in Knoxville.
summary: HEAL @ UTK aims to educate students and empower them to advocate for the homeless community in the Knoxville area.

name: Book To Build
description:
1. The mission of Book to Build is to foster an environment for students at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville who have interests in STEM, to gain experience beyond those required by the classroom. In addition, the goal of this organization is to take the ideas and concepts learned from the classroom (the book) and create/foster new ideas (the build). Events include but are not limited to hands-on workshops, tutoring, projects, internship prep, and collaborative means of connecting with various forms of other engineering outside of one’s chosen major.
  2. Short Term Goals:
  3. Spring Event - STEM Project Symposium
  4. Provide balance to all types of engineering.
  5. Collaborate!
  6. Long Term Goals:
  7. Become a strong resource for Engineering students.
  8. Provide assistance and potentially gain traction from other majors outside of engineering.
  9. Connect with the Office of Professional Practice to a substantial extent.
summary: Book to Build is a STEM project organization at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville that bridges classroom theory and real world engineering by turning ideas into working prototypes. BTB gives students the opportunity to design, build, and test ideas!

name: CSI Student Collaboration @ University of Tennessee, Knoxville
description: CSI Student Collaboration at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, is an organization affiliated with the local CSI Knoxville Chapter. It welcomes all students interested in the construction science industry. CSI is a national organization with chapters across the country, including professionals such as architects, engineers, and contractors. In this chapter, students will learn about the industry through site visits, networking events, and engaging lectures, while collaborating closely with the CSI Knoxville Chapter.
summary: CSI Student Collaboration at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, is an organization affiliated with the local CSI Knoxville Chapter.

name: Mental Health Coalition
description: Student representatives from multiple departments on campus will combine to share resources, perspectives, and together reach a common goal of improving student’s mental health on campus. We aim to inspire students to prioritize their well-being, seek support when needed, and create a community where mental health challenges are openly addressed without stigma. Faculty, staff, and campus departments are also being encouraged to better understand and respond to students' mental health needs, fostering more empathetic and inclusive environments. We are also working to change student behaviors and mindsets, encouraging them to take proactive steps for their well-being and support one another.
summary: The Mental Health Coalition will address improvements within the current mental initiatives on campus as well as form new initiatives to improve accessibility and influence.

name: Voltaire
description:
Voltaire is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering deeper connections between students and their spirituality, critical thinking, and social awareness. Grounded in the wisdom of history’s most influential philosophers, our meetings focus on examining philosophical ideas from antiquity and investigating their relevance to the complex realities of modern life. We strive to make learning both intellectually rigorous and deeply engaging, offering an open platform for students to explore Enlightenment ideals and cultivate personal and collective growth.

As a member of Voltaire, you can expect to:

  • Participate in high-level discussions and seminars on major philosophical theories.

  • Explore academic and career pathways related to philosophy and related disciplines.

  • Apply philosophical reasoning to current events, ethical dilemmas, and social issues.

  • Contribute to the campus community through meaningful, thought-provoking initiatives.

  • Share your philosophical insights through journaling, essays, and group publications.

Our mission is guided by the timeless Socratic insight: “The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.” At Voltaire, this quote isn’t just a mantra—it’s a mindset. We believe that intellectual humility is the first step toward true understanding. Philosophy, at its core, is about engaging with arguments critically and impartially, using the tools of logic and reason to approach truth—not from a place of arrogance, but of curiosity.

As a diverse student body, our varied experiences allow us to approach philosophical questions from both objective and subjective perspectives. Voltaire welcomes these perspectives in a space that is nonjudgmental, inclusive, and respectful of all viewpoints. We do not promote dogma or ideology—only the values of equality, camaraderie, open discourse, and professionalism.

Not all members may have formal training in epistemology, metaphysics, or logic—and that’s okay. Voltaire is designed to be accessible, challenging, and enriching, regardless of your background. With guidance from advisors, guest speakers, and educators, we aim to elevate every member’s capacity for critical reasoning and philosophical insight.

Our name, Voltaire, honors François-Marie Arouet—the Enlightenment writer and thinker whose fierce advocacy for reason, freedom of thought, and individual conscience continues to inspire generations. In his spirit, our organization seeks to embody those ideals and provide a community where the pursuit of wisdom never ends.
summary: Voltaire is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering deeper connections between students and their spirituality, critical thinking, and social awareness.

name: Medical Ambassadors at the University of Tennessee
description:
The University of Tennessee Medical Center Ambassadors are a team of student leaders dedicated to serving UT Medical Center. Our members guide hospital tours, support 865 programming, and assist staff in fostering a welcoming and professional environment for patients, families, and visitors.

Through these efforts, we connect students to meaningful healthcare experiences while promoting leadership, mentorship, and service. By building relationships within the hospital and community, UTMCK Ambassadors develop professional skills, support the mission of the Medical Center, and help shape the next generation of medical leaders.
summary: UTMCK Ambassadors are student leaders connecting students with hands-on opportunities at UT Medical Center. We build future medical professionals through service, mentorship, and community, while promoting leadership, compassion, and excellence

name: K-Pop Dance Association
description:
K-Pop Dance Association (KPDA)

The K-Pop Dance Association (KPDA) is a community at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, dedicated to fostering a love for dance influenced by K-Pop and modern Asian culture. Our mission is to create a dynamic and inclusive environment where dancers of all skill levels can come together to learn, perform, and share their passion.

Our Goals

1. Cultivate an Inclusive and Welcoming Environment

We believe that dance is for everyone. Our club is a supportive space where dancers, from complete beginners to advanced performers, can build their skills and confidence. By welcoming all levels of experience, we create a community built on shared enthusiasm and mutual support.

 

2. Promote K-Pop Dance on Campus and Beyond

The KPDA is committed to showcasing the energy and artistry of K-Pop dance. We perform at a variety of events, including university gatherings, club exhibitions, and local festivals throughout Knoxville. Our goal is to share the excitement of K-Pop with a broader audience and introduce them to the cultural richness of this art form.

 

3. Inspire Cultural and Artistic Appreciation

Beyond the performances, our mission is to inspire a deeper appreciation for the cultural significance of K-Pop dance and music. By actively participating in community events, we strive to broaden understanding and highlight the unique artistic contributions of modern Asian culture. We aim to be more than just a dance club; we are a platform for cultural exchange and creative expression.
summary: The K-Pop Dance Association is dedicated to fostering a vibrant community of dancers at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, united by a passion for dance influenced by K-Pop and modern Asian culture.

name: Computational Biology and Sciences Club
description: • The Computational Biology and Sciences club exists to serve as a nucleation point for all students, faculty, and staff interested in the field of applied computational sciences. Meetings will be bi-weekly and will consist of discussions of interesting papers, guest lecturers, presentations, and more.
summary: An organization that brings together students interested in the computational sciences. Meetings will consist of discussions of interesting papers, guest lecturers, presentations, and more.

name: Middle Eastern North African Student Association
summary: MENASA will serve as a community for students interested in MENA culture. Our goal is to help those who come from MENA heritage to embrace and be proud of where they come from, and to also share the beauty of Arab culture with other students on campus.

name: Letters of Love UTK
summary: The mission of Letters of Love is to provide emotional support to children battling serious illnesses through the creation of hand-crafted, heartfelt letters. We aim to build a community of love and kindness, empowering young people to change the world.

name: GradCru
description: GradCru is a graduate and professional student organization with the purpose of introducing students to Jesus Christ, helping them to grow in their faith, encouraging them to live life passionately under the lordship of Christ, and inspiring commitment to advancing the purposes of God in the world. GradCru hosts weekly Bible study meetings as well as small groups and social gatherings.
summary: GradCru is a Christian campus ministry for graduate and professional students. We build caring community together through Bible study, small groups, and social gatherings.

name: Haslam Business Law Society
description: The Haslam Business Law Society exists to bridge the gap between the fields of business and law by providing career exploration, hands-on learning opportunities, and law school preparation to students interested in this intersection. Our mission is to empower Haslam College of Business students, and others across campus, with meaningful exposure to legal concepts, alumni connections, and career pathways that involve legal and regulatory frameworks, both within and outside of law school.
summary: The Haslam Business Law Society is a student-led organization dedicated to exploring the intersection of business, law, and policy. Through speaker events and networking opportunities, we prepare members for careers in business law and related fields.

name: Society for Collegiate Leadership & Achievement
description:
The Society for Collegiate Leadership & Achievement (SCLA), the leading College-to-Career Honor
Society, empowers students to excel academically and professionally. We celebrate achievement, foster
personal growth, and provide the tools, connections, and credentials needed to build successful,
purpose-driven careers. Our members gain the confidence and skills to thrive in their fields while making
a meaningful impact on their communities. SCLA’s mission is to empower students for both academic
and professional success by celebrating their achievements and providing career-readiness tools.
Membership is offered by invitation after meeting the GPA requirements.
summary: The Society for Collegiate Leadership & Achievement is an accredited and nationally recognized honor society that celebrates academic excellence and empowers students with skills, connections, and credentials needed to succeed in college and beyond.

name: VAW Global Health Alliances @ UTK
description: The purpose of the VAW Global Health Alliances Outreach (VAW Outreach) is to provide medical education to underserved communities, both locally and internationally. The organization aims to enhance the health and well-being of individuals through volunteer services and community outreach initiatives. The UTK chapter will be a group of students committed to these goals who will fundraise and educate themselves in preparation for an international medical service trip. Throughout this process, we aim to provide students with opportunities for experience in healthcare and community service.
summary: VAW @ UTK is a group of students who will educate themselves in preparation for a medical service trip to an LMIC. We will collaborate with local and international health organizations to extend the reach and impact of our mission.

name: La Voz Estudiantil de Latino America
description: VELA aims to create a community to celebrate and raise awareness on Hispanic heritage at UT through various events such as workshops, summits, tabling, and more.
summary: VELA helps provide a safe space for Latino students, offering support, advocacy, and a centralized resource hub. We strive to provide a safe space for students to express concerns, raise awareness to issues pertaining to the Latino community.

name: The Horological Society
description: The Horological Society is a student organization at the University of Tennessee devoted to the appreciation and study of timekeeping in all its forms, with a focus on wristwatches. Our mission is to create a space where students can explore horology as both a technical science and a cultural art. By blending engineering, design, and history, we highlight the interdisciplinary nature of horology while connecting mechanical innovation with aesthetics and heritage. Members gain opportunities to learn about watchmaking, materials science, and design, while also appreciating the historical and cultural significance of timekeeping.
summary: The Horological Society is dedicated to the study of timekeeping and watches, blending engineering, design, and history while fostering community through workshops and events.

name: Percussion at UTK
description: The mission of Percussion at UTK is to inspire, educate, and support student percussionists by providing opportunities for personal and professional growth in the field of percussion.
summary: The mission of Percussion at UTK is to inspire, educate, and support student percussionists by providing opportunities for personal and professional growth in the field of percussion.

name: American Constitution Society at the Winston College of Law
description:
The American Constitution Society for Law and Policy (ACS) is the nation’s foremost progressive legal organization, with a diverse nationwide network that includes nearly 200 student and lawyer chapters, and progressive lawyers, students, judges, scholars, elected officials, and advocates.

The ACS's mission is to support and advocate for laws and legal systems that redress the founding failures of our Constitution, strengthen our democratic legitimacy, uphold the rule of law, and realize the promise of equality for all, including people of color, women, LGBTQ+ people, people with disabilities, and other historically excluded communities.
summary: ACS is a progressive constitutional law student organization.

name: Mu Sigma Lambda
summary: We are a co-ed sports administration fraternity to help students break into the sports world and develop meaningful relationships with their peers.

name: Ducks Unlimited at UT
description:
What is Ducks Unlimited? – ducks.org 

non-profit conservation organization 

Focused on conserving wetland habitat and waterfowl populations 

Over 15 million acres conserved 

Chapters across the nation focused on sharing DU’s mission and contributing to fundraising efforts 

Ducks University is the program for collegiate chapters 

What Does a Collegiate Chapter do? 

Develops leadership and professional skills 

Meetings with its members – chapter updates, guest speakers, future goals/plans Hosts a community fundraising event 

Building conservation events and community 

Member socials 

Conferences – State Convention and Third Term (collegiate leadership summit) Marketing – social media, locally, merchandise, etc. 

Has fun!
summary: What is Ducks Unlimited? – ducks.org non-profit conservation organization Focused on conserving wetland habitat and waterfowl populations Over 15 million acres conserved Chapters across the nation focused on sharing DU’s mission

name: South Asian Society
description: SASA is dedicated to bringing students together and creating a campus community where everyone feels included and welcome. Through a mix of fun events, casual hangouts, and cultural celebrations, we aim to help students connect, share experiences, and make meaningful memories. Whether it’s a lively event, a chill snack sesh, or celebrating traditions, SASA strives to make student life engaging, enjoyable, and full of good energy.
summary: SASA brings students together through fun events, cultural celebrations, and casual hangouts to connect, laugh, and make campus feel like home. We host events, casual gatherings, and cultural celebrations so that everyone can connect and create memories.

name: Vol of Duty
description: Vol of Duty is a literary gaming website for the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, aimed at providing an opportunity for students to publish articles and participate in a community that analyzes and discusses all forms of video games. Both undergraduate and graduate students can practice the process of publication by reviewing the Vol of Duty writing style guide and submitting their article on the Vol of Duty Website. After the submission is received, a VOD editor will begin working with the student to complete copy edits before approving and publishing the article to our website. Our website publishes an extensive variety of topics, including reviews, nostalgia, art, and culture. Perhaps you're reviewing nostalgic games or analyzing the cultural influences behind a game's art style. Vol of Duty wants to show that gaming is strategic, analytical, and educational while supporting creative ideas and student voices.
summary: Vol of Duty is a literary gaming website for the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, aimed at providing an opportunity for students to publish articles and participate in a community that analyzes and discusses all forms of videogames.

name: Free Grace Vols
description:
We believe that eternal life is a free gift found only through faith in Jesus Christ alone. If you are unsure that you have received this gift, please watch this video. The moment we believe in Jesus Christ as our Savior we are miraculously born into the family of God, and from that moment our salvation can never be lost. To find exactly what our club believes and preaches about salvation, here is an excellent summary.

We schedule times to study the Bible together and times to go out and preach the gospel both on the campus and in the community, declaring the good news about the free gift that we have received by faith, showing others from the scriptures how they can receive it too.

We develop meaningful friendships with each other, encouraging and helping each other, as well as provoking one another to love God, love others, and perform good works such as preaching the good news of the gospel to those who need to hear it.

Finally, we provide a local church home for students to plug into, being sponsored by Valley View Baptist Church conveniently located 10 minutes away from campus. We offer transportation to services if needed. Our club is connected with the Rooted Young Adults, where many young professionals and students at other campuses are included in our mission.

We offer many activities through the Valley View Baptist Church young adults ministry, doing various fun activities each semester in Knoxville, Gatlinburg/Sevierville, and the Great Smoky Mountains!

Romans 3

24 Being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus:

25 Whom God hath set forth to be a propitiation through faith in his blood, to declare his righteousness for the remission of sins that are past, through the forbearance of God;

26 To declare, I say, at this time his righteousness: that he might be just, and the justifier of him which believeth in Jesus.
summary: We believe that eternal life is a free gift found only through Jesus Christ. In our club, we prove the absolute freeness of salvation in Christ, help those who are saved to share their faith to others, and develop meaningful friendships along the way!

name: SGI Vols Buddhists Club
description: The fundamental purpose of SGI Vols Buddhists club is the pursuit of truth as well as development of scholarship, to enable all people to cultivate their characters and enjoy fulfilling and happy lives. SGI Vols Buddhist club seeks to contribute to peace, culture and education based on the philosophy and ideals of the Buddhism of Nichiren Daishonin as shared by the Soka Gakkai International. We seek to establish life long friendships between students that begin within the University of Tennessee and extends outward into the community at large.
summary: SGI Vols Buddhists club seeks to contribute to peace, culture and education based on the philosophy and ideals of the Buddhism of Nichiren Daishonin as shared by the Soka Gakkai International.

name: Rodeo at UT
description: UT Rodeo is a club for anyone interested in the western lifestyle, rodeo, or if you want to learn and try something new. The club will teach behind the scenes such as working the chutes, cleaning, grating, and much more. We plan on having social events, watching local rodeos,even some practice in the sport using dummies, and having guest speakers. UT Rodeo is meant to bring students together who are interested or already love rodeo. Experience is not required to join. Just your passion and interest.
summary: UT Rodeo is a club for anyone interested in the western lifestyle, rodeo, or if you want to learn and try something new.

name: The University of Tennessee Winston College of Law Pickle-Law
description: This organization aims to promote law student wellness by facilitating weekly pickleball games for students to participate in. These games are limited to law students only and occur on a weekly basis, weather permitting. Pickle-Law also provides an opportunity for law students to meet other students outside of a classroom setting.
summary: The purpose of the Club is to facilitate student wellness by encouraging law student participation in weekly pickleball games

name: Students of East Africa
description: Students of East Africa (S.E.A.) embodies and represents the diverse cultures and ethnicities of East Africa at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. This organization celebrates the heritage of countries such as Ethiopia, Eritrea, Somalia, Uganda, Djibouti, Burundi, Rwanda, Tanzania, Kenya, Sudan, and South Sudan. Through community service, S.E.A. will provide a means for students to connect with the Knoxville community and beyond by lending a helping hand to those in need. The overall goal of this organization is to create a safe and inclusive space where professional and social connections can grow. S.E.A. will offer opportunities for students to learn about various cultures, develop meaningful relationships, and unite the East African community as a whole.
summary: Students of East Africa (S.E.A.) at UTK unites students from East African backgrounds, fosters cultural awareness, promotes service in the Knoxville community, and creates an inclusive space for social and professional connections.

name: Vols for Smiles
description:
The mission of Vols for Smiles is to raise awareness of, fundraise for, promote, and support SmileTrain’s mission in providing cleft care to children internationally.

Any student who feels led to humanitarian aid, medical mission work, or volunteerism is encouraged to inform themselves and aid us in our effort to support the deployment of essential healthcare in the form of cleft and lip palate surgeries and care.
summary: Vols for Smiles focuses on providing direct support to Smile Train, a global non-profit dedicated to providing dental care in the form of cleft palate surgeries to underprivileged children while promoting dental health and JOY at UTK!

name: Girls Club Basketball Team at Tennessee
summary: A competitive and fun environment for players to continue their basketball career. This team will allow them to improve their basketball skills as well as improve character development both on and off the court.

name: Ghana Students Association-UTK
summary: The Ghana Students’ Association at UTK supports Ghanaian students, fosters academic and professional growth, promotes culture, and builds connections within the university and local community.

name: Student Association of Latinos United for the Development in Health
description: The mission of the Student Association of Latinos United for Development in Health is to empower Latino and first-generation students interested in pursuing careers in healthcare, providing mentorship, opportunities, and resources for professional development. SALUD also has the goal to work with the Latino community in Knoxville and surrounding areas, promoting more equitable and accessible healthcare.
summary: SALUD empowers Latino and first-generation students pursuing healthcare careers through mentorship, resources, and professional development, while also serving the Knoxville Latino community to promote equitable, accessible healthcare.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Searcy Chapter
description: Bound together by tradition and our common interest in the law, we share these core values: Integrity, Compassion, Courage, Professionalism, Service, Diversity, and Innovation. Phi Alpha Delta is the world’s preeminent law fraternity because of our dedication to service:  Service to the student, the school, the profession, and the community. It is through devotion to the ideals of Integrity, Compassion, Courage, Professionalism, Service, Diversity, and Innovation that we better both our profession and our community. Through accepting our differences, we become not just a law fraternity, but a family.
