summary: Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International strives to be the premier legal professional organization with a diverse membership that cultivates leaders and serves as a voice for the role of legal professionalism in our society.

name: Black Musicians Alliance!
description: The Black Musicians Alliance is dedicated to empowering and uplifting all minority artists across all musical genres. We strive to foster a supportive community that celebrates cultural heritage, advocates for equity and representation, and provides opportunities for professional growth.Through education, collaboration, and advocacy, we aim to amplify the unheard voices in the music industry, ensuring that our contributions are recognized, respected, and cherished.
summary: The Black Musicians Alliance is dedicated to empowering and uplifting all minority artists across all musical genres. We strive to foster a supportive community that celebrates cultural heritage, advocates for equity and representation.

name: Office of Campus Culture & Community
summary: The Division of Student Life has established the Office for Campus Culture and Community to foster a supportive and connected community for all undergraduate and graduate students.

name: Linguistics Club
description: We are a group of linguistics majors and minors, or those interested in linguistics, who meet to discuss anything linguistics-related. We are here to assist with anything.
summary: We are a group of linguistics majors and minors, or those interested in linguistics, who meet to discuss anything linguistics-related. We are here to assist with anything.

name: Smokies Clarinet Choir
summary: The mission of The Smokies Clarinet Choir is to unite clarinetists of all skill levels to perform diverse repertoire, foster musical growth and community, and enrich Knoxville’s musical culture through the artistry of the clarinet.

name: National Wild Turkey Federation @UTK
description: The purpose of the National Wild Turkey Federation at the University of Tennessee shall be to Conserve and Protect: Today, there are huntable turkey populations in 49 states. Through advocacy and education, we hope that the future generation of turkey hunters are afforded that same opportunity to pursue Wild Turkeys that we have. In today’s world of habitat fragmentation, modern forestry management, and evolved hunting techniques, the Wild Turkey faces a new battle. It is our hope to help bring hunters, conservationists, and educators together as a community to proactively approach the issues facing today’s turkey populations.
summary: Collegiate participation in the NWTF’s mission has the ability to positively impact the conservation of the wild turkey and the preservation of our hunting heritage.

name: Active Minds Club
description: Active Minds is a non-profit organization that works to raise awareness for mental health for youth and young adults, as well as decrease the stigmatization surrounding mental health. Active Minds amplifies diverse voices of youth and young adults who are improving mental health norms for all.
summary: Active Minds mobilizes youth and young adults to lead a transformative movement in mental health, forever changing how it is valued and prioritized in society.

name: Pre-Health Artists Collective
description: The Pre-Health Artists Collective is a student-led organization that brings together individuals interested in both the arts and healthcare. The club provides a unique space for pre-health students to express creativity, reduce stress, and explore the ways art can strengthen empathy, communication, and cultural understanding in medicine and dentistry. Members engage in activities such as collaborative art projects, creative workshops, and community service events that use art to connect with underprivileged or vulnerable populations. The organization also hosts discussions on how creative practices can enhance resilience and humanistic skills, preparing future healthcare providers to treat patients as whole individuals. By combining artistic expression with professional growth, the Pre-Health Artists Collective fosters a supportive community that values balance, compassion, and innovation in healthcare.
summary: It is a student group that connects creativity with healthcare preparation. Through art projects, workshops, and service, members explore how creativity supports empathy, communication, and balance in future healthcare careers

name: Formula – Society of Automotive Engineers
description: FSAE is a student led organization focusing on the design, manufacturing, and optimization of a formula-style racecar. Each year, members work through the full engineering and business cycle to create a competitive vehicle. The team requires interdisciplinary contributions from all fields and participates in an annual competition where universities from across the world showcase and compete their creations.
summary: FSAE is a student-led team designing, building, and optimizing a formula-style racecar. Members complete the full engineering and business cycle annually, requiring interdisciplinary skills, and compete against universities worldwide.

name: SheThrives
description: SheThrives is an organization that promotes women's health and safety in a variety of different ways through service, communication with peers, and activities. It is a supportive group of women who want to empower themselves and others to speak up. Anyone passionate about the topic is welcome to join. Monthly activities will be done in meetings to make a difficult topic seem more approachable to discuss.
summary: SheThrives is an organization that promotes women's health and safety in a variety of different ways through service, communication with peers, and activities. It is a supportive group of women who want to empower themselves and others to speak up.

name: Neuroscience, Psychology, and Law Association
description:
The Neuroscience, Psychology, and Law Association (NPLA) explores how neuroscience and psychology, which study the mind, brain, and nervous system, intersect with the law.

- The organization explores how findings from neuroscience and psychology influence legal areas such as criminal law, health law, family law, tort law, and contract law.

- Members engage in guest speaker events, case discussions, and reviews of books and articles that connect scientific research on cognition, emotion, and behavior to legal issues such as intent, consent, custody, evidence, reasonableness, capacity, privacy, sentencing, rehabilitation, human rights, ethics, policy, and justice.

NPLA’s mission is to advance understanding of how scientific insights into the mind, brain, and behavior can enhance fairness, reasoning, comprehension, decision-making, and overall justice within the legal system.
summary: The Neuroscience, Psychology, and Law Association (NPLA) explores how neuroscience and psychology, which study the mind, brain, and nervous system, intersect with the law.

name: Promoting Electric Propulsion Boat Club
description: The PEP Boat Club will compete in the annual Promoting Electric Propulsion competition against other universities hosted by the American Society of Naval Engineers (ASNE). The club will also have an associated ASNE chapter giving students access to networking and other engineering opportunities. Throughout the academic year students will research and then construct a racing boat for the competition. Qualifying members will then travel to the competition to race the boat. The club provides engineering experience for those with interest in the boating or marine industries.
summary: The PEP Boat Club competes in the annual Promoting Electric Propulsion competition hosted by the American Society of Naval Engineers (ASNE). Students build a competition racing boat and will gain engineering experience.

name: Vol Figure Skating Club
description: The Figure Skating Club welcomes skaters of all levels—from beginners to competitive athletes—to share their passion for the sport. Members have opportunities to practice regularly, improve skills, perform in exhibitions, and participate in team events. The club fosters a supportive and inclusive community that encourages personal growth, discipline, and friendship both on and off the ice. Whether you’re skating for fun, fitness, or competition, our club provides a place to stay active and connect with others who love figure skating.
summary: VOL Figure Skating Club provides students of all skill levels the opportunity to skate, train, and connect with others who share a love for the sport. Members can participate in practices, performances, and team events in a supportive, fun environment.

name: Gaelic Football Club at the University of Tennessee
description: The Gaelic Football Club at the University of Tennessee is a student-run sports organization dedicated to introducing and promoting the traditional Irish sport of Gaelic football on campus. The club offers students of all skill levels opportunities to learn, practice, and compete in a fun, inclusive, and team-oriented environment. Beyond athletic development, the club fosters cultural appreciation, camaraderie, and community engagement by sharing the history and spirit of Gaelic football with the broader university community.
summary: The Gaelic Football Club at the University of Tennessee is a student-run sports club open to all skill levels, dedicated to playing and promoting Gaelic football while building community and sharing Irish culture on campus.

name: LCMS U (at UTK)
description:
A campus ministry dedicated to bringing UT students to Christ in the confessional Lutheran Church (Missouri Synod)

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pZFxO876M5U
summary: A campus ministry dedicated to bringing UT students to Christ in the confessional Lutheran Church (Missouri Synod)

name: Natural Resource and Environmental Economics Club
description: The Natural Resource and Environmental Club is an organization centered around the NREE major but open to anyone interested. It's primary purpose is to help educate students on different career paths associated with environmental work as a whole. On top of that, it will help aid students in networking via guest speakers and field trips.
summary: Educating and networking students interested in economic and environmental career paths.

name: St. Mary Coptic Orthodox Fellowship
summary: An organization for students to engage with the Oriental Orthodox faith and is a place for those that are of the orthodox faith or for those that simply want to know more about the faith.

name: Hands to Hearts Knoxville
description: Hands to Hearts Knoxville is the UTK chapter of the Memphis-based organization that supports children with heart conditions through fundraising and awareness. All proceeds support Le Bonheur Children’s Hospital and its pediatric cardiac programs.
summary: Hands to Hearts Knoxville is the UTK chapter of the Memphis-based organization that supports children with heart conditions through fundraising and awareness. All proceeds support Le Bonheur Children’s Hospital and its pediatric cardiac programs.

name: Phi Gamma Nu
description: Phi Gamma Nu is a professional development organization that unites students through a shared commitment to professional development, philanthropy, and community. Founded on the values of diversity, integrity and entrepreneurial spirit, PGN empowers members to excel as leaders in business and beyond. Through hands-on experiences, networking opportunities, and a supportive brotherhood, members of Phi Gamma Nu cultivate the skills, confidence, and connections needed to succeed in their future careers.
summary: Phi Gamma Nu is a professional development organization that unites students through a shared commitment to professional development, philanthropy, and community.

name: Graveyard Shift Writing Club
description:
The Graveyard Shift Writing Club is one that fills a niche here at UTK. This organization appeals directly to writers of horror, thriller, or mystery in all mediums. While the GSW Club is primarily a resource and group for recreational writers from different majors, English majors are more than welcome to apply. The purpose of this club is to provide an environment for writers of the macabre to have their work seen and critiqued by peers of the same genre. The Graveyard Shift Writing Club offers a diverse group of like-minded peers where you will get a more structured workshopping experience compared to other, (slightly) more casual writing groups on campus. Since we also want to encourage community building, we urge members to present their work at Writers' Block, attend club outings (movies and literary events), and request brainstorming meetings. We do ask that all prospective members fill out an application form, including a short submission, to ensure that our organization is one that can offer what they're looking for.

Please fill out this application form :)
summary: The Graveyard Shift Writing Club is oriented towards writers of the macabre and non-English majors.We want to give recreational writers with an interest in the horror genre a place to have workshop experiences without having to enroll in a class.

name: Central Asian Society
description:
The Central Asian Society (CAS) seeks to enrich the University of Tennessee’s cultural landscape by raising awareness of Central Asian heritage and providing representation for Central Asian communities. Through service-oriented, social, and cultural activities, CAS fosters diversity, promotes cultural understanding, and supports inclusion across campus.

CAS also aims to build an inclusive community where students can celebrate their cultural identities, engage in discussions on issues affecting Central Asian Americans, and participate in meaningful social events that strengthen connections among members. The organization advocates for diversity and solidarity by working collaboratively with other cultural groups to emphasize the values of inclusion and cross-cultural unity.
summary: Promotes Central Asian heritage and inclusion at the University of Tennessee through cultural, social, and educational events that foster understanding and cross-cultural collaboration.

name: getinVOLved
description: The organization getinVOLved is an on-campus organization that works to get students engaged on campus. We have monthly BINGO cards for people to complete to win awesome prizes. All the activities on the card are campus events and student engagement activities.
summary:
The purpose of getinVOLved is to increase engagement in on-campus student
activities and events. It's also to make UT feel more like a home to the students who attend.

name: Human Development and Family Science Student Organization
description: Student organization for Human Development and Family Science students at the University of Tennessee. Our mission is to foster community and professional development among HDFS Major and Minor Students.
summary: Fellowship and professional development organization for Human Development and Family Science Majors/Minors at the University of Tennessee

name: Silver Wings, Lt General Frank M. Andrews Chapter
description: Silver Wings has been around since 1952 and has popped up multiple times at UTK. Silver wings is a National committee that esteems it self with Military and pedestrian relations. Its mission is to create proactive civic leaders through Community service and Knowledge. ANYBODY is allowed to join this club regardless of affiliation, gender, race, etc. If you are interested in ties to the military, scholarship opportunities, internship opportunities, meeting people, or community service, this is the club for you.
summary:
National, professional organization dedicated to creating proactive,
knowledgeable, and effective civic leaders through community service and education
about national defense. Silver Wings is Air Force ROTC-affiliated, but anybody can join.

name: Friends of MSF
description:
About MSF

This short video talks more in depth about MSF/Doctors Without Borders' mission. Our chapter, Friends of MSF, aims to support this organization at a university level. The mission of Friends of MSF is to raise awareness and knowledge about MSF’s work in the field and about humanitarian issues, encourage students to consider working with non-governmental organizations (such as MSF) post-graduation, and support MSF in advocacy campaigns. The chapter will work towards this by raising funds for MSF, our operational work, and specific campaigns through fundraising and volunteering. 

If you have a passion for public health, medicine, supporting underserved communities, or raising awareness for various health crises across the world, this organization is for you! Although we will not be providing direct care for those in need, we will indirectly impact people all around the globe. Most of our work will be done through fundraising and advocating for humanitarian issues, all while building a community of students with a passion for helping others.
summary: Friends of MSF supports and raises advocacy for Doctors Without Borders, a humanitarian organization that provides emergency aid and responds to health crises worldwide.

name: AI Vols
summary: AI Vols empowers UTK students to explore and apply artificial intelligence through hands-on projects, events, and collaboration across majors, thereby building skills, connections, and innovation in the growing world of AI.

name: Celebration Fellowship at University of Tennessee, Knoxville
description: CF-UTK is dedicated to serving the spiritual, academic, career, and social well-being of members.
summary: CF-UTK is dedicated to serving the spiritual, academic, career, and social well-being of members.

name: VOL About Books
summary: VOL About Books is to foster UT students’ love of reading with an inclusive and welcoming book discussion group. This club offers students a respite from course work reading and an opportunity to read purely for fun.

name: Chabad Jewish Vols
description:
The Chabad Jewish Vols Student Group at the University of Tennessee is dedicated to serving all Jewish students with Ahavat Yisrael—unconditional love and concern for every Jew, regardless of background or affiliation.
Guided by Chabad philosophy, our mission is to create a welcoming space that is truly “Your Jewish Home, Sweet Home” on campus. We strive to educate, inspire, empower, and involve students by promoting Jewish knowledge, awareness, and practice.
The Chabad Jewish Vols Student Group encourages every positive action on the part of every Jew and offers educational services to help every Jewish organization or group in its efforts to increase Jewish knowledge and observance.
Through engaging programs, Shabbat dinners, holiday celebrations, classes, and community service, the Chabad Jewish Vols Student Group seeks to help every student connect meaningfully to Jewish life, strengthen Jewish identity, and build friendships in an open and inclusive environment.
summary: The Chabad Jewish Vols at UT creates a welcoming space for all Jewish students, offering programs, Shabbat dinners, classes, and holiday events to inspire learning, strengthen identity, and build community.

name: The Sociology Club
summary: The mission of “The Sociology Club” is to provide a safe space where students can come and discuss social events while also fostering awareness of sociological theory, and research, and its implications on modern day America and the world.

name: Risk Management Society
description:
The purpose of the Risk Management Society is five-fold:

  • To promote interest in the field of Risk Management and Actuarial Science, as well as other areas such as finance, insurance, and consulting
  • To promote a better understanding of various fields among all students of The University of Tennessee
  • To further knowledge and to connect students across majors like Finance, Business, Mathematics, and Statistics through preparation for professional exams, guest speakers, career development events, and networking opportunities with industry professionals
  • To cultivate a fraternal spirit between its members
  • To cultivate personal acquaintance of its members for business and social betterment, so that the highest degree of mutual benefit and cooperation may be obtained
summary: See below

name: The Pilot's Association
description:
The Pilot’s Association is a student organization dedicated to bringing together pilots and student pilots at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. The club recognizes that there are far more pilots on campus than one might expect, yet opportunities to connect and share experiences as a flying community are limited. Its mission is to create a welcoming and inclusive space for all students who are passionate about aviation.

 

The primary goal of the Volunteer Pilots Association is to foster a strong sense of community among students who are pilots. Members have the opportunity to participate in aviation/flying related activities and events, share experiences, and learn from one another in a supportive environment. The club also hosts guest speakers from the aviation industry, including professional pilots, instructors, and aviation specialists, offering insights into careers, training, and the broader world of aviation.

 

Networking is another key focus of the club, connecting students with aviation professionals and alumni who can provide guidance, mentorship, and career opportunities. By facilitating these connections, the club helps students prepare for their future in aviation while building meaningful relationships with peers who share similar interests.

 

Above all, the Volunteer Pilot’s Association emphasizes community. It serves as a space where students can celebrate their passion for flying, exchange knowledge, and form lasting friendships. Whether a certified pilot, or a new student working toward a license, the Vol Pilots Club welcomes all students to join, learn, and take flight together.
summary: The University of Tennessee Pilot's Club brings together pilots and student pilots at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. The club offers aviation/flying events, networking opportunities, and a strong and supportive community for pilots at UTK.

name: Bold Campus Ministry
description: A campus ministry devoted to cultivating individual relationships with God in an inclusive atmosphere open to all religious perspectives. This love is shown by doing Bible Studies, having interactive events, and spending time together. Through this, we strive to inspire students to be their best selves, helping them thrive during their time in college.
summary: A campus ministry devoted to cultivating relationships with God in an inclusive atmosphere open to all religious perspectives. Inspiring students to be their best selves while creating bonds that help them prosper through college.

name: Young Americans For Prosperity
description:
Young Americans for Prosperity (YAFP) is a youth-centered leg of Americans for Prosperity (AFP). YAFP will advance similar goals to those of the national organization, including American civic education, authoring legislation, advocacy training, and helping each individual realize their version of the American Dream. 

The student-led organization will host regular meetings and special events at all times of the academic year. YAFP will also participate in the AFP-led legislative advancement trip to Nashville, TN, where students will have the opportunity to pass a crafted piece of legislation. The organization will encourage meaningful discussion about current events and urge students to find genuine solutions. As a campus chapter, YAFP's focus will be on campus issues above all. YAFP will explore public attitudes on campus, participate in student advocacy missions, and set an example for civic discussion.
summary: Young Americans for Prosperity (YAFP) is a youth-centered leg of Americans for Prosperity (AFP). YAFP will advance similar goals to those of the national organization, including American civic education, authoring legislation, and advocacy.

name: Rocky Top Racing
description: We are the first fully competitive wheel to wheel racing series at the University of Tennessee, fielding a 2023 Mazda MX-5 CRS Spec. Our team is an entrant for the sanctioning body, the Collegiate Racing Series. We are an academically cohesive program that brings the worlds of finance, marketing, public relations, and engineering all together to produce one product; the car racing on the track against countless other registered universities such as University of Georgia, University of Florida, University of Alabama, Purdue University, and more. We achieve this goal of educating by establishing a curriculum to bring students from maybe not knowing anything about running a racing team... to fully educated interdisciplinary members that know the ins and outs and can apply these skills to their future endeavors.
summary: The University of Tennessee's wheel to wheel competitive racing team, racing through the Collegiate Racing Series (CRS)

name: The B3 Club
summary: At the B3 club, our biggest desire is to inspire kids to reach their highest potential academically, socially, and physically. We partner with different programs to talk with kids, help them with their homework, and play some basketball!

name: Undergraduate Law Review At The University of Tennessee
description: The mission of the University of Tennessee Undergraduate Law Review is to establish an independent student-run academic journal dedicated to promoting rigorous undergraduate scholarship on law, public policy, and constitutional governance. In doing so, the Law Review will also establish a community of pre-law students seeking a forum for their analytical, comprehensive writing that engages legal institutions, democratic and political theory, and contemporary legal issues.
summary: The mission of the University of Tennessee Undergraduate Law Review is to establish an independent student-run academic journal dedicated to promoting rigorous undergraduate scholarship on law, public policy, and constitutional governance.

name: Data Science Club
description: The purpose of the Data Science Club is to grow and connect the next generation of data science professionals. Regular meetings and events will include group activities, workshops, presentations, and opportunities for exploration and professional development within the expanding field of data science.
summary: The purpose of the Data Science Club is to grow and connect the next generation of data science professionals. Regular meetings and events will include group activities, workshops, presentations, and opportunities for exploration and professional develop

name: Firearm Advocacy Law Society
description:
Purpose:
The purpose of this organization will focus around advocacy for maintaining and expanding firearm rights and legal protections inside and outside of the law school with the mission of publishing a yearly “Firearm Law Reference Guide of Tennessee” covering all active Tennessee statutes where it will be drafted and edited in the Fall semester and published in the Spring to provide greater access and education of current firearm laws for citizens of Tennessee and a reference guide for attorneys. The guide will be drafted to reflect current law without political commentary, focusing on accuracy and accessibility


Events:

Throughout the school year the organization will aim to host; a firearm safety class and routine range days to promote practiced and routine safe handling of firearms. 

If funds allow, send members to the yearly Gun Owners of America Leadership conference for networking and building connections in the industry.

Setting up an at least yearly, attorney-supervised pro bono educational clinic to assist individuals with understanding and preparing NFA Form 1 and Form 4 submissions, as well as the structure and purpose of NFA trusts. The event will focus on lawful compliance and access, with licensed attorneys overseeing all legal guidance to ensure families can responsibly and legally exercise Second Amendment rights.

Targeted Speakers:
The board will be focused on reaching out and connecting; with local attorneys that can speak on firearm crimes both from the defense and prosecutorial side, policy makers and firearm advocates, as well as in-house counsel within the industry to talk about what the typical life in the firearm industry looks like from the legal perspective.
summary: The Firearm Advocacy Law Society promotes academic study and professional engagement with firearm law through education, events, and publication of an annual Tennessee Firearm Law Reference Guide, drafted without political commentary only legal accuracy.

name: Women of Color In Praxis
description: Women of Color in Praxis (WOC in Praxis) is a student organization at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville dedicated to fostering an intellectual sisterhood that uplifts women of color through education, dialogue, and action. The organization centers political engagement, literacy, leadership development, and community involvement as tools for personal and collective empowerment. WOC in Praxis provides members with a supportive space to explore social issues, strengthen academic and professional skills, and translate knowledge into meaningful action through discussion-based meetings, programming, and service initiatives.
summary: Women of Color in Praxis is a student organization dedicated to uplifting women of color through intellectual engagement, leadership development, and community action.

name: Project Smile Global- University of Tennessee Chapter
summary: Project Smile Global University of Tennessee Knoxville Chapter is a part of a national nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization that works towards reducing oral health disparities in local communities.

name: Business Ethics by The Cross
summary: Business Ethics by the Cross builds an inclusive, Christ-centered community where Haslam students can explore ethical decision-making, develop as principled leaders, and learn directly from industry professionals navigating real-world ethical challenges.

name: E.N.D Alzheimer's
summary: Our mission is to raise awareness of Alzheimer’s disease, support individuals and families affected, and take action through E.N.D, Education, Networking, and Dedication.

name: Science Based Lifters Club
description:
The Science Based Lifters Club is a community dedicated to the intersection of exercise science and practical resistance training. We move beyond "bro-science" by utilizing peer-reviewed research to optimize hypertrophy, strength, and overall physical performance.

Our meetings go deep into the data, focusing on three core pillars:

Exercise Selection & Biomechanics: We analyze the effectiveness of specific exercises, comparing their stimulus-to-fatigue ratios and best use cases for individual muscle groups.

Supplementation Research: We vet the latest sports nutrition studies to discuss which supplements are backed by evidence and which are simply marketing hype.

Data-Driven Programming: We break down the variables of volume, frequency, and intensity to help members build optimized training protocols.

Whether you are a Kinesiology major or just someone tired of gym myths, join us to bridge the gap between the lab and the squat rack. Train smarter, not just harder.
summary: We bridge the gap between exercise science and the gym. Our members analyze peer-reviewed research on supplements, biomechanics, and exercise effectiveness to optimize hypertrophy and strength. Train with data to maximize your gains!

name: Strike Magazine
description:
Strike is a student-led organization committed to fostering creativity, collaboration, and meaningful engagement on campus. Our mission is to provide a supportive and inclusive environment where students can explore their interests, develop leadership and teamwork skills, and connect with like-minded peers. Through a variety of events, workshops, and collaborative projects, Strike encourages members to showcase their talents, contribute to campus initiatives, and make a lasting impact within the university community.

The organization values innovation, personal growth, and community involvement, offering opportunities for students to take on leadership roles, organize activities, and participate in programs that strengthen both professional and interpersonal skills. Strike is open to all students who are passionate about learning, creating, and making a difference, and it aims to cultivate a culture of engagement, inspiration, and empowerment that extends beyond the classroom.
summary: Strike is a student organization that brings together members to create, connect, and engage through events, projects, and community-building. It’s all about creativity, collaboration, and making an impact on campus.

name: UTK Club RedCross
description: Our organization strives to provide opportunities for students to serve the community, build professional skills, and contribute to lifesaving initiatives. Members can participate in volunteer activities such as organizing blood drives, supporting local blood drives, and fundraising for disaster relief. There are also opportunities for members to be trained in CPR certification, disaster preparedness, and Stop the Bleed training. There are many ways for members to get involved and gain hands-on experience in volunteer work as well as leadership and teamwork skills.
summary: UTK Club Red Cross is a student-led organization that partners with the local American Red Cross that support humanitarian service, emergency preparedness, and community outreach.

name: Red Sneakers for Oakley College Club of UTK
description: The mission of RSFO College Club is to promote food allergy awareness among students, faculty, and the broader community; advocate for epinephrine access everywhere, including residence halls, athletic facilities, and public spaces; and host inclusive events that empower students with food allergies and educate peers on safety and inclusion.
summary: The mission of RSFO College Club is to raise food allergy awareness on campus and beyond, advocate for accessible epinephrine everywhere, and host inclusive events that empower students with food allergies while educating peers on safety and inclusion.

name: The Verb Kind at UTK
summary: The Verb Kind at UTK recruits and organizes student volunteers to mentor justice-involved youth at the Knox County Juvenile Detention Center through The Verb Kind nonprofit, supporting mentorship, guidance, and positive community engagement.
